Our V, V-I color-magnitude diagram of Leo T reaches V 29 mag and shows features typical of a galaxy in transition between dwarf irregular and dwarf spheroidal types. A quantitative analysis of the star formation history, based on the comparison of the observed V, V-I CMD with the expected distribution of stars for different evolutionary scenarios, confirms that Leo T has a complex star formation history dominated by two enhanced periods about 1.5 and 9 Gyr ago, respectively. The distribution of stars and gas shows that the galaxy has a fairly asymmetric structure.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as the best-studied gas-rich ultrafaint dwarf benchmark, highlighting its low stellar mass, recent and sustained star formation, uncertain dynamical mass, and relevance to simulated reignition.

quotes.quote: Thanks to its early discovery and close proximity, Leo T is the best-studied example of such galaxies. Its low stellar mass, typical of ultrafaint dwarfs ( 10^5 , 7 ) is contrasted by a young stellar population born in the last 200 ( Irwin2007 ). Deep colour--magnitude measurements further reveal that Leo T has formed stars at a constant rate for, at least, the past 6 ( deJong2008, Clementini2012, Weisz2012 ). Radio observations show an extended \ reservoir that dominates over the mass of the stellar body, and with a cold and warm phase as expected from an interstellar medium ( RyanWeber2008, Adams2018 ). The lack of coherent \ rotation makes it challenging to determine Leo T's dynamical mass ( Adams2018, Patra2018 ), and stellar kinematics remain consistent with dynamical masses up to 3 10 (e.g. Errani2018, Forbes2018 ).
In preparation for these forthcoming observations, this paper studies the ability of low-mass dark matter haloes to play host to Leo T-like galaxies.

We finally note that the star formation rates and histories of the two higher mass galaxies provide a good match to those of low-mass dwarfs Leo T ( Clementini2012, Weisz2012 ) and Leo P ( McQuinn2015LeoP ).

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as its dwarf-galaxy target system, modeling its dark matter, mostly neutral inner hydrogen gas, gas density, column density, and temperature for PBH heating constraints.

quotes.quote: Throughout this work, we focus on the well-modelled Leo T dwarf galaxy as our target system due to its desirable cooling, gas, and DM properties. We stress, however, that our analysis is general and can be readily extended to other gas systems of interest We estimate that heating of Milky Way gas clouds leads to weaker bounds. . To describe Leo T, we follow the model of Refs. for the DM density, neutral hydrogen (HI) gas distribution, and ionization fraction.

The hydrogen gas in the inner r_s <350 pc of Leo T system is largely un-ionized . We consider only this central region, employing the average HI gas density of n_ H =0.07 cm ^ 3 , ionization fraction x_e = 0.01 and DM density of 1.75 GeV cm ^ 3 . Hence, the gas column density can be estimated as n_ H r_s = 9.72 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 and the mass column density as m_ H n_ H r_s = 1.627 10^ 4 g cm ^ 2 . The velocity dispersion of the HI gas in this region _v=6.9 km/s suggests a gas temperature of T 6000 K.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as a favorable dwarf-galaxy target for primordial-black-hole heating constraints, citing its low velocity dispersion, central atomic hydrogen, low metallicity, and dark-matter mass.

quotes.quote: Following Ref. , we focus on the Leo T irregular dwarf galaxy as a particularly favorable astrophysical system target.

We will focus on Leo T, a transition type of galaxy between a dwarf irregular and a spheroidal, which is a few hundred kpc away from the Milky Way. Leo T has been well studied and modeled theoretically and it has the desired properties, including low baryon velocity dispersion that is also favorable for PBH heating. In the inner 350 pc of Leo T, the gas is mostly composed of atomic hydrogen, but consists of highly ionized hydrogen outside this central region .

This average density of gas produces the column density of n r_ sys = 7.56 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 , in the right range for Leo T. Similarly, we assume a constant DM density of 1.75 GeV / cm ^3 which accounts for the total DM mass in Leo T of 10^ 7 M_ . The hydrogen gas in the central region of Leo T is comprised both of a non-rotating warm component with velocity dispersion of _g=6.9 km / s and temperature T 6000 K as well as a subdominant cold component.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo T as a faint, gas-rich isolated dwarf whose low luminosity, rotation velocity, continuing star formation, and BTFR placement challenge standard dwarf-formation models.

quotes.quote: Leo P giovanelli13 and Pisces A tollerud15 are the latest additions to a growing list of faint, gas-rich, isolated dwarf galaxies; a list that dates back at least to the discovery of Leo T irwin07 . These galaxies have luminosities of only a few 10^5 L_ ryanweber08,mcquinn13,tollerud15 and masses 3-4 times higher than their stellar mass. Rotation velocities, derived from radio observations of the , are estimated at v_ rot 12.5 km s^ 1 Leo T: ryanweber08 , v_ rot 17 km s^ 1 Pisces A: tollerud15 and v_ rot 18 km s^ 1 Leo P: giovanelli13,bernsteincooper14 . The available data suggest that all three dwarfs formed stars continuously, although at a very low and highly variable rate clementini12, weisz12, mcquinn13, mcquinn15 .
At a circular velocity of 15 km s^ 1 , comparable to Leo T, Leo P, and Pisces A, over 90 of all halos is predicted to be dark sawala14 .

In short, our simulations including feedback succeed in creating faint, gas-dominated dwarf galaxies lying on the BTFR, such as Leo T, Leo P, and Pisces A, while the control simulations without feedback do not.

sources.summary: The paper uses gas in the ultra-faint, gas-rich Leo T dwarf irregular galaxy to bound dark-matter interactions, emphasizing its low cooling rate and dark-matter-dominated Local Group status.

quotes.quote: We derive constraints on cross sections for dark matter interactions with ordinary matter at very low relative velocity, v_ rel 17 km/s, by requiring that the heating/cooling due to DM interacting with gas in the Leo T dwarf galaxy not exceed the radiative cooling rate of the gas. This gives strong limits on sub-GeV millicharged DM, which add to constraints on the recent EDGES 21 cm absorption anomaly. We also improve the bounds on DM-baryon and DM-electron cross-sections for arbitrary velocity dependences by combining the heating/cooling constraints from Leo T and suitable Galactic gas clouds, and we obtain stronger bounds than all the previous literature on ultra-light hidden photon DM for nearly all of the mass range 10^ 23 m_ DM 10^ 10 eV.

Properties of astrophysical systems used Leo T galaxy -- Leo T is an ultra-faint dwarf irregular galaxy located about 420 kpc from the Milky Way. It is the most dark-matter dominated dwarf in the Local Group and is also gas-rich, which makes it an unique astrophysical system to study DM scattering effects. Moreover Leo T is well-studied observationally and has garnered modelling attention.

sources.summary: The paper applies primordial-black-hole gas-heating limits to Leo T, emphasizing its transitional dwarf nature, low velocity dispersion, atomic hydrogen core, and modeled gas and dark-matter properties.

quotes.quote: We then apply our analysis to dwarf DM-rich galaxies, focusing on Leo T. Leo T is a transitional object between a dwarf irregular galaxy and a dwarf spheroidal galaxy that has been well studied and modeled theoretically. It has the desired properties, such as a low baryon velocity dispersion, making it a sensitive probe of PBH heating.

The gas in the inner region of Leo T, at a radius 350 pc from its center, is dominated by atomic hydrogen, while the gas outside is highly ionized 2013ApJ...777..119F . Since the free electrons in the ionized region cool very efficiently Smith:2016hsc , we limit our analysis to the central region of Leo T. From the model of , the hydrogen gas density is found to vary from 0.2 cm ^ 3 in the center to 0.03 cm ^ 3 at =350 pc .

sources.summary: The paper derives a cautious first systemic proper motion for Leo T, using looser Gaia cuts and radial velocities to support the membership solution.

quotes.quote: For Leo T and Cetus 2, the peak in f_ sat is clearly resolved with the looser cuts on the data. For Pictor 2, the situation is more ambiguous, but even here it appears that the favored model has f_ sat > 0 . Leo T has radial velocity data available, and the use of the looser quality cuts enables a solution to be determined using both Prior A and Prior B.

Nevertheless, no stars are assigned membership of Leo T that have inconsistent radial velocities. Most encouraging of all, all five stars with radial velocities that we determine to be members are also, completely independently, determined to be members by , where they use a combination of SDSS photometry and a selection of spectral characteristics to assign membership. These findings give us confidence that, despite the poorer quality photometry, our estimates of the proper motion of Leo T are reasonable given the data and priors.

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE observations of Leo T and its dark-matter halo to constrain optical ALP decay signals, improving prior coupling limits.

quotes.quote: We use MUSE spectroscopic observations of the dwarf spheroidal galaxy Leo T between 470 and 935 nm to search for radiative decays of axion like particles (ALPs). Under the assumption that ALPs constitute the dark matter component of the Leo T halo, we derive bounds on the effective ALP-two-photon coupling. We improve existing limits by more than one order of magnitude in the ALP mass range 2.7-5.3 eV.

These spectroscopic data cover the range 470-935 nm with a medium spectra resolution ( R= / >10^3 ) and excellent sensitivity, covering the area around the centre of Leo T, extending up to approximately its half-light radius. Previous astrophysical studies Grin:2006aw placed an upper limit on g_ of 5 10^ 12 GeV ^ 1 for ALP masses between 4.5 and 5.5 eV. In the following we will demonstrate that the MUSE data on Leo T improves current constraints by more of an order of magnitude for ALP masses between 2.7 and 5.3 eV.

sources.summary: Leo T has an existing proper motion and is assessed as a possible backsplash dwarf; a low tangential velocity would favor a Milky Way interaction, but current constraints are not useful.

quotes.quote: We find that NGC 6822, Leo A, IC 1613 and WLM have sufficient supergiants with reliable astrometry to derive proper motions. An additional three galaxies (Leo T, Eridanus 2 and Phoenix) are close enough that their proper motions have already been derived using red giant branch stars. Systematic errors in Gaia DR2 are significant for NGC 6822, IC 1613 and WLM. We explore the orbits for these galaxies, and conclude that Phoenix, Leo A and WLM are unlikely to have interacted with the Milky Way or M31, unless these large galaxies are very massive ( 1.6 10^ 12 M_ ). We rule out a past interaction of NGC 6822 with M31 at 99.99 confidence, and find there is a <10 chance that NGC 6822 has had an interaction with the Milky Way. We examine the likely origins of NGC 6822 in the periphery of the young Local Group, and note that a future interaction of NGC 6822 with the Milky Way or M31 in the next 4 Gyrs is essentially ruled out.

We explore the orbital parameter space for these isolated galaxies in light of these new measurement, and also include Leo T, Eridanus 2 and Phoenix, which have recent proper motion estimates from MV2020.

Leo T: If Leo T has a tangential velocity less than 80 km s ^ 1 , then it is probably a Milky Way backsplash system, as also concluded by . Interestingly, in the case of a very massive M31 (as in LG2), there is also a small region of parameter space in which Leo T could be a M31 backsplash system (this is also the case for Leo A; see later). However, the current proper motion measurement is utterly useless for providing any meaningful constraints in this respect. It is worth noting that the member stars of Leo T (and to a lesser extent, Leo A) have not been observed as frequently as in other dwarf galaxies as a result of the Gaia scanning law.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as one of six isolated Local Group dwarfs with extended stellar-halo observations, fitting ghostly-halo models to infer pre-reionization star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: We compare the model to available observations of stellar haloes in six isolated dwarf galaxies in the Local Group (Leo T, Leo A, IC 10, WLM, IC 1613, NGC 6822) to infer the star formation efficiency in dwarf galaxies at the epoch of reionization. We find an efficiency of star formation in dark matter haloes with masses 10^6-10^8 M _ at z 7 in rough agreement with independent methods using data on the luminosity function of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies but systematically higher by a factor of 3-5. The systematic uncertainty of our results is still large, mainly because available observations of stellar halo profiles do not extend over a sufficiently large distance from the center of the host dwarf galaxy. Additional observations, easily within reach of current telescopes, can significantly improve the accuracy of this method and can also be used to constrain the present day dark matter masses of dwarf galaxies in the Local Group.

KangR:2019 have compiled a list of six dwarf galaxies (Leo T, Leo A, WLM, IC 1613, IC 10, and NGC 6822) located in the Local Group, but outside the virial radii of the Milky Way and Andromeda, showing robust or tentative evidence of the existence of an extended stellar halo. In the collected observational data sets, radial density profiles of stars are provided out to several half-light radii from the central galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T's gas-rich dwarf-galaxy ISM as a dark-matter calorimeter, emphasizing its very low cooling rate, warm neutral gas, and low DM-baryon relative velocity.

quotes.quote: Following the methodology of WadFar21 , we use the interstellar medium of a gas-rich dwarf galaxy, Leo T, and a Milky Way-environment gas cloud, G33.4-8.0 to constrain DM. Leo T is a particularly strong system as its gas can have the lowest cooling rate among all the objects in the late Universe (owing to the low volume density and metallicity of the gas). Milky Way clouds, in some cases, provide complementary limits as the DM-gas relative velocity in them is much larger than that in Leo T.

The gas in the inner part of the Leo T galaxy is dominated by WNM with T 6100 K . The spatial profile of DM, hydrogen, and free electrons in Leo T was determined by Ref. upon fitting a hydrostatic model to column density observations and assuming that the DM follows a Burkert (cored) profile : _ DM = _0 (1+ r_s )(1+ ^2 ^2_s ) where is the radial distance from the halo center, r_s is the scale radius and _0 is the central core density. Recent observations suggest the presence of roughly constant density cores in most of the low-mass dwarf galaxies, therefore the choice of Burkert profile for Leo T is well-motivated.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo T as an ultra-faint dwarf with neutral gas and recent star formation, using MUSE and HST data to connect its young stars with the cold gas component.

quotes.quote: Leo T is the lowest mass galaxy known to contain neutral gas and to show signs of recent star formation, which makes it a valuable laboratory for studying the nature of gas and star formation at the limits of where galaxies are found to have rejuvenating episodes of star formation. Here we discuss a novel study of Leo T that uses data from the MUSE integral field spectrograph and photometric data from HST. The high sensitivity of MUSE allowed us to increase the number of Leo T stars observed spectroscopically from 19 to 75. We studied the age and metallicity of these stars and identified two populations, all consistent with similar metallicity of [ Fe/H ] 1.5 dex, suggesting that a large fraction of metals were ejected.

We find differences in the dynamics of young and old stars, with the young population having a velocity dispersion consistent with the kinematics of the cold component of the neutral gas. This finding directly links the recent star formation in Leo T with the cold component of the neutral gas.

sources.summary: The paper treats LeoT as a peculiar outer Milky Way dwarf with retained H I and recent star formation, requiring merged young and old populations for SDSS structural fitting.

quotes.quote: LeoT - Sitting in the outskirts of the MW at 400 , this peculiar galaxy has until now managed to retain some gas in the form of Hi irwin07,ryan-weber08 and has undergone a significant episode of star formation over the last Gyr or so irwin07,dejong08b . The SDSS data exhibit both young and old stars that are revealed to follow different density profiles from deeper data dejong08b but given the low number of LeoT stars in the SDSS, it is necessary to merge both young and old stellar populations in the same sample to allow the ML algorithm to converge. The parameters listed in Table 1 are therefore the overall parameters of the dwarf. They are nevertheless reasonably close to those determined by 0 , 0.0 and r_h 1.5' irwin07 from SDSS data or r_h=1.0 0.1' dejong08b from deeper LBT data. In this latter case, the difference in the r_h value could also be due to the different depths in the two datasets and mean that the shallow data are dominated by the more extended old population ( r_h=1.2 0.1' from the LBT data).

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as a gas-rich ultra-faint dwarf benchmark, emphasizing its distance, H I and stellar masses, ongoing star formation, and detectability limits.

quotes.quote: Leo T lies at distance of 420 kpc, safely outside the virial radius of the MW and was, until recently, the only gas-rich UFD discovered. Leo T is a star-forming galaxy with a HI mass of 2.8 10^5 , an HI diameter of 600 pc, an indicative dynamical mass within the HI extent of 3.3 10^6 , a total-mass-to-light ratio within the HI extent of 56, and a stellar mass of 1.2 10^5 \ 2008MNRAS.384..535R . Given its gas content and distance, Leo T likely represents an unperturbed UFD, allowing environmental effects to be disentangled from the evolution of the lowest-mass galaxies. Indeed, argue that Leo T is on its first infall to the Milky Way. Leo T is on the edge of detectability for SDSS; were it located further away, its stellar population would not have been detected 2010AdAst2010E...8K .

sources.summary: The paper models Leo T as a gas-rich, dark-matter-dominated dwarf with a resolved HI profile, using it to test minihalo models and infer halo and ambient-pressure constraints.

quotes.quote: As a consistency check we model the gas-rich dwarf galaxy Leo T, for which there is a well-resolved HI column density profile and a known distance (420 kpc). For Leo T we find that a subhalo with 300=8 ( 0.2) 10^6 best fits the observed HI profile. We derive an upper limit of 150 K for the pressure of any enveloping hot IGM gas at the distance of Leo T.
Leo T is a dark-matter dominated and non-rotating galaxy.
For Leo T we explicitly fit the observed HI column density profile, and also show that constraints set on the DM subhalo properties by just the HI sizes provide consistent results.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo T as the least massive dwarf in its ghostly-halo sample and the lone object suggesting a tentative sharp rise in first-galaxy star-formation efficiency at low halo mass.

quotes.quote: The only dwarf galaxy that produced a result that deviates significantly from the others is Leo T, which is also the least massive dwarf galaxy in the our sample. The results for Leo T are consistent with a significant increase of f_* for the mass range M_ dm 10^6-10^7 M _ . The results for Leo T are not consistent with the other dwarfs because are valid for halos with M_ dm,gal <10^7 M _ , which have a negligible impact on the properties of ghostly halos in the other dwarfs which are significantly more massive. If the results for Leo T are correct, the star formation efficiency appears to increase sharply from f_*=0.1 at M_ dm 10^7 M _ to f_*=5 at M_ dm 10^6 M _ . This result is very intriguing but tentative.

sources.summary: The review places Leo T near the ultra-faint boundary but excludes it from UFDs because it is brighter and uniquely retains neutral gas and recent star formation.

quotes.quote: Among the post-2005 discoveries, only four galaxies are within 1 magnitude of this boundary: CVn I ( M_ V = -8.7 ), Crater II ( M_ V = -8.2 ), Leo T ( M_ V = -8.0 ), and Eridanus II ( M_ V = -7.2 ). The first three of these systems stand out from the fainter population in obvious ways: CVn I is substantially more luminous, larger, and more metal-rich e.g., martin07,martin08,sg07,munoz18b , Crater II is a factor of 4 more extended than any fainter dwarf torrealba16 , and Leo T hosts neutral gas and recent star formation rw08,dejong08 . These objects more closely resemble the previously-known dSphs and dSph/dIrrs in the Local Group. Eridanus II, on the other hand, is distinct from other UFDs only in that it contains a star cluster crnojevic16b . Setting the dividing line such that it lands between Eridanus II and Leo T is therefore sensible, and minimizes the likelihood that future revisions to the absolute magnitudes of any of these systems will blur the boundary.
Gas Content Among the dwarfs discovered since the beginning of SDSS, only Leo T (which we do not consider a UFD; see Section sec:definition ) contains any neutral gas irwin07,rw08 .

sources.summary: The paper highlights Leo T as a Local Group gas-rich transitional dwarf with measured H I mass, radius, and dynamical mass, old and young stars, and mostly gaseous baryons.

quotes.quote: One of the most important recent discoveries in the Local Group was the galaxy Leo T (Irwin et al. 2007; Ryan-Weber et al. 2008). Based on new WSRT data, Adams Oosterloo (2018) give an \ mass of 4.1 10^5 \ (for D = 420 kpc), an \ radius of 3.3 arcmin (400 pc), and a dynamical mass of at least 1.4 10^7 . Leo T is a gas-rich `transitional' dwarf galaxy (see Fig. fig:wallaby-LG ) with both an old stellar population as well as new stars. It does not fit the idea that reionization suppressed star formation in low-mass dwarfs, although simulations suggest that some gas may remain (Revaz et al. 2009) or is subsequently gained through late accretion (Ricotti 2009). Evidence suggests that this gas can survive galactic winds and supernovae explosions as highlighted in the case of the Phoenix dwarf, where the gas is adjacent to the stars but regularly falls back to trigger more star formation (Young et al. 2007). Most baryons in Leo T are in , highlighting the potential to discover many more gas-rich dwarf galaxies in a widefield and high-resolution \ survey such as \ (DeFelippis, Putman Tollerud 2019).

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T in consistency checks and environmental interpretation, finding agreement between structural studies and citing work that suggests it may be a backsplash dwarf.

quotes.quote: Leo T featured in the MSOHO paper, as well as in H21, and Andromeda XVIII was in the PAndAS paper, as well as H21. Figure fig:compare shows a comparison of various structural parameters between these studies for these two galaxies. We find good agreement between the values derived for Leo T from MSOHO and H21, with measured values lying well within the uncertainties for all parameters.

A similar study by reaches similar conclusions regarding NGC 6822 and Leo T, but they find Cetus and Phoenix are slightly less likely to be backsplash systems (with probabilities of P 0.36 and 0.43, respectively; Tucana was not included in their analysis). Further, also suggests that Leo T is a backsplash dwarf, while Phoenix is argued to be on its first in-fall.

sources.summary: The paper compares its combined ALP-decay constraints with prior Leo T MUSE work and finds the earlier extrapolated dark-matter profile slightly overshot the directly derived profile.

quotes.quote: The analysis in  is based on spectroscopic observations of the Leo T dwarf spheroidal galaxy obtained with the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) at the Very Large Telescope (VLT)  .

In the right panel of Fig.  fig:bounds_fin , we compare the results of our combined analysis to Ref.  , obtained from the MUSE data of Leo T. The current analysis is typically more conservative, even though bounds are at a comparable level, and this is mainly due to the treatment of the DM profile. Indeed, in Ref.  , the profile was derived by extrapolating results from a Jeans analysis at larger radii, while here it is derived directly from data. We found that, in the case of Leo T, the extrapolation slightly overshoots the real DM profile.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Leo T as the sparsest sample galaxy, finds it currently comes closest to the Milky Way with Phoenix, and groups it with low-mass dwarfs dominated by early star formation.

quotes.quote: The bottom panel of Figure  figure:CMD_other shows the results for Leo T, which represents the sparsest galaxy in the sample.

Leo T and Phoenix come closest to the MW at present day, while IC 1613 and Peg dIrr come the closest to M31 at present day. Regardless of their proximity today, the orbits shown in Figures fig:orbits and fig:xy_yz_orbits do not suggest that these galaxies can be considered satellites of the MW or M31 at present with the possible exception of Peg dIrr (see below).

This is the case for our sample galaxies IC 1613, Leo I, Leo II, Leo A, Leo T, Phoenix, and WLM (see references provided in subsections below). Leo A, Leo T, Pegasus dIrr, Phoenix, Sag DIG, UGC 4879) we see a different pattern with much of the stellar mass being accumulated in the early universe, prior to the epoch of reionization, and low levels of star formation in more recent times Jacobs_2011,Weisz_2012,Weisz2014,Gallart2015 .

sources.summary: Leo T is treated as the least luminous Local Field dwarf, with mass constraints suggesting it may inhabit a comparatively massive halo despite large uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Measurements for the field dwarfs are from Kirby2013 where available; the constraints on Leo T and Tucana are from Simon2007 and Fraternali2009 , respectively. Three of the field dwarfs -- WLM, Pegasus, and Tucana -- also display evidence of rotation support, and are therefore not well described by the Wolf2010 methodology.

Note that the least luminous dwarf (Leo T) appears to reside in a fairly massive ( 30 ) halo, while the galaxy IC1613, which is 1000 times more luminous, appears to reside in a halo that is less massive ( 20 ). Given the large errors in Leo T's mass, the inferred halo sizes could be equal, but if there is any positive correlation between halo and stellar mass, it must be extremely weak. How does the implied relation compare to that expected from abundance matching?

sources.summary: The paper discusses Leo T as a gas-bearing ultra-faint dwarf near AGC198606, comparing its HI mass, size, and possible satellite-pair relation.

quotes.quote: This object is of particular note as it is located 16 \ and 1^ .2 from the gas-bearing ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Leo T while having a similar HI linewidth and approximately twice the flux density.

Leo T has a stellar mass of 1.2 10^5 , an HI mass of 2.8 10^5 , an HI radius of 300 pc (at the 2 10^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 level), and an indicative dynamical mass (based on Eqn eq:mdyn ) of 1 10^7 \ 2008MNRAS.384..535R . AGC198606 would have about twice the HI mass and size as Leo T, but its peak HI column density is significantly lower, potentially explaining the apparent lack of a stellar counterpart. In this scenario, the two systems would have a projected separation of 8.4 kpc and could be a bound pair of satellites similar to Leo IV and Leo V 2010ApJ...710.1664D .

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as an isolated, star-forming dwarf benchmark with measured histories, kinematics, photometry, and metallicities for comparison to the simulations.

quotes.quote: Before discussing the evolution of the dark matter distribution in our simulations, we first compare them with a host of observational data for two isolated field dwarfs: Leo T and Aquarius (also called DDO 210). These are two of the smallest galaxies in the Universe with on-going star formation 2007ApJ...656L..13I,2008MNRAS.384..535R,1959PDDO....2..147V . At 417^ +20 _ 19 kpc 2007arXiv0706.0516S and 1 Mpc 1999AJ....118..853L from any large spiral, they are extremely isolated. Both have measured star formation histories 2012ApJ...748...88W,2014ApJ...795...54C ; gas kinematics 2004A&A...413..525B,2008MNRAS.384..535R,2015AJ....149..180O ; stellar kinematics 2007arXiv0706.0516S,2014MNRAS.439.1015K ; photometry 2008ApJ...684.1075M,2006MNRAS.373..715M ; and stellar metallicity distribution functions 2012ApJ...748...88W,2013ApJ...779..102K,2014ApJ...795...54C .

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T-like initial conditions for dwarf-satellite stripping simulations, noting Leo T is an ultrafaint Milky Way satellite with old and young stellar populations indicating ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: Rather than taking a present day dSph and determining the gas, stellar, and dark matter properties of its pre-infall progenitor, we instead use initial conditions mimicking a current, gaseous low mass satellite of the MW, Leo T. We note that our initial conditions are not meant to be an exact fit to the observed gas and dark matter properties of this galaxy. Leo T is located at 420 kpc and was the first ultrafaint dwarf galaxy detected around the MW Irwin2007 . It contains both an old stellar population, 6-8 Gyr, and a young population, 200 Myr, suggesting ongoing star formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as a gas-bearing dwarf comparison point, deriving its central gas density and treating it as a key low-mass galaxy with HI.

quotes.quote: Beyond the virial radii of these galaxies, the detections Leo T, Phoenix and NGC6822 could be associated with the Milky Way, and LGS3, Pegasus dIrr and IC1613 with M31.
Leo T is the best HI detected comparison point for these galaxies and does have a /L _ V higher than most of these galaxies.

An estimate for the central gas density can be obtained using the current gas distribution in Leo T. find a peak column density in the central regions of 4.6 10^ 20 in their 117 32 parsec beam and they find an HI extent of 400 pc for the dwarf. This then results in an average gas density of 0.37 , which is within the range of values used in GP09 and similar to the typical values found in models within the central 100 pc emerick16 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as a newly discovered gas-rich Local Group dwarf whose low radial velocity and H I intensity make it difficult to distinguish from compact Galactic clouds.

quotes.quote: In addition to targeting Galactic clouds, the main thrust of this paper, we also investigated using this method to constrain the DGR of Local Group dwarf galaxies and potentially find new ones by examining the newly-discovered gas-rich dwarf galaxy Leo T. Gas-rich dwarf galaxies around the Milky Way are very difficult to distinguish in from compact Galactic clouds. Leo T, for example, has a radial velocity of only 30 with maximum HI intensity of 3.5K, comparable to typical Galactic local gas clouds (although relatively distinct from HVCs or LVHCs, which have a maximum HI intensity of 1 K, and are typically less compact). Finding such galaxies via their HI signature alone is of great interest, as recent studies have shown that dwarf galaxies beyond 300 kpc can have very high gas fractions, and therefore may be undetectable in starlight GP08 . Observations compiled by LF98 show that dwarf irregular galaxies with HI masses below 10^ 7.5 M_ have very low or undetectable DGRs. Thus, if Leo T, the only such confirmed dwarf galaxy in our observed area, does indeed have very low DGR, it is a confirmation that the same method by which we are attempting to distinguish LVHCs from other LVCs may be used to distinguish other dwarf galaxy candidates from compact Galactic HI clouds.

sources.summary: The paper identifies LeoT as one of the Local Group field dwarfs whose radius-velocity distribution gives a high likelihood of past passage through the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: Comparison of the radius-velocity distributions of VLII populations and measurements of Local Group galaxies presents a strong likelihood that Tucana, Cetus, NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185 have passed through the Milky Way.

From Table t4 we expect that the following Local Group Objects have with high likelihood ( > 50 per cent) at some point in time passed through the virial radius of the Milky Way: NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, Cetus, Tucana, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185.

The separation between the associated and unassociated populations in the distance-velocity plane in VLII was applied in the Local Group to identify field dwarfs that may be associated with the Milky Way: Tucana, Cetus, Antlia, NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT and NGC185.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as a prime low-mass dwarf example, giving its H I and stellar masses, recent star formation, and peripheral Milky Way location.

quotes.quote: Yet they survive, and field dwarfs even retain their gas reservoir. Leo T is a prime example: this galaxy has an \ mass of 4.2 10^5 \ and a stellar mass of only 10^5 \ . This galaxy has recent star formation and is on the periphery of the Milky Way: how has it retained its neutral gas reservoir?

sources.summary: The paper models Leo T as a dwarf galaxy whose observed gas distribution requires dark matter, finding a pseudo-isothermal halo that best explains the data.

quotes.quote: We apply our iterative method to a recently discovered dwarf galaxy Leo T and find that its observed distribution does not comply with the expected one if one assumes no dark matter in it.

To model the mass distribution in Leo T, we solve the Poisson's equation using a large number of trial dark matter halos and compare the model surface density ( _ HI ) profiles to the observed one to identify the best dark matter halo parameters.
For Leo T, we find a pseudo-isothermal halo with core density, _0 0.67 and core radius, r_s 37 parsec explains the observation best.

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo T as evidence for a reionization-quenching mass scale, emphasizing its gas reservoir, extended star-formation history, distance, and inferred halo mass.

quotes.quote: In particular, Leo T has a stellar mass of 10^ 5.5 , with a significant neutral gas reservoir rw08, adams17 and a complex star-formation history, including significant activity at z < 1 dejong08, clementini12, weisz12 . At a distance of >400 kpc from the Milky Way irwin07 , Leo T likely represents the tail of the star-forming field population, having a dark matter halo mass greater than that at which reionization suppresses gas cooling. Studies of stellar and gas kinematics in Leo T suggest a halo mass of 10^ 9 sg07, rw08 .

sources.summary: LeoT is described as a small galaxy beyond the Milky Way virial radius with H I gas, measured size and velocity dispersion, and no surprising GC absence.

quotes.quote: LeoT The LeoT galaxy lies just beyond the virial radius of the Milky Way at 410 kpc. It has a luminosity of M_V = --8 and HI gas mass of 2.8 10^5 2008MNRAS.384..535R It has = 178 pc and = 7.5 km/s and a halo mass quoted by 2017MNRAS.467.2019R of log M_ 200 = 8.54--8.88. For this halo mass we expect 1 GC, thus the apparent lack of a GC is not surprising.

sources.summary: The paper describes Leo T as one of the lowest-mass Solo dwarfs, notable for young stars, transition-type properties, HI structure, and uncertain backsplash status.

quotes.quote: Leo T Leo T is one of lowest mass and closest dwarfs in Solo . It is notable for being one of the few very faint dwarfs with a young stellar population, and like Peg DIG, it is often described as a transition-type dwarf, given that its other characteristics are generally typical of dwarf spheroidal galaxies. determined its structural parameters based on deep, wide-field Large Binocular Telescope data, and it is also included in the MegaCam Survey of Outer Halo Satellites Munoz2018b,Munoz2018a , using CFHT gr photometry. Its HI structure was most recently studied by . argued that both Leo T and NGC 6822 have likely had a close pericentric passage with the Milky Way, whereas suggested that there is only an approximately 50 chance of Leo T being a backsplash galaxy.

gmw_00001326.name: PSO J334
gmw_00001326.overview: PSO J334 is a luminous, lobe-dominated quasar located at a redshift of z = 2.06 [arXiv:2306.17632]. The source features a parsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:1902.07433, arXiv:2306.17632] and kiloparsec-scale lobes spanning 66 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1703.10227, arXiv:1902.07433], with a rare misalignment between these structures [arXiv:1703.10227, arXiv:2306.17632].

sources.summary: The paper studies PSO J334.2028+01.4075 as a putative binary-black-hole quasar, finding VLBA components, time-variable VLA emission, large-scale lobes, and a rare parsec-to-kiloparsec jet twist.

quotes.quote: In 2015 Liu et al. \ reported an optical-continuum periodicity in the quasar PSO J334.2028+01.4075, with the estimated mass and rest-frame period suggesting an orbital separation of about 0.006 pc (0.7 arcsec). The persistence of the quasar's optical periodicity has recently been disfavored over an extended baseline. However, if a radio jet is launched from a sub-parsec BBH, the binary's properties can influence the radio structure on larger scales.

Here, we use the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) and Karl G. \ Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) to study the parsec- and kiloparsec-scale emission energized by the quasar's putative BBH. We find two VLBA components separated by 3.6 mas (30 pc), tentatively identifying one as the VLBA ``core'' from which the other was ejected. The VLBA components contribute to a point-like, time-variable VLA source that is straddled by lobes spanning 8 arcsec (66 kpc). We classify PSO J334.2028+01.4075 as a lobe-dominated quasar, albeit with an atypically large twist of 39 between its elongation position angles on parsec- and kiloparsec-scales. By analogy with 3C 207, a well-studied lobe-dominated quasar with a similarly-rare twist, we speculate that PSO J334.2028+01.4075 could be ejecting jet components over an inner cone that traces a precessing jet in a BBH system.

sources.summary: The paper treats PSO J334 as a quasar and SMBHB candidate, reviewing its proposed optical periodicity, radio jet structure, and new 1.7 GHz EVN imaging.

quotes.quote: Since it is extremely rare to find supermassive black hole binaries (SMBHBs), the discovery of PSO J334.2028+1.4075 (FBQS J2216+0124; PSO J334 hereafter) attracted considerable interest. Supermassive black holes usually reside in large elliptical galaxies. Their mass could reach 10^ 10 M _ . Although SMBHBs might seem as common objects due to galaxy mergers, it is problematic to detect them, especially at small separations . The SMBHB candidate quasar PSO J334 was selected via a systematic search in the data of the Pan-STARSS1 Medium Deep Survey . Based on the observed period in the variation of the optical flux ( 542 days) and the estimated total black hole mass ( 10^ 10 M _ , with a mass ratio of 0.05<q<0.25 ), an orbital separation of 0.006 pc was inferred.

PSO J334 is a radio source and its structure has been investigated at several radio frequencies with the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) and the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) interferometers . According to the VLBA images obtained at four frequencies from 4.4 to 15.4 GHz, the quasar is resolved into two components, a compact core and a jet. Their distance is 3.6 milliarcseconds (mas), corresponding to 30 pc projected linear separation . According to the VLA images, the extended jet and lobe structure is stretching 66 kpc from the opposite sides of the core.
We studied the radio structure of PSO J334 with the technique of very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) using the European VLBI Network (EVN) at 1.7 GHz.

sources.summary: PSO J334 is presented as a luminous quasar once proposed as a binary SMBH; new EVN/VLA imaging instead favors a lobe-dominated jetted AGN with restarted jet activity and no clear binary evidence.

quotes.quote: PSO J334.2028+1.4075 (PSO J334) is a luminous quasar located at redshift z = 2.06 . The source gained attention when periodic flux density variations were discovered in its optical light curve. These variations were initially interpreted as the variability due to the orbital motion of a supermassive black hole binary (SMBHB) residing in a single circumbinary accretion disk.

We aim to study both the large- and small-scale radio structures in PSO J334 to provide additional evidence for or against the binary scenario. We observed the source at 1.7 GHz with the European Very Long Baseline Interferometry Network (EVN), and at 1.5 and 6.2 GHz with the VLA, at frequencies that complement the previous radio interferometric study. Our images reveal a single component at parsec scales slightly resolved in the southeast–northwest direction and a lobe-dominated quasar at kiloparsec scales with a complex structure.

While we also observe a misalignment between the inner jet and the outer lobes, we suggest that this is due to the restarted nature of the radio jet activity and the possible presence of a warped accretion disk rather than due to the perturbing effects of a companion SMBH. Our analysis suggests that PSO J334 is most likely a jetted AGN with a single SMBH, and there is no clear evidence of a binary SMBH system in its central engine.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PSO J334 as a binary-AGN candidate, using Chandra and multi-wavelength data to find X-ray and SED behavior more consistent with a normal AGN.

quotes.quote: We present analysis of the first Chandra observation of PSO J334.2028+01.4075 (PSO J334), targeted as a binary-AGN candidate based on periodic variations of the optical flux. With no prior targeted X-ray coverage for PSO J334, our new 40 ksec Chandra observation allows for the opportunity to differentiate between a single or binary-AGN system, and if a binary, can characterize the mode of accretion.

Similarly, PSO J334, at a redshift of z=2.06 , was identified as a potential binary system based on periodic variation of the optical flux on an observed timescale of 542 days, corresponding to a separation a 0.006 pc liu2015 . However, recently have shown that the data presented on PG 1302-102 and PSO J334 are not strong enough to support the model of a binary-AGN system.

We find the X-ray emission to be well-fit with an absorbed power-law, incompatible with the simple cavity scenario. Further, we construct an SED of PSO J334 by combining radio through X-ray observations and find that the SED agrees well with that of a normal AGN, most likely incompatible with the minidisk scenario. Other analyses, such as locating the quasar on IR color-color diagrams and analyzing the quasar mass predicted by the fundamental plane of black hole activity, further highlight the similarity of PSO J334 with respect to normal AGN.

gmw_00001327.name: NGC 4477
gmw_00001327.overview: NGC 4477 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:0912.0274] featuring a strong bar that drives gas dynamics, spiral inflows, and star formation [arXiv:0912.0274]. The galaxy hosts a central spiral disk and pseudobulge [arXiv:1409.2876], and it contains a compact, unresolved radio source with a steep spectrum [arXiv:1902.10670].

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 4477 as a compact, unresolved radio source, marks it marginal in L-band, and finds a steep radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: We detected radio emission in six out of ten sources: NGC 3185, NGC 3941, NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725. Images of the detected sources (contours and coloured maps) are shown in Figs fig:contourMaps3185 fig:contourMaps4725 . A source is considered detected if the peak intensity is 3 . However, a source exhibiting a peak intensity 3 S_ P < 6 is considered as marginal detection, following the same criterion as in HU01 . Following this criterion, the detection rates are 5/10 in the L-band (with NGC 4477 and NGC 4698 as marginal detections), 6/10 in the C-band (with NGC 3941 as marginal detection), 5/10 in the X band (with NGC 3185 as marginal detection) and 4/10 in Ku band. As can be seen from images shown in Figs fig:contourMaps3185 fig:contourMaps4725 , four out of six sources (NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725) are are not resolved in our VLA observations, and for them we could only set an upper limit to their sizes which correspond to half of the beam size, in analogy with HU01 .

We have built radio spectra for the sources which have been detected in two or more frequencies: NGC 3185, NGC 3941, NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725 (Fig. fig:radiospectra upper limits are represented as arrows), considering all the available data points. We performed a weighted linear least squares fit using the Sci-Py scipy routine curve fit , weighting for the uncertainties in the flux densities. In three cases, NGC 4477, NGC 4639 and NGC 4698, the targets were found to fit a typical power-law S_ ^ I ^ to the spectrum We define a steep spectrum as one having 0.5, and a flat one as 0.5, following PG13 , with S_ ^ . We also define an inverted spectrum as one having 0 . For NGC 4639 we considered only the 5.5, 9 and 14 GHz data points, as the 1.5 GHz data point seems to deviate with respect to the power-law behaviour, being probably associated with a different emitting component which dominates at lower frequencies. We found an inverted spectral index for NGC 4639 and NGC 4698 of = 0.40 0.01 and = 0.13 0.07, respectively, and a steep spectral index of = +0.52 0.09 for NGC 4477.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4477 as an outlier early-type galaxy whose strong bar shapes gas dynamics, drives spiral structure, funnels gas toward the ILR, and links stars, gas, and star formation.

quotes.quote: Outliers While the two classes described above neatly bring together two groups of early-type galaxies that have a large number of similar properties, several ``outlier" galaxies in our sample (NGC 2685, NGC 2974, NGC 4477) resist such straightforward classification.

Finally, NGC 4477, though unremarkable in the morphology of its star-forming region (Figure profiles a ), is notable for being the most strongly barred of the star-forming galaxies in our sample (see Figure Main-SF ), and the effects of this bar on the dynamics of the system are significant. The ionized gas kinematics are driven by the bar, producing a spiral inflow structure that creates a central star-forming disk kinematically aligned with the bar (Figure Main-SF ). The resulting strong misalignment between the stars and the ionized gas is another expected consequence of the bar, since the gas is confined to the bar's circular, non-intersecting orbits. In this sense, NGC 4477 resembles the \ galaxies in a fundamental way - the stars, ionized gas, and star formation activity are strongly related dynamically - despite more superficial differences.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4477 as well fit by bulge-bar-disk decomposition, with HST-resolved dust and inner spiral arms matching a small dispersion drop and implying a central disk plus pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: NGC 4477: The residuals obtained from the photometric decomposition of this galaxy are quite low, indicating that the parametric model for the bulge, bar, and disk describes the SBD well. The central region of this galaxy is unveiled thanks to the HST /WFPC2 image in the F606W band. Strong dust obscuration is seen throughout the bar region and inner spiral arms are clearly seen in the unsharp masked image (Fig. fig:unsharp ). The radius of this structure is =1.5 which corresponds to the small drop ( r_ drop =1.5 ) present in the velocity dispersion profile. We suggest that this galaxy hosts an central spiral disk and a pseudobulge .

gmw_00001328.name: NGC 4725
gmw_00001328.overview: NGC 4725 is a double-barred galaxy [arXiv:2010.04005, arXiv:1302.5701] hosting a classical bulge [arXiv:2010.04005] and a Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:2010.04005, arXiv:1503.01836]. It is interacting with the neighboring galaxy NGC 4747 [arXiv:2404.11656, arXiv:astro-ph/0610114], which has caused a small distortion in its neutral hydrogen distribution [arXiv:2404.11656] and created asymmetries in its low-surface-brightness ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0610114].

sources.summary: NGC 4725 is treated as an interacting, double-barred galaxy with a Seyfert nucleus and classical bulge; its non-nuclear bar region lacks molecular/neutral gas and recent star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4725 ( (J2000) = 12:50:26.6 and (J2000) = + 25:30:03) is part of the interacting pair of galaxies NGC 4725 and NGC 4747, which belongs to the spiral-rich Coma I group of galaxies Gregory_1977,Wevers_1984 . However, we note that this galaxy is marked as a field galaxy in the identification of barred galaxies and environment by vandenberg_2002 .

This is a double-barred galaxy with a peculiar long bar that twists sharply along the length of the bar Erwin_2004,Erwin_2005 . An AGN is located in the central region of the galaxy and is classified as Seyfert 2 Ho_1997b . The galaxy also hosts a classical bulge Fisher_2010 .

When we compare the FUV NUV colour map and profile with the HI and H _ 2 maps and profiles, the circular region without the central nuclear region lacks molecular/neutral hydrogen and star formation. The central sub-kpc nuclear region of the galaxy hosts molecular gas content, little star formation, and HI. The length of the region that is covered by the stellar bar coincides with the region that is devoid of recent star formation and HI.

sources.summary: NGC 4725 is one of the double-barred sample galaxies; its velocity and h3 maps support a kinematically decoupled inner stellar disc and a younger, metal-rich inner bar.

quotes.quote: The sample is composed of four double-barred galaxies: NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 4725, and NGC 5850. These galaxies were selected from the catalogue of Erwin2004 , paying special attention to the length and size of the inner bars. The main requirement for building the sample was that the inner bars were big enough to be well mapped within the \ field-of-view (hereafter FoV), but they were also small enough to allow the mapping of the transition regions between the two bars.

However, for the three cases highlighted in Figure fig:discs (NGC 2859, NGC 4725, and NGC 5850), local velocity maxima and minima appear between 5 and 10 arcseconds of the galaxy centre, aligned also with the kinematical major axis. These decoupled structures are rotating faster than their surroundings and are probably indicating the presence of stellar inner discs. This hypothesis is supported by the h _3 maps also included in Figure fig:discs , which show a clear anticorrelation with respect to the stellar velocities exactly at the locations of those features, as expected in the case of discs BureauandAthanassoula2005 .
The main result of this work is the distinct stellar populations found for the inner bars of the four galaxies: they are clearly younger and more metal-rich than the outer bars.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4725 as a double-barred galaxy with uncertain nuclear radio emission, interaction with NGC 4747, a nearby gas-rich tidal tail, and small HI distortion.

quotes.quote: The latter, i.e., NGC 4725, was identified as a double-barred galaxy by , but suggested that, at present, the inner bar is playing a minor role in promoting major morphological changes. attempted to measure its nuclear radio emission, including this object among a sample of low-luminosity Seyfert galaxies VeronCetty2006 . However, their Very Large Array (VLA) observations produced an unresolved image and a peculiar radio spectrum, which would deserve further investigation to derive meaningful information from this source.

NGC 4725 is the brightest member of the Coma I aggregation Gregory1977 and seems to be interacting with the neighbouring spiral galaxy NGC 4747 Haynes1979 . Gravitational interactions between these two galaxies likely resulted in a gas-rich tidal tail that extends about 8 ( 30 kpc) to the north-east of NGC 4747 and appears to contain two distinguishable star clusters LeeWaddell2016, LeeWaddell2018 . On the other hand, only a relatively small distortion in the HI distribution is observed in NGC 4725.

sources.summary: The paper presents integral-field kinematics for NGC 4725, finding inner-bar velocity-dispersion hollows explained as contrast between the hot bulge and the colder, more ordered inner bar.

quotes.quote: We present \ integral-field stellar velocity and velocity dispersion maps for four double-barred early-type galaxies: NGC 2859, NGC 3941, NGC 4725 and NGC 5850. The presence of the inner bar does not produce major changes in the line-of-sight velocity, but it appears to have an important effect in the stellar velocity dispersion maps: we find two hollows of amplitudes between 10 and 40 km s ^ 1 on either side of the center, at the ends of the inner bars. We have performed numerical simulations to explain these features. Ruling out other possibilities, we conclude that the hollows are an effect of the contrast between two kinematically different components: the high velocity dispersion of the bulge and the more ordered motion (low velocity dispersion) of the inner bar.

sources.summary: The paper detects extremely faint polarized emission in NGC 4725 at both minor-axis ends, avoiding the bar and lying mostly on the ring-like structure's outer periphery.

quotes.quote: NGC 4725 Extremely faint polarized emission is detected in this moderately inclined barred spiral galaxy, with the polarized emission originating at both ends of the minor axis. The polarized emission avoids the bar, which is at a position angle of about 45 degrees, and is mostly found on the outer periphery of the ring-like structure. The polarized emission is too faint to allow investigation of its detailed properties; deeper observations would be required. It is not possible to say anything about the magnetic field orientation, as too little signal is available.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4725's lopsided and ring-like far-infrared color structures, considers cirrus unlikely, and finds mixed heating in ring and non-ring regions.

quotes.quote: Sometimes the 250/350 m color temperatures appear enhanced in lopsided regions that do not correspond to enhancements in either the H or 3.6 m surface brightness, most notably in NGC 4725, 5457, 6946 and 7793.

NGC 4725, 5457, and 7793, however, are at higher latitudes where cirrus structures should not has as much of an effect on the data. Moreover, the mean and standard deviation in the background _ 353 GHz values for these galaxies are actually below the median values for the sample overall, so it is unlikely that lopsided structures in the colour temperature maps of these galaxies are related to foreground cirrus emission.

We also see in the 160/250 m maps for NGC 4725 and 4736 that star forming regions may contribute more to heating dust within ring-like structures seen at these wavelengths but that the evolved stellar population contributes more to the dust heating in other locations.

sources.summary: The paper newly detects NGC 4725 in the VLA observations, describes it as unresolved, and notes that its peculiar spectrum needs further study.

quotes.quote: We detected radio emission in six out of ten sources: NGC 3185, NGC 3941, NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725. Images of the detected sources (contours and coloured maps) are shown in Figs fig:contourMaps3185 fig:contourMaps4725 . A source is considered detected if the peak intensity is 3 . However, a source exhibiting a peak intensity 3 S_ P < 6 is considered as marginal detection, following the same criterion as in HU01 . Following this criterion, the detection rates are 5/10 in the L-band (with NGC 4477 and NGC 4698 as marginal detections), 6/10 in the C-band (with NGC 3941 as marginal detection), 5/10 in the X band (with NGC 3185 as marginal detection) and 4/10 in Ku band. As can be seen from images shown in Figs fig:contourMaps3185 fig:contourMaps4725 , four out of six sources (NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725) are are not resolved in our VLA observations, and for them we could only set an upper limit to their sizes which correspond to half of the beam size, in analogy with HU01 .
NGC 4725 exhibits a peculiar spectrum and further observations are needed to derive meaningful information for this source.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4725 as a double-barred galaxy with uncertain Seyfert 2 classification, a nuclear hard X-ray source, old central stars, and absorption-dominated GNIRS spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4725 (S2:) NGC 4725 is a double-barred galaxy in which a handful of supernovae have occurred in recent decades. The optical classification of NGC 4725 as a Seyfert 2 is somewhat uncertain Ho97 and although a hard X-ray source is detected at the nucleus, its luminosity is comparable to that of surrounding, off-nuclear sources Ho01 . Although younger than the outer regions of the galaxy, the central stellar population is fairly old, with a luminosity-weighted mean age in the region of 4-5 Gyr deLorenzo-Caceres13 .

sources.summary: The paper detects a low-surface-brightness ring and inner disk in NGC 4725, links the ring to known star formation, and attributes asymmetries to interaction with NGC 4747.

quotes.quote: A low surface brightness ring and inner disk are detected in this galaxy. The ring is coincident with the star-forming ring for which this galaxy is known (Buta ). Its interaction with nearby NGC 4747 (Wevers et al. ) may be responsible for the asymmetries seen in our images.

gmw_00001329.name: IC 3167
gmw_00001329.overview: IC 3167 is a dwarf lenticular galaxy infalling into the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2206.06548, arXiv:2312.14017]. The galaxy hosts a lopsided, slowly rotating weak bar [arXiv:2206.06548, arXiv:2312.14017] and exhibits traces of recent star formation in its central regions [arXiv:2308.16768].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 3167 as a Virgo dwarf lenticular with a lopsided weak bar, measuring its photometry, kinematics, bar strength, and slow pattern speed.

quotes.quote: We present surface photometry and stellar kinematics of IC 3167, a dwarf galaxy hosting a lopsided weak bar and infalling into the Virgo cluster. We measured the bar radius and strength from broad-band imaging and bar pattern speed by applying the Tremaine-Weinberg method to stellar-absorption integral-field spectroscopy. We derived the ratio of the corotation radius to bar radius ( R =1.7^ +0.5 _ 0.3 ) from stellar kinematics and bar pattern speed. The probability that the bar is rotating slowly is more than twice as likely as that the bar is fast. This allows us to infer that the formation of this bar was triggered by the ongoing interaction rather than to internal processes.

To start addressing this issue, here we report a detailed photometric and kinematic study of IC 3167 (VCC 407, Fig. fig:img_sdss ). This is a dwarf lenticular galaxy in the Virgo cluster Kim2014 with band effective radius R_ e , = 1.5 kpc and absolute magnitude M_r=-17.62 mag Lisker2006 assuming a distance of 17 Mpc. Its stellar mass is M_ = 10^ 9.06 M _ Bidaran2020 . In spite of being initially classified as an early-type dwarf galaxy, IC 3167 hosts an inclined disc Lisker2006 and a bar ( R_ bar 14.0 arcsec, Janz2014 ). The galaxy does not have any bright nearby companion, but it belongs to a bound group of dwarf galaxies recently accreted onto the Virgo cluster and observed at a cluster-centric distance of 1.5 Mpc with a LOS velocity of 700 km s ^ 1 with respect to M87 Lisker2018 .

sources.summary: VCC0407 has younger, more metal-rich central populations, no detected alpha-enhancement variation, and a significant positive age gradient interpreted as evidence for recent star formation.

quotes.quote: VCC0407: The stellar populations confined in the central regions of this dE are more metal-rich and somewhat younger than in its outskirts. We did not detect any variation in [ /Fe ] values across this galaxy.
We detect significant positive age gradients in three dEs of our sample, namely VCC0170, VCC0407 and VCC1833.

From this work, we conclude that: Except for VCC0170, VCC0407 and VCC1833, the main sample dEs show flat age radial profiles with negligible fluctuations in value (-0.02 < _ Age < 0.12). Among the exceptions here VCC0170 is still actively forming stars in its inner regions and the other two show traces of recent star formation episodes.

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 3167 as a Virgo dwarf comparison object: a lenticular with a slowly rotating lopsided bar likely shaped by cluster or group interactions.

quotes.quote: The barred galaxy IC 3167 originally presented by was observed within the same observational campaign of NGC 4483 and NGC 4516. It is a lenticular galaxy with stellar mass of M_ =1.2 10^9 Roediger2017 .

Using MUSE IFS and SDSS i band image, were able to characterise the bar properties and apply the TW method. The bar in IC 3167 is slowly-rotating, with R =1.7^ +0.5 _ 0.3 . Given the peculiar nature of this bar and that IC 3167 is part of a group of galaxies in-falling in the Virgo Cluster, the formation of the lopsided bar could be induced by the ongoing gravitational interaction with the cluster or by an interaction within the group of in-falling galaxies it belongs to.

gmw_00001330.name: NGC 1044
gmw_00001330.overview: NGC 1044 is a radio galaxy situated within a group-scale hot-gas environment [arXiv:0707.1853]. It features naked radio jets that remain well collimated over large distances before spreading into plumes or tails [arXiv:0707.1853].

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 1044 in a group-scale hot-gas environment and contrasts its high external-to-internal pressure ratio and naked, collimated jets with lobed FRI sources.

quotes.quote: The sample consists of 3C 76.1, NGC 1044, 3C,296, 3C 31, NGC 315 (new XMM-Newton observations), 3C 66B, 3C 449, NGC 6251 (observations published in c03 and ev05 ), NGC 4261 (archival data set). All nine radio galaxies are found to lie in group-scale hot-gas environments, with bolometric X-ray luminosities ranging from 10^ 41 - 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

Fig. profs shows the external pressure profiles for the two sources with the most extreme behaviour: 3C 296 (top left) appears to be in approximate pressure balance, assuming equipartition, so that no additional pressure contribution is required; NGC 1044 (bottom left) has a high ratio of P_ ext /P_ int ( 40 ). Fig. profs also shows the corresponding radio maps for the two sources. It is clear that their radio morphologies are quite different: NGC 1044 has naked jets that remain fairly well collimated over large distances before gradually spreading into plumes or tails, whereas the jets of 3C 296 appear to enter radio lobes at a small distance from the nucleus.

To test this hypothesis, I separated the 16 radio lobes into the two categories of `naked jet' sources and `lobed' sources. The sources classified as having `naked jets' are: 3C 31, NGC 1044, NGC 315, 3C 449, NGC 6251; those categorised as `lobed' are: 3C 296, 3C 76.1, NGC 4261, 3C 66B. The right-hand panel of Fig. pressures shows a histogram of the pressure ratios for the two categories, which demonstrates that there is indeed a signficant difference in the two populations in the expected sense: the `lobed' sources are typically closer to pressure balance at equipartition than the `naked jet' sources.

gmw_00001331.name: 3C 296
gmw_00001331.overview: Hosted by the boxy elliptical galaxy NGC 5532 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412599, arXiv:0712.0595], 3C 296 is a low-luminosity Fanaroff-Riley Class I radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0608088, arXiv:1107.2511] classified as a Seyfert [arXiv:1404.0620]. It features twin decelerating radio jets [arXiv:astro-ph/0608088, arXiv:astro-ph/9703129] aligned perpendicular to an embedded, truncated edge-on disk [arXiv:0712.0595].

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 296 as a nearby low-luminosity FR I radio galaxy with twin jets, lobes, bridge emission, deceleration, and anisotropic magnetic-field structure.

quotes.quote: We present new, deep 8.5-GHz VLA observations of the nearby, low-luminosity radio galaxy 3C 296 at resolutions from 0.25 to 5.5 arcsec. These show the intensity and polarization structures of the twin radio jets in detail. We derive the spectral-index distribution using lower-frequency VLA observations and show that the flatter-spectrum jets are surrounded by a sheath of steeper-spectrum diffuse emission. We also show images of Faraday rotation measure and depolarization and derive the apparent magnetic-field structure. We apply our intrinsically symmetrical, decelerating relativistic jet model to the new observations.

An optimized model accurately fits the data in both total intensity and linear polarization. We infer that the jets are inclined by 58^ to the line of sight. Their outer isophotes flare to a half-opening angle of 26 ^ and then recollimate to form a conical flow beyond 16 kpc from the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA observations of the FRI radio galaxy 3C296, using jet-counterjet asymmetries and polarization to infer relativistic spine deceleration and structured jet edges.

quotes.quote: 3C296 is another nearby bright FRI radio galaxy, and is an excellent object to study in the context of relativistic beaming models, because its jets (like those of 3C31 ) are straight. In this paper we present detailed observations of the total intensity and polarization of its jets, and discuss the extent to which it fits proposed models for relativistic beaming and deceleration. 3C296 has a redshift of 0.0237 ( P_ 178 = 2.8 10^ 24 W Hz ^ 1 sr ^ 1 ; at this distance 1 arcsec = 0.67 kpc, and the total linear size of the radio source is 300 kpc).

The greatest jet-counterjet ratio on this map (on the axis of the jet at 4 kpc from the core) is 12, which translates using the formula above to = 0.44 ; thus 63 in the beaming model, and the velocity of the jet at its fastest is 0.44c . Fig. \ 3C296-sslice shows the sidedness of the central regions as a function of distance along the jet. It will be seen that the jet is very asymmetrical only at the base, with the side-to-side asymmetry parameter R falling, by 6 kpc (9 arcsec) from the core, to values around 2, corresponding to 0.15 ; this drop in R corresponds in position to the change from parallel to perpendicular field and implies substantial deceleration at around this point (the inferred velocity drops by a factor 2 on scales of 1 kpc, though this does not imply that the true deceleration is this dramatic if there is a range of bulk velocities in the jet).

The jet-counterjet surface brightness ratios in 3C296 are consistent with recently developed models for decelerating relativistic jets, provided that substantial deceleration can take place on scales of a few kpc while allowing mildly relativistic velocities to persist at 10-kpc scales. Environmental asymmetries may affect the low-velocity edges of the jets, but it appears that transverse velocity gradients need not always cause longitudinal apparent magnetic field.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 296 as an FRI radio galaxy in group gas whose lobes are near pressure balance and begin close to the nucleus.

quotes.quote: The sample consists of 3C 76.1, NGC 1044, 3C,296, 3C 31, NGC 315 (new XMM-Newton observations), 3C 66B, 3C 449, NGC 6251 (observations published in c03 and ev05 ), NGC 4261 (archival data set). All nine radio galaxies are found to lie in group-scale hot-gas environments, with bolometric X-ray luminosities ranging from 10^ 41 - 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

Fig. profs shows the external pressure profiles for the two sources with the most extreme behaviour: 3C 296 (top left) appears to be in approximate pressure balance, assuming equipartition, so that no additional pressure contribution is required; NGC 1044 (bottom left) has a high ratio of P_ ext /P_ int ( 40 ). Fig. profs also shows the corresponding radio maps for the two sources. It is clear that their radio morphologies are quite different: NGC 1044 has naked jets that remain fairly well collimated over large distances before gradually spreading into plumes or tails, whereas the jets of 3C 296 appear to enter radio lobes at a small distance from the nucleus.

To test this hypothesis, I separated the 16 radio lobes into the two categories of `naked jet' sources and `lobed' sources. The sources classified as having `naked jets' are: 3C 31, NGC 1044, NGC 315, 3C 449, NGC 6251; those categorised as `lobed' are: 3C 296, 3C 76.1, NGC 4261, 3C 66B. The right-hand panel of Fig. pressures shows a histogram of the pressure ratios for the two categories, which demonstrates that there is indeed a signficant difference in the two populations in the expected sense: the `lobed' sources are typically closer to pressure balance at equipartition than the `naked jet' sources.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes 3C 296 as a lobed FR I source with flatter-spectrum jets reaching lobe edges, a northeastern cap, and deflected flows blending into steeper emission.

quotes.quote: We present deep, high-resolution imaging of the nearby Fanaroff-Riley Class I (FR I) radio galaxies NGC 193, B2 0206+35, B2 0755+37 and M 84 at frequencies of 4.9 and 1.4 GHz using new and archival multi-configuration observations from the Very Large Array. In addition, we describe lower-resolution observations of B2 0326+39 and a reanalysis of our published images of 3C 296. All of these radio galaxies show twin jets and well-defined lobes or bridges of emission, and we examine the common properties of this class of source. We show detailed images of total intensity, brightness gradient, spectral index, degree of polarization and projected magnetic-field direction.

3C 296 Fig. fig:296_i_si55 shows the 1.4-GHz total intensity and brightness gradient together with the 1.4 to 4.9-GHz spectral index distributions over 3C 296 at 5.5-arcsec resolution. The intensity data are essentially those in Figs.1(a) and 4(a) of LCBH06 with an improved deconvolution, but the grey-scale range in Fig. fig:296_i_si55 (a) is chosen to show the jets more clearly where they appear to enter the lobes. The corresponding intensity gradient is shown in Fig. fig:296_i_si55 (b). Lower limits to the spectral indices have been plotted outside the white intensity contour in Fig. fig:296_i_si55 () to provide a better representation of the large-scale spectral gradients at the edges of the radio source.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of the FRI radio galaxy 3C 296, detecting its nucleus, inner jet, and compact thermal environment linked to jet deceleration.

quotes.quote: We report here on the result of our Chandra observations of 3C 296, which is another twin-jet radio galaxy suitable for modelling in this way. 3C 296 is a well-known FRI radio source (Birkinshaw, Laing Peacock 1981; Leahy Perley 1991; Hardcastle \ 1997) hosted by the elliptical galaxy NGC 5532 at a redshift of 0.0237, a similar redshift to that of NGC 383 which contains 3C 31. Like NGC 383, NGC 5532 lies in a group of galaxies, including at least one bright nearby galaxy, NGC 5531; the association of the group galaxies has been confirmed spectroscopically (Miller \ 2002). In the X-ray, 3C 296 was detected with Einstein (Fabbiano \ 1984), the ROSAT HRI (Hardcastle Worrall 1999) and the ROSAT PSPC (Miller \ 1999). Radio sidedness studies (Hardcastle \ 1997) suggest that the jet decelerates significantly on scales of about 10 arcsec, making it a good candidate for Chandra observations.
As Fig. image shows, there is a clear detection of an X-ray core and jet, as well as extended emission extending out to scales of tens of arcsec.
Spatially resolved emission is seen in the X-ray image of 3C 296 out to at least 30 arcsec from the X-ray nucleus, although most of the counts are on small angular scales.

sources.summary: The atlas describes 3C 296 as a boxy elliptical FR I galaxy with a double-lobed jet, embedded edge-on disk, perpendicular jet geometry, and visible nucleus.

quotes.quote: 3C 296; z=0.025 3C 296 is hosted by an extremely boxy elliptical galaxy. It has a double lobed jet with clear radio emission along the jet axis (FR I). The WFPC2 image martel+99 shows a truncated edge-on disk embedded in the galaxy, similar to that seen in NGC 4261. The disk is aligned with the major axis of the elliptical galaxy, with the jet perpendicular to the disk. The nucleus is seen in both WFPC2 image (though faint) and in the NICMOS image (brighter).

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 296 as a clear case of spectral flattening during rapid deceleration, with arcs and flatter jet spectra discussed in prior detailed work.

quotes.quote: Most of the spectral flattening with distance from the AGN occurs before the jets recollimate, and appears to be associated with rapid deceleration. The clearest examples are the well-resolved and bright sources NGC 315, 3C 31, 3C 270 and 3C 296 (Figs fig:binnedprofiles b, , g and i).
In 3C 296, the spectrum is flatter in both jets roughly where the more distinct arcs are seen, but the arcs are not recognisable individually on the spectral-index images LCBH06 .
3C 296 We have given a comprehensive discussion of the spectral-index distribution of 3C 296 elsewhere LCBH06,LGBPB .

sources.summary: The paper reports intrinsic X-ray absorption in 3C 296 and links the largest absorbed sources to dusty circumnuclear disks and optical-band absorption.

quotes.quote: We obtained a statistically significant estimate for the local column density in four objects (namely 3C 83.1, 3C 270, 3C 272.1 and 3C 296) with values N^z_H 0.2 - 6 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . In the other cases (8/12) we find an upper limit to N^z_H (Table tab_lett2 ) ranging from 0.03 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 to 0.7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

Conversely, note that all three sources where we measured the largest X-ray absorbing column densities (namely 3C 83.1, 3C 270 and 3C 296) lie significantly above the correlation between L_ O and L_X . This is not surprising since, while the estimated X-ray fluxes are de-absorbed, the optical measurements are not corrected for the presence of local absorption.

In 4 galaxies we obtained a detection of local X-ray absorption. In all cases our estimates of the column density are in close agreement with previously published measurements. See Chiaberge et al. (2002) and Donato et al. (2004) for 3C 270; Harris et al. (2002) and Donato et al. (2004) for 3C272.1; Hardcastle et al. (2005) for 3C 296

sources.summary: For 3C 296, the authors subtract a companion and X-ray jet, detect ISM, and find a compact IGM distinct in temperature and centering.

quotes.quote: 3C 296 ( z=0.024 ) : Before fitting an ellipse we subtract a small companion to the northwest as well as the X-ray jet. The ISM is clearly detected see also NGC 5532 in their paper diehl07 and the lobes begin expanding outside the IGM. The IGM has a similar shape and orientation to the ISM, but it is clearly distinct in temperature and where it is centered (in between 3C 296 and a companion to the southwest) even though it is relatively compact.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 296 as an elliptical Seyfert whose aperture-dependent diagnostics descend toward TO/LINER regions, with weak line detection after starlight subtraction.

quotes.quote: 3C 296 : This elliptical galaxy is classified as Seyfert based on the analysis of the central elliptical aperture, descending towards the TO region in the [ N ii ] 6583/H diagnostic, and crossing the LINER region in the [ O iii ] 5007/H diagnostic with increasing aperture. No emission lines were measured without starlight subtraction, and even so the [ S_ II ] 6716, 6731 doublet did not show any emission.
3C 296 appears to have an activity classification change from Seyfert to TO and LINER in the case of [ N ii ] /H and [ O i ] /H diagnostics respectively.

sources.summary: 3C 296 is fit satisfactorily with a YSP; its MIR/FIR data are modeled with dust while WISE follows the old stellar spectrum.

quotes.quote: 3C 296. A YSP provides a satisfactory fit with the model SED. MIR and FIR data are available. As in 3C264, it can be fitted by dust, in this case with a temperature of 50 K ( M_d = 2.6 10^5 M_ ), while the WISE luminosities appear to follow the old stellar model spectrum out to 20 m.

gmw_00001332.name: 3C 186
gmw_00001332.overview: The radio-loud quasar 3C 186 is a compact steep-spectrum source [arXiv:astro-ph/0511464, arXiv:1202.3153] located within a high-redshift X-ray-luminous galaxy cluster [arXiv:1008.1739, arXiv:astro-ph/0511464]. Its host galaxy displays a distorted morphology with tidal features indicative of a merger [arXiv:1611.05501, arXiv:2011.11656], while its active nucleus is offset from the galactic center [arXiv:1611.05501, arXiv:1704.00809].

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra observations of 3C 186, describing its luminous quasar, associated cooling-core cluster, compact CSS radio source, black-hole power, and possible cluster heating.

quotes.quote: We present the first results from a new, deep (200ks) Chandra observation of the X-ray luminous galaxy cluster surrounding the powerful ( L 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 ), high-redshift ( z=1.067 ), compact-steep-spectrum radio-loud quasar 3C 186. The diffuse X-ray emission from the cluster has a roughly ellipsoidal shape and extends out to radii of at least 60 arcsec ( 500 kpc). The centroid of the diffuse X-ray emission is offset by 0.68 0.11 ( 5.5 0.9 kpc) from the position of the quasar. We measure a cluster mass within the radius at which the mean enclosed density is 2500 times the critical density, r_ 2500 = 283^ +18 _ 13 kpc, of 1.02 ^ +0.21 _ 0.14 10^ 14 M _ .

Intermittent Radio Source 3C 186 radio source belongs to a class of young CSS radio sources see for review odea1998 . measured a synchrotron age of 5 10^5 years for the entire radio structure. 3C 186 has a double radio morphology with a one-sided jet. The double radio source has a total length equal to 1.8 (see and references therein) corresponding to 15 kpc projected size.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 186 as a powerful radio-loud quasar whose active nucleus and broad lines are offset from a distorted merger-like host, consistent with a possible recoiling black hole.

quotes.quote: We study the intriguing properties of the powerful (L _ bol 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 ) radio-loud quasar 3C 186. This object shows peculiar features both in the images and in the spectra. We utilize near-IR Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images to study the properties of the host galaxy, and HST UV and Sloan Digital Sky Survey optical spectra to study the kinematics of the source.

HST imaging shows that the active nucleus is offset by 1.3 0.1 arcsec (i.e. 11 kpc) with respect to the center of the host galaxy. Spectroscopic data show that the broad emission lines are offset by 2140 390 km/s with respect to the narrow lines.
