The host galaxy of the quasar displays a distorted morphology with possible tidal features that are typical of the late stages of a galaxy merger. A number of scenarios can be envisaged to account for the observed features. While the presence of a peculiar outflow cannot be completely ruled out, all of the observed features are consistent with those expected if the QSO is associated with a gravitational wave (GW) recoiling BH.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 186 as a luminous radio-loud CSS quasar in a high-redshift X-ray cluster, with an overpressured young radio source that may heat the cluster gas.

quotes.quote: We report the first significant detection of an X-ray cluster associated with a powerful (L _ bol 10^ 47 erg sec ^ 1 ) radio-loud quasar at high redshift (z=1.06). Diffuse X-ray emission is detected out to 120 kpc from the CSS quasar 3C 186. A strong Fe-line emission at the z_ rest =1.06 confirms its thermal nature. We find that the CSS radio source is highly overpressured with respect to the thermal cluster medium by 2-3 orders of magnitude.

3C 186 is a luminous quasar (L _ bol 10^ 47 erg sec ^ 1 ). It has a strong big blue bump in the optical-UV band and broad optical emission lines (Simpson Rawlings 2000, Kuraszkiewicz et al. 2002). It is therefore a typical quasar except for its radio properties. It is classified in radio as a compact steep spectrum (CSS) source.

sources.summary: The paper models the radio-loud quasar 3C 186, describing its compact FRII/CSS jet structure and testing whether jet emission can explain its X-ray spectrum and cluster-environment interaction.

quotes.quote: We present the X-ray analysis of a deep ( 200 ksec) observation of the compact steep spectrum radio-loud quasar 3C 186 (z=1.06) and investigate the contribution of the unresolved radio jet to the total X-ray emission. The spectral analysis is not conclusive on the origin of the bulk of the X-ray emission. In order to examine the jet contribution to the X-ray flux, we model the quasar spectral energy distribution (SED), adopting several scenarios for the jet emission. For the values of the main physical parameters favored by the observables, a dominant role of the jet emission in the X-ray band is ruled out when a single zone (leptonic) scenario is adopted, even including the contribution of the external photon fields as seed photons for inverse Compton emission.

3C 186 is a luminous quasar L_ bol 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 Sie05 , with a compact FRII morphology, located at redshift z=1.067 . The radio structure is characterized by two components, identified as the radio lobes, separated by 2 ^ , and a knotty jet connecting the core to the northwest lobe Spe91 . The steep radio spectrum, the relatively small projected linear size ( 16 kpc), and an estimated spectral age of 10^ 5 yrs Mur99 indicate that the source belongs to the class of Compact Steep Spectrum (CSS) radio quasars.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 186 as a recoiling black-hole candidate, explaining its quasar-host offsets and jet geometry with a fine-tuned gravitational-wave kick scenario.

quotes.quote: The compact object in quasar 3C 186 is one of the most promising recoiling black-hole candidates, exhibiting both an astrometric displacement between the quasar and the host galaxy as well as a spectroscopic shift between broad and narrow lines. 3C 186 also presents a radio jet which, when projected onto the plane of the sky, appears to be perpendicular to the quasar/galaxy displacement. Assuming a gravitational-wave kick is indeed responsible for the properties of 3C 186 and using state-of-the-art relativistic modeling, we show that current observations allow for exquisite modeling of the recoiling black hole. Most notably, we find that the kick velocity, the black-hole spin, and the line of sight are almost collinear and the former appear perpendicular to each other only because of a strong projection effect. The targeted configuration requires substantial fine-tuning: while there exists a region in the black-hole binary parameter space that is compatible with 3C 186, the observed system appears to be a rare occurrence.

In particular, we develop two observational tests that rely on the brightness ratio between the approaching and receding jet as well as the asymmetry of the jet lobes. While the available radio data provide loose constraints, deeper observations have the unique potential of unveiling the nature of 3C 186. black-hole physics -- quasars: supermassive black holes -- relativistic processes The formation of supermassive black holes (BHs) likely involves initial seeds undergoing accretion and merger episodes 2021NatRP...3..732V .

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 186 as a luminous radio-loud quasar in a z=1.1 X-ray cluster, with a compact steep-spectrum jet, possible relic lobes, and cluster interaction.

quotes.quote: Our recent Chandra discovery of > 100 kpc scale diffuse X-ray emission revealed the presence of an X-ray cluster associated with the radio loud quasar 3C 186 at redshift z=1.1 and suggests interactions between the quasar and the cluster. In contrast to the majority of X-ray clusters the 3C 186 cluster contains a quasar in the center whose radiative power alone exceeds that which would be needed to quench the cluster cooling.

The 3C 186 quasar is a powerful Compact Steep Spectrum radio source expanding into the cluster medium. The 2 arcsec radio jet is unresolved in the Chandra observation, but its direction is orthogonal to the elliptical surface brightness of the cluster. The radio data show the possible presence of old radio lobes on 10 arcsec scale in the direction of the radio jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 186 as a cluster-associated quasar whose X-ray-confirmed intracluster medium, red-sequence ETGs, and source overdensity validate the selection methods.

quotes.quote: 3C 186 is also known to reside in a cluster, but in that case the cluster was confirmed by the clear detection of X-ray emission from the intracluster medium siemiginowska10 . We use these clusters to test the reliability of our red sequence method. Reassuringly, this method correctly identifies both fields as cluster candidates because of the presence of a substantial number of ETGs within the region of the CMD where the corresponding model red sequences lie.

As for the red sequence method in Sect. redsequencemodeling , we test our overdensity method against the two confirmed clusters in our sample, which are associated with 3C 186 and 3C 210. Our second method correctly identifies both fields as cluster candidates because of the presence of a significant overdensity of objects around the radio source.

sources.summary: 3C 186 is a CSS quasar in an overdense likely cluster region, with an elliptical diffuse host and a nearby optical blob indicating star formation.

quotes.quote: 3C 186 (z=1.069) The target, a well-studied CSS source odea98 appears in our IR image in an overdense region which is most likely a cluster of galaxies semi2008 . The host galaxy is clearly visible as a roughly elliptical area of diffuse emission extending to the northeast and southwest. A blob of relatively bright emission, about 1 in size, has its center located roughly 2 northeast of the target. Our optical image reveals a well-defined area of signal in the same area, suggesting active star formation. Very few of the galaxies surrounding the target have appreciable signal in the optical image, supporting the assertion that they are cluster members at the redshift of the QSO.

sources.summary: The paper interprets 3C 186 as a radio-loud quasar hosting a recoiling supermassive black hole offset from its galaxy, using the velocity shift to constrain the progenitor binary.

quotes.quote: Recent detailed observations of the radio-loud quasar 3C 186 indicate the possibility that a supermassive recoiling black hole is moving away from the host galaxy at a speed of nearly 2100km/s. If this is the case, we can model the mass ratio and spins of the progenitor binary black hole using the results of numerical relativity simulations. We find that the black holes in the progenitor must have comparable masses with a mass ratio q=m_1/m_2>1/4 and the spin of the primary black hole must be _2=S_2/m_2^2>0.4 . The final remnant of the merger is bounded by _f>0.45 and at least 4 of the total mass of the binary system is radiated into gravitational waves.

A recent detailed study of the radio-loud quasar 3C 186, which has an active nucleus offset from the galactic center by 1.3 0.1 arcsec (i.e. 11 kpc) and broad line emissions offset from the narrow line spectra by 2140 390 km/s, has concluded that the most likely explanation is that the central supermassive black is recoiling away from the center of the galaxy at 2000 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 186 as a well-studied CSS quasar with 20 kpc radio jets, an S-shaped one-sided jet and bent lobes, and little optical-UV correction from its torus model.

quotes.quote: -3C 186 is a very well studied CSS ODea1998 with radio jets of 20 kpc and 4 core contribution at 5 GHz Spencer1991,Ludke1998 . The radio emission is best described with a parabola (a combination of multiple power-laws). MERLIN observation at 1.6 MHz indicates a one-sided jet and and two bent lobes at 60 ^ and 90 ^ with respect to the source axis, creating a S-shaped source Spencer1991,Ludke1998 . The curvature in the lobes is presumably due to the sufficiently dense ISM that affect the expansion of the radio structure. The best-fit torus is a combination of clumps with a small filling factor and a homogeneous disk with moderate opacity which results in no significant correction of the optical-UV emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 186 as a CSS quasar with surrounding diffuse cluster X-ray emission; its hot cluster gas does not confine the expanding radio source.

quotes.quote: We found X-ray jets in two quasars (PKS 1127-145, B2 0738+32), an X-ray cluster surrounding a CSS quasar (z=1.1, 3C 186), detected a possible binary structure in 0941-080 galaxy and an extended diffuse emission in galaxy PKS B2 1345+12.

In our sample we detect an extended diffuse X-ray emission associated with the X-ray cluster of 3C 186, z=1.063 quasar and an extended emission in GPS galaxy, PKS 1345+125. The properties of the 3C 186 cluster were discussed in siem2005 . One important conclusion from the studies of 3C 186 is that the hot cluster gas is not able to confine the expanding radio source and that the jet moves out of the host galaxy with no significant energy loss.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 186 as a radio-loud QSO recoiling-SMBH candidate with large spatial and velocity offsets, merger-like host tidal features, and IFU results consistent with recoil.

quotes.quote: One such object is the radio-loud QSO 3C 186 which has an 11 kpc spatial offset from its host galaxy and a 2140 390 km s ^ 1 velocity offset between the broad and narrow emission lines Chiaberge2017 . The tidal features of the host galaxy indicate recent merger activity. Integral field spectroscopy was performed to study the complex kinematics and determine if the velocity offset could result from a peculiar outflow Chiaberge2018 . The results were consistent with the recoiling SMBH scenario but final confirmation will require both James Webb Space Telescope IFU spectroscopy to map the H region with 0.1" resolution and deep imaging from HST to rule out the presence of a second low mass galaxy Chiaberge2018 .

gmw_00001333.name: ESO 137--G34
gmw_00001333.overview: ESO 137--G34 is an obscured Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1005.3253] classified as a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1005.3253, arXiv:2310.09093]. It exhibits deep silicate absorption [arXiv:2310.09093], complex soft X-ray structure [arXiv:1005.3253], and a possible water-ice libration shoulder [arXiv:2310.09093].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 137--G34 as Compton thick, preferring a reflection-dominated interpretation despite acceptable TD and RD fits, with strong K-line emission and complex soft X-ray structure.

quotes.quote: Both a RD model ( ^2/d.o.f 169/139 see Table 3) and a TD model ( ^2/d.o.f 160/138) provide an acceptable fit to the broad band spectrum of ESO 137--G34. In the TD fit, the primary hard X--ray continuum ( 1.8), is obscured by a column density of 1.2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . The intensity of the scattering fraction ( f_ scatt 5 ) is higher than that measured in the sources best fitted with a TD model, while the reflection component is relatively weak ( R 0.3). Even though none of the two models cannot be preferred on the basis of purely statistical arguments, the individual parameters of the TD model are highly degenerate, and cannot be reliably constrained. As a consequence, a RD model is preferred.

sources.summary: The paper classifies ESO 137-G034 as an obscured, Compton-thick AGN with deep silicate absorption, high X-ray column density, and a possible water-ice libration shoulder.

quotes.quote: All the galaxies are classified as obscured AGN (log N _H^ X-ray (cm ^ 2 ) > 22.0), and two of them are also classified as Compton thick (CT) AGN (NGC 5728 and ESO 137--G034; i.e. log N _H^ X-ray (cm ^ 2 ) > 24.0; e.g. Ricci17 ).

We find the deepest 9.7 m silicate absorption in NGC 7172 (see also Roche07 ), NGC 5728, ESO 137 G034 and NGC 5506 (S _ Sil ^ 9.7 m of -2.4, -1.9, -1.0, -0.9, respectively). Two of these galaxies (NGC 5728 and ESO 137--G034) have high hydrogen column densities (CT-like source), as measured from the X-rays.
NGC 5728 and ESO 137-G034 also show a shoulder in the 9.7 m silicate absorption band that might be related with the H _2 O libration band ( 11-16 m).

gmw_00001334.name: P95080
gmw_00001334.overview: P95080 is a moderately inclined spiral galaxy that may contain a central bar [arXiv:1905.08971]. The galaxy features a regular velocity field and several detached ionized-gas clouds that extend beyond its visible stellar disk [arXiv:1905.08971].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes P95080 as a moderately inclined spiral with real detached ionized-gas clouds extending beyond the stellar disk, star-forming ionization, regular kinematics, high central metallicity, and low extinction.

quotes.quote: P95080, seen in the top panels of Fig. fig:rgb_image , is a spiral galaxy with a moderate inclination and a possibly a bar. The right panel shows the MUSE map for , uncorrected for intrinsic dust extinction, but corrected for stellar absorption and Galactic extinction. We plot only the spaxels with S/N > 3 . The distribution is quite patchy in the central regions of the galaxy, where many peaks are visible. It emerges that the ionised gas is characterised a number of clouds detached from the main body that extend beyond the visible light.

The velocity field is quite regular and spans the range ( 100< v/ <100 ). The median error on the gas velocity in the spaxels is 5 . Uncertainties on the stellar motion are the formal errors of the fit calculated using the original noise spectrum datacube and have been normalized by the ^2 of the fit. The velocity of the detached clouds is that expected given their position with respect to the galaxy, suggesting that they indeed belong to the object.

gmw_00001335.name: P19482
gmw_00001335.overview: P19482 is a spiral galaxy with a spiral arm extending toward the southwest [arXiv:1905.08971]. It features numerous detached gas clouds that are kinematically tied to the galaxy, with both gas and stars rotating around the same axis [arXiv:1905.08971].

sources.summary: The paper presents P19482 as a spiral galaxy with many detached clouds tied kinematically to the galaxy, star-forming line ratios, declining metallicity, low extinction, and a normal field-galaxy SFR-mass position.

quotes.quote: We now focus on P19482, whose color composite image is presented in the second row of Fig. fig:rgb_image . This is a spiral galaxy, with a slightly higher inclination than P95080. A spiral arm extends towards South-West. We detect the presence of 42 clouds , whose size is much larger than the typical size of the noise, seen e.g. in the corners of the image. Most of the detached clouds follow the spiral arms, but especially in the North -East region no stellar disk seems to be associated to the presence of the clouds.

The analysis of the velocity field (panel (a)) indicates that these clouds belong to the galaxy, as their velocity is consistent with that of the part of the galaxy that is close to them. As we did for P95080, we integrated the spaxels of each cloud and run on the integrated spectra. Values obtained on the spatially resolved and integrated spectra are largely in agreement. Overall, in each position, the gas and the stars (panel ()) rotate around the same axis and at similar speed ( 180< v/ <180 ).

gmw_00001336.name: P63661
gmw_00001336.overview: P63661 is a moderately inclined spiral galaxy with a complex, extended ionized-gas structure that reaches well beyond its stellar disk [arXiv:1905.08971]. The galaxy's gas and stars rotate around the same axis at similar speeds, and its detached external gas clouds share this consistent velocity [arXiv:1905.08971].

sources.summary: The paper describes P63661 as a moderately inclined spiral with a complex extended ionized-gas structure, detached gas belonging to the galaxy, regular gas-star rotation, low extinction, and expected field-galaxy SFR-mass placement.

quotes.quote: We now move our attention to P63661, whose RGB image is presented in the third row of Fig. fig:rgb_image . Similarly to the previous galaxies, this is a spiral galaxy, with a moderate inclination. A spiral unwinding arm extends towards South-West. The map shown in Figure fig:rgb_image and Figure fig:P63661 unveils a much more complicated structure. Focusing on the distribution, we find that it extends well beyond the stellar disk.

From the analysis of the velocity field (panel (a)) it results that these clouds belong to the galaxy, as their velocity is consistent with that of the part of the galaxy that is close to them. Running on the integrated spectra of each cloud, we obtained values compatible with those obtained on the spaxels. Overall, in each position, the gas and the stars (panel ()) rotate around the same axis and at similar speed ( 100< v/ <100 ). In the external regions, where there are no stars in correspondence of the gas, the gas reaches velocities of |v| 120 .

gmw_00001337.name: P8721
gmw_00001337.overview: P8721 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy with a regular gas velocity field [arXiv:1905.08971]. Its stellar disk extends mostly toward the northeast, whereas its ionized-gas disk extends mostly toward the southwest and features twelve detached gas clouds [arXiv:1905.08971].

sources.summary: The paper identifies P8721 as a highly inclined spiral with asymmetric stellar and ionized-gas disks, detached clouds consistent with galaxy membership, regular kinematics, central composite spectra, and distinct metallicity gradients.

quotes.quote: P8721 is a spiral galaxy with a quite high inclination. Projection effects can therefore affect the interpretation of the results and possible detached clouds seen in projection might appear as part of the galaxy. The ionised gas is much extended, especially towards South-West. The maximum extension of the disk is 4.6 r_e . Strikingly, Fig. fig:rgb_image shows that the stellar disk extends mostly towards North-East with respect to the galaxy center while the ionised gas disk extends mostly towards South-west.

12 clouds of detached gas are visible both towards South-West and towards North-East. The velocity of the gas (panel (a) in Fig. fig:P8721 ) in these clouds is consistent with them belonging to P8721. The gas velocity field is regular and spans the range -220 <v/ < 220. The median error on the gas velocity is 4 . The gas velocity dispersion, shown in panel (b) is overall quite low ( <35 ), except for the core and two external regions.

gmw_00001338.name: NGC 4454
gmw_00001338.overview: NGC 4454 is a disk galaxy that deviates from the stellar mass-size relation [arXiv:2203.11450]. The galaxy also falls off the baryonic Tully-Fisher relation for reasons that remain unidentified [arXiv:2203.11450].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4454 deviates from the stellar mass-size relation for unidentified reasons, also falls off the baryonic Tully-Fisher relation, and is excluded from later analysis.

quotes.quote: There are three 2-sigma outliers and we shall exclude them on the basis of inaccurate mass measurements. For NGC 5347 and NGC 5602, the composite fit (Fig. fig:radprofs .1) departs from the radial profile at the 25.5 AB mag/arcsec ^ 2 level. This could be why these two galaxies fall off the relation. We cannot identify why galaxy NGC 4454 deviates from the relation, because the fit follows the radial profile. However, this galaxy along with NGC 5347 falls off the baryonic Tully-Fisher relation, as can be seen in Fig. fig:btfr_all_relation .

Two of them are already 1-sigma outliers from the stellar mass--size relation (NGC 5347 and NGC 4454). In summary, we exclude seven more galaxies (apart from three edge-on galaxies) as outliers either from the stellar mass--size relation or the baryonic Tully-Fisher relation.

gmw_00001339.name: J0906+6930
gmw_00001339.overview: J0906+6930 is a high-redshift blazar [arXiv:1807.06837] and radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1702.03925] at a redshift of z=5.47 [arXiv:1807.06837]. It features a Doppler-boosted inner jet with a sharp helical bend [arXiv:1807.06837], a clear radio spectral turnover [arXiv:1701.06622], and moderate flux variability at 15 GHz [arXiv:1702.03925, arXiv:1701.06622].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0906+6930 as a z=5.47 blazar with Doppler-boosted radio emission, accurate VLBI coordinates, and a sharply bent helical mas-scale inner jet.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension 09 ^ h 06 ^ m 30 74875 and declination +69 30 30 8363

quotes.quote: The source J0906+6930 was classified as a blazar, i.e. a flat-spectrum radio quasar with one of its relativistic jets approaching and pointing nearly to our line of sight. The redshift of this source is z=5.47 romani04 . The Doppler-boosted jet emission makes J0906+6930 the most radio-luminous quasar and the highest redshift blazar known e.g. coppejans16,zhang17 . However, if radio flux density measurements below 1 GHz are considered, its overall radio spectrum has a clear turnover at around 6 GHz coppejans17 . The accurate equatorial coordinates International VLBI Service for Geodesy and Astrometry IVS, schuh12 celestial reference frame, aus2017b solution, ftp://ivs.bkg.bund.de/pub/vlbi/ivsproducts/crf of the source are right ascension 09 ^ h 06 ^ m 30 74875 and declination +69 30 30 8363 .

One of the highest redshift blazars, J0906+6930 ( z=5.47 ), is a well-studied object with VLBI at multiple frequencies romani04,zhang17 . However, the new observations at 2.3 GHz and 8.6 GHz presented in this paper could significantly advance our knowledge about the source. At 8.6 GHz, we clearly confirmed that the Doppler-boosted inner jet undergoes a sharp bending, as speculated recently by zhang17 from the analysis of archival 15 GHz VLBA data.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q 0906 + 6930 as a high-redshift blazar-like quasar with a modeled SED, mid-infrared excess, a tentative EGRET detection, and no Fermi-LAT counterpart.

quotes.quote: The SED of Q 0906 + 6930 has also been described with a similar model (Romani 2006). Q 0906 + 6930, along with PMN J0525 3343 and GB 1713 + 2148, have notably stronger mid-infrared emission than that of the comparison SED, especially in the longer wavelength bands (W3 at 12 m and W4 at 22 m). This mid-infrared excess may have a contribution from the jet synchrotron radiation (e.g., Ghisellini et al. 1998).

Q 0906 + 6930 has a tentative claimed detection ( 1.5 ) in its \ observation by the Energetic Gamma-Ray Experiment Telescope (EGRET) onboard the Compton Gamma-Ray Observatory (Romani et al. 2004). The Fermi LAT has an order of magnitude better sensitivity and positional accuracy than EGRET. Romani (2006) suggested Q 0906 + 6930 should be detected by the Fermi LAT in its first year of operation. However, although the first two-year survey of the Fermi LAT has detected >800 blazars at z 3 (e.g., Ackermann et al. 2011), none of our HRLQs, including Q 0906 + 6930, is detected at present.

sources.summary: The paper observes J0906 + 6930 as a high-redshift radio-loud quasar, detecting compact 22 GHz emission, moderate 15 GHz variability, and blazar-like radio properties.

quotes.quote: The quasar J0906 + 6930 was detected at 22 GHz but not at 43 GHz. The other two sources were not detected and upper limits to their compact radio emission are given. Archival VLBI imaging data and single-dish 15-GHz monitoring light curve of J0906 + 6930 were also acquired as complementary information. J0906 + 6930 shows a moderate-level variability at 15 GHz.

The radio image is characterized by a core--jet structure with a total detectable size of 5 pc in projection. The brightness temperature, 1.9 10^ 11 K, indicates relativistic beaming of the jet. The radio properties of J0906 + 6930 are consistent with a blazar.
J0906 + 6930 J0906 + 6930 (also known as CGRaBS J0906 + 6930) was discovered as a high-redshift quasar from its optical spectroscopy by 2004ApJ...610L...9R .

sources.summary: The paper builds an SED for QSO J0906 + 6930, finding a massive black hole, an X-ray break, no Fermi detection, and a constrained jet Doppler factor.

quotes.quote: We report on Gemini, NuSTAR and 8-year Fermi observations of the most distant blazar QSO J0906 + 6930 ( z=5.48 ). We construct a broadband spectral energy distribution (SED) and model the SED using a synchro-Compton model. The measurements find a 4 10^9 M_ mass for the black hole and a spectral break at 4 keV in the combined fit of the new NuSTAR and archival Chandra data. The SED fitting constrains the bulk Doppler factor of the jet to 9^ +2.5 _ 3 for QSO J0906 + 6930.

In our Fermi LAT data analysis, we do not detect Q0906, with a 95 flux upper limit in the GeV band 10^ 13 \ erg \ s^ 1 . This is approximately two orders of magnitude lower than that implied by the EGRET excess counts. We thus infer that the excess was most likely a statistical fluctuation.

sources.summary: The paper finds J0906+6930 has a clear spectral turnover, variability at 15 GHz, and a rest-frame turnover frequency near 41.4 GHz at z=5.47.

quotes.quote: J0906+6930 The spectrum of J0906+6930 (Fig. fig:J0906+6930 ) shows a clear spectral turnover. RMP2011 observed J0906+6930 55 times at 15 GHz between 2009 March 19 and 2009 December 29. During this time they observed the flux density to vary between 97 and 180 mJy. As the source is variable, the value in Fig. fig:J0906+6930 is the intrinsic mean 15 GHz flux density ( 136 2 mJy) calculated by RMP2011. Fitting the spectrum, we find a turnover frequency of 6.4 0.8 GHz. Since J0906+6930 is at z=5.47 , this translates to a rest-frame turnover frequency of 41.4 5.2 GHz.

gmw_00001340.name: J2102+6015
gmw_00001340.overview: J2102+6015 is a high-redshift quasar at z=4.575 [arXiv:1807.06837] that has been classified as a gigahertz peaked-spectrum source [arXiv:1807.06837, arXiv:2108.02142] and a compact symmetric object [arXiv:2209.10760, arXiv:2108.02142]. Its milliarcsecond-scale radio structure is elongated east-west [arXiv:1807.06837, arXiv:2108.02142] and exhibits high-frequency variability [arXiv:1609.00575].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2102+6015 as a z=4.575 GPS quasar, gives VLBI coordinates, and resolves its east-west mas-scale radio structure into three components.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension 21 ^ h 02 ^ m 40 21912 and declination +60 15 09 8364

quotes.quote: The redshift of the quasar J2102+6015 is z=4.575 sowards04 . The accurate VLBI-determined coordinates are right ascension 21 ^ h 02 ^ m 40 21912 and declination +60 15 09 8364 . Considering its broadband radio spectrum, J2102+6015 also belongs to the gigahertz peaked-spectrum (GPS) class, with an observed-frame turnover frequency 1 GHz coppejans17 . Both sources have been imaged with VLBI at different frequencies in the past, as we discuss it in Sect.  disc in the context of our new results.

As a quasar with one of the highest measured radio powers among currently known AGN at z>4.5 P>10^ 28  W Hz ^ 1 ; see also Table  model coppejans16 , J2102+6015 ( z=4.57 ) would deserve more attention. The 8.6 GHz VLBI image presented in this paper has the highest resolution obtained for this GPS source to date. We found that its mas-scale radio structure is elongated in the east--west direction. The rather symmetric central 2-mas section is resolved into three components for the first time.

sources.summary: The paper favors J2102 + 6015 as a high-redshift compact symmetric object, noting GPS-source evidence, resolved eastern and western features, and a measured component separation speed.

quotes.quote: J2102 + 6015 Previous studies of this source claim it as an FSRQ with moderate Doppler boosting effects 2016MNRAS.463.3260C , but recent works prefer J2102 + 6015 to be a GPS radio source e.g. 2017MNRAS.467.2039C,2018AA...618A..68F . We find further interesting characteristics of this source. Considering the high-resolution images, the spectral index behavior, and the component separation speeds, we believe this high- z AGN is most likely a CSO. From the deep imaging and model-fitting results, we found that neither the E nor the W features are unresolved. To further constrain the source nature, we collected more high-resolution VLBI data from further epochs, and used somewhat more conservative positional error estimates (i.e. one-tenth of the restoring beam size) to conduct component identification and proper motion estimates.
A more robust proper motion estimate of 0.023 0.011 mas yr ^ 1 was obtained. The details are presented in a separate paper 2021MNRAS.507.3736Z .

sources.summary: The paper finds conflicting VLBI variability indicators for J2102+6015, concludes high-frequency behavior indicates variability, and classifies it as a Doppler-boosted FSRQ.

quotes.quote: Similarly the two 2.3 GHz VLBI observations of J2102+6015 (Table tbl:vlbi_z>4.5 ) indicate that it is not variable. However, to reconcile the 8.3 and 8.6 GHz VLBI flux densities requires an unphysical spectral index of 15 , indicating that J2102+6015 is variable.

J1146+4037 , J1427+3312 , J1429+5447 , J1454+1109 , J1606+3124 , J1611+0844 and J2102+6015 all have S_ pred /S_ FIRST >1.1 . In the discussion above, we already concluded that J1429+5447 and J1454+1109 are variable. For J1146+4037, J1427+3312, J1606+3124, J1611+0844 and J2102+6015, S_ pred /S_ FIRST >1.1 could indicate that they are also variable.
J2102+6015 is a FSRQ because it is Doppler-boosted and variable, the latter of which likely explains its steep negative spectral index.

sources.summary: The paper reports multi-epoch VLBI imaging of the z=4.57 quasar J2102 + 6015, interpreting its stable two-component radio morphology as a young compact symmetric object.

quotes.quote: The 8.4-GHz VLBI images reveal two major features along the east--west direction separated by 10 milli-arcsec (mas). From the spectral index map, both features show flat/inverted spectra. The separation between the two features remains almost unchanged over an observation period of 13 years, placing an upper limit of the separation speed as about 0.04 mas year ^ 1 . Previous studies have classified the source as a GHz-peaked spectrum quasar. Our results indicate that J2102 + 6015 is most likely a young, compact symmetric object rather than a blazar-type core--jet source.

gmw_00001341.name: AGC 203001
gmw_00001341.overview: AGC 203001 is a quenched lenticular S0 galaxy with a very low star formation rate for its stellar mass [arXiv:1910.08794]. Despite being quenched, the galaxy contains a copious amount of gas and is surrounded by a large, off-centered ring measuring approximately 115 kiloparsecs in diameter [arXiv:1910.08794].

sources.summary: The paper reports a 115 kpc off-centered ring around the quenched S0 galaxy AGC 203001, finding it gas rich, optically faint, and possibly formed by interaction or merger scenarios.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the discovery of a large ( 115 kpc in diameter) ring off-centered around a massive quenched galaxy, AGC 203001 using the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT). We also present deep optical g , , and i band imaging of the ring using the Canada France Hawaii Telescope (CFHT). In the context of this discovery, we discuss possible formation mechanisms for large rings. Throughout this paper, we use the standard concordance cosmology from WMAP9 Hinshaw13 with _M = 0.286 , _ = 0.714 and h_ 100 = 0.69 . Our target, AGC 203001 (RA: 10h22m03.315s, DEC: +13d46m10.20s) belongs to a sample of quenched galaxies selected in the following manner.

AGC 203001 is at a redshift of 0.01867 (v _ helio = 5597 \ km/s), has a stellar mass (M _* ) of 1.5 10^ 10 \ M _ Salim16 and a S0 morphology Nair10 . It has a SF rate (SFR) of 0.011 M _ /yr which is very low for its stellar mass, and hence the galaxy is considered quenched. Yet, it has copious amounts of ( ( M / M _ ) = 9.53 0.05 ) as measured by the single dish ALFALFA survey.

gmw_00001342.name: G2
gmw_00001342.overview: G2 is a member galaxy of the AGC 203001 group [arXiv:1910.08794]. It has detected gas and is identified as a possible intruder whose recent high-speed collision with the host galaxy may have triggered ring formation [arXiv:1910.08794].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G2 as an AGC 203001 group galaxy with detected gas and discusses it as a possible intruder whose collision could have formed the ring.

quotes.quote: Here galaxies denoted by G2 (RA: 10h21m49.584s, DEC: +13d42m45.83s; AGC 205059), G4 (RA: 10h22m21.728s, DEC: +13d50m58.67s) and G1 (AGC 203001) are classified to be part of the same group based on the friends-of-friend group-finding technique Tempel14 . AGC 203001 along with G2 and G3 are also likely to be part of the loose group of three galaxies, WBL 263 White99 and USGC U292 with a virial radius of 0.72  Mpc Ramella02 . is detected in G2, a known member of the group.

We identify a nearby galaxy (G2) at a projected distance of 109 kpc, which could be the possible intruder. A recent collision between G2 and the host galaxy at a relatively high speed could have triggered the formation of this ring. We also see asymmetrical outer isophotes in the optical image of the host galaxy which could be the signs of such an interaction. We note, however, that both the stellar and components of G2 do not show any signs of recent interaction.

gmw_00001343.name: G3
gmw_00001343.overview: G3 is a dwarf galaxy located at a systemic velocity of 5525 km/s [arXiv:1910.08794]. It is a likely member of a loose group of galaxies that also includes AGC 203001 and G2 [arXiv:1910.08794].

sources.summary: The paper detects G3 near AGC 203001, identifies it as a dwarf galaxy lacking SDSS spectroscopy, and places it at a similar distance to the group.

quotes.quote: AGC 203001 along with G2 and G3 are also likely to be part of the loose group of three galaxies, WBL 263 White99 and USGC U292 with a virial radius of 0.72  Mpc Ramella02 . is detected in G2, a known member of the group. Additionally, we also detect G3 (RA: 10h21m31.94s, DEC: +13d41m22.4s) in . G3 is a dwarf galaxy which does not have spectroscopic data in the SDSS DR 15 SDSS_DR15 . From our detection we obtain a systemic velocity of 5525 km/s. Hence, G3 is at a similar distance as AGC203001 and likely part of the group.

gmw_00001344.name: UGC 09519
gmw_00001344.overview: UGC 09519 is a dwarf lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.07261] that exhibits a large disc or ring morphology [arXiv:1910.08794] and a LINER-type active nucleus [arXiv:1312.6701]. The galaxy features disturbed molecular gas [arXiv:1312.6318] and a warped polar gaseous disk [arXiv:1312.6701] that rotates in a different plane than its stars [arXiv:1312.6701, arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: UGC 9519 has extended emission-line structures, low gas velocity amplitude indicating non-stellar-plane rotation, a warped polar gaseous disk, and signs of recent gas accretion.

quotes.quote: Emission-line measurements for the other galaxies -- NGC 3248, UGC 9519, and the most part of UGC 4551 -- fall into the AGN/LINER-dominated (or shock-dominated) region of the BPT-diagram.

UGC 9519 has emission-line structures extending (projecting?) all over the stellar disc of the galaxy. The low velocity amplitude of the gas when compared to the stars indicates that the gas rotates in another plane than the stars. The warped polar disc in this system has already been mapped by Serra2012_atlas3d13_HI in the 21 cm line of the H , the polar rotation of the molecular and ionized gas rotation is reported by Alatalo_atlas3d_CO_2013 and atlas3d_10, Davis2013_atlas3d . The conclusion about the polar orientation of the gaseous disc in the very centre of the galaxy is also supported by a circumnuclear red dust lane visible at the SDSS images of the galaxy which is roughly perpendicular to the major axis of the central isophotes (to the bar?); the whole picture is consistent with the gaseous disc, polar in the centre and strongly warped farther from the centre. Let us note that systematic velocities of the gaseous and stellar subsystems differ by 30--40 , and the symmetry centre of the gas velocity profile is also shifted with respect to the galactic centre along the slit.
Perhaps, the gas subsystem of UGC 9519 was accreted recently and has not settled down yet.

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 09519 as a red-sequence outlier with possible recently quenched star formation, dust reddening, and disturbed molecular gas.

quotes.quote: The only notable differences are two outliers in the ( ) -- \ relation, NGC 1266 and UGC 09519, which are both red sequence galaxies in \ colours but have strong \ absorption. They might be experiencing a recent shutdown in star formation activity, as an abrupt shutdown would show up in the NUV flux before the \ line strength see also katey1266sf . These galaxies are also known to have internal reddening from dust, and colour images are presented in DRpaper .

Another indicator of a galaxy's interaction and/or gas accretion history can come from disturbances to the gas distribution and kinematics. We make qualitative assessment of such disturbances from the CO position-velocity slices in davis13 and from the CO integrated intensity maps and velocity fields in DRpaper . Markers of disturbances include gas tails, strong asymmetries in the gas distribution, and complex velocity fields, and we thereby consider the following galaxies to have disturbed molecular gas: IC 1024, NGC 1222, NGC 3619, NGC 4150, NGC 4550, NGC 4694, NGC 4753, NGC 5173, NGC 7465, PGC 058114, and UGC 09519. These are the galaxies noted in DRpaper as classes `M' (mildly disturbed) or `X' (strongly disturbed), or which are sufficiently lopsided that their integrated CO spectrum from the CARMA data has a flux density twice as large on one side of the systemic velocity as on the other side.

sources.summary: The paper reports rapid apparent stellar rotation in UGC 9519, divergent stellar and ionized-gas kinematics, evidence for an inner polar gaseous disc, and outward-increasing stellar ages.

quotes.quote: UGC 9519 This galaxy, although having an almost face-on orientation with respect to the line of sight ( i=23 , HyperLeda), demonstrates very rapid apparent rotation of the stars (Fig. kin_stpop_profiles:Katkov_n ). We ilg_gas noted earlier strong divergency of the kinematics of the stars and ionized gas in our long-slit data. Panoramic spectral data of the ATLAS-3D survey cappellari_atlas7_2011,davis_2011 gives rather evidence for the inner polar gaseous disc in this galaxy. As in the case of NGC 7351, the SSP-equivalent age of the stars monotonically rises along the radius of the galaxy, from 1.5 Gyr in the nucleus to about 5 Gyr in the disc. The disc, being the oldest stellar component of the galaxy, is also the most dynamically hot, 100 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 9519 as a dwarf lenticular with an outer gas ring, decoupled gas kinematics, polar inner gas, and an outer ionized component tied to neutral hydrogen.

quotes.quote: UGC 9519. It is a dwarf lenticular galaxy elongated in PA=75 . However in the frame of the ATLAS-3D survey an outer \ ring was found by atlas3d_13 , well beyond the optical borders of the galaxy, that is elongated in the north-south direction. The long-slit cross-section along the isophote major axis (Fig. fig_ls ) has confirmed the decoupled kinematics of the gas and its probable external origin. To obtain a full picture, we have observed UGC 9519 with the scanning Fabry-Perot interferometer in two emission lines, and [ NII ] 6583.

The inner gas rotates in the nearly polar plane with respect to the stellar disk; the outer ionized gas being related surely to the neutral hydrogen detected by atlas3d_13 is quite decoupled from the main body of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper observes UGC09519 with HARP, assigns an intermediate kinetic temperature, and notes relatively narrow low-confidence contours in the three-dimensional model-parameter space.

quotes.quote: For 5 galaxies (IC0676, IC1024, NGC6014, PGC058114 and UGC09519), the HARP receiver and ACSIS backend were used in a configuration yielding a 1 GHz total bandwidth and 2048 channels each 488 kHz-wide.

The kinetic temperatures obtained for the best-fit models are either low ( T_ K =10 20 K for IC1024, NGC1222, NGC1266, NGC2764, NGC3032, NGC3665, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694, NGC4710 and NGC5866), intermediate ( 30 70 K for IC0676, NGC3607, PGC058114 and UGC09519) or high ( 120 K for NGC6014 and 150 K for NGC7465).
Despite this, we note that the low confidence level contours are relatively narrow in the 3D parameter space for IC0676, NGC3032, NGC4526, NGC5866 and UGC09519.

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGC 9519 as a striking isolated lenticular example with decoupled, visibly counterrotating ionized gas whose complex velocity structure suggests inclined gas motions.

quotes.quote: Kinematical profiles for stars and ionized gas in a few most striking examples (NGC 2350, NGC 6798, UGC 9519) are presented in Fig.

We have found that 7 of 12 ( 58 14 ) lenticular isolated galaxies studied by us reveal a presence of extended ionized-gas discs which rotation is mostly decoupled from the stellar kinematics: in 5 of 7 ( 71 17 ) -- NGC 3248, NGC 6798, NGC 7351, UGC 4551, UGC 9519 -- the gas demonstrates visible counterrotation.

Shocks or post-AGB stars are the main agent of gas excitation in the remaining galaxies (NGC 3248, UGC 4551, UGC 9519) whose velocity profiles possess asymmetries and complex features which are probably resulted from gas motions in inclined planes.

sources.summary: The paper compares UGC 09519 with AGC 203001, noting its large disc/ring morphology, optical-extent ring, and diffuse optical ring in deeper imaging.

quotes.quote: The other galaxy, UGC 09519, is classified to have a large disc/ring morphology Serra12 . However, disc/ring is well within the optical extent of the galaxy which is also very different from AGC 203001. Moreover, in the relatively deeper Mayall z band Legacy Survey/Beijing-Arizona Sky Survey (MzLS/BASS) images Dey19 it clearly shows a diffuse optical ring unlike in AGC 203001.

gmw_00001345.name: CGCG254-021
gmw_00001345.overview: CGCG254-021 is a brightest cluster galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus with bent radio lobes [arXiv:1510.03708]. It is associated with an extraordinarily luminous X-ray tail, which likely consists of gas stripped from the galaxy or its small group [arXiv:1510.03708].

sources.summary: The paper links CGCG254-021 to an unusually long, luminous X-ray tail, arguing that the tail is stripped gas from this dominant former starburst galaxy or its small group.

quotes.quote: We derive the properties of the galaxy cluster environment and give hints on the substructure present in this X-ray tail, which is very likely associated to the galaxy CGCG254-021. The X-ray tail is extraordinarily luminous ( 2e42 erg s^ 1 ), the thermal emission has a temperature of 0.8 keV and the X-ray luminous gas might be stripped off completely from the galaxy. From the assumptions on the 3D geometry we estimate the gas mass fraction ( < 0.1 ) and conclude that the gas has been compressed and/or heated.

The 14 mag_V galaxy CGCG254-021 is very close to this X-ray object and several indications point to the scenario that the X-ray tail is stripped gas from this galaxy or from a small galaxy group with this galaxy as its BCG: The projected distance between the peak of the X-ray tail and the galaxy center is only 40 kpc . The redshift of galaxy and X-ray tail are consistent with the cluster redshift. The apparently bent radio lobes from the galaxy's AGN are consistent with the inferred direction of motion. The galaxy is the brightest galaxy within this region with the highest star formation rate in the past.

gmw_00001346.name: Arp 187
gmw_00001346.overview: Arp 187 is a merger-remnant infrared galaxy that hosts a dying active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1908.10864, arXiv:1510.06037]. While large-scale radio jets and narrow-line regions remain visible [arXiv:1510.06037, arXiv:1811.07447], the central engine has experienced a drastic luminosity decline within the last 10,000 years [arXiv:1908.10864, arXiv:1811.07447].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 187 as a merger-remnant infrared galaxy and dead-quasar candidate, using hard X-ray limits and large-scale AGN indicators to infer a >1000 luminosity decline within about 10^4 yr.

quotes.quote: Here we present the most drastic AGN luminosity decline by a factor of 10^ 3 constrained by a X-ray observation of the nearby galaxy Arp 187, which is a promising ``dead'' quasar whose current activity seems quiet but whose past activity of 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 is still observable at a large scale by its light echo. The obtained upper bound of the X-ray luminosity is (L_ 2-10 keV / erg s ^ 1 ) < 41.2 , corresponding to (L_ bol / erg s ^ 1 ) < 42.5 , indicating an inactive central engine. Even if a putative torus model with 1.5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 is assumed, the strong upper-bound still holds with (L_ 2-10 keV / erg s ^ 1 ) < 41.8 or (L_ bol / erg s ^ 1 ) < 43.1 . Given the expected size of the narrow line region, this luminosity decrease by a factor of 10^3 must have occurred within 10^4 yr.

Out of those 20 sources, Arp 187, a merger remnant infrared galaxy located at z=0.04 ( D_L = 178 Mpc), is the most promising ``dying'' or ``dead'' quasar candidate, which completely lack current AGN signatures on small scales ( <10 pc), but previous AGN activity estimated by the large scale AGN indicators ( 1 kpc) must have reached quasar level luminosity. Previous VLA and ALMA 5--100 GHz radio observations have revealed the bimodal jet lobes with 5 kpc size, whose kinematic jet age of 8 10^4 yr. On the other hand, the central radio-core is absent, suggesting that the central engine is already faint or even quenched. The optical spectrum indicates that Arp 187 has narrow line region with the estimated size of 1 kpc, and the expected AGN luminosity reaches L_ bol = 1.5 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ich19b .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 187 as a merger remnant with young radio jets, optical/IR narrow-line AGN signs, weak present-day core emission, and likely quenched AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We investigate optical, infrared, and radio active galactic nucleus (AGN) signs in the merger remnant Arp 187, which hosts luminous jets launched in the order of 10^5 yr ago but whose present-day AGN activity is still unknown. We find AGN signs from the optical BPT diagram and infrared [ Oiv ] 25.89 m line, originating from the narrow line regions of AGN. On the other hand, Spitzer /IRS show the host galaxy dominated spectra, suggesting that the thermal emission from the AGN torus is considerably small or already diminished.

Those results suggest that the AGN activity of Arp 187 has already been quenched, but the narrow line region is still alive owing to the time delay of emission from the past AGN activity. A link between active galactic nuclei (AGN) and major galaxy mergers has been seen in luminous infrared galaxies e.g., san88 .

Arp 187 is a merger remnant at the distance of z=0.04 (and the equivalent luminosity distance of d_L = 171 Mpc). Using ALMA Cycle 0 observations, ued14 find two 3 mm continuum components located at both sides of the nucleus of Arp 187, spanning 4 kpc. These components are identified as small scale radio jets from high-resolution VLA archival images at 4.9 GHz and 8.5 GHz, which clearly show the morphology of the radio lobes.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 187 as a dying AGN: large-scale radio and narrow-line indicators remain, but the central engine has faded dramatically within about 10^4 years.

quotes.quote: Arp 187 is one of the fading active galactic nuclei (AGN), whose AGN activity is currently decreasing in luminosity. We investigate the observational signatures of AGN in Arp 187, which trace various physical scales from less than 0.1 pc to the nearly 10 kpc, to estimate the longterm luminosity change over 10^ 4 years. The VLA 5 GHz, 8 GHz, and the ALMA 133 GHz images reveal bimodal jet lobes with 5 kpc size and the absence of the central radio-core. The 6dF optical spectrum shows that Arp 187 hosts narrow line region with the estimated size of 1 kpc, and the line strengths give the AGN luminosity of L_ bol =1.5 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 .

These multi-wavelength signatures indicate that Arp 187 hosts a ``dying'' AGN: the central engine is already dead, but the large scale AGN indicators are still observable as the remnant of the past AGN activity. The central engine has experienced the drastic luminosity decline by a factor of 10^ 3-5 fainter within 10^ 4 years, which is roughly consistent with the viscous timescale of the inner part of the accretion disk within 500 years.

gmw_00001347.name: PHL 293B
gmw_00001347.overview: PHL 293B is a compact, extremely metal-poor blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1409.1189, arXiv:2009.11600] currently undergoing a very young starburst [arXiv:1208.5461]. Although historical records showed no optical variability between 1988 and 2013 [arXiv:1409.1189, arXiv:2003.02242], recent observations have revealed long-term optical variability [arXiv:2002.12369] and substantial spectral variations in its broad hydrogen emission lines [arXiv:2003.02242].

sources.summary: HL 293B is described as a faint Haro-Luyten dwarf BCG with a very young starburst, compact NIR burst flux, no extended underlying component, and evidence for old stars.

quotes.quote: HL 293B (SDSS J223036.79-000636.9) This faint dwarf is a Haro-Luyten object, quasi-stellar in appearance. It was first classified as a BCG by 1981ApJ...247..823T , which was later refined to nE BCG by 2001ApJS..133..321C . This galaxy is currently undergoing a starburst only a few Myr old, as evidenced by the detection of luminous blue variable stars 2009ApJ...690.1797I . It seems to have a lot less NIR than optical flux, even though the exposure times are reasonably large ( 42 minutes in K ). The NIR flux is coming from a very compact region spatially coextensive with the burst nucleus.

2001ApJS..133..321C similarly do not find an extended underlying component in this galaxy. There is nevertheless an old stellar population present, although it does not appear to be spatially dominant. While all other colors show signs of strong contamination by nebular emission, V-K 2.0 at V-I 0.5 would require the presence of old stars.

sources.summary: The paper presents spectra and photometry of compact low-metallicity HII galaxy PHL 293B, finding broad Balmer components, blueshifted absorptions, low luminosity, low metallicity, and no significant optical variability.

quotes.quote: X-shooter and ISIS WHT spectra of the starforming galaxy PHL 293B also known as A2228-00 and SDSS J223036.79-000636.9 are presented in this paper. We find broad (FWHM = 1000km/s) and very broad (FWZI = 4000km/s) components in the Balmer lines, narrow absorption components in the Balmer series blueshifted by 800km/s, previously undetected FeII multiplet (42) absorptions also blueshifted by 800km/s, IR CaII triplet stellar absorptions consistent with [ Fe/H ] < -2.0 and no broad components or blushifted absorptions in the HeI lines. Based on historical records, we found no optical variability at the 5 level of 0.02 mag between 2005 and 2013 and no optical variability at the level of 0.1mag for the past 24 years.

PHL 293B , also known as HL 293B, Kinman's dwarf, A2228-00 and SDSS J223036.79-000636.9 is a very low luminosity galaxy M _B = -14.37; Cairos01 at a distance of 23.1 Mpc obtained from the radial velocity Mould00 taken from NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) corrected for Virgo Infall, Great Attractor and Shapley, with a Hubble constant of 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . Its metallicity was first measured by French80 who gave a value of 12+log(O/H) = 7.78 and, more recently, by Izotov07 who derive a value of 12+log(O/H) = 7.66 from SDSS data.

PHL 293B also looks compact in the images obtained by Cairos01 , and Geha06 give an effective radius for this galaxy of only 0.4 kpc. Its absolute magnitude according to the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) is, M _g = -14.77. These facts make of it a very low luminosity and compact HII galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes PHL 293B as a metal-poor blue compact dwarf with persistent broad-line features and optical variability best explained by a long-lived transient.

quotes.quote: PHL 293B Also known as the Kinman dwarf or SDSS J223036.79-000636.9. is a metal-poor ( 12+ O / H = 7.71 0.02 ; Izotov2011 ) BCD emission-line galaxy at z=0.00517 0.00001 (NED) For consistency with Terlevich2014 , a distance of 23.1 Mpc is adopted throughout (corrected for the Virgo cluster + Great Attractor + Shapley) using H_0=73.0 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _m=0.27 , and , _ =0.73 .

PHL 293B has a stellar mass of 2 10^ 7 M_ and an HI gas fraction of 0.75 2006ApJ...653..240G . The source previously exhibited striking P Cygni-like broad emission in Balmer series lines Izotov2009,Izotov2011 . The narrow absorption lines were blueshifted by 800 km s ^ 1 and the broad H emission had a FWHM of about 1500 km s ^ 1 .

Here we report on variability measured in the optical light curves using difference imaging on Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and years 1 -- 6 (Y6) Dark Energy Survey DES; Flaugher2015,DES2016,DW2018 images. The previously-undetected variability has an amplitude of 0.12 mag in the g band between 1998 and 2018. This observation rules out stationary winds and other non-transient interpretations for the mechanism of the broad emission lines in PHL 293B.

sources.summary: The paper maps PHL 293B with MEGARA IFU spectroscopy, finding an extremely metal-poor, high-ionization dwarf starburst with extended He II emission and spatially homogeneous oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: Here we report the first spatially resolved spectroscopic study for the galaxy PHL 293B using the high-resolution GTC/MEGARA integral field unit (IFU). PHL 293B is a local, extremely metal-poor, high ionization galaxy. This makes PHL 293B an excellent analogue for galaxies in the early Universe. The MEGARA aperture ( 12.5'' 11.3'') covers the entire PHL 293B main body and its far-reaching ionized gas.

A chemically homogeneity, across hundreds of parsecs, is observed in O/H. We take the oxygen abundance 12+log (O/H) = 7.64 0.06 derived from the PHL 293B integrated spectrum as the representative metallicity for the galaxy. Our IFU data reveal for the first time that the nebular HeII 4686 emission from PHL 293B is spatially extended and coincident with the ionizing stellar cluster, and allow us to compute its absolute HeII ionizing photon flux.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the blue compact dwarf PHL 293B, noting its low metallicity, distance, and changing broad emission features associated with an LBV or supershell scenario.

quotes.quote: In an effort to improve our understanding of very massive stars at low Z , we have monitored the blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxy PHL 293B. This galaxy lies at a distance of 23.1 Mpc Mould , and has a metallicity of Z 0.1Z_ Izo_thuan_st_2007 . Spectroscopic observations of the compact galaxy obtained between 2001--2011 consistently featured broad, strong emission components in the hydrogen Balmer lines.

Photometric analysis of PHL 293B revealed no optical photometric variability at the level of 0.1 mag between 1988 and 2013 Terlevich_shell . Based on this, Terlevich_shell suggested that the blueshifted absorptions of H I and Fe II were not caused by an LBV, but instead by an expanding supershell generated by the cluster wind of PHL 293B. The substantial spectral variation we report disfavours such a hypothesis.

gmw_00001348.name: And IX
gmw_00001348.overview: And IX is a metal-poor, low-mass dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:2302.07674] that is the closest satellite to M31 [arXiv:1904.03192, arXiv:2302.07674]. It contains a population of long-period variable stars [arXiv:2302.07674, arXiv:2302.07599] and experienced star formation quenching approximately 3.65 billion years ago [arXiv:2302.07674].

sources.summary: And IX is included despite M31 halo contamination; the authors isolate blue HB stars, find HB/TRGB agreement, and argue the Conn et al. TRGB distance is incompatible with the HST CMD.

quotes.quote: The upper RGB of M31 halo stars are 0.2-0.3 mag redder than the RGB of And IX, due to the increased metallicity of M31 stars. We confirmed this by constructing the F606W-F814W CMD of the parallel UVIS field for And IX, which only consists of M31 halo stars. Thus, the TRGB measurement of And IX is unaffected by M31.

Given the quality of our data, the red clumps and red HBs of M31 and And IX cannot be cleanly separated. Instead, we limited our HB analysis to the blue HB stars of And IX, none of which are present in the UVIS CMD of M31 stars. We find that our TRGB and HB distances to And IX are in good agreement, providing reassurance that contamination from M31 is not a large problem.

From inspection of the CMD in Figure fig:outlier_compare , it is clear that the conn2012 distance of =23.89_ 0.08 ^ +0.31 is not compatible with the TRGB location of And IX. Given And IX's projected proximity to M31, it is possible that contamination from M31 affected the conn2012 measurement.

sources.summary: The paper studies And IX as a nearby M31 dwarf spheroidal, deriving its distance, LPV population, star-formation history, quenching, stellar mass, and dust-return rate.

quotes.quote: We present a photometric study of the resolved stellar populations in And IX, the closest satellite to the M31, a metal-poor and low-mass dwarf spheroidal galaxy. We estimate a distance modulus of 24.56_ 0.15 ^ +0.05 mag based on the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB). By probing the variability of asymptotic giant branch stars (AGB), we study the star formation history of And IX. We identified 50 long period variables (LPVs) in And IX using the Isaac Newton Telescope (INT) in two filters, Sloan i' and Harris V .

Our findings suggest an outside-in galaxy formation scenario for And IX with a quenching occurring 3.65_ 1.52 ^ +0.13 Gyr ago with the SFR in the order of 2.0 10^ 4 M yr ^ 1 at redshift < 0.5 . We calculate the total stellar mass by integrating the star formation rate (SFR) within two half-light radii 3.0 10^5 M . By employing the spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting for observed LPVs in And IX, we evaluate the mass-loss rate in the range of 10^ 7 M 10^ 5 M yr ^ 1 . Finally, we show that the total mass deposition to the interstellar medium (ISM) is 2.4 10^ 4 M yr ^ 1 from the C- and O-rich type of dust-enshrouded LPVs.

sources.summary: The paper studies the And IX dwarf spheroidal using long-period variables, reconstructing its star-formation history, distance modulus, and metallicity-dependent stellar mass-return rate.

quotes.quote: For this purpose, we chose the And IX dSph galaxy, which is one of the observational targets of the Isaac Newton Telescope (INT) survey. A total of 50 long-period variables (LPVs) were found in And IX in two filters, Sloan i' and Harris V at a half-light radius of 2.5 arcmin. The And IX's star formation history (SFH) was constructed with a maximum star formation rate (SFR) of about 0.00082 0.00031 M yr ^ 1 , using LPVs as a tracer. The total mass return rate of LPVs was calculated based on the spectral energy distribution (SED) of about 2.4 10^ 4 M yr ^ 1 .
Fig. \ 1 presents SFH in two half-light radii (5 arcmin) of And IX in three metallicities. The highest peak of SFR reached at a level of 0.00082 0.00031 M yr ^ 1 in 6 Gyr ago at Z = 0.0001.
The total mass return rate is about 2.4 10^ 4 M yr ^ 1 in And IX at Z = 0.0001.

sources.summary: The paper groups Andromeda IX among M31's closest satellites, finding possible plane-consistent orbits but arguing its apparent plane membership is likely accidental.

quotes.quote: M32, NGC205 and Andromeda IX are M31's three closest satellites, with distances 23kpc, 42kpc and 40kpc from the centre of M31 respectively. As long as their velocity is small enough, there are orbits consistent with the plane for all angles between the satellite's angular momentum and the plane normal. It is likely these satellites are not truly a part of the planar structure, with their position only consistent with the plane due to their close proximity to the origin of M31. Indeed, 2013Natur.493...62I considered M32 and NGC205 as less likely planar members for this reason. The discontinuous nature of the contours in plots for M32 and NGC205 is caused by the fact that the distance uncertainties are significant compared to their actual distance from M31 and hence our five samples from the uncertainty band are not fine enough to produce smooth results.

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative shallow abundance trend for And IX and finds it remains relatively metal-rich compared with other dwarf spheroidals of similar mass.

quotes.quote: We also find tentative evidence for a slope in the - \ distribution for And IX ( m = -0.12 0.10 ). Using HST photometry, martin17 found And IX was relatively metal-rich for its stellar mass, but they noted that their sample likely had some contamination from the M31 halo. Such contamination would affect the slope by making it more flat. However, even considering our strict membership criteria, we still find that And IX is more metal-rich than other dSphs of similar mass. We note that the slope ( d / d ) we measure from coadded stars in And IX is significantly shallower compared to other dSphs with similar stellar masses.

gmw_00001349.name: HIPASS J1348-37
gmw_00001349.overview: HIPASS J1348-37 is a transition-type dwarf galaxy that contains neutral gas but shows an absence of star formation activity for ages younger than 60 Myr [arXiv:1203.5817]. It is a rather isolated galaxy with a total stellar mass estimated at approximately 1.9 million solar masses [arXiv:1203.5817].

sources.summary: The paper describes HIPASS J1348-37 as an isolated, faint CenA/M83 dwarf with neutral gas, little recent star formation, low metallicity, and a stellar mass near 10^7 solar masses.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J1348-37 Within the CenA subgroup of late-type dwarfs, HIPASS J1348-37 is the faintest one (together with KK196, see Table infogen ), and is the galaxy with the lowest density. Due to its large deprojected distance from CenA ( 2.1 0.7 Mpc, computed using the radial distances found in kara07 ), we also try to compute its deprojected distance from M83, and find that HIPASS J1348-37 is actually closer to the latter than to CenA ( 1.0 0.5 Mpc). The subgroup membership of our late-type targets was initially assigned following the classification of kara05 . However, with the new and more precise distance measurements of kara07 , HIPASS J1348-37 (together with other two galaxies of this subsample, namely KK182 and ESO384-16) is found to have a negative tidal index ( 1.2 , kara07 ) instead of the positive one that was reported in older papers, and thus turns out to be a rather isolated galaxy.

HIPASS J1348-37 contains a few 10^ 7 M _ of neutral gas banks99, georgiev08 , but has not been studied in H yet. Its CMD, presented in Fig. fore and containing 3300 stars, shows an absence of MS stars and presence of a number of BL, RSG and luminous AGB stars. As ESO444-78 is in the M83 sample, from the characteristics of its CMD HIPASS J1348-37 could be classified as transition-type dwarf, i.e. it contains >10^6 M _ of neutral gas but shows an absence of star formation activity in the past 60 Myr e.g. grebel03 . We derive the SFH for HIPASS J1348-37, although the information stemming from the CMD is mainly based on the old RGB stars and on a few intermediate-age stars, and plot it in Fig. sfhsnew . From the lack of bright MS and BL stars, we find no significant star formation for ages younger than 60 Myr, and the average SFR from ages older than that is 0.0014 0.0005 M _ yr ^ 1 . As in crnojevic11b , we adopt a standard CDM cosmology with t_0=13.7 Gyr, H_0=71 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ =0.73 and _ m =0.27 . Assuming the formation epoch of the galaxy to have been around 13.5 Gyr ago, we thus estimate that HIPASS J1348-37 had already formed 50 ^ +10 _ 40 of its stellar content prior to 5 Gyr ago ( z 0.5 ). Note that the errorbars reflect the age uncertainty in the SFH at these old ages. In particular, this galaxy has been slightly more active in its last 1 Gyr of life, as also indicated by the parameters listed in Table ressfh , with moderate enhancements of the star formation 80 and 400 Myr ago. The star formation episode at 400 Myr ago is very uncertain, because this is the oldest age where the blue loop stars are included in the CMD and it may indicate increased noise at the lower end of the CMD instead of a real increase in star formation. However, the enhancement in the 250-1000 Myr range is real because of the large number of stars on the blue side of the RGB (they are too blue to be ancient, metal-poor stars). The average metallicity of HIPASS J1348-37 derived from the SFH recovery is [ Fe/H ] =-1.50 0.07 . As a test of our derived SFHs, we compute the total stellar mass for the galaxy in two different ways. We find a value of 1.9^ +1.1 _ 1.0 10^ 7 M _ from our SFH. On the other hand, we consider the B band luminosity and assume a stellar mass-to-light ratio of 1 or 2 (typical values for gas-rich dwarfs, e.g., banks99 ). We can then estimate the stellar mass to be 0.9 10^ 7 M _ to 1.7 10^ 7 M _ , which is consistent with the previously derived value.

gmw_00001350.name: KK196
gmw_00001350.overview: KK196 is a dense dwarf galaxy located close to Centaurus A [arXiv:1203.5817]. It has experienced little star formation activity over the past 300 to 400 million years, though it contains a localized, star-forming region detected in H-alpha [arXiv:1203.5817].

sources.summary: KK196 is treated as a dense CenA dwarf with little very recent star formation, an H detection, enhanced activity hundreds of Myr ago, and metallicity consistent with oxygen-abundance constraints.

quotes.quote: KK196, AM1318-444 As faint as HIPASS J1348-37, but with a higher stellar density, KK196 is the next object considered in our sample, with 7300 stellar sources detected. Although its CMD shows features similar to HIPASS J1348-37 (Fig. fore , few BL and RSG stars, with a small stellar concentration in the luminous AGB region), KK196 is located much closer to CenA, at a deprojected distance of 260 390 kpc and in a denser environment ( =2.2 , kara07 ). Moreover, its projected position puts it in the middle of CenA's southern radio lobe see israel98 . KK196 has properties similar to ESO444-78 and IC4247 in the M83 subgroup, however, it is not detected in HI e.g., kara07 . Curiously, this dwarf shows almost no sign of a MS in its CMD, but it has an H detection from lee07 . Finally, LEDA http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/. reports an internal extinction value in the B band of 0.50 mag due to the inclination of the galaxy.

We show the result of our SFH recovery in Fig. sfhsnew . As for HIPASS J1348-37, the CMD does not show the presence of stars with ages 100 Myr. The average SFR is 0.002 0.0011 M _ yr ^ 1 , with an enhancement at 400-900 Myr ago, preceded by 1 Gyr of low activity. In the period 3.5-7 Gyr ago, KK196 also experienced a SFR slightly higher than the average one, although at such old ages we do not have the resolution to see smaller fluctuations. Overall, this galaxy has not been very active in the past 300-400 Myr (see Table ressfh ), and the H detection mentioned above can be the result of a short, localized, burst of star formation, which does not produce enough stars to be significantly detected in the CMD. Indeed, if we look at the HST image of KK196 and check where our detected stars are, the (small) central region where H was detected by lee07 is not resolved. From the H data we are able to compute the current SFR for KK196 (for details see the Section about IC4247), which has a value of 0.0046 0.0004 M _ yr ^ 1 , and which we also plot in Fig. sfhsnew . Since this data point refers only to one single star-forming region in this galaxy, it is not representative of the most recent ( 10 Myr) star formation activity of the galaxy as a whole, which lies well below this value. KK196 has formed 60 ^ +20 _ 30 of its stars more than 5 Gyr ago. From the SFH recovery, we derive an average metallicity value of [ Fe/H ] =-1.43 0.25 , which is in excellent agreement with the value we compute starting from the oxygen abundance listed in lee07 , [ Fe/H ] =-1.43 0.02 .

gmw_00001351.name: KK182
gmw_00001351.overview: KK182 is an isolated gas-rich dwarf galaxy located near M83 [arXiv:1203.5817]. It features a peculiar triangular shape, low metallicity, and a stellar mass of approximately 1.3 × 10⁷ solar masses [arXiv:1203.5817].

sources.summary: KK182 is described as an isolated gas-rich dwarf near M83, with young stellar populations, bursty recent star formation, low metallicity, and a stellar mass of about 10^7 solar masses.

quotes.quote: KK182, Cen6 The CMD for KK182 (Fig. fore ) has 4100 stars, and shows a fairly well populated MS/BL region, extending to much younger ages than in HIPASS J1348-37 and KK196, although there are comparatively few RGB and luminous AGB stars. This galaxy has a high deprojected distance from CenA ( 2.0 0.6 Mpc) but it is actually closer to M83 ( 1.3 0.3 Mpc), with a negative tidal index that makes it a quite isolated object kara07 .

KK182 contains a moderate amount of neutral gas ( 5 10^ 7 M _ ) and has also been detected in H cote09 . The internal extinction (due to inclination) given by LEDA is A_B 0.78 . Finally, the optical image of KK182 reveals a peculiar triangular shape for this object.

KK182 has experienced most of its star formation in the past 0.5 Gyr, with many short-lived episodes of star formation ( 10-100 Myr in duration) for ages <0.5 Gyr, the strongest of which had strengths of several times the average SFR . This is reflected in the high values of b_ 100 , b_ 500 , and b_ 1G reported in Table ressfh . KK182 thus formed only 35 ^ +15 _ 25 of its stars more than 5 Gyr ago. This result is quite similar to the SFH for ESO443-09 in the M83 subgroup crnojevic11b , the latter being also a very isolated object. The star formation value we derive for the youngest time bin (resolving ages of 4 Myr) is not in disagreement with the value that cote09 find starting from the H flux of this galaxy ( 0.0004 M _ yr ^ 1 , see also Fig. sfhsnew ), considering its off-center star forming region. Their study does not give errorbars, but it is likely that the two values are similar once the observational errors are considered. The metallicity content of this galaxy is low and in line with its luminosity ( [ Fe/H ] =-1.46 0.21 ). The stellar mass as obtained from the SFH is 1.3^ +1.0 _ 0.7 10^ 7 M _ . If we compute again the stellar mass from the luminosity of KK182 and a given stellar mass-to-light ratio (1 or 2), we get 1.5 10^ 7 M _ and 3.0 10^ 7 M _ . In this case we are thus slightly underestimating the stellar mass, and this is likely to happen because of the small number of old RGB stars present in the shallow CMD.

gmw_00001352.name: ESO384-16
gmw_00001352.overview: ESO384-16 is a transition-type or lenticular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1203.5817] that hosts two globular clusters [arXiv:1203.5817]. It contains an asymmetric distribution of neutral gas [arXiv:1203.5817] and has experienced sparse recent star formation alongside older enhanced episodes [arXiv:1203.5817].

sources.summary: ESO384-16 is described as a transition or lenticular dwarf with asymmetric gas, sparse recent star formation, enhanced older episodes, and a relatively high mean metallicity for the sample.

quotes.quote: ESO384-16 ESO384-16 is considered to be a transition-type or lenticular dwarf e.g., jerjen00b, beaulieu06, bouchard07 , with a rather regular elliptical shape and a high central stellar density ( 3.5 stars per arcsec ^ 2 corresponding to 660 stars per 0.1 kpc ^ 2 , at luminosities between the TRGB and one mag below the TRGB, and after correcting for incompleteness).

In the mentioned study, the neutral gas mass is reported to be 10^ 6 M _ , and its spatial distribution is described as asymmetric. For this reason, beaulieu06 suggest that ESO384-16 is actually falling toward the center of the group. bouchard07 also suggest that this galaxy may be experiencing a mild ram-pressure stripping from the intra-group medium, based on the asymmetries in its HI distribution.

In its CMD, which has 17200 stars, we identify an overdensity of luminous AGB stars above a prominent RGB, but the BL and RSG regions are sparsely populated, indicating very little star formation in the past 100 Myr. However, this galaxy was detected in H by bouchard08 . ESO384-16 is located halfway between the dominant giants of the group, at a deprojected distance of 1.0 0.4 Mpc from CenA and 0.9 0.4 Mpc from M83, and has a negative tidal index kara07 . According to georgiev08 , ESO384-16 hosts two globular clusters. When deriving its SFH, we immediately notice that this galaxy has been one of the most active in our CenA sample, with an average SFR of 0.006 0.0023 M _ yr ^ 1 . As reported in Table ressfh , the parameter b_ 14G is the only one being >1 , meaning a SFR higher than the average for ages older than 1 Gyr ago. About 60 ^ +15 _ 25 of the stars in ESO384-16 were born more than 5 Gyr ago. The recovered SFH (Fig. sfhsnew ) shows two episodes of enhanced star formation, one taking place 250-400 Myr ago, and an older one occurring between 2 and 3 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper reports a defined upper-AGB population, hints of young main-sequence stars, faint diffuse emission, detected H I, and a 3D distance from NGC5128.

quotes.quote: ESO384-G016 This dS0/Im galaxy has a clearly defined upper-AGB population and some hints of a young main sequence karachentsev2007 while the emission is faint and diffuse. beaulieu2006 found 6 10^6 of in this galaxy. It is situated at 790 180 kpc (3D) from NGC5128.

gmw_00001353.name: ESO269-58
gmw_00001353.overview: ESO269-58 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located approximately 300 kpc from Centaurus A [arXiv:2308.13121]. The galaxy contains 25 million solar masses of neutral gas [arXiv:1203.5817], features a central dust lane [arXiv:1203.5817], and exhibits a high average star formation rate [arXiv:1203.5817, arXiv:2308.13121].

sources.summary: ESO269-58 is presented as a rich, crowded dwarf with dust and gas, high average star formation, intermediate-age enhancement, rapid chemical enrichment, and a massive stellar component.

quotes.quote: ESO269-58 ESO269-58 is perhaps the most intriguing object in this sample, as can already be seen from its rich CMD ( 136000 stars). While relatively few BL and RSG stars are present in this galaxy, the most prominent features are a very broad RGB and an extremely well populated luminous AGB region (see Fig. fore ). This peculiar irregular galaxy shows a prominent dust lane in its central regions, and contains 25 10^ 6 M _ of neutral gas.

The average rate at which ESO269-58 has been forming stars is the highest in our sample ( 0.07 0.04 M _ yr ^ 1 ). Just as KK196 and ESO384-16, this object had a SFR higher than the lifetime average value just for ages >1 Gyr ago. ESO269-58 formed 50 ^ +15 _ 15 of its stellar content more than 5 Gyr ago. The SFH recovery of this object has been made more difficult by the high crowding exhibited by this object. Its peak stellar density is 10 stars per arcsec ^ 2 (down to I 27 ), and the 1 photometric errors at a magnitude of I=25 are already 0.15 mag in magnitude and 0.24 mag in color, which has the effect of significantly broadening the observed features in the CMD. The observed broadening could in part also be due to differential reddening, but the fact that the TRGB is not stretched along the reddening vector, and that the MS does not look heavily affected, points toward ordinary crowding effects. We find new best-fitting values for the reddening and the distance modulus, namely E(B-V)=0.15 instead of 0.10, and (m-M)_ 0 =27.80 instead of 27.90 0.16 . As can be seen from the SFH plotted in Fig. sfhsnew , ESO269-58 experienced an enhanced star formation between 3 and 5 Gyr ago, which was followed by a rapid chemical enrichment.

This is confirmed by the curvature of the RGB and the metal-rich red turnover of the luminous AGB (see Fig. fore ), and the resulting mean metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] =-0.98 0.20 ) is in agreement with the absolute luminosity of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO269-58 as a Centaurus A dwarf irregular within 600 kpc, about 300 kpc from Centaurus A, with notable giant-branch features and higher star formation than KK196.

quotes.quote: Unlike the Milky Way satellites, extensive observational studies on star formation histories of Centaurus A satellites are not available, with the exception of KK197, ESO-269066, ESO-381018 Marakova+2007 and five dwarf irregulars KK182 (Cen6), ESO269-58, KK196 (AM1318-444), HIPASS J1348-37, ESO384-16 Crnojevic+2012 .

Two of the dwarf irregulars (KK196 and ESO269-58) studied are within 600 kpc (see Figure 6 of ). Positioned in the middle of Centaurus A's southern radio lobe, KK196 has a star formation rate of 0.0046 0.0004 M _ yr^ 1 and has formed more than 60 _ 30 ^ +20 of stars more than 5 Gyrs ago Crnojevic+2012 . Meanwhile, ESO269-58, located 300 50 kpc from Centaurus A, has few blue loop, red supergiants and a very broad red giant branch stars, and dense asymptotic giant branch zone. This dwarf has a higher star formation rate compared to KK196 with 0.07 0.04 M _ yr^ 1 , and has formed 50 _ 15 ^ +15 of stars more than 5 Gyrs ago.

gmw_00001354.name: system 10
gmw_00001354.overview: System 10 is a newly recognized multiply lensed background galaxy [arXiv:1512.07916]. Only a portion of this background galaxy is multiply lensed, and its brightest counterimage has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.73 [arXiv:1512.07916].
