This residual flexion is plausible since many group-member galaxies are not accounted for in our lens model except for the brightest eight. Our model has 17 free parameters in total, and there are 24 data points from 4 positions for each of the A, B, and C image sets. The lens model is optimized by minimizing the total distances between the mapped positions of each image set on the source plane. Figure fig:lens_model1 illustrates the best-fit lens model with the caustic and critical curves. The lower panels of Figure fig:lens_model1 show the intrinsic galaxy morphology from HST and KCWI continuum images, reconstructed in the z=1.87 source plane. The three star-forming complexes (A, B and C) span a physical distance of 8 kpc in the source plane, using our fiducial cosmology. We find that the mean areal magnification of the southwestern counter-image of the galaxy is | |= . We obtain a magnification corrected stellar mass and SFR of _ 10 (M_*/ )= and _ 10 ( SFR/( yr^ 1 ))= after scaling the BEAGLE outputs presented in ramesh_CSWA . ht Galaxy morphology CSWA13 is a moderately dusty ( _V 0.5 ) galaxy with specific SFR placing it above the star-forming main sequence at z 2 e.g., z2_mainsequence . The spatially resolved kinematics from this work uniquely allows us to tie the observed morphology with the kinematics of the gas around the galaxy. The clumpy morphology of CSWA13 (Figure fig:lens_model1 ) resembles a tadpole or a chain galaxy e.g., cowie-1995-HST,sidney-1996-hubbledeepfield,Elmegreen_2005,forster-schreiber-SINS-survey,elmegreen-tadpolegalaxies-udf with a bright head (region A) and a tail (regions B and C). These tadpoles are common at z 2 , comprising 10 of the galaxies in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field (HUDF) and 44 if these tadpoles are indeed edge-on projections of the clump-cluster Elmegreen_chains_and_disks and chain galaxies. While the nebular kinematics for a handful of tadpole galaxies at z 2 were measured as part of the SINS survey forster-schreiber-SINS-survey and show similar nebular kinematics as CSWA13, this paper is the first study to simultaneously map nebular and outflow kinematics in a galaxy of this type. Our overall aim is to quantify the key properties of outflows in CSWA13 such as the spatial extent, mass loss rate, and mass loading factor.

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J1237+5533 as a bright lensed source at z=1.87, reporting spectroscopic emission or absorption features that establish its redshift and characterize its observed gas or continuum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1237+5533, z=1.87: In addition to being among the brightest lensed galaxies in our sample, the arc toward SDSS J1237+5533 is fairly compact, translating into a very high surface brightness and easily detectable continuum emission. Strong absorption lines are readily apparent across the rest-UV (Figure 2). We obtain a redshift of z=1.863 from CII 1334 and z=1.868 from Ly 1216. The EW of Ly emission is uncertain due to the weak continuum signal at 3400 . The velocity offset between Ly emission and CII absorption is 570 km s ^ 1 , consistent with expectations from spectra at z 2-4 (Shapley et al.

gmw_00001382.name: SDSS J1958+5950
gmw_00001382.overview: SDSS J1958+5950 is a high-redshift lensed galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 2.22 [arXiv:2104.07812, arXiv:1302.2663]. Its source-plane morphology, kinematics, and clumpy star-forming regions suggest that the system is undergoing an active galaxy merger [arXiv:2104.07812, arXiv:1808.10468].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS1958+5950/cswa128 as a z=2.225 lensed galaxy case study, reconstructing its source-plane clumpy star-forming morphology with a pixelated Bayesian method.

quotes.quote: We describe the first application of this technique on one of the brightest targets from the Cambridge Sloan Survey of Wide Arcs in the Sky CASSOWARY; Stark13 sample, SDSS1958+5950 (hereafter referred by its survey ID: cswa128; z = 2.225 ). We show that this technique can successfully recover clump sizes with a higher SNR in the source plane as compared to the traditional ray-tracing. b 0.50 b 0.49 b 0.50 b 0.49 This work builds upon the observations of cswa128 and detailed lens modelling results from presented in (hereafter Paper I). The target galaxy, lensed by a galaxy group at z = 0.214 , is composed of two merging high magnification arcs a1 and a2 (Figure fig:fig1 ).

In this work, we recover the physical sizes of H ii regions that probe star-formation in cswa128 using an automated forward modelling technique for source-plane reconstruction of lensed galaxies. The technique delenses a uniformly pixelized grid, deconvolved from the source plane PSF, using efficient Bayesian optimization. The case study of the pixelated technique on cswa128, as presented in this work, clearly shows that the approach is successful in minimizing the anisotropic distortion due to the PSF in the source plane, yielding more accurate source profiles. The power of the pixelated method is shown through (1) the larger number of clump detections (2) the higher SNR values in clump detections, some of which are undetected with the direct reconstruction, and (3) the recovery of the most distorted and faintest clumps near the caustics in the source plane.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs the lensed z=2.225 galaxy SDSS1958+5950/cswa128, finding 170 pc source-plane resolution and evidence that its morphology and kinematics arise from an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: We present spatially resolved analysis of a lensed galaxy, SDSS1958+5950 at z = 2.225 , from the Cambridge Sloan Survey of Wide Arcs in the Sky (CASSOWARY). We use our new high resolution imaging data to construct a robust lens model for the galaxy group at z = 0.214 . We employ the updated lens model to combine the Integral Field Spectrographic observations on two highly distorted images of the lensed target. We adopt a forward-modeling approach to deconvolve the effects of point spread function from the combined source-plane reconstruction. The approach is adapted to the lens model magnification and enables a resolution of 170 pc in the galaxy-source plane.

We propose an ongoing merger as the origin of the lensed system on the basis of its source-plane morphology, kinematics and rest-frame emission line ratios. Using our novel technique of adaptive coadded source plane reconstruction, we are able to detect different components in the velocity gradient that were not seen in previous studies of this object, plausibly belonging to different components in the merging system.

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J1958+5950 as a bright lensed source at z=2.22, reporting spectroscopic emission or absorption features that establish its redshift and characterize its observed gas or continuum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1958+5950, z=2.22: At =19.6, this is one of the brightest lensed galaxies known in SDSS. Multiple strong absorption lines are detected in the MMT spectrum (Figure 2). We derive a redshift of z=2.222 from CII 1334 absorption, consistent with the redshifts derived from the other low ionisation absorption lines. While the lens is 6 arcsec away from the slit centre, the red continuum spectral slope possibly points to some low level contamination from one of the lensing galaxies. However the presence of strong absorption lines from the z=2.22 galaxy suggests that contaminating stellar continuum is not dominant in the wavelength regime we are considering.

gmw_00001383.name: SDSS J2158+0257
gmw_00001383.overview: SDSS J2158+0257 is a gravitational lens system situated in a group environment at a redshift of z=0.3 [arXiv:1306.2948]. The system acts as a lens for a bright background source located at a redshift of z=2.08 [arXiv:1302.2663].

sources.summary: The paper models the CASSOWARY lens J2158+0257 using lensing, central stellar dynamics, and group dynamics, finding a normal-concentration dark halo and a stellar mass-to-light ratio favoring a Salpeter IMF.

quotes.quote: We exploit the group environment of the CAmbridge Sloan Survey Of Wide ARcs in the skY (CASSOWARY) z=0.3 lens J2158+0257 to measure the group dynamical mass as a complement to the central dynamical and lensing mass constraints. Follow-up spectroscopy of candidate group members is performed using VLT/FORS2. From the resulting N=21 confirmed members we measure the group dynamical mass by calibrating an analytic tracer mass estimator with cosmological simulations. The luminosity weighted line-of-sight velocity dispersion and the Einstein radius of the lens are used as mass probes in the inner regions of the galaxy.

We find a halo mass of log_ 10 M_ 200 /M_ = 14.2^ +0.2 _ 0.2 , halo concentration c_ 200 =4.4^ +1.6 _ 1.4 and stellar mass-to-light ratio M/L ^*=5.7^ +1.2 _ 1.2 . Note that this M/L ^* corresponds to a stellar mass of M^* 1.2 10^ 12 M_ for the central galaxy.

Our maximum likelihood model is in excellent agreement with the predictions from simulations; our best-fit halo mass and concentration fall almost exactly on the mass-concentration relation found by . The stellar mass-to-light ratio M/L ^* 5.7 favors a Salpeter IMF, and is 2 away from the expectations for a Chabrier IMF (but see Section sec:beta_diss ).

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J2158+0257 as a bright lensed source at z=2.08, reporting spectroscopic emission or absorption features that establish its redshift and characterize its observed gas or continuum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2158+0257, z=2.08 Multiple absorption lines are detected throughout the MMT blue channel spectrum. We derive a mean low ionisation absorption line redshift of z=2.079 from CII 1334 and OI+SiII 1303. Ly is seen in absorption.

gmw_00001384.name: SDSS J2222+2745
gmw_00001384.overview: SDSS J2222+2745 is a gravitationally lensed quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.8 [arXiv:2011.02007, arXiv:2103.16960]. Lensed by a foreground galaxy cluster, the quasar is split into six multiple images [arXiv:2103.16960, arXiv:2301.11240] and exhibits strong intrinsic variability [arXiv:1505.06187].

sources.summary: The paper monitors multiply imaged quasar SDSS J2222+2745, builds six-year emission-line and continuum light curves, measures velocity-resolved lags, places it on the BLR radius-luminosity relation, and derives its black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: We have presented the first results of a 4.5 year spectroscopic and photometric monitoring campaign of the multiply imaged quasar SDSS J2222+2745 at z=2.805 . Our main results can be summarized as follows: After performing percent-level spectrophotometric flux calibration, we produce emission line light curves spanning over six years in duration in the observed frame. We give light curves for the integrated \ emission line, a narrow band filter covering the \ core, and four wavelength ranges covering the blue and red wings of . We measure integrated and velocity-resolved \ emission line lags with respect to the g band continuum. The integrated \ lag is _ cen = 36.5^ +2.9 _ 3.9 days in the rest frame ( 139^ +11 _ 15 days observed frame). We see velocity-resolved lag structure in which the core of the line responds the slowest ( 86.2^ +4.5 _ 5.0 days) and the wings respond the fastest ( 25^ +11 _ 15 and 7.5^ +4.2 _ 3.5 days for the blue and red wings, respectively).

We place SDSS J2222+2745 on the r_ BLR L relation with 33 data points from the literature and fit a linear regression to the data, finding _ 10 ( / days ) = (1.00 0.08) + (0.48 0.04) _ 10 [ L_ (1350 ) / 10^ 44   erg s ^ 1 ] with an intrinsic scatter of 0.32 0.06 dex. SDSS J2222+2745 lies 0.58 dex above the mean relation. We calculate emission line widths and pair these with the measured lags to obtain the virial product, M_ vir = M_ BH /f , for the four combinations of the (mean, rms) spectrum and (FWHM, _ line ) emission line widths. Assuming values of f based on , we compute the black hole mass for each combination.

sources.summary: The paper reports LOFAR 144 MHz emission from lensed images of SDSS J2222+2745, deriving intrinsic flux and luminosity while noting contamination from lensing galaxies.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2222+2745 SDSS J2222+2745 is a gravitationally lensed quasar at redshift 2.805 that was identified from follow-up observations of a candidate lensing cluster at redshift 0.49 dahle2013 . The lensing cluster has three similar-mass elliptical galaxies at the centre, which together produce a complex set of lensing caustics. This results in six lensed images of SDSS J2222+2745 being formed, with a maximum image separation of 15.1 arcsec.

The LoTSS image of SDSS J2222+2745 at 144 MHz is quite complex; there is clear radio emission associated with lensed images A, B and C, but there is also extended radio emission at the position of the lensing galaxies within the cluster. The emission from lensed images A and B is blended with a peak surface brightness of 745 Jy beam ^ 1 , which is equivalent to a 7 detection. Lensed image C is well resolved from the other lensed images and the lensing galaxies, and is detected at the 4 level; this is the first time that gravitationally lensed images of the same background source have been fully resolved with LOFAR.

Given their lensing magnifications, this implies an intrinsic (lensing corrected) flux density of 58 6 Jy for SDSS J2222+2745. Similar to above, this intrinsic flux density is below the detection threshold of LoTSS.

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J2222+2745 as a multiply imaged z=2.805 quasar with a thick inclined BLR disk, near-circular/inflowing kinematics, lags, and a black-hole mass estimate.

quotes.quote: We present \ BLR modeling results for the multiply imaged z=2.805 quasar SDSS J2222+2745. Using data covering a 5.3 year baseline after accounting for gravitational time delays, we find models that can reproduce the observed emission-line spectra and integrated \ fluctuations. The models suggest a thick disk BLR that is inclined by 40 degrees to the observer's line of sight and with a emissivity weighted median radius of r_ median = light days. The kinematics are dominated by near-circular Keplerian motion with the remainder inflowing.

The rest-frame lag one would measure from the models is _ median = days, which is consistent with measurements based on cross-correlation. We show a possible geometry and transfer function based on the model fits and find that the model-produced velocity-resolved lags are consistent with those from cross-correlation. We measure a black hole mass of _ 10 (M_ BH /M_ ) = , which requires a scale factor of _ 10 (f_ mean , ) = .

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J2222+2745 as a six-image cluster-lensed quasar with HST imaging, host-galaxy Ly-alpha work, lens modeling, and updated time delays.

quotes.quote: , discovered by , is a cluster at z = 0.49 that strongly lenses a quasar at z = 2.805. The quasar is imaged six times Sharon2017 with a maximum image separation of 15 1 (Table table:quasar_table ).

We used archival HST multi-color imaging from GO-13337 (PI: Sharon): WFC3/F160W, F110W (1 orbit) and ACS/F814W, F606W, F435W (6 orbits). These data were originally proposed to detect any additional quasar images and to compute a mass model Sharon2017 . Additionally, these \ data have enabled a spatially resolved study of the Lyman-alpha emission in the quasar host galaxy Bayliss2017 .

first published the time delay measurements between images A and B ( t _ AB = 47.7 6.0 days) and A and C ( t _ AC = -722 24 days). Then Dyrland2019 reported updated values for the time delays between images A and B ( t _ AB = 42.44 ^ +1.36 _ 1.44 days) and images A and C ( t _ AC = -696.65 ^ +2.00 _ 2.10 days). These measurements were based on data from a monitoring campaign at the 2.5m Nordic Optical Telescope.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J2222 + 2745 as a six-image lensed background quasar, selecting three usable MUSE spectra for absorption analysis across 4-15 arcsec image separations.

quotes.quote: The fields SDSS J1029 + 2623 (J1029 hereafter) and SDSS J2222 + 2745 (J2222 hereafter) were observed with MUSE for 4.8 h and 4.4 h on target, respectively [ PID: 0102.A-0642(A), 0103.A-0554(A); P.I.: C. Grillo ] .

The field J2222 (see left panel of Fig. fig:j2222 ) contains six multiple images (A, B, C, D, E, F) of a background quasar at \ = 2.801 that is lensed by a galaxy cluster at \ = 0.489. The quasar images D, E and F are contaminated by the light from nearby bright cluster members in the MUSE data, and their one-dimensional spectra cannot be utilized for an absorption line analysis. This work exploits the spectra of the quasar images A, B, and C (see right panel of Fig. fig:j2222 ). The angular separation between these images ranges from 4 to 15 arcsec. All the MUSE and UVES quasar spectra were converted into the heliocentric frame and vacuum wavelength for the analysis.

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J2222+2745 as a bright lensed source at z=2.30, reporting spectroscopic emission or absorption features that establish its redshift and characterize its observed gas or continuum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2222+2745, z=2.30 and z=2.81: We confirmed a lensed galaxy at z=2.31 and a quasar at z=2.81 in the first spectrum of we took of the SDSS J2222+2745 system. Both sources were recently reported in Dahle et al. (2012). The galaxy redshift is easily identified via the presence of Ly emission and weak interstellar absorption lines. The Ly rest-frame EW is 20 , the strongest in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper monitors SDSS J2222+2745 as a cluster-lensed six-image quasar, measuring A-B and C-A delays, strong intrinsic variability, and predicted brightening of images A and B.

quotes.quote: We report first results from an ongoing monitoring campaign to measure time delays between the six images of the quasar , gravitationally lensed by a galaxy cluster. The time delay between A and B, the two most highly magnified images, is measured to be _ AB = days (95 confidence interval), consistent with previous model predictions for this lens system. The strong intrinsic variability of the quasar also allows us to derive a time delay value of _ CA = days between image C and A, in spite of modest overlap between their light curves in the current data set. Image C, which is predicted to lead all the other lensed quasar images, has undergone a sharp, monotonic flux increase of 60-75 during 2014. A corresponding brightening is firmly predicted to occur in images A and B during 2016.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the lensed quasar SDSS J2222+2745, measuring image separations and ellipticities and deriving feasible SIS-plus-external-shear model parameter ranges.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2222+2745 The six images of the quasar SDSS J2222+2745, gravitationally lensed by a galaxy cluster, are the third set of images known of a quasar with maximum image separation over 10 arcseconds and the first example with five spectroscopically confirmed images, . We use the images labelled A and B by , as shown in Fig. fig:J2222 taken from this work, and assume that they are located near a fold singularity. Then, we measure their relative distance to be _ AB = 4.16 0.1 arcseconds and determine their ellipticities by running SExtractor in its standard configuration on the g band image of the field obtained from the SDSS DR12 Science Archive Server as _A = 0.193 0.035 _B = 0.121 0.045 . Evaluating the potential-derivative ratios from these measurements yields 1.26 10^ 5 _ 222 ^ (0) _ 11 ^ (0) rad ^ 1 1.466 . Without further information from velocity dispersions, compatible SIS+E models can be found within the parameter ranges a 1,9 10^ 6 and _1 0.03, 0.3 .

gmw_00001385.name: SDSS J2300+2213
gmw_00001385.overview: SDSS J2300+2213 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.93 [arXiv:1302.2663]. It is observed as a bright gravitationally lensed source that exhibits strong continuum emission and several absorption lines [arXiv:1302.2663].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J2300+2213 as a bright lensed source at z=1.93, reporting spectroscopic emission or absorption features that establish its redshift and characterize its observed gas or continuum.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2300+2213, z=1.93: We have observed the arcs in SDSS J2300+2213 on numerous occasions with MMT. We identify strong continuum and a number of absorption lines. In the MMT blue channel data, we identify absorption lines at 3816, 3906, 4473, 4543, and 4893 , which appears to correspond to absorption from OI+SiII 1303, CII 1334, Si II 1526, and CIV 1549.

gmw_00001386.name: J1606 + 3124
gmw_00001386.overview: J1606 + 3124 is a high-redshift compact symmetric radio galaxy [arXiv:1701.06622, arXiv:2209.10760] that was previously classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2209.10760]. The source is non-variable at radio frequencies [arXiv:1701.06622] and exhibits a flat X-ray spectrum consistent with heavy Compton-thick obscuration [arXiv:2007.01342].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1606+3124 as a peaked-spectrum source with no strong variability evidence and a Compact Symmetric Object VLBI structure.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of J1606+3124 is shown in Fig. fig:J1606+3124 . RMP2011 observed J1606+3124 98 times at 15 GHz between 2008 January 1 and 2009 December 28 with the 40 m telescope at the Owens Valley Radio Observatory. From this they concluded that J1606+3124 is not variable. While we discarded the matches to 2012AandA...544A..25M , we note that the authors did observe J1606+3124 six times with the RATAN-600 telescope between 2006 July and 2010 May at 21.7, 11.2, 7.7, 4.8 and 2.3 GHz, and five times at 1 GHz over the same period. These observations also indicate that J1606+3124 is not variable at these frequencies. The average 15 GHz flux density of RMP2011 at each frequency are plotted in Fig. fig:J1606+3124 .

sources.summary: The paper presents J1606 + 3124 as a hard, X-ray-deficient outlier whose spectrum is consistent with heavy Compton-thick obscuration.

quotes.quote: As discussed in Section sect:hr , J1606 + 3124 has a hardness ratio of +0.71 +0.15 0.10 that deviates from the sample average of 0.29 +0.15 0.21 by more than 3 . Additionally, our spectral analysis of J1606 in Section sect:lumin found its spectrum to be abnormally flat with a 2 keV luminosity _ 2 keV = 0.2 10^ 27 erg s^ 1 Hz^ 1 , well-below the remaining sample (Figure fig:hr ). These properties are consistent with a dense absorption column at the source. Analysis of the observed spectrum for J1606 in Section sect:xray found N_ H ^ i = 1.34 +0.45 0.38 10^ 24 cm^ 2 when was fixed to the best-fit value from the remaining sample.

sources.summary: The paper reinterprets J1606 + 3124 from a flat-spectrum quasar classification toward a high-redshift compact symmetric radio galaxy, supported by spectral-index maps and archival spectra.

quotes.quote: J1606 + 3124 This source was previously identified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar 2007ApJS..171...61H,2007AA...469..451T,2016MNRAS.463.3260C . However, further radio spectral studies based on simultaneous multi-frequency observations suggested that the source is a GPS radio galaxy in the early Universe RATAN-600, 2012AA...544A..25M,2019AstBu..74..348S . In our study, by analyzing the spectral index map and the archival radio spectra, we conclude that the source could be the a high-redshift CSO source.

gmw_00001387.name: J0311 + 0507
gmw_00001387.overview: J0311+0507 is a high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:1610.02057, arXiv:1701.06622] with a Fanaroff-Riley II radio structure [arXiv:2007.01342, arXiv:1701.06622]. It is classified as a steep-spectrum source [arXiv:1701.06622] and exhibits compact oxygen emission with a complex line profile [arXiv:1610.02057].

sources.summary: RC J0311 + 0507 is confirmed at high redshift with oxygen emission, complex line profiles, compact line emission, a velocity gradient, and broad line widths.

quotes.quote: RC J0311 + 0507 RC J0311 + 0507 (4C + 04.11) had previously a spectroscopic redshift of z = 4.514 measured only from Ly pariiski98 . We identify [ OII ] 3726,3729, and [ NeIII ] 3869,3968 in the K and H-band (Fig. fig:tspec ). This is consistent with the previously published redshift, which was based only on Ly . [ OII ] 3726,3729 is found at good S/N in the integrated spectrum (Fig. fig:tspec ). RC J0311 + 0507 has a complex line profile, with a broad component of FWHM = 1400 km s ^ 1 , superimposed on a component which is narrow enough (FWHM = 112 km s ^ 1 ) for the doublet to be spectrally resolved.

The line emission in RC J0311 + 0507 appears fairly compact at the spatial resolution of our data (FWHM = 1.0 0.9 \ along right ascension and declination, respectively, corresponding to 6 5.5 kpc at z = 4.51). Although nominally, the source appears spatially unresolved, the velocity map (measured from [ OII ] ) does show a gradient of 220 km s ^ 1 . The presence of a velocity gradient suggests that the source is marginally spatially resolved with an intrinsic size that is very near the size of the seeing disk. collet14a used Monte-Carlo simulations to investigate how low spatial resolution affects measurements of velocity gradients in marginally spatially resolved SINFONI maps akin to our data. A velocity offset of 400-500 km s ^ 1 would however not strongly affect the FWHM line widths, which are above 900-1000 km s ^ 1 on average, and up to 1300 km s ^ 1 in the western part of the emission-line region.

sources.summary: The paper explains J0311 + 0507's anomalous radio-to-X-ray ratio as arising from extended radio emission, while Chandra localizes detected X-rays to the core.

quotes.quote: The comparison of radio and X-ray luminosities in Figure fig:radio shows two outliers with above-average radio--X-ray luminosity ratios, J1606 + 3124 and J0311 + 0507. The X-ray deficit in J1606 is likely attributed to the intrinsic absorption at the source, as examined in Section sect:J1606 . However, there is no evidence of intrinsic absorption in J0311, necessitating further analysis. Previous 1.7 and 5 GHz observations with both the Multi-Element Radio Linked Interferometer Network (MERLIN) and European Very Long Baseline Interferometer Network (e-EVN) indicated J0311 to have an FR II structure with a high degree of asymmetry and a linear scale of 18.7 kpc, or 2 8 Parijskij2014 .

However, our analysis in Section sect:extend found no evidence of extended emission from the source greater than the telescope's spatial resolution of limit 0 5. Thus, the observed X-rays are localized to the core of J0311. As noted in Section sect:radio , the reported radio luminosities represent the total emission for each source. Taking the ratio of the core to the total radio flux from , we estimated the rest-frame 5 GHz for the core of J0311 to be _ 5 GHz = 12.6 10^ 33 erg s^ 1 Hz^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0311+0507 as steep-spectrum, possibly ultra-steep, after rejecting blended catalog matches and noting its FR II VLBI structure.

quotes.quote: J0311+0507 was classified as a USS source by 1994AandAS..108...79R who found it to have a spectral index of 1.17 0.03 between 150 MHz and 4.85 GHz. We fitted the spectrum (shown in Fig. fig:J0311+0507 ) with a single power law with a spectral index of =-0.94 0.06 , and therefore classify J0311+0507 as a steep-spectrum source that could also be a USS source. We do note that our spectral index is higher than the spectral index of 1.31 between 365 and 4850 MHz found by 1992AZh....69..673G and and references therein 2014MNRAS.439.2314P . As a final point, we note that the 1.7 and 5 GHz PTK2014(V) VLBI observations of J0311+0507 showed that it has a FR II structure, and an angular and linear size of 2.8 arcsec and 18.7 kpc, respectively.

gmw_00001388.name: M1540-1453
gmw_00001388.overview: M1540-1453 is a radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.1 [arXiv:2202.11755]. Its radio jets exhibit a Fanaroff-Riley Class I morphology and encounter a substantial amount of gas about 70 parsecs from the active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2202.11755].

sources.summary: The paper describes M1540-1453 as a powerful radio-loud quasar with FRI morphology, dense jet-interacting gas, widespread 21-cm absorption, and a possible ghostly DLA.

quotes.quote: we present optical spectroscopy and 1.5 GHz milliarcsecond (mas)-scale imaging of a powerful radio quasar - M1540-1453 ( = 2.1; RA: 15:40:15.2353 0.0003; Dec: -14:53:42.409 0.011). The radio source was identified as a high- z quasar through a large optical survey of mid-infrared color-selected powerful radio sources Gupta21salt . The associated radio continuum emission corresponds to a 1.4 GHz spectral luminosity, L_ 1.4GHz = 5.9 10^ 27 W Hz ^ 1 and radio loudness, R = f_ 5GHz /f_ 2500 = 4864).

In the case of M1540-1453, assuming the `+' in Fig. fig:vlba denotes the location of the radio core i.e., AGN, both the eastern and western jets are bright near the base and gradually fade into diffuse extended emission. No significant brightness asymmetry is observed on either side, but slight bends in the trajectory of jets at 4 mJy beam ^ 1 (fifth contour in Fig. fig:vlba ; 8 mas i.e., 70 pc from the core) are noticeable. These are reminiscent of the onset of dentist-drill effect beyond which the interaction with the ambient medium makes it harder for the jets to remain collimated and relativistic Scheuer1974 . Clearly, the radio jet has FRI morphology and is encountering substantial amount of gas at 70 pc from the AGN.

gmw_00001389.name: J0924--2201
gmw_00001389.overview: J0924--2201 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 5.2 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501447, arXiv:astro-ph/0703797]. It is a young, primeval galaxy undergoing massive star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0501447] and is surrounded by an overdensity of Lyman-alpha emitters that may form a massive cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0408520, arXiv:astro-ph/0610567].

sources.summary: The paper reports CO detections from TN J0924--2201 at z=5.2, interpreting its molecular gas as evidence for massive star formation in a young high-redshift radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: Using the Australia Telescope Compact Array we have detected (1-0) and (5-4) from TN J0924--2201 at z=5.2 , the most distant radio galaxy known to date. This is the second highest redshift detection of published so far. The (1-0) is 250-400 wide with a peak flux density of 520 115 Jy beam ^ 1 whilst the (5-4) line emission is 200-300 wide with a peak flux density of 7.8 2.7 mJy beam ^ 1 . Both transitions are spatially unresolved but there is marginal evidence for spatial offsets between the and the host galaxy; the (1-0) is located 28 11 kpc ( 4 5 1 7 ) north of the radio galaxy whilst the (5-4) is located 18 8 kpc ( 2 8 1 2 ) south of the radio galaxy.

TN J0924--2201 was initially discovered cdb00 from radio surveys using the well established technique for finding HzRGs of selecting ultra-steep spectrum sources with faint K-band counterparts (e.g. cha96,blu98 ). Follow-up optical spectroscopy detected faint Lyman- emission from the host galaxy at a redshift of 5.1989 0.0006 wvb99,ven04 . Based on its compact ( 1 3 ) radio morphology, under-luminous Lyman- emission compared with other HzRGs and rest frame 350 nm emission composed of several clumpy optical features, it was suggested that TN J0924--2201 is a young, primeval galaxy actively forming the bulk of its stars wvb99 .

sources.summary: The paper presents VLT imaging around the z=5.2 radio galaxy TN J0924--2201 and finds an overdensity of nearby emitters that may trace a forming massive cluster.

quotes.quote: We present the results of narrow-band and broad-band imaging with the Very Large Telescope Based on observations carried out at the European Southern Observatory, Paranal, Chile, programs LP167.A-0409 and 70.A-0589 of the field surrounding the radio galaxy TN J0924--2201 at z = 5.2 . Fourteen candidate \ emitters with a rest-frame equivalent width of > 20 \ were detected. Spectroscopy of 8 of these objects showed that 6 have redshifts similar to that of the radio galaxy. The density of emitters at the redshift of the radio galaxy is estimated to be a factor 1.5--6.2 higher than in the field, and comparable to the density of \ emitters in radio galaxy protoclusters at z = 4.1, 3.1 and 2.2. The \ emitters near TN J0924--2201 could therefore be part of a structure that will evolve into a massive cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes TN J0924--2201 as the then highest-redshift radio galaxy, with six nearby Ly-alpha emitters and an overdense field comparable to other radio-galaxy protoclusters.

quotes.quote: TN J0924--2201 is currently the highest redshift radio galaxy known with a redshift of z = 5.2 bre99 . To select \ emitters in this field, a custom narrow-band filter encompassing the wavelength of the \ line of the radio galaxy at 7537 \ was purchased. The details of the observations and candidate selection in this field are presented in ven04 . Follow-up spectroscopy of eight of 14 candidate \ emitters resulted in the discovery of six \ emitters near the radio galaxy ven04 . The field is overdense in \ emitters by a factor of 1.5--6.2 times the field density at that redshift and comparable in density to the radio galaxy fields 1138, 0316 and 1338 at z = 2.2, 3.1 and 4.1 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0924--2201 as a z=5.2 radio galaxy, searches for associated HI absorption, and reports stringent non-detection limits around its CO-determined host-galaxy redshift.

quotes.quote: We have searched for HI absorption toward the two brightest radio AGN at high redshift, J0924--2201 at z = 5.20 , and J0913+5919 at z=5.11 , using the Giant Meter Wave Radio Telescope (GMRT). These data set a 3 upper limit to absorption of < 30 at 40 km s ^ 1 resolution for the 30 mJy source J0913+5919, and < 3 for the 0.55 Jy source J0924--2201 at 20 km s ^ 1 resolution. For J0924--2201, limits to broader lines at the few percent level are set by residual spectral baseline structure.

The source TN J0924--2201 is a radio galaxy with an optical redshift of z = 5.19 0.01 (van Breugel et al. For J0924--2201 the host galaxy redshift is z = 5.202 0.001 , placing the line at a frequency of 229.0 0.1 MHz.

gmw_00001390.name: J0913+5919
gmw_00001390.overview: J0913+5919 is a luminous, high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2006.13762, arXiv:astro-ph/0703797] located at a redshift of approximately z = 5.11 [arXiv:2006.13762, arXiv:astro-ph/0703797]. High-resolution radio observations reveal that the source has a compact structure [arXiv:2006.13762] and a relatively narrow Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:2006.13762].

sources.summary: The paper presents J0913+5919 as a z=5.11 narrow-Ly quasar with compact VLBA radio emission, a failed H I absorption search, and strong GMRT detection.

quotes.quote: SDSS J091316.56+591921.5 (hereafter J0913+5919) Anderson2001 reported this target to have a relatively narrow Ly emission line at a redshift of z = 5.11 . The redshift measurement was improved to 5.1224 by Hewett2010 through investigating the empirical relationships between redshifts and multiple line emissions. The high-resolution 1.4 GHz VLBA observations Momjian2003 revealed a compact radio property.

Motivated by the compact nature and the narrow Ly line, Carilli2007 searched for the redshifted H i 21 cm absorption line to this object with GMRT, but unfortunately found nothing. This object is a point source in our new GMRT observations. The 323 MHz peak flux density is 9.80 0.12 mJy beam ^ 1 , which makes it the second strongest detection in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper treats J0913+5919 as a z=5.11 luminous quasar target for HI absorption, measuring its low-frequency flux and deriving optical-depth and column-density limits.

quotes.quote: We have searched for HI absorption toward the two brightest radio AGN at high redshift, J0924--2201 at z = 5.20 , and J0913+5919 at z=5.11 , using the Giant Meter Wave Radio Telescope (GMRT). These data set a 3 upper limit to absorption of < 30 at 40 km s ^ 1 resolution for the 30 mJy source J0913+5919, and < 3 for the 0.55 Jy source J0924--2201 at 20 km s ^ 1 resolution. For J0913+5919 the column density limit per 40 km s ^ 1 channel is: N(HI) < 2.2 10^ 19 T_s cm ^ 2 within 2400 km s ^ 1 of the optical redshift.

The source SDSS J0913+5919 is a luminous quasar at z = 5.11 0.02 (Anderson et al. J0913+5919 was found to have a flux density of 30 3 mJy at 230MHz, while J0924--2201 had a flux density at of 0.55 0.05 Jy.

gmw_00001391.name: 7C 1753 + 6311
gmw_00001391.overview: 7C 1753 + 6311 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a spectroscopically confirmed redshift of z = 1.58 [arXiv:1511.05150]. The galaxy is characterized by very red colors [arXiv:astro-ph/0003290] and is embedded within a galaxy cluster overdensity [arXiv:1511.05150].

sources.summary: The paper revises 7C 1753+6311's near-infrared identification, finding a very red galaxy closer to the radio center while leaving its z<2 redshift estimate tentative.

quotes.quote: The identification of 7C 1753+6311 in Lacy et al. \ (1999b), which had a provisional redshift of 1.96, actually corresponds to a blue galaxy along the radio axis. Our Lick image showed that the true identification is a very red ( R-K^ ' > _ 5 ) galaxy 4.7 arcsec away which is significantly closer to the likely radio central component. Nevertheless, the original identification may well be associated with the radio galaxy, and the magnitude and colours are consistent with z < _ 2 , so we have not revised our redshift estimate. As we aligned the slit with the radio axis, though, we might have expected to see Ly in the spectrum if the redshift were indeed 1.96 . Thus we note that the redshift may well be lower.

sources.summary: The paper reports a z=1.58 mature cluster around the high-redshift radio galaxy 7C 1753 + 6311 and shows the radio galaxy lies in a significant galaxy overdensity.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of a z=1.58 mature cluster around the high-redshift radio galaxy 7C 1753 + 6311, first identified in the Clusters Around Radio-Loud AGN survey. Here we report the first robust spectroscopic redshift for C, confirming it to instead be at z=1.58 , and examine the surrounding cluster environment. Using the selection criteria defined in Section sec:method , the peak overdensity around C is an 8.9 significance of galaxies within a 30 arcsec aperture, centred 16 arcsec (136 kpc) from the radio galaxy.
We show this radio galaxy is located in an 8.9 galaxy overdensity, implying that it is embedded in a high redshift galaxy cluster.

gmw_00001392.name: NGC 1374
gmw_00001392.overview: NGC 1374 is a low-luminosity elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1906.08187, arXiv:astro-ph/0511770] that may alternatively be a face-on S0 galaxy [arXiv:2104.13496]. It features a low maximum stellar rotation [arXiv:1906.08187] and hosts a globular cluster system embedded within that of NGC 1399 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511770].

sources.summary: FCC 147 is described as a bright elliptical ancient infaller in the north-south clump, with small rotation, high central dispersion, and central line-strength gradients.

quotes.quote: FCC 147 (NGC 1374) -- Fig. fig:FCC147map FCC 147 is one of the brightest elliptical galaxies of the sample. It is located on the western side of the cluster at R_ proj =0.7^ from FCC 213 (NGC 1399). It is an ancient infaller belonging to the north-south galaxy clump. Its stellar kinematics is characterised by a small maximum rotation ( v_ max 50 km s ^ 1 ) and a high central velocity dispersion ( 200 km s ^ 1 ).
The gradient measured in line-strength maps of the Mg b and Fe5015 follows the gradient of velocity dispersion. The H index in the central parts is 1 \ smaller than in the surrounding regions.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC1374 as having several significant GC structures near D25, with elongated multi-peak morphologies and different detections in red and blue residual maps.

quotes.quote: The GC cluster system of NGC1374 has been investigated, using wide-field imaging data, by . They observed an evident color bimodality and reported that the radial profiles of blue and red subclasses at the largest probed galactocentric distances are not consistent with the background source density probably because of contributions from the GC systems of companion galaxies NGC1375 and NGC1373. The spatial distribution of all ACSFCS GCs in NGC1374 (upper panel in Figure fig:ngc1374 ) features H1, H2 and H4, three very significant structures located close to the D _ 25 elliptical isophotes of the host galaxy in the S, E and W sides of the observed field respectively. These structures have elongated shapes with multiple overdensity peaks suggestive of spatial substructures. H2 points in an almost radial direction reaching well beyond the D _ 25 ellipse, while H1 and H4 lie almost tangentially to the same isophote.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1374 as a low-luminosity Fornax elliptical whose globular cluster system was imaged across a wide field and remains embedded in NGC 1399's cluster system.

quotes.quote: Received ; in original form firstpage - lastpage 2005 We present a photometric study of the globular cluster systems of the Fornax cluster galaxies NGC 1374, NGC 1379, and NGC 1387. The data consists of images from the wide-field MOSAIC Imager of the CTIO 4-m telescope, obtained with Washington C and Kron--Cousins R filters. The images cover a field of 36 36 arcmin, corresponding to 200 200 kpc at the Fornax distance. Two of the galaxies, NGC 1374 and NGC 1379, are low-luminosity ellipticals while NGC 1387 is a low-luminosity lenticular. Their cluster systems are still embedded in the cluster system of NGC 1399.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1374 has regular but comparatively low rotation, the lowest sample velocity dispersion, and a central dispersion gradient among its modeled early-type galaxies.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies exhibit regular rotation: NGC 1374, NGC 4751, NGC 6861 and NGC 7619. In these galaxies, h_3 is anti-correlated with the rotational velocity, matching a widely observed trend (e.g. Bender-94,Krajnovic-08,Krajnovic-11,Fabricius-12 ). Of the four galaxies, NGC1374 has the lowest rotation; it also has the lowest velocity dispersion among the galaxies in the sample. The profile of NGC 1374 shows a rather strong and clear gradient, peaking at around 260 \ in the center.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1374 as an ambiguous core case, contrasts the Lauer core classification with Graham concerns, and excludes it from the core sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 1374 represents a case where the core detection is ambiguous, so it is discussed independently in a separate section.
Lauer-05 classify NGC 1374 as a core galaxy, based on a Nuker profile fit to their HST imaging data.

Graham et al. \ (2003) pointed out that fitting a Nuker model to a limited light profile, such as the case of NGC 1374 in Lauer-05 , could lead to a false conclusion that a core exists. Having no definite evidence of a depleted core in NGC 1374, we exclude it from the core sample.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1374 as one of three low-luminosity Fornax early-type galaxies, estimating its globular-cluster population, blue-cluster fraction, and low specific frequency.

quotes.quote: We present the combined results of two investigations: a large-scale study of the globular cluster system (GCS) around NGC 1399, the central galaxy of the Fornax cluster, and a study of the GCSs around NGC 1374, NGC 1379 and NGC 1387, three low-luminosity early-type galaxies located close to the centre of the same cluster. As there were several low-luminosity early-type galaxies with their own GCSs in the western field (NGC 1374, NGC 1379 and NGC 1387), we decided to keep this field to study them , and we used the eastern fields, where there were no conspicuous galaxies, to study the NGC 1399 GCS over a larger field . In fact, the fraction of blue clusters, with respect to the total GC population is low for the three systems, but even lower for NGC 1387 (43 , 45 , and 24 for NGC 1374, NGC 1379 and NGC 1387, respectively). By means of the luminosity functions we estimate the total GC populations in NGC 1374, NGC 1379 and NGC 1387 between 200 and 400 clusters, and obtain specific frequencies S_ N = 2.4, 1.4 and 1.8, respectively.

sources.summary: NGC 1374 has steep inner rotation, two rotation maxima, asymmetric velocity-dispersion behavior, and outer dispersion changes linked to the rotation-curve rise.

quotes.quote: NGC 1374 The inner gradient of the rotation velocity is very steep (250 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 ). After reaching a first maximum at 2 from the center, the RC remains approximately flat on both sides, until reaching a second maximum at 30--35 . The asymmetries of the RC correspond to the analogous behavior of the VDP which, after an initial strong gradient, and after reaching a plateau of 145 km s ^ 1 at 7--10 , shows different slopes on the two sides. The VDP is approximately flat, at 120 km s ^ 1 , on the E side in the range 15-35 before dropping to a minimum of 70 km s ^ 1 at the same position (35 ) of the maximum of the RC.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 1374's pCMD with NGC 1381, noting that its nominal elliptical appearance may be ambiguous and could indicate a face-on S0 morphology.

quotes.quote: It is worth noticing that the pCMDs of NGC 1374, classified as E0, and NGC 1381, classified as S0, are quite similar. For example, while NGC 1381 is a clear edge-on S0 galaxy, NGC 1374 looks like an elliptical galaxy but it could also be a face-on S0 (Figure Figure4 ). For example, from the particular cases of NGC 1374 and NGC 1381, we wonder if this kind of diagrams could become a powerful tool to clarify dubious morphological classifications, besides allowing the identification of internal substructures.

gmw_00001393.name: PUDG R15
gmw_00001393.overview: PUDG R15 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2111.06007, arXiv:2309.15148]. It is classified as globular cluster-poor [arXiv:2111.06007] and is situated in the early-infall region of the cluster's phase space [arXiv:2111.06007, arXiv:2309.15148].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PUDG R15 as a GC-poor Perseus UDG, obtains KCWI spectroscopy, and interprets it with R16 as likely earlier-infall cluster material.

quotes.quote: We therefore selected 2 GC-poor (PUDG R15 and PUDG R16) and 2 GC-rich UDGs (PUDG S74 and PUDG R84) from the initial GALFIT fitting of these catalogues with closely matched sizes, surface brightnesses and cluster--centric radii (see Table tab:imaging ). We have confirmation of our qualitative assessment of GC richness from Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging for three of these UDGs: PUDG R15, PUDG R16 and PUDG R84 Harris2020 . R15 and R84 are sufficiently small that we were able to target the galaxies with the medium slicer and include on-chip sky.

Observations of R15 were taken in split configurations, dithering between having sky observed on the CCD above and below the UDG. Our data suggest that the GC-poor UDGs (R15 and R16) are more likely to have fallen into the cluster at earlier times than the GC-rich UDGs (S74 and R84).

sources.summary: PUDG-R15 is a Perseus UDG with prior kinematic work, early-infall phase-space placement, and abundance-space agreement with globular clusters and dwarf ellipticals.

quotes.quote: Details about each observation can be found in the original papers presenting the dynamical and kinematic analysis of those UDGs: the Virgo cluster UDG VCC 1287 Gannon2020G , NGC 5846-UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group Forbes2021 , UDG1137+16 from the UGC 6594 group Gannon2021 , several UDGs in the Perseus cluster (i.e., PUDG-R15, PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84 and PUDG-R24; Gannon2022 ), and Yagi358 in the Coma cluster Gannon2022b . 9 UDGs (PUDG-R27, VCC 1287, Yagi358, Yagi093, Yagi098, Yagi276, Yagi392, Yagi418 and OGS1) are located in the `very early infall' region, with other 2 (DF07 and PUDG-R15) in the `early infall' region. Three UDGs (PUDG-R15, PUDG-R27 and DF07) match the GCs distribution, although in a region that also matches the dEs.

gmw_00001394.name: PUDG R16
gmw_00001394.overview: PUDG R16 is a globular cluster-poor ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2111.06007]. It is surrounded by a large amount of Galactic cirrus and likely fell into the cluster at an earlier epoch [arXiv:2111.06007].

sources.summary: The paper presents PUDG R16 as a GC-poor Perseus UDG, notes difficult cirrus contamination, and groups it with R15 as likely earlier-infall material.

quotes.quote: We therefore selected 2 GC-poor (PUDG R15 and PUDG R16) and 2 GC-rich UDGs (PUDG S74 and PUDG R84) from the initial GALFIT fitting of these catalogues with closely matched sizes, surface brightnesses and cluster--centric radii (see Table tab:imaging ). We have confirmation of our qualitative assessment of GC richness from Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging for three of these UDGs: PUDG R15, PUDG R16 and PUDG R84 Harris2020 . This was challenging for R16 as it is surrounded by a large amount of Galactic cirrus and it was not possible to fully mask some of the very low surface brightness cirrus.

R16, R24 and S74 are of sufficient size to fill the field of view of the medium slicer and so offset sky observations are used to perform sky subtraction using the technique described in Gannon2020 . Our data suggest that the GC-poor UDGs (R15 and R16) are more likely to have fallen into the cluster at earlier times than the GC-rich UDGs (S74 and R84).

gmw_00001395.name: PUDG R24
gmw_00001395.overview: PUDG R24 is a globular cluster-poor galaxy located in the outskirts of the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2111.06007, arXiv:2403.14873]. It exhibits a disturbed, blue morphology and a scaled-solar pattern, indicating a recent infall and late, slow quenching [arXiv:2309.15148, arXiv:2403.14873].

sources.summary: The paper describes PUDG R24 as a Perseus outskirts galaxy currently too bright for the UDG definition but likely fading into the UDG regime while quenching.

quotes.quote: One of our sample, PUDG R24, appears to be undergoing quenching and is expected to fade into the UDG regime within the next 0.5 Gyr. The sample of four Perseus cluster UDGs was then supplemented with a fifth UDG, PUDG R24 that is located in the cluster outskirts. We note R24 does not formally meet the UDG definition, having a _ e,g 0.25 mag too bright.

Combining this with the surface brightness measurements in Section sec:HSC_Data and Table tab:imaging we confirm the status of our sample as four `bona fide' Perseus UDGs and one galaxy, R24, which is likely soon to fade into the UDG regime. For R24 we assume 5 GCs to place it in a representative position within the GC-poor class.

sources.summary: PUDG-R24 is a Perseus UDG without line-index measurement here, showing a scaled-solar pattern, recent infall, low enhancement, and late/slow quenching behavior.

quotes.quote: Details about each observation can be found in the original papers presenting the dynamical and kinematic analysis of those UDGs: the Virgo cluster UDG VCC 1287 Gannon2020G , NGC 5846-UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group Forbes2021 , UDG1137+16 from the UGC 6594 group Gannon2021 , several UDGs in the Perseus cluster (i.e., PUDG-R15, PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84 and PUDG-R24; Gannon2022 ), and Yagi358 in the Coma cluster Gannon2022b . PUDG-R24 is the only UDG that shows a scaled-solar pattern. However, PUDG-R24 has been identified as a very recent infall into the Perseus cluster and UDG1137+16 presents tidal features, which can in both cases explain the young stellar ages.

From these late-and-slow UDGs, the two located in clusters are the ones with the lowest enhancements ( 0.2 dex; PUDG-R24 and Yagi275). 3 UDGs (PUDG-R24, Yagi275 and Yagi090) are located in this region, in agreement with their long quenching timescales and relatively young ages.

sources.summary: The paper treats PUDG R24 as a GC-poor Perseus UDG whose dynamical mass is simulation-consistent, with disturbed blue morphology suggesting recent infall and quenching.

quotes.quote: Most GC-rich UDGs in NGC 5846 UDG1, PUDG R84, PUDG S74, VCC 1287 Gannon_et_al_22 , as well as one GC-poor UDG (PUDG R24), have dynamical masses consistent with simulated galaxies with infall redshifts z 1 3 . However, Gannon_et_al_22 noted that PUDG R24 has a disturbed morphology and is significantly bluer than other Perseus UDGs, which may indicate recent infall and quenching in the cluster. The GC-rich UDGs with the highest masses (DFX1, DF44) have dynamical masses consistent with simulated galaxies over the full redshift range. In contrast, the inferred masses of observed GC-poor UDGs are generally inconsistent with those of the GC-poor simulated galaxies, being a factor of 5--10 lower than expected from the simulations. Of the GC-poor UDGs, only PUDG R24 is consistent with the lowest enclosed masses found for simulated galaxies.

gmw_00001396.name: PUDG R84
gmw_00001396.overview: PUDG R84 is a globular cluster-rich ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2111.06007, arXiv:2309.15148]. The galaxy is characterized by an early, fast formation history, a mixed-timescale quenching of six to eight gigayears, and a potentially large dark matter halo [arXiv:2309.15148].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PUDG R84 as a GC-rich Perseus UDG, obtains spectroscopy, and finds its dynamical mass compatible with cored halos over a broad halo-mass range.

quotes.quote: We therefore selected 2 GC-poor (PUDG R15 and PUDG R16) and 2 GC-rich UDGs (PUDG S74 and PUDG R84) from the initial GALFIT fitting of these catalogues with closely matched sizes, surface brightnesses and cluster--centric radii (see Table tab:imaging ). We have confirmation of our qualitative assessment of GC richness from Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging for three of these UDGs: PUDG R15, PUDG R16 and PUDG R84 Harris2020 . R15 and R84 are sufficiently small that we were able to target the galaxies with the medium slicer and include on-chip sky.

R84 is the only UDG where the additional regions and template fits did not report recessional velocities within 1 pixel ( 13 km \ s^ 1 ). For example, it is clear that our dynamical mass measurement for R84 agrees well with cored halos of both mass 10^ 10 \ M_ and 10^ 11 \ M_ and so cannot constrain the total halo mass of the UDG (Figure fig:mdyn_mhalo_compare , bottom panel ).

sources.summary: PUDG-R84 is a Perseus UDG observed in two configurations, with mixed-timescale quenching, early fast formation, GC richness, and a possible large dark halo.

quotes.quote: Details about each observation can be found in the original papers presenting the dynamical and kinematic analysis of those UDGs: the Virgo cluster UDG VCC 1287 Gannon2020G , NGC 5846-UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group Forbes2021 , UDG1137+16 from the UGC 6594 group Gannon2021 , several UDGs in the Perseus cluster (i.e., PUDG-R15, PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84 and PUDG-R24; Gannon2022 ), and Yagi358 in the Coma cluster Gannon2022b . PUDG-R84 was observed in both configurations as a consistency check. 4 UDGs are located in the `mixed times' regions (PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84, DFX1, DF44) all presenting quenching timescales of 6--8 Gyr.

These are Yagi358, PUDG-R84, DF44 and DGSAT I. From those, PUDG-R84 and Yagi358 (in high-density environments), and DF44 (as infall galaxy), present early and fast formation and are GC-rich, indicative of a large dark matter halo.

gmw_00001397.name: PUDG S74
gmw_00001397.overview: PUDG S74 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2111.06007, arXiv:2309.15148]. It is rich in globular clusters [arXiv:2111.06007] and exhibits a quenching timescale of approximately 6 to 8 Gyr [arXiv:2309.15148].

sources.summary: The paper treats PUDG S74 as a GC-rich Perseus UDG, discusses masking and sky subtraction issues, and reports consistent velocity and dispersion measurements.

quotes.quote: We therefore selected 2 GC-poor (PUDG R15 and PUDG R16) and 2 GC-rich UDGs (PUDG S74 and PUDG R84) from the initial GALFIT fitting of these catalogues with closely matched sizes, surface brightnesses and cluster--centric radii (see Table tab:imaging ). S74 is close to a saturated star and a bright background galaxy. For this UDG we slightly loosened the masking criteria to ensure the main stellar body of S74 was not masked.

An additional sky subtraction on S74 was performed using the on-chip `sky'. The final recessional velocity ( 6217 2 km \ s^ 1 ) and velocity dispersion ( 21 3 km \ s^ 1 ) agree well with the values reported for our on chip sky subtraction of S74. For S74 we assume 30 GCs to place it at a similar level of GC richness to R84.

sources.summary: PUDG-S74 is a Perseus UDG with prior kinematic work, no line-index measurement here, and mixed-timescale quenching behavior over roughly 6-8 Gyr.

quotes.quote: Details about each observation can be found in the original papers presenting the dynamical and kinematic analysis of those UDGs: the Virgo cluster UDG VCC 1287 Gannon2020G , NGC 5846-UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group Forbes2021 , UDG1137+16 from the UGC 6594 group Gannon2021 , several UDGs in the Perseus cluster (i.e., PUDG-R15, PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84 and PUDG-R24; Gannon2022 ), and Yagi358 in the Coma cluster Gannon2022b . While this does not have any impact on the full spectral fitting procedure detailed below, it means that we can not perform a line index measurement for VCC 1287, NGC 5846-UDG1, UDG1137+16, PUDG-R24 and PUDG-S74. 4 UDGs are located in the `mixed times' regions (PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84, DFX1, DF44) all presenting quenching timescales of 6--8 Gyr.

gmw_00001398.name: Arp 102B
gmw_00001398.overview: Arp 102B is an E0 LINER and Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1308.1891, arXiv:1907.08632] that serves as the prototype for active galactic nuclei with double-peaked broad emission lines [arXiv:1308.2407, arXiv:0708.3221]. It features a nuclear mini-spiral structure [arXiv:1907.08632], a bent radio jet [arXiv:1308.1891], and gas likely captured during a past interaction with Arp 102A [arXiv:1308.1891].

sources.summary: Arp 102B is monitored as a broad-line radio/LINER AGN with double-peaked Balmer lines. The study measures continuum and line variability, disk-like line structure, and central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Here we present results of the long-term (1987-2010) optical spectral monitoring of the broad line radio galaxy Arp 102B, a prototype of active galactic nuclei with the double-peaked broad emission lines, usually assumed to be emitted from an accretion disk. To explore the structure of the broad line region (BLR), we analyze the light curves of the broad H and H lines and the continuum flux. We aim to estimate the dimensions of the broad-line emitting regions and the mass of the central black hole.

During the monitoring period, the broad H and H lines of Arp 102B show double-peaked profiles. The blue peak is located around -5,000 km s ^ 1 and red around 6,000 km s ^ 1 from the line center. A big distance between the peaks ( 11,000 km s ^ 1 ) indicates a fast rotating disk, that is probably close to the black hole. As it can be seen from Fig. mp (mean profiles and rms), the changes in the line profile have also double-peaked rms, that indicates that changes in the blue wing is significantly (some) bigger, than in the red wing. In the rms there is one central peak, that may be caused by a central component in the BLR pop04,bo06,bo09 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Arp 102B as a double-peaked type 1 AGN, applying ZDCF and SPEAR reverberation methods and noting larger H than H lags plus possible spurious H lag peaks.

quotes.quote: We used the Z-transformed Discrete Correlation Function (ZDCF) and the Stochastic Process Estimation for AGN Reverberation (SPEAR) methods for the time series analysis of the continuum and the H and H line fluxes of a sample of well known type 1 active galactic nuclei (AGNs): Arp 102B, 3C 390.3, NGC 5548, and NGC 4051, where the first two objects are showing double-peaked emission line profiles. The aim of this work is to compare the time lag measurements from these two methods, and check if there is a connection with other emission line properties. We found that the obtained time lags from H are larger than those derived from the H analysis for Arp 102B, 3C 390.3 and NGC 5548. This may indicate that the H line originates at larger radii in these objects.

Since the diversity in the shape and width of the line profiles implies the diversity in the shape of transfer function and thus significant variations in the strength of the line response to continuum variations see R95 , we selected our sample to have two types of objects: AGNs with double-peaked broad line profiles (Arp 102B and 3C 390.3) and typical type 1 AGNs with single-peaked profiles (NGC 5548 and NGC 4051). The double-peaked broad lines of Arp 102B and 3C390.3 were successfully modeled with the circular relativistic Keplerian accretion disk around a SMBH C89,CH89,Halp90,E97,E09 . However, their broad emission line profiles vary in a complex way, thus more complex BLR models are needed Gez07, Pop11 . The other two objects, NGC 5548 and NGC 4051 are typical broad single-peaked line AGNs. Over the 6-year monitoring campaign since 1996, NGC 5548 sometimes crosses the boundary between a Sy1 type and a Sy1.8 type AGN Sh04 , and NGC 4051 is relatively nearby object that was part of the extensive reverberation mapping campaign see P04,D06 .

In order to test the statistical relationship between the ZDCF and SPEAR results, we calculated the Pearson correlation coefficient and the p-value between different sets of time lags, which confirmed that results obtained from H of 3C 390.3 are an outlier. Such behavour of H line of this object could have roots in bad median and average sampling period and very broad the ACF of the continuum and the line itself. The secondary (spurious) time lags are obtained for H lines of Arp 102B and 3C 390.3. These spurious lags could arise due to the fact that the H line and continuum of both objects have large sampling period. In the case of 3C 390.3 average and median sampling periods are larger than 100 days and in the case of Arp 102B average sampling period is 100 days.

sources.summary: The paper monitors Arp 102B as a radio/active galaxy with double-peaked broad lines, finding stable broad-line profile shapes but variability not fully consistent with a simple emission-disc model.

quotes.quote: We investigate a long-term (26 years, from 1987 to 2013) variability in the broad spectral line properties of the radio galaxy Arp 102B, an active galaxy with broad double-peaked emission lines. We use observations presented in Paper I (Shapovalova et al. 2013) in the period from 1987 to 2011, and a new set of observations performed in 2012--2013. To explore the BLR geometry, and clarify some contradictions about the nature of the BLR in Arp 102B we explore variations in the H and H line parameters during the monitored period.

We find that the broad line profiles are double-peaked and have not been changed significantly in shapes, beside an additional small peak that, from time to time can be seen in the blue part of the H line. The positions of the blue and red peaks have not changed significantly during the monitored period . The H line is broader than H line in the monitored period. The disc model is able to reproduce the H and H broad line profiles, however, observed variability in the line parameters are not in a good agreement with the emission disc hypothesis. It seems that the BLR of Arp 102B has a disc-like geometry, but the role of an outflow can also play an important role in observed variation of the broad line properties.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Arp 102B line-profile changes as apparent hot spots with problematic orbital interpretations, and notes polarization inconsistent with a simple symmetrical disk model.

quotes.quote: 7 of sergeev+02 ) newman+07 and sergeev+00 interpreted the changing excesses of emission in the Balmer-line profiles of Arp 102B as ``hot spots'' orbiting with a period of a little over 2 years. There are several serious problems with this. The first is that a second ``hot spot'' appearing in Arp 102B from 2000--2005 gave a mass that differed by 30 times the measuring error gezari+07 .

Spectropolarimetric observations of Arp 102B antonucci+96 did indeed show polarization of the line but this was inconsistent with the predictions of chen+halpern90 . The line polarization was twice as great as predicted and, contrary to the model predictions, the polarized flux was higher in part of the blue peak than in the red peak, and it also dropped too low in the far blue wing. The polarization of the blue side changed dramatically with wavelength, contrary to the prediction. The polarization angle changed relative to that of the continuum by 30^ compared with the continuum rather than the predicted 90^ .

sources.summary: The paper maps Arp 102B with IFU spectroscopy, finding nuclear spiral arms, LINER-like gas, jet-driven outflow signatures, and gas likely captured during interaction with Arp 102A.

quotes.quote: Received ; in original form firstpage - lastpage We present a two-dimensional analysis of the gaseous excitation and kinematics of the inner 2.5 1.7 kpc ^2 of the LINER/Seyfert 1 galaxy Arp 102B, from optical spectra obtained with the GMOS integral field spectrograph on the Gemini North telescope at a spatial resolution of 250 pc. Emission-line flux maps show the same two-armed nuclear spiral we have discovered in previous observations with the HST-ACS camera. One arm reaches 1 kpc to the east and the other 500 pc to the west, with a 8.4 GHz VLA bent radio jet correlating with the former. Gas excitation along the arms is low, with line ratios typical of LINERs, and which rule out gas ionization by stars.

We propose a scenario in which gas has been recently captured by Arp 102B in an interaction with Arp 102A, settling in a disk rotating around the nucleus of Arp 102B and triggering its nuclear activity. A nuclear jet is pushing the circumnuclear gas, giving origin to the nuclear arms. A blueshifted emitting gas knot is observed at 300 pc south-east from the nucleus and can be interpreted as another (more compact) outflow, with a possible counterpart to the north-west.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 102B as a double-peaked type 1 AGN with unusually broad, weakly variable Balmer profiles, weak line-continuum correlation, and possible non-photoionization contributions in its BLR.

quotes.quote: The objects have been selected so that representatives of type 1 AGN with different BEL profiles are in the sample: Arp 102B and 3C 390.3 have BELs with double-peaked line profiles, Ark 564 is a Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLSy1), and NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 are well-studied Seyfert 1.5 with strongly variable BEL profiles, with bumps and asymmetries present in the line profiles (see Figure 1).

In order to show how the line profiles vary in two DPL objects, we plot the mean and rms profile for the H line of 3C 390.3 (Figure 5, upper panel) and Arp 102B (Figure 5, bottom panel). From the shape and intensity of the rms profiles, it is obvious that in case of 3C 390.3 the line profiles vary significantly, while in Arp 102B the line profiles remain almost unchanged.

It is also interesting that in case of Arp 102B, that is the other extreme object with the largest line widths (FWHM 15000 km/s), the variability is also low ( 20 ). The lack of variability in both of these cases is supported by the weak correlation between the continuum and line fluxes (Figure 4, Table 2), e.g. for H of Arp 102B it is =0.37 ( P_ null =0.5E-04), and even worse for H of the same object ( =0.25 , P_ null =0.22). The lack of variability can be due to poor sampling of the data, e.g. in case of Arp 102B the mean sampling is 40 days Kov14 . However, the lack of correlation between the line and continuum flux also points to additional sources of ionization in the BLR apart from the central AGN continuum source.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 102B as a double-peaked broad-line AGN, finding intrinsic Baldwin-effect behavior and describing its broad Balmer-line morphology alongside 3C 390.3.

quotes.quote: We investigate the intrinsic Baldwin effect (Beff) of the broad H and H emission lines for six Type 1 active galactic nuclei (AGNs) with different broad line characteristics: two Seyfert 1 ( NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 ), two AGNs with double-peaked broad line profiles ( 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B ), one narrow line Seyfert 1 ( Ark 564 ), and one high-luminosity quasar with highly red asymmetric broad line profiles ( E1821+643 ). We find that a significant intrinsic Beff was present in all Type 1 AGNs in our sample. Moreover, we do not see strong differences in intrinsic Beff slopes in different types of AGNs, which probably have different physical properties such as inclination, broad line region (BLR) geometry, or accretion rate.

Double-peaked broad line AGNs 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B , whose broad Balmer lines show two distinguished peaks with very large overall width (FWHM 10000 \ kms^ 1 ). This type of objects are called double-peaked line (DPL) AGNs. These lines are supposed to be emitted from an accretion disk ch89 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 102B as an E0 LINER/Seyfert with compact non-thermal radio emission, an inner mini-spiral from possible gas accretion, and jet interaction with circumnuclear gas.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3998 and Arp 102B, we measured the brightness temperature ( T_ b ) of the radio emission to investigate the power source. In general, T_ b of non-thermal emission is known to be higher ( T_ b > 10 ^ 7 K) than thermal emission ( T_ b 10 ^ 4 10 ^ 5 K) e.g., Middelberg2004, Bontempi2012 . Using the extent of the nuclear radio component and its flux density, the brightness temperature can be estimated by the following equation: T_ b (K) = 1.222 10 ^ 3 I ^ 2 _ maj _ min where I is the flux density in mJy, is the observing frequency in GHz, and is the size of the radio component in arcsec. For the unresolved compact sources, we measured the lower limit of brightness temperature using the synthesized beam size, yielding 10 ^ 8.4 K and 10 ^ 8.6 K for NGC 3998 and Arp 102B, respectively, i.e. sufficiently high to expect a non-thermal origin. Therefore we conclude that the radio emission detected in our VLBI image for these two cases also originates from non-thermal synchrotron radiation.

Arp 102B is an E0 which is classified as LINER/Seyfert 1. A two-fold mini spiral structure is found in H in the inner 1 kpc, which could have been formed by a recent gas accretion event Fathi2011 . This also might have triggered some nuclear activity and formed a jet which seems to be pushing the circumnuclear H gas Couto2013 .

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 102B as a LINER and archetypal double-peaked disc emitter whose light curves lack periodic variability, likely from weak oscillator coupling.

quotes.quote: The characteristics of these objects encompasses the entire range of type 1 AGN activity (see Table  tab:data ): from Seyfert 1 objects NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 as prototypes of spectral type variation (from Seyfert 1.0 to 1.8), a very broad line and radio loud object 3C 390.3, then Arp 102B that is considered as a LINER and archetypical disc accretion emitter due to extremely broad, double peaked Balmer lines, and finally radio quiet but optically powerful near-by quasar E1821+643.

Arp 102B. While every period is incidentally correlated with itself (correlation coefficient equals 1 on the diagonal), any transient oscillations having physical origin which are hidden within the light curves should be reveled as clusters close to the diagonal. The main common feature in all correlation maps is the absence of any periodic variability (i. e correlation clusters), even in autocorrelation maps. This is well illustrated in Fig.  fig:perarp .
A specific topology of Arp 102B correlation maps indicates either that periodicities are absent from light curves or that coupling between oscillatory processes is weak.

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 102B as the prototype double-peaked AGN, comparing its emission-line profile, broad components, and Eddington ratio with NGC 2110.

quotes.quote: Several properties of NGC 2110 suggest that it is an obscured twin of Arp 102B, the prototypical double-peaked emission-line active galactic nucleus (AGN).

Such a kinematic component is also observed in the H and Ly profiles of Arp 102B (it is in fact the dominant component of the Ly line; Halpern et al. \ 1996). Based on a four-component Gaussian fit to the H + [ N 2 ] lines in the residuals spectrum, we estimate the width of the bell-shaped broad component to be 3100 km s ^ 1 (FWHM), similar to the widths of the Ly and H lines of Arp 102B (Halpern et al. \ 1996).

Despite the rough numbers used here, this is in close agreement with the value of L_ bol /L_ Edd = (1-2) 10^ 3 obtained by Lewis Eracleous (2006) for Arp 102B, which further strengthens the case that NGC 2110 is an obscured twin of Arp 102B.

sources.summary: The paper extends Arp 102B monitoring and argues binary broad-line fits require implausible masses, far above direct estimates from reverberation mapping.

quotes.quote: Those three objects, Arp 102B, 3C 390.3, and 3C 332, are included in our present collection but their time series are now 2, 1.5, and 1.3 times longer, respectively.

These measurements extend the previously published spectroscopic time series for Arp 102B, 3C 390.3, 3C 227, and Mrk 668 by factors of 1.7, 1.25, 2.7, and 1,6 respectively, which substantially influences the binary orbital parameters we derive below.

Lacking clear spectroscopic evidence for orbital motion in the velocity curves, the three objects chosen for study by , 3C 390.3, Arp 102B, and 3C 332, were found to require masses >10^ 10 M_ under the binary BL region hypothesis, and our extension of that monitoring has only increased those limits. Such masses are far in excess of those measured using direct methods such as reverberation mapping, from which estimated Arp 102B to have M=1.1 10^8 , and estimated 3C 390.3 to have M=2.0 10^9 , both much less than 10^ 12 and 10^ 14 , respectively, obtained under the binary BL region assumption (see Tables P_fitting_chi and P_fitting_lsq ).

sources.summary: The Swift-BAT radio survey identifies Arp 102B as near the radio-loudness threshold, with compact 22 GHz morphology but known low-frequency radio jets and small elongated structures.

quotes.quote: Four objects in our sample are near or above this threshold: Arp 102B ( R_O=44 ), NGC 5506 ( R_O=13 ), Mrk 477 ( R_O=10 ), and NGC 1052 ( R_O=9 ). Two of these, Arp 102B and NGC 1052, have small radio jets at low frequencies Helmboldt2007,Wrobel1984 .

Concerning the objects with compact 22 GHz morphologies but low-frequency jets: two of these sources were observed at higher resolutions than our survey, and the elongated structures are small enough to lie within our 1 beam: MCG-01-24-012 Schmitt2001 and Arp 102B Helmboldt2007 .
Two of these are radio-loud objects Arp 102B and NGC 1052.

sources.summary: The review uses Arp 102B as a representative double-peaked Population B source, noting its brightness and disk-profile interpretation with moderate viewing angles.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:inclination illustrates the appearance of line profiles for two objects of Pop. B with widely different line widths, Arp 102B and the blazar PKS 0438-436. Both objects are fairly well representative of their classes: Arp 102B for double-peaked sources, and PKS 0438-436 for blazars.

The prototypical source Arp 102B is relatively bright and luminous ( M_ R 25.7 ) but nowadays very similar profiles are being discovered in lower luminosity AGN bianchietal19 , thanks to the careful subtraction of the host galaxy continuum made possible by the use of integral field spectroscopy. Models of accretion disks are consistent with viewing angle around 30 — 50 degrees, and show the red wing with redshift amplitude z consistent with the effect of gravitational and transverse redshift zhengsulentic90,corbin95,popovicetal95,bonetal15 , i.e. z 3 2 r_ g _ BLR where r_ g \ is the gravitational radius, and _ BLR \ corresponds to innermost radii of the BLR, if the z \ measurement is obtained from the line centroid close to the line base (for example, 0.1 or 0.25 of maximum intensity).

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 102B as a radio-quiet double-peaked-line galaxy with moderate variability and contrasts its BLR geometry with 3C 390.3.

quotes.quote: Here we present some interesting findings of the long-term (more than a decade) optical monitoring campaign of a sample of seven type 1 AGN of different spectral types: Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 5548, NGC 4151, NGC 7469), Narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy - NLSy 1 (Ark 564), double-peaked line radio loud (3C 390.3) and radio quiet (Arp 102B) galaxy, and a luminous quasar (E1821+643), a supermassive binary black hole candidate.

If we compare the normalized light-curves shown in Figure fig:1 , the strongest variable objects are NGC 5548, NGC 4151, and 3C 390.3 (see also in Table 1 the variability parameter F _ var which is 40 ), followed by Arp 102B and NGC 7469 (F _ var 20 ).

For example, in case of 3C 390.3 it is clear that the BLR is following the accretion disk geometry Po11 , whereas in case of the other double-peak line emitter Arp 102B this is maybe not the case Po14 .

gmw_00001399.name: E1821+643
gmw_00001399.overview: E1821+643 is a highly luminous, radio-quiet quasar hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy at the center of a rich cool-core cluster [arXiv:1105.4912, arXiv:1901.02267]. The system is a post-merger candidate featuring a core-dominated jet [arXiv:1002.4681, arXiv:2208.03418] and a supermassive black hole that may be undergoing gravitational recoil [arXiv:2208.03418, arXiv:2210.03267].

sources.summary: The paper describes E1821+643 as a radio-quiet quasar outlier with a powerful, core-dominated jet, high UV luminosity, and redward CIV asymmetry despite its formal radio-quiet classification.

quotes.quote: The case for E1821+643 is not as strong, but still highly compelling. This quasar is a powerful radio source with a 151 MHz luminosity intermediate between that of a very strong FR I and an FR II radio source blu01 . Physically, the 151 MHz luminosity is believed to be a measure of the long term time averaged kinetic power delivered by the jet to the distant radio lobes wil99 . The methods of wil99 indicate a jet kinetic luminosity of 2 10^ 44 ergs/s. VLA observations indicate that the source is core dominated and flat spectrum blu96 .

The flat spectrum nature of the jet and its large high frequency luminosity are consistent with a powerful jet viewed close to the line of sight. The very large high frequency flux of the core might be indicative of a current episode of increased jet power. Within the two component model described above, The dominant flat spectrum core is the manifestation of a powerful FR II jet viewed near the line of sight. This dynamics and geometry of the current episode of jet ejection are inducing a redward asymmetry that swamps the blueward asymmetry produced by the powerful accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper reports long-term optical monitoring of QSO E1821+643, finding continuum and line variability, broad-line red asymmetry, a massive black hole, and possible binary or recoiling black-hole behavior.
