quotes.quote: We report the results of the first long-term (1990--2014) optical spectro-photometric monitoring of a binary black hole candidate QSO E1821+643, a low-redshift high-luminosity radio-quiet quasar. In the monitored period the continua and H fluxes changed for around two times, while the H flux changed around 1.4 times. We found the periodical variations in the photometric flux with the periods of 1200, 1850 and 4000 days, and 4500 days periodicity in the spectroscopic variations.

The line profiles have not significantly changed, showing an important red asymmetry and broad line peak redshifted around +1000 km s ^ 1 . However, H shows broader mean profile and has a larger time-lag ( 120 days) than H ( 60 days). We estimate that the mass of the black hole is 2.6 10^9 M_ . The obtained results are discussed in the frame of the binary black hole hypothesis. To explain the periodicity in the flux variability and high redshift of broad lines we discuss a scenario where dense gas-rich cloudy-like structures are orbiting around a recoiling black hole.

sources.summary: The paper presents E1821+643 as a luminous post-merger quasar in a cool-core cluster, with extended ionized gas, possible recoil SMBH, and kinematic features indicating merger or wind-driven structure.

quotes.quote: The most luminous quasars are created by major, gas-rich mergers and E1821+643, an optically luminous quasar situated at the center of a cool-core cluster, appears to be in the late stages of the post-merger blowout phase. This quasar is also identified as a gravitational recoil candidate, in which the supermassive black hole (SMBH) has received a recoil kick due to anisotropic emission of gravitational waves during the coalescence of a progenitor SMBH binary. We analyze long-slit spectra of the extended, ionized gas surrounding E1821+643 to study its kinematics and ionization.

The luminous quasar, E1821+643 (henceforth, E1821), has a number of highly unusual characteristics that make it an object of great interest. It appears to be a post-merger system, which is transitioning from an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy to an optically luminous quasar Aravena2011 and its host galaxy resides at the center of a rich cool-core cluster (CCC) Russell2010 . E1821 has also been identified as a potential supermassive black hole (SMBH) binary Blundell2001TheStructure , which may have already merged, imparting a gravitational recoil kick to the product SMBH Robinson2010 .

The E1821+643 system is a highly unusual one, which may provide important insights into the growth and evolution of massive galaxies and their SMBH in cluster environments. Our long-slit spectra reveal that ionized gas, predominantly photoionized by the AGN, is widespread around the nucleus, and has kinematical and morphological features that point to a recent or ongoing merger.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes H1821+643 as a luminous radio-quiet quasar in a cool-core cluster, estimating high accretion, weak mechanical feedback, and limited impact on large-scale cluster gas.

quotes.quote: We present a Chandra observation of the only low redshift, z=0.299 , galaxy cluster to contain a highly luminous radio-quiet quasar, H1821+643. By simulating the quasar PSF, we subtract the quasar contribution from the cluster core and determine the physical properties of the cluster gas down to 3 ( 15 ) from the point source. The temperature of the cluster gas decreases from 9.0 0.5 down to 1.3 0.2 in the centre, with a short central radiative cooling time of 1.0 0.1 , typical of a strong cool-core cluster.

The quasar bolometric luminosity was estimated to be 2 10^ 47 , requiring a mass accretion rate of 40 , which corresponds to half the Eddington accretion rate. We explore possible accretion mechanisms for this object and determine that Bondi accretion, when boosted by Compton cooling of the accretion material, could provide a significant source of the fuel for this outburst. We consider H1821+643 in the context of a unified AGN accretion model and, by comparing H1821+643 with a sample of galaxy clusters, we show that the quasar has not significantly affected the large-scale cluster gas properties.

H1821+643 has a gas cooling time profile typical of a strong cool-core cluster with a passive central engine indicating that the quasar has not strongly affected the large-scale cluster gas properties.

sources.summary: The paper includes H1821+643 as a quasar in a luminous cool-core cluster with X-ray cavities, pile-up affected observations, and literature-derived nuclear and cluster luminosities.

quotes.quote: Finally, we include H1821+643 ( z=0.299 ), which is a quasar embedded in a luminous cool-core cluster Rus2010402 . Since H1821+643 shows evidence for X-ray cavities that coincide with radio lobes, we included this object in the initial sample of 40 clusters with known X-ray cavities. H1821+643 is however not part of any of the catalogues, and this quasar is heavily affected by pile-up. However, Rus2010402 have accounted for the pile-up by simulating the quasar PSF to disentangle the quasar emission from the intracluster medium. We therefore use the quantities quoted in Rus2010402 throughout this paper instead of reanalyzing the source.

Rus2010402 furthermore estimate that the cluster luminosity in the 0.5-7 range is 10^ 45 , of which most originates from the 0.1-2.4 energy band. It is therefore reasonable to assume that this cluster is luminous enough to be included in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper includes E1821+643 as a low-redshift radio-quiet quasar with red asymmetric broad lines, weak variability, flare-like outbursts, and intrinsic Baldwin-effect behavior.

quotes.quote: We investigate the intrinsic Baldwin effect (Beff) of the broad H and H emission lines for six Type 1 active galactic nuclei (AGNs) with different broad line characteristics: two Seyfert 1 ( NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 ), two AGNs with double-peaked broad line profiles ( 3C 390.3 and Arp 102B ), one narrow line Seyfert 1 ( Ark 564 ), and one high-luminosity quasar with highly red asymmetric broad line profiles ( E1821+643 ). We find that a significant intrinsic Beff was present in all Type 1 AGNs in our sample. Moreover, we do not see strong differences in intrinsic Beff slopes in different types of AGNs, which probably have different physical properties such as inclination, broad line region (BLR) geometry, or accretion rate.

High-luminosity quasar E1821+643 , a low-redshift ( z =0.297) radio-quiet quasar. Its broad emission lines (FWHM > 5000 \ kms^ 1 ) are redshifted by 1000 km s ^ 1 and have strongly asymmetric profiles in the red part. These effects make this object a candidate for either a supermassive binary black hole (SMBBH) or a recoiling black hole after SMBH collision ro10, sh16 . It is a weakly variable quasar, but some flare-like outbursts have been detected see sh16 .

sources.summary: Chandra observations show H1821+643 is a cluster-hosted quasar whose hot atmosphere brightens, cools, compresses, and forms a cooling flow with mass deposition up to 3000.

quotes.quote: H1821+643 is the nearest quasar hosted by a galaxy cluster. The energy output by the quasar, in the form of intense radiation and radio jets, is captured by the surrounding hot atmosphere. Here we present a new deep Chandra observation of H1821+643 and extract the hot gas properties into the region where Compton cooling by the quasar radiation is expected to dominate.

Using detailed simulations to subtract the quasar light, we show that the soft-band surface brightness of the hot atmosphere increases rapidly by a factor of 30 within the central 10 . The gas temperature drops precipitously to <0.4 and the density increases by over an order of magnitude. The remarkably low metallicity here is likely due to photo-ionization by the quasar emission.
H1821+643 is therefore embedded in a cooling flow with a mass deposition rate of up to 3000 .

sources.summary: The paper presents H1821+643 as a luminous radio-quiet QSO in a giant elliptical host and a local ULIRG-to-QSO transition system.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: _ J2000 =18^h21^m57.21^s , _ J2000 =64^ 20'36.23'' (6 tracks; centered at the position of H1821+643)

quotes.quote: One of the most prominent local templates of candidate ULIRG-to-QSO transition objects is IR/optically luminous QSO [ HB89 ] 1821+643 system (hereafter H1821+643) at z=0.297 .

H1821+643 With an optical absolute magnitude M_ V =-27.1 at a redshift of 0.297, H1821+643 is one of the most luminous QSOs in the local universe Hutchings1991, Schneider1992 . H1821+643 has been subject to a number of studies aimed at determining the morphology of its host galaxy. This bright QSO is hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy, with a half-light radius of 14 kpc in extent, as revealed by modeling of the point-spread function (PSF) subtracted HST images McLeod2001, Floyd2004 . The luminous nuclear component outshines the underlying host with a luminosity ratio of 11 in the HST I-band Floyd2004 .

Radio continuum imaging of this H1821+643 indicates this is a radio-quiet QSO, with a radio-lobe extending out to 250 kpc from the bright core in south-west direction Papadopoulos1995, Blundell1995, Blundell1996, Blundell2001 .

sources.summary: The paper treats H1821+643 as the luminous central broad-line quasar in CL1821+643, associated with an FR I radio source and affecting X-ray morphological measurements of the cluster core.

quotes.quote: This cluster was discovered surrounding a highly luminous broad-line quasar, namely H1821+643 sch92 . An FRI type radio source of extent 200 kpc has been found to be associated with the central quasar with possibly precessing jets blu01 . rus10 performed detailed modeling of this quasar using Chandra data and were able to subtract the quasar to estimate the internal properties of the cluster itself.
Source B is an FR I radio source associated with the central quasar H1821+643 blu01 .

The parameters for CL1821+643 were calculated with and without the central quasar in order to understand the effect of the quasar on the morphology. For the subtraction of the quasar, we excluded 25 kpc central region around RA 18h21m57.239s DEC +64d20m36.22s similar to that in B14.

sources.summary: The paper presents E1821+643 as a powerful nearby quasar and binary-black-hole candidate with 12-, 6-, and 5-year light-curve periods and stable, sinusoid-like phase dynamics.

quotes.quote: The characteristics of these objects encompasses the entire range of type 1 AGN activity (see Table  tab:data ): from Seyfert 1 objects NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 as prototypes of spectral type variation (from Seyfert 1.0 to 1.8), a very broad line and radio loud object 3C 390.3, then Arp 102B that is considered as a LINER and archetypical disc accretion emitter due to extremely broad, double peaked Balmer lines, and finally radio quiet but optically powerful near-by quasar E1821+643.

E1821+643. Using the data for all light curves, our method yielded periods at 12 yr, 6 yr and 5 yr (see Fig.   fig:pere ). It is interesting that periodicities have been revealed in the case of H emission line. One can note that the period of 12 yr is about twice of 6 yr period.

Particularly, if we look at phase portraits of the light curves (Fig.   fig:phe ) normal limit cycles are observed in the dynamics of E1821+643. They are quite similar to phase portrait of regular sinusoids.

sources.summary: The paper treats H1821+643 as an AGN needing decomposed star-formation estimates and as a radiation-dominated AGN whose radiation may cool the hot intracluster medium.

quotes.quote: Based on the mid-IR color criteria of , we find evidence for an AGN which may contaminate the estimate of SFR in the following systems: H1821+643, IRAS 09104+4109, Cygnus A, RBS 797, 3C295, 3C388, Zw2089, Abell 1068, and Abell 2667. For each of these systems, we require an estimate of the SFR that either spatially or spectrally separates the AGN and starburst component. We were unable to find such well-measured SFRs for 3C295 or 3C388, so we will refer to SFRs for these systems as upper limits. For the remaining 7 systems, along with Phoenix and Perseus, we obtained either spatially decomposed Abell 611, RBS797; cavagnolo11,donahue15 or spectrally decomposed H1821+643, 3C186, IRAS 09104+4109, Abell 2667, Cygnus A, Zw2089, Phoenix, Perseus; privon12,rawle12,ruiz13,mittal15,podigachoski15,mittal17 SFR estimates.

showed that H1821+643, which is a radiation-dominated AGN at the center of a massive galaxy cluster, appears to be cooling the surrounding hot ICM. The fact that the bulk of the accretion disk is cooler than the > 10 ^7 K ICM means that the radiation from the AGN will lead to Compton cooling of the hot ICM. This cooling is observed as a rapid decrease in the entropy of the ICM in the inner 10 kpc of the cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes E1821+643 as a luminous quasar whose supermassive black hole is likely recoiling from its host galaxy after a merger that also produced a radio-jet kink.

quotes.quote: E1821+643 is an extremely luminous quasar at redshift z=0.297 . The central supermassive black hole candidate is estimated to have a mass of 2.6 10^9 M_ in a host galaxy with M_ gal 2 10^ 12 M_ Floyd2004MNRAS,Shapovalova2016ApJS . There is a 90 kink in the south-west radio jet Blundell1996ApJ , likely due to a spin-flip of the central SMBH engine during a recent merger MerrittEkers2002Sci . Spectropolarimetric analysis has found the broad-line region is moving away from the observer relative to the host galaxy Robinson2010ApJE1821,Jadhav2021MNRAS . Thus, E1821+643 is interpreted to be exiting the host galaxy at a speed of 2070 50 km s^ 1 along the line-of-sight Robinson2010ApJE1821 .

While E1821+643 is not universally accepted as a recoiling supermassive black hole, there are three features of this object, which lead us to regard the recoiling black hole hypothesis as probable: the spatial offset of E1821+643 from the center of the host galaxy photocenter, the large spectral offset from the host galaxy, and a 90^ kink in the associated radio jet consistent with a spin flip of the central engine MerrittEkers2002Sci .

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS18216+6418 to demonstrate BayeSED posterior inference for a starburst-plus-AGN model, finding several starburst, AGN, and infrared-luminosity parameters well constrained.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:iras18216SBclumpy , we show the posterior PDFs of all parameters of the `SB+AGN' model for IRAS18216+6418. Given the very limited observations and the large number of parameters, it is clear that not all of the parameters can be well constrained. From the detailed posterior PDFs of parameters, it is much easy to find out if a parameter is well constrained or not. For example, the basic parameters R , L_ tot of starburst component and , q of AGN component are well constrained. The derived parameters log(L_ SB ^ IR ) , log(L_ AGN ^ IR ) and log(L_ TOT ^ IR ) are nicely constrained.

sources.summary: The paper describes E1821+643 as a luminous quasar with little variability, red-asymmetric broad lines, possible recoiling-SMBH interpretation, and uncertain radius-luminosity placement.

quotes.quote: Here we present some interesting findings of the long-term (more than a decade) optical monitoring campaign of a sample of seven type 1 AGN of different spectral types: Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 5548, NGC 4151, NGC 7469), Narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy - NLSy 1 (Ark 564), double-peaked line radio loud (3C 390.3) and radio quiet (Arp 102B) galaxy, and a luminous quasar (E1821+643), a supermassive binary black hole candidate.

The least variable objects are the NLSy1 Ark 564 and the highly luminous quasar E1821+643, for which the line and continuum flux are almost constant for decades (for the H line, F _ var 7 ). The case of the quasar E1821+643 is specially puzzling since its broad emission lines show a peak shift (of the order 1000 km/s) and a strong asymmetry in the red wing, extended up to 15,000 km/s see and their Figure 15 Sh16 . This object was suggested to be a candidate for a binary SMBH or a recoiling black hole after the collision of two SMBH Ro10 , however found it difficult to explain the unchanging line-profiles on such a long time-scales with the binary SMBH hypothesis, suggesting that the broad-line emitting region may be connected with some sort of gas-rich clouds that are orbiting around the recoiling SMBH.

Finally, the quasar E1821+643 is much below the theoretical radius-luminosity relation predicted by the photoionization theory ( R_ BLR L^ 0.5 ), and very low-variable AGN in the optical lines and continuum, therefore this SMBH mass estimate is also more uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper cites H1821+643 as a luminous radio-quiet quasar hosted by a central massive galaxy in a rich cool-core cluster, comparable to massive quasars in cluster environments.

quotes.quote: The central galaxy in the cluster SPT-CL J2344-4343 McDonald2012,McDonald2013 and H1821+643 Russell2010,Reynolds2014,Walker2014 are two examples. SPT-CL J2344-4343 is a massive cool-core cluster at z=0.596 with M_ halo 10^ 15 M_ . The central galaxy of the cluster has a powerful AGN with a bolometric luminosity of 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 and an EMBH of 10^ 10 M_ . Similarly, H1821+643 is a highly luminous radio-quiet quasar at z=0.299 with a bolometric luminosity of 2 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 and a massive BH of 3 10^ 9 M_ hosted by the central massive galaxy in a rich cool-core cluster of M_ 500 The cluster mass in r_ 500 , where r_ 500 is the radius within which the mean density is 500 times the critical density of the universe at the redshift of the cluster. 9 10^ 14 M_ . This quasar is within the redshift range of our search but outside the SDSS coverage area. If it were in the SDSS area, we would have considered it a massive quasar in a massive cluster.

sources.summary: The paper treats H 1821+643 as a quasar sightline for WHIM broad Ly absorption, reporting STIS redshift coverage, S/N, detection limits, and six reliable BLA candidates.

quotes.quote: To extend previous BLA measurements we have reanalyzed archival STIS data of the two quasars H 1821+643 and PG 0953+415 and have identified 13 BLA candidates along a total (unblocked) redshift path of z=0.440 .

The wavelength range available for detecting intervening broad Ly absorption towards H 1821+643 ( z_ em =0.297 ) is from 1230.0 to 1556.5 ( 5000 km s ^ 1 away from the quasar), corresponding to a redshift interval of z=0.269 . The typical S/N in this range is 21 per resolution element, so that the detection limit for broad Ly absorption (see equation 4) is log (N/b) 11.2 . The blocking of certain spectral regions due to other interstellar and intergalactic lines reduces the total unblended redshift interval to a value of z=0.238 . We find six reliably detected single-component BLA candidates at z=0.02642, 0.12221, 0.16352, 0.18047, 0.22616 , and 0.26659 .

sources.summary: H 1821+643 has an associated absorber previously reported in the literature, detected in Lyman-series and doublet transitions consistent with full coverage.

quotes.quote: H 1821+643 ( =0.2967) -- This associated absorption-line system, previously reported by tripp08 , is detected in the Lyman series ( ), 977.020, and the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet (Figure fig:h1821 ). The 1238.821, 1242.804 doublet is covered, but not detected. The Ly \ and Ly \ lines reach zero flux indicating full coverage in that ion. Similarly, the doublet shows complete consistency with full coverage (i.e., a coverage fraction of unity).

sources.summary: The paper identifies H1821+643 as the central quasar and BCG of CL1821+643, hosted by a dominant elliptical, radio-quiet but FR I in radio, and used as the cluster center.

quotes.quote: From the optical point of view, CL1821 (at z 0.299, Schneider et al. sch92 ) is dominated by the central quasar H1821+643 (e.g. Hutchings Neff hut91 ; Aravena et al. ara11 ; Reynolds et al. rey14 ; Walker et al. wal14 ), an uncommon example of an optically very luminous quasar hosted by the central dominant elliptical galaxy of a massive cluster (see Fig.  figimage ).

The cluster galaxy population is dominated by the quasar H1821+643 (ID 94 and hereafter BCG; see its optical spectrum in Fig.  figimage ). Although classified as a radio-quiet quasar, the BCG is a Fanaroff--Riley type I source (Blundell Rawlings blu01 ), and is evident in high-resolution radio images of CL1821 taken by B14 (see their fig. 1) and at X-ray wavelengths.

We also assume a magnitude ^ +17 for the host galaxy (the BCG) of the central quasar H1821+643 (from measurements performed by Hutchings and Neff hut91 and Floyd et al. flo04 ) and choose n=10 as the minimum number of galaxies in substructures (e.g. Durret et al. dur15 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses H1821+643 as a powerful radio-quiet quasar in a cool-core cluster, modeling its Chandra grating spectrum to constrain photon-ALP mixing.

quotes.quote: We present a 571 \ ks combined High and Low Energy Transmission Grating (HETG/LETG) Chandra observation of the powerful radio-quiet quasar H1821+643, hosted by a cool-core cluster at redshift 0.3 . The spectrum is well described by a double power-law continuum and broad + narrow iron line emission typical of type-1 Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN), with remaining spectral features < 2.5 . Using a cell-based approach to describe the turbulent cluster magnetic field, we compare our spectrum with photon-ALP mixing curves for 500 field realisations, assuming that the thermal-to-magnetic pressure ratio remains constant up to the virial radius. At 99.7 credibility and taking =100 , we exclude all couplings g_ a > 6.3 10^ 13 \ GeV ^ 1 for most ALP masses < 10^ 12 \ eV .

sources.summary: The paper finds KUV 18217+6419 favors warm absorption, notes hard-band features, and says no soft-band absorption studies were found.

quotes.quote: I Zw1, Mrk 841, KUV 18217+6419 and Akn 120 favour the scenario of warm absorption, yielding column densities of the orders of N_ H 10^ 20 10^ 21 \ cm^ 2 and a range of ionization parameters of 0.2< <3.0 .

KUV 18217+6419 The best fit from our analysis indicates the presence of a warm absorber. This source shows interesting features in the hard band, JB20072 , but we found no studies on absorption in the soft band.

gmw_00001400.name: ALESS49.1
gmw_00001400.overview: ALESS49.1 is a dusty, star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z ~ 3 [arXiv:1901.10027]. High-resolution observations reveal a compact core of carbon emission and dust continuum [arXiv:1901.10027], alongside a double-peaked CO profile that suggests a disk-like velocity structure [arXiv:1804.06852].

sources.summary: The paper resolves ALESS 49.1 with ALMA, finding compact [CII] and FIR emission, an extended low-surface-brightness [CII] component, and central ISM conditions driving a [CII]/FIR deficit.

quotes.quote: Wardlow Physics Department, Lancaster University, Bailrigg, Lancaster LA1 4YB, United Kingdom We present 0.15-arcsec (1 kpc) resolution ALMA observations of the [ C 2 ] 157.74 m line and rest-frame 160- m continuum emission in two z 3 dusty, star-forming galaxies - ALESS 49.1 and ALESS 57.1, combined with resolved CO(3--2) observations. In both sources, the [ C 2 ] surface brightness distribution is dominated by a compact core 1 kpc in radius, a factor of 2--3 smaller than the extent of the CO (3--2) emission. In ALESS 49.1, we find an additional extended (8-kpc radius), low surface-brightness [ C 2 ] component.

Both galaxies exhibit a strong [ C 2 ] /FIR deficit on 1-kpc scales, with FIR-surface-brightness to [ C 2 ] /FIR slope steeper than in local star-forming galaxies. A comparison of the [ C 2 ] /CO(3--2) observations with PDR models suggests a strong FUV radiation field ( G_0 10^4 ) and high gas density ( n (H) 10^4-10^5 cm ^ 3 ) in the central regions of ALESS 49.1 and 57.1.

For the two-Gaussian models, the compact and extended [ C 2 ] components in ALESS 49.1 have half-light radii of 1.01 0.12 kpc and 8.7 1.6 kpc, respectively. The compact and extended 160- m components in ALESS 57.1 have half-light radii of 0.86 0.07 and 5.3 1 kpc, respectively. For the two-component [ C 2 ] model in ALESS 49.1, the extended [ C 2 ] components accounts for up to 80 of the total [ C 2 ] luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper observes ALESS49.1 in CO with ALMA, adds Cycle 4 data, finds a double-peaked CO profile, detects continuum emission, and interprets its velocity structure as disk-like.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA Cycle 2 observations of the CO emission from four SMGs, ALESS49.1, ALESS57.1, ALESS67.1 and ALESS122.1. These sources were selected from the ALESS survey hodge13a, karim13 : an ALMA Cycle 0 follow-up program of 126 sources detected in the single-dish LABOCA Extended Chandra Deep Field South (ECDFS) Submm Survey LESS, weiss09 . These four sources were selected as they are spectroscopically confirmed at redshifts 2.1 <z<2.9 as the result of an extensive spectroscopic follow-up campaign danielson17 .

The CO spectrum of ALESS49.1 is clearly best described by a double-peaked profile and was thus fitted using an asymmetrical two-Gaussian model. The red and blue component line widths are 280 40 and 390 30 (FWHM), respectively, and are separated by 300 20 .

The continuum emission is detected in only two of the four sources in our data set: ALESS49.1 and ALESS122.1. In both cases, the naturally weighted dust continuum emission at (rest-frame 850 m) is detected at 4- significance at sub-arsecond resolution (Table tab:COprops ).

gmw_00001401.name: ALESS57.1
gmw_00001401.overview: ALESS57.1 is a high-redshift, dusty star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1804.06852, arXiv:1901.10027] that is classified as an active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1804.06852, arXiv:2003.08631]. Its compact core exhibits highly elliptical carbon emission [arXiv:1901.10027], while velocity gradients in its gas suggest a rotating disk-like geometry [arXiv:1804.06852, arXiv:2003.08631].

sources.summary: The paper observes ALESS57.1 in CO with ALMA, treats it as an AGN candidate, measures a marginally resolved line, estimates gas content, and notes disk-like velocity gradients.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA Cycle 2 observations of the CO emission from four SMGs, ALESS49.1, ALESS57.1, ALESS67.1 and ALESS122.1. These sources were selected from the ALESS survey hodge13a, karim13 : an ALMA Cycle 0 follow-up program of 126 sources detected in the single-dish LABOCA Extended Chandra Deep Field South (ECDFS) Submm Survey LESS, weiss09 . These four sources were selected as they are spectroscopically confirmed at redshifts 2.1 <z<2.9 as the result of an extensive spectroscopic follow-up campaign danielson17 .

ALESS57.1 and ALESS122.1 are reported as AGN candidates wang13, danielson17 , while the origin of the X-ray emission in ALESS67.1 is most probably related to star formation. ALMA observations were taken in Cycle 2 as part of the project 2013.1.00470.S (PI: Hodge), with a total integration time of 2.5 hours in ALMA Band 3, covering the spectral range expected for the line emission of the CO(3-2) transition at these redshifts.

The CO spectrum of ALESS57.1 is marginally spectroscopically resolved. Nevertheless, the line width is appropriately approximated by a Gaussian fit and the resulting values are summarized in Table tab:COprops .

sources.summary: The paper resolves ALESS 57.1 with ALMA, finding compact elliptical [CII] emission, a strong [CII]/FIR deficit, and central FUV fields and gas densities.

quotes.quote: Wardlow Physics Department, Lancaster University, Bailrigg, Lancaster LA1 4YB, United Kingdom We present 0.15-arcsec (1 kpc) resolution ALMA observations of the [ C 2 ] 157.74 m line and rest-frame 160- m continuum emission in two z 3 dusty, star-forming galaxies - ALESS 49.1 and ALESS 57.1, combined with resolved CO(3--2) observations. In both sources, the [ C 2 ] surface brightness distribution is dominated by a compact core 1 kpc in radius, a factor of 2--3 smaller than the extent of the CO (3--2) emission.

Both galaxies exhibit a strong [ C 2 ] /FIR deficit on 1-kpc scales, with FIR-surface-brightness to [ C 2 ] /FIR slope steeper than in local star-forming galaxies. A comparison of the [ C 2 ] /CO(3--2) observations with PDR models suggests a strong FUV radiation field ( G_0 10^4 ) and high gas density ( n (H) 10^4-10^5 cm ^ 3 ) in the central regions of ALESS 49.1 and 57.1.

The [ C 2 ] emission is detected at 5- and 8 significance in ALESS 49.1 and 57.1, respectively. The [ C 2 ] emission is relatively compact ( < 0.5 arcsec diameter) in both ALESS 49.1 and 57.1, similar in extent to the 160- m continuum. The [ C 2 ] emission in ALESS 57.1 is highly elliptical (axis ratio 2:1) and elongated in the east-west direction; the [ C 2 ] does not show any significant offset from the 160- m peak.

sources.summary: The paper describes XID 42 (ALESS57.1) as a high-redshift obscured AGN host with prior resolved CO and [C ii], ALMA line/continuum detections, and a velocity gradient indicating differential kinematics.

quotes.quote: XID 42 (ALESS57.1) has been recently studied by an by , who resolved the CO(3-2) and [ C ii ] emission, respectively.
Three out of six sources (namely XID 42, XID 539 and XID 666) were detected in both the line and continuum emission.

Notes on the morphology of the sources XID 42 shows a gradient along one direction in the velocity map, at least with a beam separation, suggesting the presence of a differential kinematic structure. In addition, its velocity dispersion is mostly concentrated in the central core of the galaxy. have inferred its inclination angle ( 40^ 50^ ) from the axial ratio of the CO(3--2) emission assuming a disk--like geometry, while have fit the [ C ii ] emission with a rotating disk model.

gmw_00001402.name: ALESS67.1
gmw_00001402.overview: ALESS67.1 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2.12 [arXiv:1804.06852, arXiv:1708.08937]. It exhibits a disturbed, clumpy morphology and tidal features indicative of a merger [arXiv:2108.05596, arXiv:1708.08937], with a compact dust continuum and a broad, double-peaked CO emission line that may trace a molecular outflow [arXiv:2108.05596, arXiv:2103.05011].

sources.summary: The paper observes ALESS67.1 as a high-redshift SMG, attributes its X-rays to star formation, and discusses broad asymmetric CO emission suggestive of multiple components or merging.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA Cycle 2 observations of the CO emission from four SMGs, ALESS49.1, ALESS57.1, ALESS67.1 and ALESS122.1. These sources were selected from the ALESS survey hodge13a, karim13 : an ALMA Cycle 0 follow-up program of 126 sources detected in the single-dish LABOCA Extended Chandra Deep Field South (ECDFS) Submm Survey LESS, weiss09 . These four sources were selected as they are spectroscopically confirmed at redshifts 2.1 <z<2.9 as the result of an extensive spectroscopic follow-up campaign danielson17 .

ALESS57.1 and ALESS122.1 are reported as AGN candidates wang13, danielson17 , while the origin of the X-ray emission in ALESS67.1 is most probably related to star formation. ALMA observations were taken in Cycle 2 as part of the project 2013.1.00470.S (PI: Hodge), with a total integration time of 2.5 hours in ALMA Band 3, covering the spectral range expected for the line emission of the CO(3-2) transition at these redshifts.

The CO line of ALESS67.1 shows a very large line width (FWHM 800 km s ^ 1 ) and appears asymmetric although the asymmetry is not statistically significant, due to the low SNR in this source. We postulate that the CO line of ALESS67.1 is produced by two sources of emission. This is supported by the velocity averaged image (Fig. fig:velaver ), where two spatial components are recognizable independently in both CO (ALMA) and optical/near-infrared ( HST ) imaging: one main right component of around 1 arsec radius and a second extended emission in the east.

sources.summary: The paper describes ALESS067.1 as a z=2.121 DSFG with disturbed optical morphology and CO kinematics that may trace a rotating molecular disk or AGN-driven outflow, implying approaching quenching.

quotes.quote: ALESS067.1 (J033243.19-275514.3) ALESS067.1 is a DSFG at redshift z _ spec =2.1212^ +0.0014 _ 0.0005 (this work from CO(3-2) spectral line, see Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ).

In the HST/WFC3 H _ 160 image (Fig. fig:cont ) the galaxy shows an extended (i.e. _ H 6.5 kpc; Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ) and disturbed/clumpy morphology Targett2013:2013MNRAS.432.2012T,vanderWel2012:2012ApJS..203...24V , that could be interpreted either as a signature of an ongoing heavily obscured and intense star formation episode, or as a possible indication of some companions claim it is the dominant galaxy of a multiple system Targett2013:2013MNRAS.432.2012T . We can gain some indications on the overall picture by analysing the two CO J>0 emission lines detected for ALESS067.1 (see Tab. tab:COemission and Fig. fig:panel_CO_ALESS0671 ). The velocity map (mom1) of the CO(3-2) line show a clear velocity gradient that could indicate the presence of a rotating disc of molecular gas. However, the velocity dispersion map (mom2) shows an evident central peak with a high-velocity tail towards the upper left corner, that could indicate the presence of an outflow, possibly due to a central AGN. Similarly, the double-peaked line profile (Fig. fig:panel_CO_ALESS0671 ) could be ascribed either to the inclination of the rotation plane with respect to the line of sight or to the presence of a double outflow powered by the active nucleus, where one jet is receding and the other is approaching the observer.

Multiple evidences (such as its X-ray 2-10 keV luminosity, that is >10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , and the eventual molecular outflow observed in the B3 ALMA map, mentioned before) suggest a forthcoming quenching of star formation in the host galaxy by the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper presents resolved ALMA, SINFONI, and HST observations of ALESS67.1, finding a z=2.12 SMG with compact dust, extended gas and stars, and merger-like kinematics.

quotes.quote: We present detailed studies of a z=2.12 submillimeter galaxy, ALESS67.1, using sub-arcsecond resolution ALMA, AO-aided VLT/SINFONI, and HST /CANDELS data to investigate the kinematics and spatial distributions of dust emission (870 m continuum), ^ 12 CO( J =3-2), strong optical emission lines, and visible stars. Dynamical modelling of the optical emission lines suggests that ALESS67.1 is not a pure rotating disk but a merger, consistent with the apparent tidal features revealed in the HST imaging.

ALESS67.1 is one of the few SMGs so far that is covered by all the necessary follow-up observations, and it is representative of the ALESS sample; ALESS67.1 has a spectroscopic redshift at z_ spec =2.1230 Danielson:2017aa with a SFR of 500 M _ yr ^ 1 Swinbank:2014aa,da-Cunha:2015aa and a stellar mass of 2 10^ 11 M _ Simpson:2014aa,da-Cunha:2015aa . ALESS67.1 appears to be a merger remnant in the HST imaging Chen:2015aa , and it is detected by Chandra in the 0.5--2 keV X-ray band Wang:2013aa .
We have presented analyses and measured sizes and spatial distributions of CO, optical emission lines, 870 m and near-infrared continuum for ALESS67.1.

sources.summary: BLAST 318 has reassuring 350 and 500 m detections, a robust radio ID associated with an interacting pair at z about 2, and LABOCA support.

quotes.quote: BLAST 318: z_ est = 2.09 This 250 m source has clear detections at 350 m and 500 m , both reassuringly close to the 250 m position. The radio identification is robust and unambiguous, and associated with an interacting galaxy pair (Fig. 7) which the photometry constrains to lie at z 2 (Fig. 5). The high-redshift nature of this source gains support from its detection at 870 m by LABOCA. VLT FORS2 spectroscopy has been attempted for this object, but yielded no redshift.

sources.summary: ALESS067.1 has radio and GMRT detections, an ALMA-derived angular size, and broad double-peaked CO emission interpreted as possible molecular outflow and AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: From this association all the 4 AzTEC-LABOCA sources in our sample (ALESS067.1, AzTEC.GS25, AzTEC.GS21, AzTEC.GS22) own a robust radio counterpart. Moreover, ALESS067.1 has an additional detection at 610 MHz by the Giant Metre-Wave Radio Telescope GMRT; Thomson2014:2014MNRAS.442..577T .

Just for two sources (ALESS067.1, AzTEC.GS22) we found the angular size (i.e., S e rsic effective radius R _ e ) of their ALMA counterparts in the DANCING ALMA catalog by Fujimoto2017:2017ApJ...850...83F , that we exploited to derive the circularized radii.
AGN feedback can be associate also to the broad ( v 500 km/s), double-peaked CO emission line profile observed for UDF3, UDF8 and ALESS067.1, that may indicate the presence of a molecular outflow.

sources.summary: The paper confirms J033243's previous z = 2.122 identification and describes it as the high-FWHM outlier, the second-brightest H emitter, and a high-H-implied-SFR source.

quotes.quote: Three of these seven sources (J033246, J033152, and J033243) were already identified in previous spectroscopic surveys using the Very Large Telescope/FOcal Reducer and low dispersion Spectrograph 2 and the Gemini Near-Infrared Spectrograph vanzella08a,kriek08a at z = 1.382, 2.336 and 2.122 respectively.

Both line width measurements, 415 \ and 530 , are consistent with the dynamics of active star forming HII regions, except the high FWHM outlier: J033243 at z = 2.123 with FWHM = 800 . J033243 is also the second brightest \ emitter with a high \ implied SFR, 335 ; its SFR _ FIR /SFR _ H = 7, which is the lowest SFR ratio of the sample indicative of a less \ obscuration.

sources.summary: The paper notes SMMJ0332 2755 has an [NII]/H upper limit, clearly separated H components, lower-quality kinematic maps, and HST-matched components.

quotes.quote: In all sources we clearly detect [ NII ] 6583 emission except SMMJ0332 2755 for which we quote a 3 upper limit to the ratio of [ NII ] /H .

All sources, except SMMJ2217+0017 and RGJ0332 2732, exhibit multiple peaks in the H intensity distributions with two sources showing clear separations between multiple components (SMMJ0332 2755 and SMMJ0217 0503).
Owing to the lower quality of the velocity and dispersion maps of SMMJ0332 2755 we bin the maps by 2 2 pixels in order the run the kinemetry.

sources.summary: The paper uses ALESS67.1 as a key SMG where FIR continuum is much more compact than other tracers and where a large fraction of H-based SFR lies outside dusty regions.

quotes.quote: This paper is motivated by Chen:2017aa , in which we found that in a submillimeter galaxy (SMG), ALESS67.1, at z=2.12 where we managed to gather sub-arcsecond UV-to-NIR continuum, FIR continuum, ^ 12 CO, and H , the size of FIR continuum is a factor of 4-6 smaller than that of all the other emissions.
Chen:2017aa found a similar result on one SMG, ALESS67.1, which is included in our sample.
For the simplest quantification, we find that the fraction of H based SFRs that are located outside the central dusty regions ranges from zero (AS2UDS412.0) to 40 (ALESS67.1).

gmw_00001403.name: ALESS122.1
gmw_00001403.overview: ALESS122.1 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift between 2.1 and 2.9 [arXiv:1804.06852]. Observations of the galaxy reveal bright carbon monoxide emission and a complete spatial offset between its gas and stellar emission [arXiv:1804.06852].

sources.summary: The paper observes ALESS122.1 as a high-redshift SMG and AGN candidate, detecting bright CO and continuum, disk-like velocity structure, and an offset between gas and stellar emission.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA Cycle 2 observations of the CO emission from four SMGs, ALESS49.1, ALESS57.1, ALESS67.1 and ALESS122.1. These sources were selected from the ALESS survey hodge13a, karim13 : an ALMA Cycle 0 follow-up program of 126 sources detected in the single-dish LABOCA Extended Chandra Deep Field South (ECDFS) Submm Survey LESS, weiss09 . These four sources were selected as they are spectroscopically confirmed at redshifts 2.1 <z<2.9 as the result of an extensive spectroscopic follow-up campaign danielson17 .

The CO spectrum of ALESS122.1 is the brightest in our sample, and its line profile is well described by a single-Gaussian fit. Table tab:COprops lists the CO(3-2) line properties and best-fit parameter values. A detailed description of the velocity field sampled by these CO lines will be provided in Section subsubsec:channelmaps .

ALESS122.1 displays the striking feature that the gas and stellar emission are completely offset. We must point out, however, that the stellar emission in ALESS122.1 is exclusively represented by the ACS I_ 814 band imaging (rest-frame 270 nm), in contrast to the other three sources which are covered by at least one WFC3/IR band.

gmw_00001404.name: RS14
gmw_00001404.overview: RS14 is an extremely red spiral galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 2.463 [arXiv:2208.00132]. Its stellar population is best modeled as old and passive with moderate dust attenuation and a current star formation rate of nearly zero [arXiv:2208.00132].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RS14 as an extremely red JWST spiral with spectroscopic redshift 2.463, best fit by an old passive stellar population with moderate dust attenuation.

quotes.quote: The selected red spiral galaxies (RS13 and RS14) were already detected by HST WFC3 and Spitzer IRAC images; however, the angular resolution of these previous instruments did not allow us to study detailed morphology. We can now access the resolved morphology of the red spiral galaxies by JWST's unprecedented spatial resolution and sensitivity in these NIR wavelengths (Fig. fig:nasaimage ).

RS14 Within the extremely red spiral galaxies, RS14 has already been studied in several papers. In particular, Sun22 reported ALMA continuum detection in 1.1 mm image obtained among the ALMA large program ALCS: 2018.1.00035.L (PI: K. Kohno). Also, Carnall22 reported spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =2.463 determined by multiple emission lines such as H , [ NII ] , and [ SII ] emission lines (ID 9239 of NIRSpec spectroscopy as part of the same JWST ERO).

The best-fit model for RS14 is passive, old stellar population, and moderate dust attenuation ( A_ V =0.84^ +0.12 _ 0.03 ). The age is much longer than the star-formation time-scale, _ SF 0.1 Gyr , and the current SFR is almost zero.

gmw_00001405.name: SDSS J072301.42+362117.1
gmw_00001405.overview: SDSS J072301.42+362117.1 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Lynx-Cancer void [arXiv:1608.08284]. It forms a triplet with two nearby, even fainter dwarf companions that were resolved using GMRT mapping [arXiv:1608.08284].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J072301.42+362117.1 as a new Lynx-Cancer void LSB dwarf and discusses NRT profile confusion with nearby faint companions resolved using GMRT mapping.

quotes.quote: SDSS J072301.42+362117.1 and J072313.46+362213.0 . The first galaxy, a LSB dwarf, has been identified as a new Lynx-Cancer void galaxy after its redshift was obtained at the SAO 6m telescope (BTA). Its NRT \ profile suggested a possible contribution from a nearby galaxy which has been found as a very low surface brightness dwarf, 2 ^m fainter than the main component, at 3 \ to E (J072313.46+362213.0). An additional NRT observation in the direction partly disentangled the confusion between the \ contributions. The final \ parameters for these two galaxies and a third even fainter one (J072320.57+362440.8, see Table 3) have been adopted after the subsequent GMRT \ mapping of this triplet (Chengalur, Pustilnik ).

gmw_00001406.name: SDSS J080238.15+052551.2
gmw_00001406.overview: SDSS J080238.15+052551.2 is a very faint and compact optical galaxy located close to a bright star [arXiv:1608.08284]. It was initially identified as a gas-rich void-sample candidate, but subsequent observations failed to detect a confirming neutral hydrogen signal [arXiv:1608.08284].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J080238.15+052551.2 as a faint void-sample candidate, finds no confirming NRT H I signal, and excludes its unreliable data from statistical analysis.

quotes.quote: SDSS J080238.15+052551.2 . This very faint and compact optical object close to a bright star was included in the void sample after its assumed identification with a faint ALFALFA source (Haynes et al. ) with V ( )=830 , F ( )=0.43 0.04 Jy \ with S/N ratio of 6.2 (AGC 188988). With such parameters, this galaxy has a very high ratio M ( )/ L_ B 4.8 (using the total SDSS g and magnitudes, transformed to B_ tot =19.8). Since very gas-rich galaxies are rare objects, we conducted \ observations of AGC 188988 with the NRT. Our NRT data indicate no signal at the respective velocity with an upper limit of F ( ) < 0.16 Jy \ (2 ).

The probable interpretation of this case is a false ALFALFA detection. If, however, there is \ associated with the suggested faint optical object, its ratio M ( )/ L_ B < 1.8 is not so large. We consider this object's data to be unreliable and excluded it from the following statistical analysis.

gmw_00001407.name: SDSS J090018.30+322226.2
gmw_00001407.overview: SDSS J090018.30+322226.2 is a newly identified void galaxy [arXiv:1608.08284]. It exhibits faint H-alpha emission [arXiv:1608.08284] and has a moderate gas-to-light ratio [arXiv:1608.08284].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J090018.30+322226.2 as a new void galaxy with BTA H-alpha redshift, weak NRT signal, upper-limit H I flux, and moderate gas-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: SDSS J090018.30+322226.2 . This galaxy is a new void object with V_ hel = 740 30 , as measured on the faint H emission in the spectrum obtained at the BTA. At NRT the signal at this radial velocity is within 1.4 , so the numbers below should be treated as upper limits. With W_ 50 = 30 , typical of galaxies with M_ B -12, which results in the total flux of 0.14 0.10 Jy . For parameter M ( )/ L_ B the respective value is 0.72 0.52.

gmw_00001408.name: SDSS J094003.27+445931.7
gmw_00001408.overview: SDSS J094003.27+445931.7 is a galaxy with a radial velocity of approximately 1358 km/s derived from its optical emission-line spectrum [arXiv:1608.08284]. Neutral hydrogen has been marginally detected near this redshift [arXiv:1608.08284].

sources.summary: The paper reports marginal H I near the SDSS redshift of SDSS J094003.27+445931.7 and discusses alternative optical counterparts for another nearby H I component.

quotes.quote: SDSS J094003.27+445931.7 . This galaxy has marginally detected \ with the radial velocity close to that derived from the SDSS emission-line spectrum (1358 4 ). However, there is also a similar flux detection (at the level of (2-3) ) at V_ hel = 1202 8 . The search for a possible optical counterpart on the SDSS image to this \ component produced two candidates within the NRT beam.

The nearest one is a small and almost edge-on blue disc SDSS J093951.28+445921.9 with g =19.34, =19.15. Its BTA spectrum has revealed the velocity of the H line of 14000 . The second candidate is the almost face-on LSB disc SDSS J093950.11+444800.1 with g =17.55, =17.18 ( B 17.90), 11.5 \ to the south and 13.16 s ( 140 ) to the west. Owing to the NRT beam offset, its nominal flux should drop by a factor of 6.5.

gmw_00001409.name: MRK 407=J094747.60+390503.0
gmw_00001409.overview: Mrk 407 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1004.2858, arXiv:1608.08284] that serves as the brightest member of a galaxy triplet [arXiv:1608.08284]. It features a regular central emission morphology [arXiv:1004.2858], moderate metallicity [arXiv:1004.2858], and an inhomogeneous extinction pattern caused by a crossing dust lane [arXiv:1004.2858].

sources.summary: Mrk 407 is mapped as a Markarian BCD with regular central emission, distorted continuum outskirts, inhomogeneous extinction, older stellar absorption features, moderate metallicity, and tentative rotation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy Mrk 407 is included in the Atlas of Markarian galaxies, where it appears classified as an S0. J , H , and K NIR surface brightness photometry was published in , but the quality of these data was insufficient for assessing whether or not this galaxy has an older stellar low surface brightness component underlying the SF regions. The only spectroscopic data available in the literature are its redshift and the equivalent widths and flux ratios of the strongest lines Ugryumov1998 . The PMAS FOV covers an area of 2.1 2.1 kpc with a spatial sampling of 130 pc per spaxel.

Emission line and continuum maps show an overall regular morphology, with the intensity distribution peaking roughly at the center of the outer elliptical isophotes. The galaxy has an inhomogeneous extinction pattern, with a dust lane crossing it southwest--northeast, where / \ reaches values of up to 5.5 --- which translates into an interstellar reddening \ of up to 0.6. We found a value of 0.4 Z_ , which places Mrk 407 in the high metallicity BCD group.

sources.summary: The paper describes MRK 407 as a blue compact galaxy and brightest triplet member, then disentangles its NRT H I contribution from neighboring LSBD companions.

quotes.quote: MRK 407=J094747.60+390503.0 . This blue compact galaxy (BCG) is the brightest member of a triplet, which also includes 1.7 mag fainter LSBD UZC J09475+3908 at 3 \ to the north and 3 mag fainter LSBD SDSS J094758.45+390510.1 at 2 \ to the east. Each galaxy contributes to the F ( ) for any NRT pointing in the direction of the triplet. We used our NRT results, accounting for the a priori known decrease of \ flux for the sources NRT beam offset, as well as the earlier observations of MRK 407 by Thuan Martin ( ) to disentangle the contribution of each component of the triplet. The typical estimated accuracy of the resulted F ( ) is 20 .
Follow-up GMRT mapping of this triplet (Chengalur et al., in preparation) will give a better understanding of its properties.

gmw_00001410.name: SDSS J101014.96+461744.1
gmw_00001410.overview: SDSS J101014.96+461744.1 is a faint galaxy with an optical redshift corresponding to a heliocentric velocity of 1092 km/s [arXiv:1608.08284]. It features a high signal-to-noise emission-line spectrum but has no detectable neutral hydrogen gas flux [arXiv:1608.08284].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J101014.96+461744.1 as a faint galaxy with a good SDSS emission-line spectrum, optical redshift, no detected H I, and an adopted upper-limit gas ratio.

quotes.quote: SDSS J101014.96+461744.1 . This is a faint galaxy with a good S/N SDSS emission-line spectrum. Its optical redshift corresponds to V_ hel = 1092 3 . On our data, there is no detectable \ flux, for _ noise 2.4 mJy. For statistical analysis, we adopt for its F the upper limit F ( ) < 0.12 Jy \ and the value of M ( )/ L_ B < 0.34.

gmw_00001411.name: Arp 240
gmw_00001411.overview: Arp 240 is an early-stage luminous infrared merger consisting of two massive spiral galaxies, NGC 5257 and NGC 5258 [arXiv:1305.5865, arXiv:2006.12523]. The system exhibits tidal tails, a connecting bridge, and intense star formation, including extremely luminous star clusters within its hinge regions [arXiv:1305.5865, arXiv:1601.02664].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5257/8 as an early-stage equal-mass spiral merger whose tidal tails, enhanced spiral structure, cluster ages, and kinematics are reproduced about 220 Myr after first passage.

quotes.quote: The pair NGC 5257 and NGC 5258 both show clear evidence for tidal distortion, with tidal tails and enhanced spiral features visible in stellar light. Neither nucleus shows evidence for the presence of a strong AGN. Photometric estimates and SED modeling of the system provide stellar mass estimates of 1-3 10^ 11 M _ Howell2010,U2012 .

NGC 5257/8 is an early stage merger between two massive spiral galaxies (Figures fig:idkN5257 and fig:N5257evol ). The disk spin axis of NGC 5257 is nearly perpendicular to the orbital plane while the disk spin axis of NGC 5258 is only moderately inclined. The best-fit disk inclinations are ( 85^ , 65^ ). The system is viewed t _ now 220 Myr after first pericentric passage. The galaxies are approaching apocenter but have not yet reached the point of greatest separation.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 240 as an early-stage LIRG merger whose two spiral galaxies have passed first encounter and show ULIRG-like CO conversion factors and intense star formation.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study Arp 240, which is another LIRG at an early merging stage. Arp 240 is composed two massive spiral galaxies, NGC 5257 and NGC 5258. The two galaxies are intertwined in HI Iono_2005 but well separated in optical images. The projected distance of the two galaxies is about 40 kpc, which is larger than the separation of Arp 55 ( 9 kpc). Simulations Privon_2013 show the two galaxies are in the early stage of the merging process and have just been through first passage.

Factors impacting the _ CO value By using RADEX modeling, we derived the \ for three regions of Arp 240 with \ detection. We find that all of these regions seem to have \ close to the commonly used ULIRG value (1.1 \ including helium). This is also supported by LTE analysis across the entire galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 240 as a Galaxy Zoo: Mergers example target whose volunteer-selected simulations reproduce its interacting-galaxy morphology, including symmetric tidal tails and convergent trajectories.

quotes.quote: Arp 240 - An example system from Galaxy Zoo: Mergers Our sample target was selected from the list of targets in the SDSS. Table mzsumtgt summarizes the Galaxy Zoo: Mergers activity for the target. It lists the total number of simulations viewed by all volunteers, how many they rejected, how many they selected, and the number that were enhanced.

For this particular pair of galaxies, the volunteers have done an excellent job. Each galaxy has symmetric tails that are recreated in the simulation with the proper size and orientation. Figure fig:587722984435351614traj comprises four panels that each show a set of simulated trajectories for the secondary galaxy relative to the primary galaxy. The trajectories are calculated as part of the simulation.

sources.summary: The paper explains why its dynamical modeling did not converge for NGC 5257/8, citing NGC 5257's H I tail and a prior model placing NGC 5258 on a nearly polar orbit.

quotes.quote: NGC 5257/8: NGC 5257 has a prominent tail in HI that is absent in the optical image. This tail played an important role in the modeling by . Without the information in the extended tail we do not have the same constraints to pin down the encounter parameters. Furthermore, the model presented by indicates that NGC 5258 is in a nearly polar orbit. According to , the modeling method we used does not result in a good convergence when any of the galaxies is in a polar or retrograde orbit, so assuming that the model of is correct we should have expected a similar failure.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5257 as a widely separated interacting partner with tidal tails, a bridge, star-forming spiral arms, older nuclear stars, extinction, and possible LINER activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS F13373+0105 W (NGC 5257) Although NGC 5257 and its equal mass partner NGC 5258 are still widely separated and retain much of their structure, they exhibit interaction features including tidal tails and a bridge between the two galaxies. The spiral arms show regions of strong star formation with some signatures of post-starburst populations away from the nucleus. The nuclear regions of NGC 5257 are dominated by an older stellar population as the intense nuclear starburst associated with the later stages of major mergers has not yet begun. The extinction is higher in the nuclear regions and the measurable line-ratios place the nucleus in the composite region of the standard diagnostic diagrams, indicating possible LINER activity-previous nuclear observations and integrated spectrophotometry are consistent with the higher nuclear extinction and overall line ratios we observe Veilleux95,Kewley01b,Moustakas06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5258 as a near twin of NGC 5257, with higher extinction, older nuclear stars, weak nuclear line emission, and southwest star formation.

quotes.quote: IRAS F13373+0105 E (NGC 5258) NGC 5258 is a near-twin to NGC 5257 in mass and luminosity. Our spectra indicate higher extinction and a flatter gradient, though the extent of measurable region metallicities is smaller than in NGC 5257. Again similar to NGC 5257 the nuclear region of NGC 5258 is dominated by older stars and very little line-emission and there is some evidence of younger post-starburst populations away from the nucleus. The strongest line emission is associated with the knot of star formation to the southwest of the nuclear region.

sources.summary: Arp 240 hosts three of the sample’s most extreme hinge clumps, with high star-formation rates and luminous HST-resolved clusters comparable to blended Antennae cluster complexes.

quotes.quote: As discussed below, the distance-limited 1.0 kpc sample lacks some of the most extreme regions (i.e., the hinge regions in Arp 240 and Arp 256). The Highest SFR Clumps A comparison of the top and bottom panels in Figures 7 and 8 shows that the most luminous clumps are found in the interacting galaxies. On a 2.5 kpc radius scale (Figure 7), no clumps are found in the spiral sample with SFR 2 M _  yr ^ 1 , while five such clumps are found in the interacting sample. These include three hinge clumps in Arp 240 (3.3, 6.2, and 9.0 M _  yr ^ 1 ), the `overlap region' between the two disks of the Antennae galaxies Arp 244 (4.8 M _  yr ^ 1 ), and the hinge clump in Arp 256 (2.0 M _  yr ^ 1 ).
Hubble Telescope images reveal extremely luminous star clusters (M _ I between 12.2 and 16.5) at the centers of the Arp 256, Arp 240, and NGC 2207 clumps smith14 .

If smoothed to the effective resolution of Arp 240 and Arp 256, these clusters would be blended together to produce a source similar in size and luminosity to the sources seen in those galaxies smith14 .

gmw_00001412.name: NGC 4486a
gmw_00001412.overview: NGC 4486a is a low-luminosity elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:0705.1758]. It hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1102.2736, arXiv:0705.1758] and features an edge-on nuclear disk composed of stars and dust [arXiv:1102.2736, arXiv:0705.1758].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4486A as a low-luminosity Virgo elliptical with an edge-on nuclear disk, old stellar population, metallicity gradient, and no stellar-population evidence for an ancient merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 4486A is a low-luminosity elliptical galaxy harbouring an edge-on nuclear disk of stars and dust. It is known to host a super-massive black hole. We study its large-scale kinematics and stellar population along the major axis to investigate the link between the nuclear and global properties.

The SSP-equivalent age is about 12 Gyr old throughout the body of the galaxy, and its metallicity decreases from [ Fe/H ] = 0.18 near the centre to sub-solar values in the outskirts. The metallicity gradient is 0.24 dex per decade of radius within the effective isophote. The velocity dispersion is 132 3 at 1.3 from the centre and decreases outwards. The rotation velocity reaches a maximum V_ max 115 5 at a radius 1.3 < r_ max < 2 . NGC 4486A appears to be a typical low-luminosity elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4486a with SINFONI, describes its low-luminosity elliptical nature and nuclear disc, and derives a central black-hole mass consistent with the low-dispersion M-sigma relation.

quotes.quote: With SINFONI we observed the central region of the low-luminosity elliptical galaxy NGC 4486a at a spatial resolution of 0.1 in the K band. The stellar kinematics was measured with a maximum penalised likelihood method considering the region around the CO absorption band heads. We determined a black hole mass of M_ =(1.25^ +0.75 _ 0.79 ) 10^7 M_ (90 C.L.) using the Schwarzschild orbit superposition method including the full 2-dimensional spatial information.

NGC 4486a is a low-luminosity elliptical galaxy in the Virgo cluster, close to M87. It contains an almost edge-on nuclear disc of stars and dust Kormendy-4486a . The bright star 2.5 away from the centre makes it impossible to obtain undisturbed spectra with conventional ground-based longslit spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC4486A has nuclear-disk extra light and a nearby bright star that complicate central fitting, with published Sersic indices spanning 2.04 to 4.2.

quotes.quote: NGC4486A has extra light from the nuclear disk visible in almost all wavelengths. In combination with a very bright star next to the centre it is difficult to provide a reliable fit at the centre. tex:FC06a,tex:KF09,tex:H09 found S e rsic indices values 2.7, 2.04 and 4.2 respectively.

gmw_00001413.name: FW4871
gmw_00001413.overview: FW4871 is a massive galaxy at a redshift of z = 1.893 [arXiv:1109.6323] that is part of a major merger pair [arXiv:2206.05293] and exhibits a roughly face-on disk structure [arXiv:2206.05293]. Although the galaxy features tidal tails indicative of a recent merger [arXiv:1109.6323], it has no ongoing star formation within one effective radius [arXiv:1109.6323].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GOODS-S 43114 as one member of a z=1.89 major merger, finding interaction signatures, similar declining star-formation history to its companion, and a roughly face-on disk-like structure.

quotes.quote: In this study, we present a merger at z=1.89 between the galaxies GOODS-S 43114 and GOODS-S 43683, where galaxy IDs are drawn from the 3D-HST survey catalogs of ske14 . GOODS-S 43114 was targeted by the MOSDEF team, and high-quality J , H , and K_ s band Keck/MOSFIRE spectra were obtained. GOODS-S 43683 was not a MOSDEF target; however, it was serendipitously captured in the MOSFIRE slit given the mask position angle.

GOODS-S 43114 and GOODS-S 43683 have been identified as a merger pair in van10 and the CANDELS morphology catalog kar15 . The latter classifies mergers and other galaxy features based on the visual inspection. For GOODS-S 43114 (GOODS-S 43683), 100 (66 ) of people classified it as having ``any interaction'' while 66 (100 ) claimed that tidal arms exist. GOODS-S 43114 is a class 4 merger, while GOODS-S 43683 is a class 3 merger, both of which are high confidence merger classifications.
GOODS-S 43114 has a low inclination indicating that it is roughly a face-on disk based on its morphology and smaller than expected second velocity moment estimate.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes FW4871 as a massive z=1.893 quiescent galaxy with tidal tails, old stellar populations, little ongoing star formation, and a recent merger linked to quenching.

quotes.quote: We present here a detailed analysis of the star formation history (SFH) of FW4871, a massive galaxy at z= 1.893 0.002 . We compare rest-frame optical and NUV slitless grism spectra from the Hubble Space Telescope with a large set of composite stellar populations to constrain the underlying star formation history. Even though the morphology features prominent tidal tails, indicative of a recent merger, there is no sign of on-going star formation within an aperture encircling one effective radius, which corresponds to a physical extent of 2.6 kpc.

The colour profile of FW4871 does not suggest a significant radial trend in the properties of the stellar populations out to 3R _ e . We suggest that the recent merger that formed FW4871 is responsible for the quenching of its star formation.

gmw_00001414.name: NGC 7457
gmw_00001414.overview: NGC 7457 is an isolated, low-mass lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:1907.03770] that hosts a small bar [arXiv:1310.2607, arXiv:1311.1530] and a pseudo-bulge [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:1310.2607]. It features a counter-rotating nuclear core [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:1907.03770] and a small, unimodal globular cluster system [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:1806.11242] that exhibits rapid rotation [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:1310.2607].

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 7457 as an isolated S0 with a pseudo-bulge, possible merger-related counter-rotating core, unimodal GC colour distribution, and rapidly rotating GC system.

quotes.quote: NGC 7457 is an isolated S 0 with a pseudo-bulge detected both photometrically Tomita and kinematically Pinkney that shows an unusually low central velocity dispersion for its luminosity. Emsellem07 revealed that this galaxy has a small counter rotating core that might be the result of a merger.

They provide wide field WIYN/Minimosaic photometry of the GC system of this galaxy, finding that the colour distribution is not bimodal and showing the total population to be made up of 210 30 GCs with a radial extent of 12 2 kpc. We find 21 spectroscopically confirmed GCs in two DEIMOS masks observed in average 0.9 arcsec seeing conditions. We also obtained spectra for 7 of the 13 GCs already observed by Chomiuk , finding their LRIS and our DEIMOS radial velocities to be consistent within the errors, but with a mean difference of 12 (see sec:Repeated ). Therefore, we add the remaining 6 Chomiuk GCs corrected for the velocity offset to our GC sample. The final GC catalogue consists of 27 GC radial velocities.
We find that the GC system of NGC 7457 is rapidly rotating along the photometric major axis, with a flat rotation curve at 80 up to 2 .

sources.summary: NGC 7457 is modeled as a peculiar low-mass S0 dominated by warm and hot orbits, with a thick disc, counter-rotating center, and externally driven bulge/cylindrical-rotation features.

quotes.quote: We construct Schwarzschild orbit-based models of NGC 7457, known as a peculiar low-mass lenticular galaxy. Our best-fitting model successfully retrieves most of the unusual kinematics behaviours of this galaxy, in which, the orbital distribution of stars is dominated by warm and hot orbits. The reconstructed surface brightness of the hot component matches fairly well the photometric bulge and the reconstructed LOSVD map of this component shows clear rotation around the major photometric axis of the galaxy. In the absence of a dominant cold component, the outer part of our model is dominated by warm orbits, representing an exponential thick disc. Our orbital analysis also confirm the existence of a counter-rotating orbital substructure in the very centre, reported in previous observational studies.

By comparing our model with a variety of simulation studies, and considering the stellar kinematics and populations properties of this galaxy, we suggest that the thick disc is most likely a dynamically heated structure, formed through the interactions and accretion of satellite(s) with near-polar initial inclination. We also suggest a merger-driven process as the most plausible scenario to explain the observed and dynamically-modelled properties of the bulge of NGC 7457. We conclude that both the high level of cylindrical rotation and unusually low velocity dispersion reported for the NGC 7457 have most-likely external origins.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7457 as a field S0 with a small non-bimodal GC system, mostly disc-associated clusters, and evidence favoring secular evolution over recent mergers.

quotes.quote: NGC 7457 is a field S0 galaxy brodie14 with a distance of 12.9 Mpc alabi17 . In contrast with the other galaxies in this sample, it shows no signs of bimodality in its GC population hargis11, pota13 . Previous studies of this galaxy also proposed a counter-rotating galaxy core sil02 and a possible major merger origin hargis11 . Furthermore, it presents the smallest amount of GCs of all galaxies in the sample, with a total number of 210 30 GCs hargis11 .

For NGC 7457 we find that 70 of its GCs are likely to belong to the disc. Overall, our results suggest that these galaxies assembled into S0s through different evolutionary paths. Mergers seem to have been very important for NGC 2768 and NGC 3115 while NGC 7457 is more likely to have experienced secular evolution.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7457 as an isolated disk-dominated S0 with a young counter-rotating nuclear component, rotationally supported tracers, and aligned PNe and globular-cluster kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 7457 is a disk-dominated S0 galaxy found in isolation with the possible evidence of a bar-like structure Michard1994 . Early studies have identified the presence of a small, compact nucleus within the innermost galaxy regions with a young ( 2 2.5 Gyr old) stellar population, which displays counter-rotation with respect to the galaxy bulge and disk Silchenko2002 . Evidence for a kinematically distinct core (KDC) characterized by young stellar ages in NGC 7457 has been also found by Kuntschner2006,Kuntschner2010 , who measured a strong H _ absorption.

The combined kinematics of both the PNe and GC systems of NGC 7457 were previously studied by Zanatta2018 , who found this galaxy to be rotationally supported with V_ rot / > 2 at all radii. Kinematic profiles NGC 7457 shows an increasing stellar rotation velocity profile out to 2 R_ e . Such behaviour is observed from both ATLAS ^ 3D (within 0.5 R_ e ) and SLUGGS, which show good agreement in the rotation velocity. Both PNe and GCs also show a rising rotation velocity profile out to 2 R_ e , very consistent with the stars.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 7457’s globular cluster system, finding low specific frequency, steep radial distribution, unimodal colors, and weak support for spiral-merger formation.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present the study of the GCS of the isolated, low-luminosity S0 galaxy NGC 7457. It is a disky galaxy (Michard Marchal ) and its central light distribution is fitted by a power-law without evidence for the presence of a core (Lauer et al. ). This galaxy does not show any trace of past interactions or merging (Schweizer Seitzer ). Nevertheless a steep central power-law in a disky galaxy can be the consequence of dissipative merging of gaseous galaxies (Bender ). Therefore the GCS around NGC 7457 may well have kept some traces of some merger events.

At face value NGC 7457 does not seem to confirm the hypothesis of a formation by spiral--spiral merging, at least when compared to the predictions of the simple model of Ashman Zepf ( ). NGC 7457 is found to have a rather low number of GCs steeply distributed around the galaxy, and the color distribution of these GCs appears unimodal.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 7457 as an isolated S0 in a six-galaxy lenticular sample, using planetary nebulae to separate spheroid and disc kinematics out to large radii.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we therefore apply the kinematic bulge--disc decomposition technique developed in Ari to the sample of six S0s with suitable PNe data presented in Ari2 . This sample was selected to span a range of environments in order to see if the evolutionary properties depend systematically on surroundings: NGC 7457 and NGC 3115 are isolated; NGC 1023 and NGC 2768 are the dominant galaxies of two small groups (5-6 members); while NGC 3384 and NGC 3489 are satellite galaxies of the Leo Group (30 members). The Hubble types of these galaxies and the adopted distance moduli are listed in Table tab:galfitinput .

In this paper, we have analysed the kinematics of a sample of six lenticular galaxies using the planetary nebulae data presented in Ari2 to determine their dynamical properties out to large radii. This analysis has emphasised the importance of treating the spheroidal and disc components of these systems as distinct entities both photometrically and kinematically, and doing a careful job of separating them. Through this process, the rather heterogeneous properties of S0s over-all start to be resolved into more consistent sub-components, and a reasonably coherent picture begins to emerge.

sources.summary: NGC 7457 is described as a small barred S0 with a pseudo-bulge, fast-rotator classification, global rotation, low dispersion, and rapidly rotating globular clusters.

quotes.quote: This small S0 hosts a small bar Michard:1994tl and a pseudo-bulge Pinkney:2003jb . As with other lenticular galaxies, it is classified as a fast rotator based on its inner kinematics Emsellem:2011br . Our combined kinematic map shows global rotation (also see Cortesi:2013b ) and anti-correlated h_3 . The velocity dispersion values are universally low. The kinematics of this poor GC system have been presented by Pota:2013en , who found that the GCs are globally rapidly rotating, in agreement with the stars.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 7457 with M85, noting its unusually high GC rotation parameter, intermediate-age clusters, possible 1:10 merger origin, and much lower mass than M85.

quotes.quote: The rotation parameters, R / _ , cor , of most early-type galaxies in the previous studies are smaller than 1, while NGC 7457 shows a much larger value, R / _ , cor =1.68^ +0.37 _ 0.40 pot13 . NGC 7457 (S0) is the faintest among the galaxies in Table tab:kinematics_etg . csb08 investigated the GCs in NGC 7457 using HST photometry and Keck/LRIS spectroscopy, and found that there is an intermediate-age population with an age of 2--2.5 Gyr. Based on N- body simulations of galaxy mergers given by bjc05 , pot13 pointed out that the strong rotation feature of NGC 7457 GCs could be explained by uneven-mass galaxy mergers with a 1:10 mass ratio. M85 ( M_K = -25.1 mag) is much brighter (much more massive) than NGC 7457 ( M_K = -22.4 mag). According to the hierarchical galaxy formation scenario, M85 must have undergone many more merging events than NGC 7457.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC7457, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC7457 (Fig. fig:NGC7457 ) is a lenticular galaxy with a prominent large-scale disk. The residuals from a standard model are relatively good, but show signs of a bar, which is not aligned with the disk nor the bulge, and a nucleus. Additional justification for attempting a more complex model is given by the fact that the best fit bulge in a standard model is too extended and over-predicts the data at large radii ( 120 ), where it dominates the surface brightness.

sources.summary: The paper includes low-mass NGC 7457 as an IMF-sensitive case with short X-ray exposure, WFPC2 coverage, and a need for deeper data.

quotes.quote: We also include the galaxy NGC 7457, which has a shorter exposure time of 30ks. This galaxy is of particular interest to our study because its relatively low mass should result in a large effect on its LMXB population, if the IMF relationship is present.
For NGC 7457 only two pointings, taken with the wide field planetary camera 2 (WFPC2), are available - although these cover all of the detected X-ray sources.
We do not quote an n_ x,37 for NGC 7457 because its high detection limit and low number of sources do not allow us to accurately extrapolate its XLF to these lower luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 7457 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: NGC 7457 This is a field S0. The velocity maps from 2004MNRAS.352..721E and show that this galaxy is a regular rotator along the major axis. Our PN velocity map shows that the rotation velocity is growing with radius, reaching 100 at 3.5 . No twisting of the kinematic major axis is observed.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 7457 appears unimodal and has only seven CaT-measured globular clusters, too few for GMM bimodality testing.

quotes.quote: NGC 7457 appears unimodal; see 2011ApJ...738..113H .
NGC 821, NGC 1400 and NGC 7457 do not have enough measured GCs to determine whether their distributions are bimodal or not.
We did not run GMM on NGC 821 (17 GCs), NGC 1400 (34 GCs) and NGC 7457 (7 GCs) due to the low number of GCs with CaT measurements in these galaxies.

gmw_00001415.name: HS 0128+2832
gmw_00001415.overview: HS 0128+2832 is a compact blue dwarf galaxy characterized by a low oxygen abundance [arXiv:1105.4516]. It exhibits an elevated nitrogen abundance with a spatially uniform nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio, yet its integrated spectrum shows no trace of Wolf-Rayet stars [arXiv:1105.4516].

sources.summary: The paper studies HS 0128+2832 as a nitrogen-overabundant compact blue dwarf with low oxygen abundance, no reported WR features, and a spatially uniform N/O ratio from the direct method.

quotes.quote: HS 0128+2832 is a compact galaxy studied by Izotov Thuan (2004), who derived a low O abundance [ 12+log(O/H) = 8.12 ] combined with an elevated abundance of N [ log(N/O) = -1.23 ] . No trace of WR stars was reported in the integrated spectrum of this object. HS 0837+4717 is apparently another compact galaxy which has been extensively studied by Pustilnik et al. (2004), who found very low O abundance [ 12+log(O/H) = 7.65 ] and a very high value of the relative abundance of N [ log(N/O) = -0.75 ] . These authors detected the WR blue bump which was used to estimate approximately 1000 WR stars in the starburst. Although the stellar origin of the He ii line shown by Pustilnik is not clear, this galaxy also appears in the catalog of WR objects in the SDSS (WR101) as a class 2 object ( i.e. convincing WR feature but not obvious after continuum subtraction). Our third object, Mrk 930, presents a quite disturbed morphology, characterized by an ellongated shape and whose main burst of star formation is in the south, but other several fainter knots are also well visible in the north part. Fig. acs shows an ACS-HST This image has been taken from the Multimission Archive at STScI (MAST) in the webpage http://archive.stsci.edu/ image taken with the F140LP filter centered in the ultraviolet at a wavelength 1400 , where it is remarkable the presence of these super star clusters along the galaxy in a complex structure. Guseva et al. (2000) analyzed both the blue and red WR bumps detected by Izotov Thuan (1998) for this galaxy and they estimated around 2000 WR stars, although this is very uncertain due to the noise of the spectrum. A very noisy hint of this WR feature is also pointed out by Adamo et al. (2011) using long-slit spectroscopy of the brightest knot of this galaxy. The chemical analysis carried out by Izotov Thuan (1998) for this galaxy revealed a low metallicity [ 12+log(O/H) = 8.07 ] and a value of the N/O ratio slightly enhanced [ log(N/O) = -1.42 ] . The study of these three galaxies by means of IFS constitutes an ideal test to find out to what extent the relative enrichment of N found in some BCDs is related to the presence of WR stars in their knots of star formation.

We find that: 1) for HS 0128+2832, only the N/O ratio as derived from the direct method is uniform; 2) for HS 0837+4717, all properties are uniform; and 3) for Mrk 930, the uniformity is found for all parameters, except for electron density and reddening. The rotation curve together with the Ha map and UV images, reveal a perturbed morphology and possible interacting processes in Mrk930.
