All three are low-metallicity (12 Z _ Z 49 Z _ ; see below) dwarf galaxies located within 2 Mpc. These systems were chosen for their proximity (maximizing spatial resolution) and for their ongoing star formation. Further, the metallicities of the galaxies in this sample bracket the aforementioned empirical PAH transition metallicity. While our sample size is modest, the spatially resolved data for each system provide new insights into the variations of PAH intensities in metal-poor environments.

sources.summary: The paper gives IC 5152 HST TRGB distance context, identifies its brightest H II region, and reports a new direct oxygen abundance below older measurements.

quotes.quote: With resolved photometry from the HST, have recently derived a TRGB distance of 2.07 Mpc. The brightest H II region is located at the northeast corner of the dwarf and is labelled H II region A by ; this H II region is believed to be the same one measured by .

We obtained an \ measurement of H II region A and the resulting oxygen abundance is 12 + log(O/H) = 7.92 0.07 . Our value of the oxygen abundance is about one-tenth of the solar value, which is consistent with the stellar metallicity inferred by , but 2.7 times lower than the values reported by talent80 and webster83 .

sources.summary: IC 5152 is discussed as a southern dwarf with nearly constant inclination apart from an outlier, high non-circular-motion contribution, and an inner slope close to CDM predictions.

quotes.quote: IC 5152 Except for one outlier in the inner 1 kpc, the inclination of IC 5152 is roughly constant with radius (see Fig.
In comparison to the other galaxies of our sample, IC 5152 shows the highest contribution of non-circular motions to the maximum rotation velocity.
Only IC 5152, which has an inner slope of =-0.82 , is close to CDM predictions.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5152 as a well-resolved LVHIS dwarf with segmented star-forming regions whose radio continuum generally matches its infrared and emission-line morphology.

quotes.quote: LVHIS 078: IC 5152 IC 5152 is another example of well resolved dwarf galaxy in LVHIS sample. In Figure fig:ic5152 we show the highly segmented star forming regions in this galaxy. Different from NGC 5408, the radio continuum emission is generally consistent with 24 \ and \ emission.

gmw_00001438.name: A1413-arc2.1a
gmw_00001438.overview: A1413-arc2.1a is a galaxy that is likely interacting with its smaller companion, A1413-arc2.1b [arXiv:1503.07873]. It exhibits a possible double-peaked velocity dispersion profile and marginal rotation-to-dispersion support, which may indicate a late-stage merger [arXiv:1503.07873].

sources.summary: A1413-arc2.1a is described as the larger member of an interacting pair, with possible double-peaked velocity dispersion and marginal rotation-to-dispersion support.

quotes.quote: A1413-arc2.1b is probably interacting with the larger A1413-arc2.1a, from which it is offset by only 1000 km s ^ 1 , and the morphology of RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2 with its extended tail suggests that it also may be an interacting system.

In the remaining four galaxies - MACS0744-system3, A1413-arc2.1a, MS1621-system1 and A1689-arc1.2 - there are suggestions of a double peak in the velocity dispersion profile, which may also be indicative of a late-stage merger.

Three of the galaxies that appear to be interactions or mergers - A1413-arc2.1a, A1413-arc2.1b and RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2 - all have marginal v_ rot / within 1 of the boundary between rotation-dominated and dispersion-dominated systems.

sources.summary: A1413-arc2.1b lacks a central peak, is probably interacting with A1413-arc2.1a, is undetected in HST imaging, and has marginal dynamical classification.

quotes.quote: In two cases, A1413-arc2.1b and RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2, there is no central peak.

A1413-arc2.1b is probably interacting with the larger A1413-arc2.1a, from which it is offset by only 1000 km s ^ 1 , and the morphology of RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2 with its extended tail suggests that it also may be an interacting system.

When calculating stellar masses, we omit the arcs in three clusters that do not have imaging in at least two bands either side of the 4000 ' break (MS1621, RXJ1720 and Abell 2895), as well as the galaxy A1413-arc2.1b which is undetected in the HST imaging.

gmw_00001439.name: RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2
gmw_00001439.overview: RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2 is a high-redshift galaxy that lacks a central peak [arXiv:1503.07873]. Its extended-tail morphology suggests it may be an interacting system, and its dynamical ratio is marginal between rotation-dominated and dispersion-dominated [arXiv:1503.07873].

sources.summary: RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2 lacks a central peak; its extended-tail morphology suggests interaction, and its dynamical ratio is marginal between rotation and dispersion dominance.

quotes.quote: In two cases, A1413-arc2.1b and RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2, there is no central peak.

A1413-arc2.1b is probably interacting with the larger A1413-arc2.1a, from which it is offset by only 1000 km s ^ 1 , and the morphology of RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2 with its extended tail suggests that it also may be an interacting system.

Three of the galaxies that appear to be interactions or mergers - A1413-arc2.1a, A1413-arc2.1b and RXJ1720-arc1.1+1.2 - all have marginal v_ rot / within 1 of the boundary between rotation-dominated and dispersion-dominated systems.

gmw_00001440.name: A1835-arc7.1
gmw_00001440.overview: A1835-arc7.1 is a high-redshift galaxy subject to gravitational lensing [arXiv:1503.07873]. It features a strong magnification gradient near the critical line and exhibits a low ratio of rotation velocity to dispersion [arXiv:1503.07873].

sources.summary: A1835-arc7.1 has a strong magnification gradient but precise critical-line constraints; the paper classifies it as undetermined and finds low vrot over dispersion.

quotes.quote: In the cases of A1835-arc7.1 and A2895-arc2.2, the high magnification gradient in the vicinity of the critical line causes this method to omit data from the image plane.

The smallest error, 5 , is found in A1835-arc7.1, which also has a strong magnification gradient, but in this case the two images straddling the critical line provide good constraints on the precise position of the line.
Of our combined sample, only A1835-arc7.1 falls beneath this cut, with v_ rot / = 0.2 0.2 .

gmw_00001441.name: SDSS J1547
gmw_00001441.overview: SDSS J1547 is a blue active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2108.09714]. Its broad Balmer emission lines exhibit differing profiles, and its long-term light curve shows a periodicity of approximately 2159 days, suggesting the presence of a central binary black hole system [arXiv:2108.09714].

sources.summary: SDSS J1547 is presented as a blue broad-line AGN whose differing broad Balmer profiles and long-term periodicity support an expected central binary black hole system.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage In this manuscript, an interesting blue Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN) SDSS J154751.94+025550 (=SDSS J1547) is reported with very different line profiles of broad Balmer emission lines: double-peaked broad H but single-peaked broad H .
The very different line profiles of the broad Balmer emission lines can be well explained by different physical conditions to two central BLRs in a central BBH system in SDSS J1547.

Furthermore, the long-term light curve from CSS can be well described by a sinusoidal function with a periodicity about 2159days, providing further evidence to support the expected central BBH system in SDSS J1547.

gmw_00001442.name: NGC 3621
gmw_00001442.overview: NGC 3621 is a bulgeless, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1807.08765] located approximately 7 million parsecs away [arXiv:2012.00757]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1807.08765] and exhibits a warped outer disk with asymmetric, disturbed kinematics [arXiv:1810.05467, arXiv:1406.3354].

sources.summary: The paper uses MeerKAT data to study NGC 3621, deriving an extended rotation curve, systemic velocity, inclination parameters, and noting asymmetric, disturbed kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 Unlike NGC 7424, the galaxy NGC 3621 has been extensively observed in e.g., Barnes2001,Koribalski2004,DeBlok2008,Koribalski2018 and appears as one of the brightest sources in the southern hemisphere, at a distance of 6.6 Mpc Freedman2001 . Rotation curve The process used to obtain the rotation curve of NGC 3621 is similar to that described in sec:n7424-rotcur . The systemic velocity obtained for the galaxy is 730.1 1.4 , consistent with the measurements of 730 2 Koribalski2004 and the value of 728.8 1.5 , derived from the global profile with the task mbspect . The Rotcur fit of the galaxy revealed large scatters in the galaxy's inclination and position angle at radii larger than 19.2 kpc ( fig:n3621-tiltedring ), probably a result of the galaxy's complex kinematics. The average values of these parameters, derived in the inner regions of the galaxy, are i=59.9 3.2 and =345.2 6.7 .

The approaching and receding curves of the galaxy are very different in shape, with the receding (northern) side being more disturbed. This asymmetry of the galaxy is likely caused by the presence of a cloud-like structure located in the receding side of the galaxy (as is seen in the velocity field in fig:n3621-maps ). The PV diagram in fig:n3621-pv suggests the presence of a warp in the line-of-sight of NGC 3621, that is more pronounced in the receding side of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3621 as Peekaboo’s nearest massive spiral neighbor, giving its distance, virial radius, and role in a loose low-density association around the dwarf.

quotes.quote: The closest neighbours of HIPASS J1131--31 are the dwarf irregular galaxy HIPASS J1132--32 ( \ = 699 ) and the large spiral galaxy NGC 3621 (HIPASS J1118--32; 730 ) at projected distances of 80 and 182 arcmin, respectively Koribalski2004,Meyer2004 . ATCA \ images of both galaxies were also obtained as part of the LVHIS project Koribalski2018 . The distances of NGC 3621 ( 6.70 0.37 Mpc) and HIPASS J1132--32 ( 5.47 0.15 Mpc) were obtained by Ferrarese2000 via a supernova luminosity and Anand2021 via a tip of the red giant branch magnitude, respectively. Based on the likely association of HIPASS J1131--31 with NGC 3621 we initially assigned the same distance, i.e.

Due to the large projected separation of 370 kpc, Peekaboo cannot confidently be considered a true satellite of NGC 3621, although their radial distances are almost the same within uncertainties in D_ TRGB . The other five dwarf galaxies are also located far beyond the virial radius of NGC 3621, which is about 190 kpc. At the same time, the probability of an accidental grouping of these seven galaxies is extremely small. The radial velocity dispersion for the entities around NGC 3621 is only 56 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3621 as having no torus evidence and possibly an X-ray-binary interpretation, while its NuSTAR spectrum has an odd high-energy feature but insufficient S/N to confirm reflection.

quotes.quote: We find that 12 objects do not present any signs of reflection (namely UGC 5101, NGC 3628, NGC 3718, M 106, NGC 7331, NGC 612, 2MXJ 1105+5856, NGC 3621, NGC 3786, M 88, NGC 5252 and 2MXJ 2325-3826).

Similarly, NGC 3621 has no evidence of the torus, according to . Moreover, suggest that this object might be a X-ray binary due to its low luminosity. However, we find in the spectrum an odd feature above 20 keV.

This object seems to need an obscured power-law, although when we tested this possibility, the statistics were no good. Indeed, in the fourth top panel in Fig. fig:candidates , the spectrum above 20 keV presents several upper limits, which might place a role in the contraining of the obscuration. The poor S/N in this case might prevent us from finding a reflection component.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3621 as the nearby spiral host of SN 2024ggi, using its Cepheid distance, recession velocity, and absorption features to characterize the supernova environment.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution spectroscopic observations of the nearby core-collapse (CC) supernova (SN) 2024ggi, a Type II SN that ocurred in the galaxy NGC 3621, at a distance of 7.11 Mpc ( z 0.002435 ). These observations represent the earliest high-resolution spectroscopy of a CCSN ever made.

SN 2024ggi was first reported to the Transient Name Server https://www.wis-tns.org/object/2024ggi , made on 2024-04-18), in one of the south-east spiral arms of the galaxy NGC 3621, at a projected distance of 3.87 kpc from the galaxy center (see Figure fig:ngc3621 ). The Cepheid distance to NGC 3621 is 7.11 Mpc 2006ApJS..165..108S , reported at the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/byname?objname=NGC+3621 .

The spectra presented here were corrected by the heliocentric recession velocity of NGC 3621 of 730 \ km \ s ^ 1 z 0.002435 2004AJ....128...16K . In Appendix app:na , Figure fig:NaI shows the detail of the Na I D and Ca II H K absorption lines detected in the spectra. Using these absorption features, we measure a total Galactic extinction in the line of sight of SN 2024ggi of E(B-V)_ Gal = 0.12 0.02 mag and a host galaxy extinction of E(B-V)_ host = 0.036 0.007 mag, resulting in a total extinction of E(B-V)_ total = 0.16 0.02 mag (see our methodology in Appendix app:na ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3621 as a distant bulgeless spiral with a Seyfert 2 nucleus whose nuclear stellar population formed early and was rapidly enriched.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 is a bulgeless field spiral galaxy (SA(s)d) in the constellation Hydra at a distance 6.7 Mpc and an inclination angle of 25^ . NGC 3621 hosts an active galactic nucleus AGN satyapal+2007,gliozzi+2009 and it is classified as a Seyfert 2, fueled by a SMBH with a mass of 3 10^6 barth+2009 . carson+2015 note that the NSC is more extended in the UV than in the IR, at a difference with the other two spirals, which could either mean that young stars are situated in the outskirts of the cluster, or that the UV emission from the central AGN is stronger at larger radii due to dust obscuration in the inner regions.

Nevertheless, the composite stellar population fit suggests that the NSC of NGC 3621 built most of its mass in the early Universe and continued forming stars until several hundred Myr ago, similarly to the other late-type NSCs in this sample. NGC 3621's NSC likely experienced an extremely rapid enrichment because even the oldest SSPs contributing to the fit appear to be very metal rich. We note that boardman+2017 analysed a sample of early-type galaxies using the same code (but with different models) and found a similar effect that most of the mass is situated in an old, metal rich population. This is due to the very high M / L ratios of these SSP models (see Figure fig:ml_templates ), which leads to significant mass weights even if only a very small fraction of the total light is attributed to these templates. The spectrum has very strong emission lines and although it is not excluded that there has been some recent star formation, NGC 3621 is classified as a Seyfert 2 type galaxy with an X-ray source at its centre barth+2009 , which is the main source of emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3621 as an ordinary late-type spiral with unexpectedly many massive clusters, mostly projected on its near side, possibly reflecting real asymmetric young-cluster and HII-region excess.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 This galaxy is at first sight a quite ordinary late-type spiral, and has not received much attention. It was observed with the HST by Rawson et. al. (1997) as part of the Extragalactic Distance Scale Key Project , and cepheids were discovered and used to derive a distance modulus of 29.1. Our data show that NGC 3621 contains a surprisingly high number of massive star clusters. The galaxy is rather inclined ( i=51^ , Rawson et. al. 1997), and nearly all the clusters are seen projected on the near side of the galaxy, so a number of clusters on the far side may be hidden from our view. Ryder Dopita (1993) noted a lack of HII regions on the far side of the galaxy, and pointed out that there is also a quite prominent spiral arm on the near side that doesn't appear to have a counterpart on the far side.
So it remains possible that the excess of young clusters and HII regions on the near side is real.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 3621, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 NGC 3621 (Fig. fig:ngc-3621 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] The 3.6 m IRAC image of NGC 3621 is dominated by a flocculent spiral structure, which contains a central point source. The prominent star forming region in the south-west is connected to the center of the galaxy by a spiral arm which is clearly visible in the total \ map and the IRAC image. While this star forming region has a counterpart in the radio continuum, the IRAC nuclear point source has not. We use the coordinates of the nuclear point source in the IRAC image ( = 4 2, = 1 1) as an initial estimate for a \ fit with all parameters left free.

sources.summary: The paper reports excellent MOND fits for NGC 3621 with well-constrained parameters, but replacing the best-fit stellar scale-height with a razor-thin disk badly fits the central rotation curve.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 The ^2 minimum for NGC 3621 is obvious in the contour plots of Fig fig:com3621 and puts strong constraints on all three parameters. The fits, whose parameters are given in table tab:par , at the top left hand panel show the best fit (solid line) and another fit with slightly larger distance (dashed line), more in keeping with the measured distance. Both are excellent fits. The dotted line shows the best fit model when the best fit stellar scale-height is replaced with a razor thin stellar disk and is a very poor fit to the central rotation curve. The best fit parameters are all within acceptable bounds and this is an excellent fit for MOND.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3621 as a luminous late-type spiral whose inner rotation curve is fitted, while outer data trend flatter than the model.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621. At a distance D = 6.6 \ Mpc , NGC 3621 is a late type spiral with a regular HI distribution. Although it is quite luminous with M_B = -20.06 , no other determination of the rotation curve was available before the THINGS one which have also determine the position angle and inclination as 345^o and 65^o , respectively.

NGC 3621. Contrary to NGC 2841, this galaxy is well fitted by the modified potential. However, a deeper look at the data shows that, while the inner regions are perfectly matched, the agreement between the theoretical curve and the data, although overall good, is getting worse as R increases. Indeed, while the data suggests a flat behaviour, the best fit circular velocity starts increasing linearly as a consequence of the ^2 term in the HL potential leading to v_c(R) R for R/R_d >> _A .

sources.summary: NGC 3621 is described as a regular disk with outer kinematic irregularities, a strong warp, no nearby companion, and outer-disk motions consistent with ram pressure on a warped gas disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 exhibits a similar regular gas and stellar distribution as NGC 6946 but with larger kinematic irregularities. Deep observations with GALEX in the UV-light show evidence for a strong warp in the outer disk Gil07 . However, NGC 3621 has no nearby companion and shows no sign of tidal interaction. In particular at both ends of the major axis the gas extends beyond the regularly rotating disk with strong irregular velocities that are not necessarily associated with the main rotation of the disk for a large-scale image see deB08 .
Our results demonstrate that the kinematics of the outer disk of NGC 3621 can be well-characterised by ram pressure acting on a moderately warped gas disk.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 3621 as the most distant sample galaxy, with a massive nuclear cluster, mid-IR and X-ray evidence for an AGN, and successful host-plus-cluster fitting.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 is the most distant galaxy in our sample, at 7.3 Mpc saha2006 . The NC has a velocity dispersion of 43 , one of the largest ever measured for any NC in a late-type spiral galaxy, which sets a fairly high upper limit on the mass of any central BH of 3 10^ 6 \ barth2009 . satyapal2007 discovered a faint AGN in the NC from mid-IR [ Ne 5 ] line emission and based on estimates of its bolometric luminosity, set a lower limit on the mass of the central BH of 4 10^ 3 . gliozzi2009 found further evidence for an AGN in NGC 3621 with the detection of a weak X-ray point source coincident with the nucleus. We successfully fit an exponential profile plus a flat sky level to the host galaxy in every band, and we fit a single \ component to the NC in all seven bands.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3621 as the unbarred THINGS velocity-map test case for direct shear mapping, fitting its H I map and masking the regular velocity field.

quotes.quote: Two objects were fitted from THINGS; NGC 3621 with morphological type SA(s)d, and NGC 5236 (M83) with morphological type SAB(s). The morphology of these two choices was of particular relevance, as it allowed for the effect of bars on the accuracy of the fit to be investigated. The HI emission and velocity map of NGC 3621 are shown in Figures fig:NGC3621_0 and fig:NGC3621_1 , and those of NGC5236 are shown in Figures fig:NGC5236_0 and fig:NGC5236_1 . In both cases the mask radius is 12 pixels and is shown by the black line. Two velocity maps were fitted from THINGS; one barred (NGC 5236) and one not barred (NGC 3621).
This is roughly 1/3 of the radio extent for both NGC 3621 and NGC 5236.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3621 is already fit reasonably by MOND, but a modest external field improves chi-squared and overlaps with acceleration from nearby ESO 377-G 003.

quotes.quote: (7) NGC 3621 : The MOND rotation curve without the EFE agrees with the observed curve, but an external field of about a_e=0.125^ +0.090 _ 0.125 a_0 can improve the quality of the fit such that the ^2 decreases from 1.7 to 1.5. ESO 377-G 003 with a luminosity of L_V=3.9 10^ 8 L_ is the nearest gravitational source to NGC 3621 and is located at ( 11^h03^m55.2^s, -34^d21^m30^s ). With an angular distance of 2.52 degree, ESO 377-G 003 can produce a gravitational acceleration of about a_e=5.9 10^ 4 a_0 at the location of NGC 3621. Due to the large uncertainty in the distance of NGC 3621, the minimum and maximum distance to ESO 377-G 003 are 386 kpc and 4.8 Mpc, respectively n3521-jj, n3198-dm which leads to a gravitational acceleration between a_e=4.0 10^ 3 a_0 and 3.2 10^ 4 a_0 . Therefore, the gravitational acceleration imposed by the nearby source has an overlap with the external field obtained from the MOND fit rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper measures two deeper PHANGS-HST TRGB fields in NGC 3621's outer disk and adopts their averaged distance of 7.06 Mpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 is an isolated spiral galaxy and member of the Leo Spur, a filamentary structure whose members have, on average, relatively high negative peculiar velocities towards us. This observed effect is due to the expansion of the Local Void pushing galaxies residing in the Local Sheet (including the Milky Way) down towards the Leo Spur, which then imprints the negative peculiar velocities onto members of the Leo Spur 2015ApJ...805..144K,2019ApJ...880...52A . The existing CMDs/TRGB catalog measurement for NGC 3621 of 6.65 0.18 Mpc was obtained from data taken by GO-9492 (PI: F. Bresolin), with observations in the F555W and F814W bands. The usage of the F555W is less optimal, due to many of the higher metallicity (redder) RGB stars being pushed off to the right of the observable CMD. PHANGS-HST provides two parallel fields, which are both deeper than other existing data. Both fields cover part of the outer disk, which we isolate to reduce contamination from Pop I stars. From the two fields (only the outermost one is shown in Figure fig:existingCMD ), we find m_ TRGB = 25.31 0.03 and m_ TRGB = 25.27 0.02 mag, which result in D = 7.10 0.30 Mpc and D = 7.02 0.28 Mpc, respectively. The results from these two fields agree very well, and we take an average of these two measurements with a conservative error estimate ( D = 7.06 Mpc 0.28 Mpc) as the adopted distance to this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS low-surface-brightness galaxy in Group A, finding fitted BDM cores with r_s exceeding r_c, typical transition energy near 0.11 eV, and BDM fits preferred over other cored profiles.

quotes.quote: We obtain seven galaxies (DDO 154 (A1), NGC 2841 (A2), NGC 3031 (A3), NGC 3621 (A4), NGC 4736 (A5), NGC 6946 (A6), and NGC 7793 (A7)) (we call Group A) with fitted values r_s > r_c 0 that are shown in Table tab:onlydm_bdm . For these galaxies we obtain average values for: the core r_c 40 pc , r_s 5 kpc , and the energy E_c 0.11 \ eV for the min.disk analysis, that are typical values for a galaxy, except for DDO 154 which has a larger core, r_c = 1.35 kpc . We notice that for all these galaxies the ^2 in BDM is smaller than NFW's but the extra parameter ( r_c ) in BDM makes the reduced ^2_ red equivalent for both profiles. When comparing the BDM profile with the other two cored profiles, Burkert and ISO (Ref. ) we conclude that BDM is still better.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3621 as the lone strong metallicity-gradient outlier versus Moustakas et al., attributing the difference to larger radial coverage and calibration-dependent gradients.

quotes.quote: We find relatively consistent results, with only one strong outlier (NGC3621). The average ^2 1.7 , which drops to ^2 1.0 if the outlier is discarded. Our gradient for NGC3621 is likely different since we have data out to larger radii (though there is no evidence for a change in the gradient between the central and outer regions). Our gradient for NGC3621 is also more similar to the gradient for other calibrations ( Moustakas2010 find a big difference between their KK04 and PT05 calibration for NGC3621).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3621 is well fit by a simple axisymmetric disk-flow model, though anomalous outer gas was masked from the analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 This galaxy is well fitted by a simple axisymmetric disc flow model, as shown in Figure  fig.N3621 . The rotation curve agrees well with that given by dB08, although our uncertainties are smaller. Note that we masked away emission from gas outside the elliptical region shown, which appeared to have velocities that did not fit so well with our flat axisymmetric model. It is possible that a more sophisticated masking algorithm, that excludes gas having anomalous velocities, would allow our model to be extended to larger radii.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3621 as a late-type spiral with nearly constant inclination, small inner radial velocity, and large asymmetric structures in the outer velocity field.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 NGC 3621 is a late-type spiral galaxy . The TRM detects that the inclination angle is almost constant but with some small fluctuations; the P.A. increases by 10^ going from the inner disk to the outermost regions of the galaxy, i.e., for R>500'' (see Fig. NGC3621 ). As a result, for R<500'' we find v_t(R) v_c(R) and the radial velocity, with an amplitude of v_r<10 km s ^ 1 , agrees well with the measurements by .

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra evidence for a weak nuclear X-ray source in the bulgeless disk galaxy NGC 3621, supporting a heavily absorbed AGN with a small, rapidly accreting SMBH.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of a weak X-ray point source coincident with the nucleus of the bulgeless disk galaxy NGC 3621, recently discovered by \ to display high ionization mid-infrared lines typically associated with AGN. These \ observations provide confirmation for the presence of an AGN in this galaxy, adding to the growing evidence that black holes do form and grow in isolated bulgeless disk galaxies. Although the low signal-to-noise ratio of the X-ray spectrum prevents us from carrying out a detailed spectral analysis of the nuclear source, the X-ray results, combined with the IR and optical spectroscopic results, suggests that NGC 3621 harbors a heavily absorbed AGN, with a supermassive black hole (SMBH) of relatively small mass accreting at a high rate.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3621 as a well-fitted rotation-curve example, with the Burkert model closely tracking the data and leaving small, trend-free residuals.

quotes.quote: The left panel in Fig. fig: rcex shows the rotation curve data for NGC 3621 and the superimposed best fit model ( ^2 = 0.75 ). This is a typical case where one could rely on the expectation based on the reduced ^2 value are in accordance with our definition of well fitted system . The best fit model closely tracks the data and there is no trend of the very small residuals with the distance from the galaxy centre.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3621 as a pure disk THINGS galaxy with multiple spiral arms and a good MOND rotation-curve fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621 is representative of a pure-disc galaxy, with multiple spiral arms.
NGC 3621 is a galaxy included in the H i Nearby Galaxy Survey (THINGS) described in wal08 , which consists of high-resolution H i observations of a sample of 34 nearby galaxies.
We see that the fit is good for NGC 3621 and NGC 5949, acceptable for NGC 6689, but poor for NGC 4605, 5963 and 6503.

sources.summary: The catalog flags NGC 3621 as an AGN candidate because its point-like nuclear position is unreliable in 2MASS but matches a NED point-source position and an X-ray source.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of NGC 3621 looks like a point-like source on 2MASS images, and the position from the 2MASS extended source catalog may not be reliable. The nuclear position from NED is consistent with that in the 2MASS point source catalog, and coincident with an X-ray source. This object is then classified as an AGN candidate.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3621 as an exception whose steep H I profile likely marks a transition from its main disk to an extended stream or tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 3621, on the other hand, has an extended H i distribution. The integrated H i intensity map shows a tail or stream of gas extending along the major axis, which could be the result of past interactions or signatures of gas accretion. The break around 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 for this galaxy therefore corresponds to the transition between the main disk of NGC 3621 and the stream or tail (See also Figure fig:sectors ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3621 outside the bar and arm subpopulation masks and reports high molecular-cloud virial parameters, including milder but significant inner-radius increases.

quotes.quote: GMCs from galaxies without arms or bars (NGC 3521, NGC 3621, NGC 4826) are not included in any of these sub-populations of GMCs.
NGC 4826 represents a unique starburst case and shows high _ vir , NGC 2903 and to a lesser extent NGC 3621, NGC 3627, NGC 6300 also show regions with high _ vir .
NGC 1637, NGC 2903, NGC 3627, NGC 3621, and NGC 5643 show milder but still significant increased _ vir in the inner 0.5 1 R_e and all of these are barred systems except NGC 5643.

gmw_00001443.name: NGC 925
gmw_00001443.overview: NGC 925 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0810.2116, arXiv:2303.00763] featuring a clumpy, low-surface-brightness disk [arXiv:0810.2116, arXiv:astro-ph/0610114] and loosely wound, lopsided spiral arms [arXiv:2404.09422, arXiv:2306.12902]. The galaxy exhibits a southern neutral hydrogen tail [arXiv:0803.0109, arXiv:1909.04048] and a stripped-gas stream likely produced by a minor merger [arXiv:1012.0816].

sources.summary: HALOGAS finds NGC 925 to be a moderately inclined spiral with extended disturbed H I, clumpy inner gas, an outer stripped-gas stream, and anomalous gas suggestive of minor-merger accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 is a moderately inclined spiral. We detect bright diffuse continuum emission, with enhancements corresponding to the locations of the most prominent features in the optical spiral arms. NGC 925 has been observed previously in the \ line, notably by pisano_etal_1998 and as part of THINGS The \ Nearby Galaxy Survey; walter_etal_2008 . Neither of those studies detected \ at such large galactocentric radii as revealed in Figure figure:n0925 .

The inner disk within the optical body has an unsmooth appearance, with clumps and holes. Many of the \ overdensities correspond to the locations of bright knots of optical light in the spiral arms. The appearance of the outer disk of NGC 925 is that of a complicated stream of gas stripped during a minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 925 with THINGS rotation data, finding its stellar component dominates inside 5, dark matter dominates farther out, molecular gas is minor, and the NFW fit gives unrealistic halo parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 In Figure fig:N0925-MMs we plot the best fitting models using the THINGS rotation curve. The stellar mass distribution comprises only a single component, and is the dominant component within 5 . At greater radii the dark matter distribution becomes more important.

The value 2g=14.3 is much larger than the Milky Way value. The predicted molecular gas rotation curves using and 2g do not exceed a maximum of 10 at 3 . The 2g curve is slightly higher than the curve, but not sufficiently to change the predicted molecular gas rotation curve due to the small amount of molecular gas detected.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 925 as a representative sample galaxy, showing its MOM2 map varies across the disk and deriving a radial MOM2 profile from annular averaging.

quotes.quote: In Fig. sampl_mom2 , we show an example MOM2 map of a representative galaxy, NGC 925, from our sample. As can be seen from the figure, MOM2 in NGC 925 shows considerable variation across the galaxy. To capture this variation as a function of radius and to obtain a MOM2 profile, we construct a number of annuli as a function of the radius with a width of 2 times the spatial resolution. In Fig. sampl_mom2 , we show one such annulus with red ellipses at a radius of 10 kpc. We generate a MOM2 profile by averaging all the MOM2 values within an annulus as a function of the radius.
In Fig. sig_opt , the first panel shows the resulting MOM2 profile (solid red circles with error bars) for NGC 925.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 925 as a weakly interacting face-on galaxy whose lopsided arms and trailing diffuse HI may reflect tidal interaction and shocks.

quotes.quote: The weakly interacting, relatively face-on galaxies (NGC 628 and NGC 925) have neighbors, and show lopsided spiral arms, but neither nor optical arms link to their neighbors. In these two systems, the diffuse tends to follow the trailing side of the more open arm.

Secondly, tidal interactions drive spiral arms in galaxies, and produce the diffuse through shocks of gas running into arms. Possible candidates in support of this scenario include NGC 628 and NGC 925. They have lopsided spiral arms, and the diffuse tends to follow the trailing side of the more open arm which is likely rotating faster toward large radius than the other arm. Spiral arms in NGC 628 and NGC 925 have a pattern speed of 41.8 and 7.7 kpc ^ 1 respectively derived in the literature MartinezGarcia14, Elmegreen98 .

sources.summary: The review says NGC 925 has a southern H I tail whose gas co-rotates with the galaxy and whose optical morphology is strongly asymmetric.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 has a tail of \ extending to the South. The tail contains about 3 10^8 of , or 5 of the total \ mass of NGC 925. Also in NGC 925 the kinematics of the gas in the tail is not too different from that of the gas in the galaxy, i.e. \ it appears to co-rotate at similar projected velocities. The winding up and disappearance of this structure may take about 0.5-1.0 10^9 yr. NGC 925 is different from e.g. \ NGC 210 in that in the optical the galaxy is fraught with asymmetries, both morphological and kinematical, suggesting that the accreted object may have been relatively more massive than in NGC 210.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 925, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 NGC 925 (Fig. fig:ngc-925 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 925 is a barred late-type spiral galaxy whose \ disk looks rather clumpy. The 3.6 m IRAC image shows a weak central bar and two spiral arms in the outer region. NGC 925 lacks a clear center in the IRAC image as well as in the radio continuum. We used ellfit at varying isophote levels to derive a central position from the IRAC image.

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS galaxy in Group B, where BDM fits outperform NFW, imply r_c roughly comparable to r_s, and favor a strong dark-matter core.

quotes.quote: In the second case, Group B, (IC 2574 (B1), NGC 2366 (B2), NGC 2903 (B3), NGC 2976 (B4), NGC 3198 (B5), NGC 3521 (B6), and NGC 925 (B7)), we get a fitted value of r_c r_s and BDM profile fits better than NFW. In fact, the NFW profile in some cases over-predicts the velocity in the inner parts of the galaxy or does fit with unphysical values for r_s > O (10^4) or _0 < O (10^3) M_ /kpc^ 3 , see Table tab:onlydm_bdm and tab:onlydm_nfw . The BDM profile gives r_c r_s for all stellar mass models, having an average r_c value of 6 and 5 kpc for Min.Disk and Min.Disk+Gas, respectively. Since r_c r_s , implying that our BDM profile is of the type (r_c + )^ 3 and hence having a strong core behavior. For this set of galaxies the Burkert and ISO profiles, as well as the BDM, fit much better than NFW, indicating that a core is needed.

sources.summary: The paper treats 0925 as a THINGS disc galaxy with an increasing, insufficiently extended rotation curve; its NFW model ignores the disc and Burkert fitting is problematic.

quotes.quote: In the Model B we allowed them to vary in a narrow range between 0.45 obtained using Tully-Fisher relation in a good agreement with the models of stellar population by McGaugh2015 and 0.6 Meidtetal2014 solar units at 3.6 m For 0925 and 7331 absented in S4G with photometrical data in V-band we used the ranges of M/L_V : 0.54-0.88 and 1.62-1.9 , correspondingly, according to their total (B-V)_0 colors and model relations from for Chabrier IMF and for scaled Salpeter IMF.
For example, for 0925 the Burkert halo density profile gives the bad fit to the rotation curve according to CardoneDelPopolo2012 .

Only two galaxies of our sample ( 0925 and 2976) do not have a plateau on the rotation curves, their rotational amplitudes are lower than 120 km/s. The rotation curves of 0925 and 2976 are not extended enough (the outermost radius is <R_ opt in contrast with other galaxies of the sample which have more extending rotation curves). In both cases, the Models A for NFW halo ignore the disc presence (in the case of 0925 the disc mass-to-light ratio also equals to zero inside the entire 1 contour of ^2 map, for 2976 there are solutions with M/L 0.04 which is quite low).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 925 has a simple velocity map reasonably fitted by an inclined axisymmetric disk, with few residuals and no strong non-axisymmetric signal.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 This dwarf galaxy has a simple velocity map that is reasonably well fitted as an axisymmetric, inclined disc. The black points with error bars in the third row right panel of Figure  fig.N925 indicate our fitted rotation curve and its uncertainties, which are in reasonable agreement with the same quantities estimated by dB08, marked by the red line and grey shading. The kink in the red line occurs at the radius where their \ analysis finds a discontinuity in the disc inclination, which we do not reproduce in our flat disc fit. The residuals map has few large values but does not reveal any tell-tale coherent features that would suggest a non-axisymmetric potential. We conclude that our axisymmetric model is a reasonable, albeit imperfect, fit to the data.

sources.summary: NGC 925 is described as a spiral with an extended gas distribution and XUV disk; one nearby detection may belong to its low surface-brightness disk and lacks an optical counterpart.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 is a spiral galaxy with an extended gas distribution and a XUV disk. Besides the target we find two more \ sources. One detection (source 2) is found relatively close to the target galaxy and could be part of the extended low surface brightness disk. The projected distance to the center of the target galaxy is 23 kpc and its systemic velocity only differs by 30 \ from NGC 925. We could find no optical counterpart and hence it is classified as a cloud candidate.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 925 as a late-type barred spiral host with full ALFALFA virial-area coverage, high local HI noise, one 2R200 neighbor, and no R200 satellites.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 NGC 925 is a late-type SBcd galaxy with a bright optical and H- bar. Its ALFALFA distance, 9.1 Mpc, agrees with the NED average result of 9.16 Mpc, which our stellar mass reference (Table table:analog_properties ) adopts. Its projected R_ 200 area of 1 is fully covered in the ALFALFA sky but is relatively close to the < 36 survey edge. NGC 925 has one of the largest rms noises among our host sample _ S21 = 4.01 mJy, lowering its local HI detectability from M_ HI 10^ 6.8 M_ (Figure fig:survey_sensitivity_with_count ) to about 10^ 7.0 M_ . We find UGC 1924, a relatively massive Scd galaxy, within its 2R_ 200 , and no satellites within its R_ 200 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 925 as a poorly fitted rotation-curve case where the model overestimates inner velocities, underestimates outer velocities, and misses flattening hints.

quotes.quote: Finally, the right panel of Fig. fig: rcex shows the data and the best fit model for NGC 925 which is a good example of why one must not only rely on ^2 value. Indeed, we get ^2 = 1.31 for this galaxy (smaller than for NGC 5055), but the best fit model fails to follow the data overestimating the circular velocity in the inner regions and underestimating it in the outer ones. Moreover, the best fit circular velocity profile keeps almost linearly increasing, whereas the data shows some hints of a flattening of the rotation curve. Motivated by these discrepancies, we therefore consider this fit an example case of a poorly fitted system .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 925 as a low-metallicity, low-attenuation member of the sample with shallow SFR gradients and among the highest k_i values at the late-type end.

quotes.quote: Galaxies such as NGC 925 and NGC 7793, which are the two lowest metallicity galaxies in the sample, have consistently low attenuations across the disk.
Conversely NGC 628, NGC 925, and NGC 7793 have much shallower radial gradients.

At the other end of the Hubble sequence of spiral galaxies, NGC 925 and NGC 7793 are respectively of SABd and SAd types. We see in Fig. fig:hist-k that they indeed have among the highest values of k_i .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 0925 has a southern HI tail probably from a faint interaction, but the model fits the remaining anomalous emission and fountain trend well.

quotes.quote: NGC 0925. This galaxy shows an extended \ tail towards the south, possibly caused by a recent interaction with a very faint system H11 Sancisi+08 . This tail seems to be efficiently masked by our procedure, as can be seen from the extent of the white regions in minor axis pv-slices, and the model appears to fit reasonably well the leftover anomalous emission.

Interestingly, NGC 925 follows very well the trend of the non-interacting systems despite the presence of a low-mass nearby perturber. We have used distinct symbols in Fig. fig:fountain_prediction to emphasise that the magnitude of perturbation expected for NGC 925 is small compared to that of NGC 672.

sources.summary: The review describes FPI observations of NGC 925 that produced a line-of-sight velocity field, rotation curve, spiral-wave gas perturbations, and density-wave parameters.

quotes.quote: The first interferograms of the NGC 925 galaxy were acquired directly on photographic plates (October 28, 1978), during observations in September 1979 a two-stage image RSA tube was mounted in front of the photo cassette. The acquired data were used to construct the \ line-of-sight velocity field. Despite averaging of observed velocities over rather large 14'' 14'' ``pixels'' it was possible not only to reconstruct the rotation curve of the galaxy, but also estimate perturbations in the gas motions induced by the spiral density wave Marcelin1982A&A...108..134M .

These data were later analyzed using the method of harmonic decomposition Sakhibov1989SvA....33..476S , which made it possible to determine the parameters of the two-arm density wave, understand the direction of radial gas motions in the arms, and measure the pattern speed of the spirals and bar (in NGC 925).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 925 as a barred spiral with loosely wound arms, varying inclination, outer radial velocities, and rough outer velocity anisotropies.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 NGC 925 has a distinct bar structure as well as loosely wound spiral arms (see panel (f) of Fig. NGC925 ) . The bar is elongated along the kinematic axis. The TRM analysis shows that the P.A. remains relatively constant for radii smaller than 500", a radius larger than R_ 25 320" , while the inclination angle varies by approximately 25 degrees between the inner and outer regions of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0925 as a late-type barred galaxy with bright patchy spiral arms, regions along the bar, consistent photometric and kinematic data, and central non-axisymmetric motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 0925: This late type SBcd galaxy has a bright optical and bar and two bright patchy spiral arms beginning at the ends of the bar. Many \ regions lie along the bar. Photometrical and kinematical data agree. The PV diagram shows non axisymmetric motions near the centre.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 925 among metal-poor H I-dominated systems with roughly balanced inflow and outflow, while noting external H I work found outflow rather than inflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403, 925, 3198, 4625 which comprise the group with relatively large and roughly equal and values in the diagonal of Fig.  figure15 are all characterised by relatively metal-poor H i dominated gas disks with much lower H _2 column densities and an almost flat radial profile of the total gas mass column densities ( _g 0.3  dex  R_ 25 ^ 1 ; see figures in schruba11 ).

For NGC 925, bigiel14 find no signs of inflows but instead significant outflows. With =0.67 and =0.54 , we detect in NGC 925 both outflows and inflows, but the inflow rate within 3 can still be consistent with zero (Fig.  figure7 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports long-term X-ray monitoring of spiral galaxy NGC 925, which lies 8.9 Mpc away and contains three known ultraluminous X-ray sources with variable behavior.

quotes.quote: Here we present the results of our monitoring campaign of the galaxy NGC 925, performed with the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory .

NGC 925 is a spiral galaxy (SAB(s)d) at a distance of 8.9 Mpc Tully2013 . It contains 3 known ULXs: NGC 925 ULX-1, NGC 925 ULX-2, whose data were extensively studied in Pintore2018b , and NGC 925 ULX-3, which is a transient ULX discovered by Earnshaw2020 .

sources.summary: For NGC 925, the paper reports a very high model parameter among low-mass galaxies, roughly constant mass accretion, and gas velocity dispersion below a previous estimate.

quotes.quote: For most of the low-mass galaxies ( M_* < 10^ 10 M _ ) where no EMPIRE data are available, the are considerably higher than those found for the high-mass galaxies, with values ranging from about 3000 for NGC 2976 to more than 7000 for NGC 925.
In addition, NGC 925 and NGC 7793 have roughly constant mass accretion rates.
Again, our gas velocity dispersion is significantly smaller than that of 2011AJ....141...24V . The same observation is true for NGC 925 and NGC 2976.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 925 as an asymmetric late-type galaxy with a low-surface-brightness disk, possible infall or encounter-driven asymmetry, and low radio-continuum star formation density.

quotes.quote: A low surface brightness disk is detected in this asymmetric late-type galaxy. The overall asymmetry may be attributed to infall and frequent gravitational encounters of the past few gigayears (Pisano et al. ). Yet the star formation density reflected by the radio continuum brightness is low.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 925 as an example of intergalactic neutral gas, describing a massive cloud attached by a streamer and noting its nearest neighboring galaxy is 200 kpc away.

quotes.quote: NGC 925 represents our final example of intergalactic \ which may be primordial or tidal in origin. NGC 925 has a 10 ^7 \ \ cloud attached to the main galaxy by a streamer pis98 . NGC 925 does reside in a group, but the nearest galaxy to NGC 925 is 200 kpc away.

gmw_00001444.name: dw1343+58
gmw_00001444.overview: The galaxy dw1343+58 is classified as a blue compact dwarf candidate [arXiv:1701.03681, arXiv:1911.09694]. It features an irregular high-surface-brightness central region surrounded by a low-surface-brightness envelope [arXiv:1701.03681], and is likely a member of the M101 group [arXiv:1701.03681, arXiv:1911.09694].

sources.summary: The paper classifies dw1343+58 as a blue compact dwarf candidate with an irregular high-surface-brightness center, low-surface-brightness envelope, and photometric distance consistent with M 101 membership.

quotes.quote: The membership status of dw1343+58 which we classified as Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) has to be assessed in a different way. Morphologically, the galaxy consists of a high-surface brightness (HSB) irregular central region and an elliptical low-surface brightness component around it, which is characteristic for BCDs e.g. 1988A&A...204...10K .

Now, the estimated P_ 25 size of our candidate dw1343+58 is 15 . Assuming P_ 25 =0.55 kpc would then put it at a distance of 7.8 Mpc, which is indeed in accord with M 101 group membership. We note that this candidate is listed as galaxy in the HYPERLEDA catalog 2003A&A...412...45P , but not as BCD nor M 101 dwarf.

sources.summary: The paper detects HI in dw1343+58, places it near the M101 group, and supports a blue compact dwarf classification from morphology, gas richness, and GALEX UV emission.

quotes.quote: The systemic velocity of the HI detection of dw1343+58, V_ sys =195 (Table table:detectiontable ), confirms that it is a nearby dwarf. Its projected separation from M101, d_ proj 580 kpc , and similar to M101 ( V_ M101 = 241 ) suggests that it is likely a member of the M101 group. The SDSS spectrum for dw1343+58, V_ SDSS = 165 33 , is consistent with the that we derive, although the SDSS pipeline mis-classified it as a star.

Given its optical morphology, M17 suggest that dw1343+58 is a blue compact dwarf (BCD). The relatively low gas richness of dw1343+58 compared to typical star-forming dwarfs e.g. huangalfalafa,Bradford2015MAIN , and the UV counterpart that we identify in our search of archival GALEX data provide further evidence in support of this classification BCDsgeneral,bcdgil .

gmw_00001445.name: SDSS J011341.11+010608.5
gmw_00001445.overview: SDSS J011341.11+010608.5 is a galaxy at z=0.281 that hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.07018, arXiv:1607.06481]. The system features a 10-kiloparsec bipolar outflow [arXiv:1709.07018], two symmetric [O III] superbubbles [arXiv:1607.06481], and a curved outer arc connected by a faint bridge [arXiv:1607.06481].

sources.summary: The paper finds J0113 hosts a powerful, variable, heavily obscured AGN with leaking ionizing radiation and a 10 kpc bipolar outflow consistent with AGN flickering.

quotes.quote: This paper focuses on the two X-ray brightest LABs at z 0.3 , SDSS J0113+0106 (J0113) and SDSS J1155 0147 (J1155), comparable in size and luminosity to `B1', one of the best-studied LABs at z 2. Our hard X-ray (3--30 keV) observations reveal powerful active galactic nuclei (AGN) with L_ 2-10 keV =(0.5 - 3) 10^ 44 . J0113 also faded by a factor of 5 between 2014 and 2016, emphasizing that variable AGN may cause apparent ionization deficits in LABs. Joint spectral analyses including data constrain column densities of N_ H =5.1^ +3.1 _ 3.3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 (J0113) and N_ H =6.0^ +1.4 _ 1.1 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 (J1155).

J0113 is likely buried in a torus with a narrow ionization cone, but ionizing radiation is also leaking in other directions as revealed by our Gemini/GMOS 3D spectroscopy. The latter shows a bipolar outflow over 10 kpc, with a peculiar velocity profile that is best explained by AGN flickering.

sources.summary: J0113+0106 has two luminous [O III] superbubbles and a curved outer arc connected by a bridge, interpreted as possible older AGN ejecta or tidally disturbed gas.

quotes.quote: J0113+0106 (z=0.281) A spectacular system with two symmetric luminous [ O III ] superbubbles on the northwestern and southeastern side of the nucleus, at a projected distance of 5.5 kpc each. The northwestern bubble is somewhat fainter than the one in the Southeast, and their diameters are 5-8 kpc. There is also a 14 kpc long curved arc in [ O III ] at 24 kpc separation from the nucleus. It is connected with the main system through a fainter [ O III ] bridge. This could be an old ejecta from the AGN, or gas pulled out by tidal interaction.

gmw_00001446.name: SDSS J115544.59 014739.9
gmw_00001446.overview: SDSS J115544.59 014739.9 is likely an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1607.06481] that hosts a weakly absorbed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.07018]. It is situated at the geometric center of a low-mass galaxy group [arXiv:1607.06481] and is surrounded by a 64-kiloparsec emission-line nebula [arXiv:1607.06481].

sources.summary: J1155--0147 is the sample standout, with the largest 64 kpc [O III] nebula, high FUV and X-ray signal, and a central position in a low-mass group.

quotes.quote: J1155--0147 (z=0.306) This galaxy is extraordinary. First, its [ O III ] emission has a diameter of 64 kpc and is the largest nebula in our sample. It also has the highest GALEX FUV luminosity, the highest X-ray counts and relatively low column density (see Section j1155chandra ). The host galaxy is likely an elliptical. The [ O III ] emission roughly decomposes into three main parts: a luminous centre with a diameter of 12 kpc, a medium-bright and somewhat elliptical zone of 30 kpc, and a fainter envelope extending over 64 kpc.

Interestingly, J1155 0147 is located at the geometric centre of a low mass galaxy group. The group has at least a dozen members, mostly ellipticals and red spirals, distributed over 220 kpc. Using the mass-richness relation for red sequence galaxies hmw05,hsw09,jsw07 , we estimate the total mass to M_ 200 =(1.3 0.9) 10^ 13 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1155 as a low-redshift LAB hosting a powerful, weakly absorbed AGN whose broad ionization geometry matches the Gemini 3D spectra.

quotes.quote: This paper focuses on the two X-ray brightest LABs at z 0.3 , SDSS J0113+0106 (J0113) and SDSS J1155 0147 (J1155), comparable in size and luminosity to `B1', one of the best-studied LABs at z 2. Our hard X-ray (3--30 keV) observations reveal powerful active galactic nuclei (AGN) with L_ 2-10 keV =(0.5 - 3) 10^ 44 . J0113 also faded by a factor of 5 between 2014 and 2016, emphasizing that variable AGN may cause apparent ionization deficits in LABs. Joint spectral analyses including data constrain column densities of N_ H =5.1^ +3.1 _ 3.3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 (J0113) and N_ H =6.0^ +1.4 _ 1.1 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 (J1155).

X-ray analysis of J1155 reveals a weakly absorbed AGN that may ionize over a wide solid angle, consistent with our 3D spectra. Extinction corrected [ O III ] log-luminosities are high, 43.6 . The velocity dispersions are low, 100 - 150 km s ^ 1 , even at the AGN positions. We argue that this is a combination of high extinction hiding the turbulent gas, and previous outflows that have cleared the escape paths for their successors.

gmw_00001447.name: AzTEC-3
gmw_00001447.overview: AzTEC-3 is a compact, dusty submillimeter galaxy located at redshift z = 5.3 within a massive protocluster [arXiv:1607.02520, arXiv:1808.04372]. It is classified as an ultraluminous infrared starburst galaxy [arXiv:1106.2092] that exhibits a star-formation driven outflow [arXiv:2202.03883] and interacts with the nearby star-forming galaxy LBG-3 [arXiv:2202.03883].

sources.summary: The paper maps Ly emission around AzTEC-3, finding an extended, redshifted nebula, gas bridge to LBG-3, possible starburst outflow, interactions, and a protocluster-scale halo.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The field around the AzTEC-3 (RA=150.0863, dec=2.589) submillimeter galaxy

quotes.quote: In this work, we study the members of the protocluster around AzTEC-3, a submillimeter galaxy at z=5.3 . We use Ly emission and its synergy with previous CO and [ C II ] 158 m observations.

At 7665 , the emission is mainly concentrated on top of the SMG, but shows a kind of a bridge between SMG and LBG, which could represent a region of interaction between the two close-by galaxies (see Sect. 6). The Ly emission of the bridge is observed from 7660 to 7666 , with a maximum at 7664 . It occupies a wide range in Ly wavelengths, that could correspond to a region of a large range of gas velocities, together with large HI column densities. These gas characteristics could be the result of the interactions in the AzTEC-3+LBG-3 system.

At the position of the main star-forming knot of the SMG and of the peak of the Ly emission associated to the SMG, the Ly emission extends from 200 to 600 km sec ^ 1 . The velocity could be associated to a star-formation driven outflow of neutral gas, departing from the SMG and mixing with the interacting gas in the bridge between SMG and LBG.

sources.summary: The paper describes LBG-3 as a star-forming galaxy near AzTEC-3 whose Ly emission is extended, bridged to the SMG, and shaped by tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: We identified ten Ly emitting sources, including two regions with extended emission: one embedding AzTEC-3 and LBG-3, which is a star-forming galaxy located 2 " (12 kpc) north of the SMG and another toward LBG-1, which is a star-forming galaxy located 15 '' (90 kpc) to the southeast. The two regions extend for 27 38 kpc ^2 ( 170 240 ckpc ^2 ) and 20 20 kpc ^2 ( 125 125 ckpc ^2 ), respectively. The sources appear distributed in an elongated configuration of about 70 '' (430 kpc) in extent.

For the AzTEC-3+LBG-3 system, the Ly emission appears redshifted and more spatially extended than the [ C II ] line emission. Similarly, the Ly line spectrum is broader in velocity than [ C II ] for LBG-1. In the former spectrum, the Ly emission is elongated to the north of LBG-3 and to the south of AzTEC-3, where a faint Ly emitting galaxy is also located. The elongated structures could resemble tidal features due to the interaction of the two galaxies with AzTEC-3. Also, we find a bridge of gas, revealed by the Ly emission between AzTEC-3 and LBG-3.

sources.summary: The paper models AzTEC-3 as a z=5.3 ultraluminous starburst, constraining its SED, stellar and dust masses, age, star-formation history, and dust growth in molecular clouds.

quotes.quote: No such ambiguity exists for , an ultraluminous IR galaxy located at redshift z = 5.3 , that was first detected at submillimeter wavelengths by . It is a radio-dim source younger07 with no X-ray counterpart capak11 , and part of a young cluster, representing an overdensity of early massive galaxies at the same redshift capak11 . Optical to submillimeter observations summarized in show that the energy output is dominated by its far-infrared (IR) emission with luminosities between (0.6-2) 10^ 13 . The lack of any radio or X-ray counterparts suggests that this luminosity is powered by a 1800 \ starburst riechers10,capak11 .

We first use the UV to far-IR SED of the galaxy to determine its bolometric luminosity and dust mass. Its SFR depends on the adopted IMF, and we generate a series of models relating the bolometric luminosity to the stellar mass and galactic age for different stellar IMFs (Section 2).

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC3 as a z=5.298 SMG in a protocluster, with millimeter flux, multiwavelength counterparts, a large molecular gas reservoir, and modeled dust/star-formation properties.

quotes.quote: AzTEC3 was initially detected in the same survey by Scott et al. (2008) as AzTEC1, and its counterparts at other wavelengths were identified by Younger et al. (2007). The deboosted 1.1 mm flux density is 5.9 1.3 mJy (where the deboosting factor is 1.3 ). Based on Keck II DEIMOS spectra Capak et al. \ (2011) have shown that AzTEC3 is located at a redshift of z_ spec =5.298 , and that it is associated with a proto-cluster at the same redshift.

Follow-up observations with the VLA and PdBI (targeting CO 2-1, 5-4, and 6-5; ; ) revealed a large molecular gas reservoir with a mass of 5.3 10^ 10 ( _ CO /0.8 ) (where _ CO is the CO luminosity-to-molecular gas mass conversion factor). Riechers et al. \ (2014) find a deconvolved [ C II ] size (FWHM) of 3.9 2.1 kpc ^2 , 2.5 kpc in dust continuum, and a dynamical mass of 9.7 10^ 10 . Detailed modeling of the SEDs of the source ( ) yielded that the dust contained in the source likely formed over a period of 200 Myr with a SFR of 500 \ and a top-heavy IMF (the SFR can rise up to 1800 \ if other scenarios are assumed; see for details).

sources.summary: The paper treats AzTEC3 as an unresolved 3 GHz source, comparing its upper radio size limit with ALMA far-infrared and C II sizes and a compact NIR diameter.

quotes.quote: AzTEC3 . This source is unresolved in our 3 GHz image, similarly to that found by Smol i et al. (2015a) in their 3 GHz image (upper size limit was set to <0 7 ). Riechers et al. (2014) used ALMA to observe AzTEC3 at an angular resolution of 0 63 0 56 . In the _ obs =1 mm ( _ rest =159 m) continuum, the deconvolved FWHM size of AzTEC3 was derived to be 0 40^ +0.04 _ 0.04 0 17^ +0.08 _ 0.17 , while in the _ rest =158 m [ C II ] line emission the size was found to be larger, 0 63^ +0.09 _ 0.09 0 34^ +0.10 _ 0.15 . We have derived the _ rest =1.6 cm upper FWHM size limit of AzTEC3 to be <0 38 , which is smaller than the aforementioned rest-frame FIR continuum and [ C II ] sizes (although the major axis FWHM at _ rest =159 m is marginally consistent with our upper size limit; see Fig. figure:sizecomparison ). The observed-frame NIR diameter of AzTEC3 derived by Toft et al. (2014) is <4.8 kpc, i.e. <0 76 , which is also consistent with our radio emission FWHM size, and with the FIR FWHM size from Riechers et al. (2014).

sources.summary: AzTEC-3 is described as a compact, dusty z>5 hyper-starburst in an early proto-cluster, with detected [N ii] emission and unusually low [N ii]/FIR luminosity.

quotes.quote: AzTEC-3 AzTEC-3 is one of the most intensely star-forming starbursts known at z>5 (not amplified by gravitational lensing). It is centrally located in one of the earliest galaxy proto-clusters known showing an 11-fold over-density of LBGs and color-selected galaxies within 2 Mpc, suggesting a halo mass >4 10^ 11 M_ ; C11 and is known to be compact and dusty R10a,R14 . Its very compact starburst ( 2.5 kpc in diameter), leads to a high dust temperature ( 50 - 90 K; R14), indicating extreme conditions even among high redshift SMGs. We have detected the [ N ii ] line in AzTEC-3, with a measured [ N ii ] luminosity of (2.9 1.0) 10^8 L_ implying L_ NII /L_ FIR =(2.6 1.0) 10^ 5 , which is very low compared to local (U)LIRGs Z16 .

sources.summary: The survey identifies AzTEC-3 as the brightest COSMOS CO(2-1) candidate, a z=5.3 submillimeter galaxy in a massive protocluster with compatible prior line and continuum measurements.

quotes.quote: COLDz.COS.0 : We identify the brightest candidate in the COSMOS field with CO(2--1) from the z=5.3 sub-millimeter galaxy AzTEC-3, detected at a SNR of 15, and which was chosen to be near the center of our survey region. This galaxy is known to reside in a massive proto-cluster Riechers10a,Riechers14, Capak11 . The line flux is compatible with the previously measured value of 0.23 0.03 Jy km s ^ 1 Riechers10a within the relative flux calibration uncertainty. This source is also detected at 3 GHz, with a flux of 20 3 Jy Smolcic17 , and by SCUBA-2 at 850 m as part of the S2COSMOS survey with a significance of 9.3 and a flux of 8.1^ +1.1 _ 1.3 mJy (J.M. Simpson, et al. in prep).

sources.summary: The paper reports AzTEC3 has an optical redshift of z=5.3 confirmed by multiple CO lines, and notes that its 350 micron photometry is the exception to otherwise reliable measurements.

quotes.quote: There was also substantial effort in performing deep optical spectroscopy for optically faint millimeter sources in the COSMOS field using the Keck telescopes by capak2008, capak2011, smolcic2011 , and obtained optical spectroscopic redshifts for Capak4.55, AzTEC1 and AzTEC3 at z = 4.55 , 4.64 and 5.3, respectively.

Subsequent millimeter spectroscopic observations detect of the CO [ 4 3 ] line from Capak4.55 schinnerer2008 and CO [ 2 1 ] , CO [ 5 4 ] , and CO [ 6 5 ] lines from AzTEC3 riechers2010 , confirming their optical spectroscopic redshifts.
We argue that the 350 m photometry is reasonably reliable except for AzTEC3.

gmw_00001448.name: COLDz.COS.1
gmw_00001448.overview: COLDz.COS.1 is a massive, main-sequence galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2 [arXiv:1808.04372]. It has been detected in CO(1-0) and CO(3-2) line emissions, as well as at 3 GHz radio and 850 micrometer submillimeter wavelengths [arXiv:1808.04372].

sources.summary: COLDz.COS.1 is described as a high-SNR CO(1-0) detection matched to a COSMOS2015 galaxy, ALMA-confirmed in CO(3-2), and detected at radio and submillimeter wavelengths.

quotes.quote: COLDz.COS.1 : This high signal-to-noise detection is matched in position (offset 0.3^ 0.3^ ) and CO(1--0) redshift to a source with photometric redshift ( z_ phot =2.6--2.9) in the COSMOS2015 catalog Laigle16 . We have confirmed its redshift with ALMA through a detection of the CO(3--2) line (Pavesi et al., in prep.). This source is also detected at 3 GHz, with a flux of 15 2 Jy Smolcic17 , and at 850 m with a significance of 6.0 and a flux of 4.9_ 1.2 ^ +1.1 mJy (J.M. Simpson, et al. in prep).

In particular, our sub-sample of independently confirmed CO emitters contains previously known starbursts like AzTEC-3 (by design), GN10, GN19, and HDF850.1 but also COLDz.COS.1, 2 and 3 and COLDz.GN.S1 which belong to the massive end of the Main Sequence at z 2 (Pavesi et al., in prep.).

gmw_00001449.name: COLDz.COS.2
gmw_00001449.overview: COLDz.COS.2 is a high-redshift galaxy with a confirmed CO redshift of 2.477 [arXiv:1808.04372] or 2.4790 [arXiv:1412.1262]. It is characterized by a broad CO emission line [arXiv:1412.1262, arXiv:1808.04372], a large cold molecular gas mass [arXiv:1412.1262], and detections in both the radio and submillimeter bands [arXiv:1808.04372].

sources.summary: The paper reports COLDz J100018.21+023456.7 as a high-redshift CO line emitter with infrared counterparts, z_CO=2.4790, broad CO emission, and a large cold molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: Applying our line detection method to the VLA data, we detect with high significance a previously unknown line emitter at = 10 ^h 00 ^m 18.21 ^s , = 02 ^d 34 ^m 56.7 ^s (J2000), with an emission line frequency of 33.142 GHz and an integrated intensity of 0.076 0.010 Jy km s ^ 1 (Figures figure:cont figure:5chan ). The linewidth is FWHM 570 80 km s ^ 1 . We hereafter refer to this source as COLDz J100018.21+023456.7.

An initial fit to the spectral energy distribution of the counterparts to J100018.21+023456.7 leads to an estimate on the photometric redshift of z_ phot = 3.2 0.4 2013A&A...556A..55I , which corresponds within 2 with the proposed CO redshift z_ CO =2.4790 0.0003 , suggesting this is CO J =1--0 line emission. The CO J =1-0 line luminosity is then L'_ CO = (2.2 0.3) 10^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 with FWHM of 570 80 km s ^ 1 .
Assuming this range in values for the conversion factor, the estimated cold molecular gas mass for J100018.21+023456.7 is M _ gas (2 - 8) 10^ 10 M _ .

sources.summary: COLDz.COS.2 is a high-SNR CO source with ALMA-confirmed redshift, radio and submillimeter detections, a broad line, and placement among massive z~2 main-sequence galaxies.

quotes.quote: COLDz.COS.2 : This high signal-to-noise detection is matched in position (offset 0.3^ 0.3^ ) to a source in the COSMOS2015 catalog Laigle16 . We have confirmed its redshift with ALMA through a detection of the CO(3--2) line (Pavesi et al., in prep.), and some of its properties were previously presented in Lentati15 . The photometric redshift in the COSMOS2015 catalog is highly uncertain, and not compatible with the CO redshift of 2.477 within 1 ( z_ phot =2.9--4.4). This source is also detected at 3 GHz, with a flux of 19 3 Jy Smolcic17 , and at 850 m with a significance of 5.9 and a flux of 4.0_ 1.0 ^ +0.9 mJy (J.M. Simpson, et al. in prep).

The spread and distribution in the line FWHM we find is comparable to those previously measured (e.g., Tacconi13 ), although the occurrence of a particularly broad line (in COLDz.COS.2) in our limited, highest quality sample, suggest that there may be a larger incidence of broad lines in blindly selected CO sources, compared to optical/NIR selections.

gmw_00001450.name: COLDz.COS.3
gmw_00001450.overview: COLDz.COS.3 is a high-redshift galaxy with a CO redshift of 1.97 [arXiv:1808.04372]. It is a spatially extended CO source that hosts a very large cold gas reservoir spanning approximately 30 to 40 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1808.04372].

sources.summary: COLDz.COS.3 is an extended CO source matched to two catalog galaxies, ALMA-confirmed in CO(4-3), detected at 3 GHz, and highlighted as hosting a large cold gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: COLDz.COS.3 : This high signal-to-noise detection, is a significantly spatially extended CO source with a deconvolved size of (4.0^ 1.1^ ) (1.8^ 1.2^ ) . It is matched in position to two galaxies in the COSMOS2015 catalog ( Laigle16 ; offsets of 0.14^ 0.3^ and 1.8^ 0.3^ ). We have confirmed its CO(1--0) redshift with ALMA through a detection of the CO(4--3) line (Pavesi et al., in prep.). The cataloged photo- z for both galaxies ( z_ phot =1.8--1.9) is not compatible with the CO redshift of 1.97 within 1 . This source is also detected at 3 GHz, with a flux of 27 3 Jy Smolcic17 .

By taking advantage of this new source selection method, we have blindly detected significantly extended CO(1--0) line sources like COLDz.COS.3, which hosts a very large cold gas reservoir ( 30-40 kpc). One of our top candidates, COLDz.COS.3, harbors a very extended gas reservoir, with SNR peaking in the 2 ^ template.

gmw_00001451.name: GN10
gmw_00001451.overview: GN10 is an ultra-massive, dust-obscured starburst galaxy [arXiv:2309.02492, arXiv:1808.04372] located at a redshift of approximately 5.3 [arXiv:1808.04372, arXiv:2309.04529]. It features a large molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:1808.04372] and multiple components that suggest a merging nature [arXiv:2309.04529] within a compact overdensity environment [arXiv:2302.10323].

sources.summary: The paper describes GN10 as an extremely red z=4.04 SMG whose SED requires an older/extincted star-forming component; its X-rays, old stars, and FIR output suggest late-stage merging with central accretion beginning.

quotes.quote: GN10 is extremely red and not detected in the deep HST H-band imaging with H [ 3.6 ] > 4.5 wang2009,huang2011 , corresponding to A _v > 8 if we use the same 25Myr old young stellar population. But this extreme dusty model also predicts much redder [ 3.6 ] [ 8.0 ] color than what is actually observed. huang2011 found 4 additional objects with H [ 3.6 ] > 4.5 in the GOODS-S field and suggested that both dust extinction and presence of an old stellar population component could be responsible for such an extreme color. We fit the SED of GN10 with an 1Gyr constant star formation template and A _V = 2.7.

X-ray imaging in GOODS-N is very deep with an exposure time of 2Ms, but only GN10 is detected laird2010 with L_ x = 10 ^ 42.93 erg s ^ 1 , which corresponds to a star formation rate of 1700 M _ yr ^ 1 or L_ FIR 10 ^ 12.8 L _ . laird2010 argued that intensive star formation in GN10 accounts for both the X-rays and FIR luminosities.

Both wang2009 and huang2011 suggested that there is a significant number of old stars in GN10, indicating a late stage of merging. Thus, it is very likely that its central black accretion is switching-on in GN10, but not yet in GN20 and GN20.2. The X-ray luminosity for GN10 is much lower than 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , suggesting that it has not yet entered in the QSO phase.

sources.summary: The paper identifies S2, also called GN10, as an ultra-massive, optically dark z~5.5 galaxy with strong dust obscuration, high infrared luminosity, and rapid obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: Among the parent sample, three optically dark galaxies at z_ spec 5-6 show the most extreme properties, i.e., extremely high masses with log( M_ /M_ ) 11.0 (Chabrier initial mass function, IMF ; Figs. fig1 , fig2 ). These sources are named S1, S2, and S3. One of these has been known previously as a sub-millimeter galaxy GN10 . Their extreme mass makes metals and dust inefficient to escape through stellar feedback from their ultra-deep gravitational potential, which is why they have been missed by optical observations until now.

For S2, the so-called GN10, we obtain its L_ IR =1.03_ 0.15 ^ +0.19 10^ 13 L_ and SFR _ IR =1030_ 150 ^ +190 M_ yr ^ 1 from ref. . These values are also derived from CIGALE SED fits, based on extensive data from Herschel, JCMT/SCUBA, SMA, and NOEMA observations. The SFR _ IR of S1 is similar to the SFR value derived from the UV-to-NIR SED fit (Extended Data Table tab1 ). However, the SFR _ IR of S2 and S3 are about three times higher than their SFR _ SED , implying that there may be hidden dust regions in these galaxies that absorb all the UV photons, which cannot be reproduced with a dust extinction correction , or that our assumed star-formation histories are not appropriate.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GOODS 850-5 as a bright high-redshift submillimeter galaxy, detects its millimeter/radio emission, derives a precise position, and argues its SED indicates a starburst at about z 4.

quotes.quote: We have obtained a position (at sub-arcsecond accuracy) of the submillimeter bright source GOODS 850-5 (also known as GN10) in the GOODS North field using the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer at 1.25 mm wavelengths (MM J123633 + 6214.1, flux density: S_ 1.25 mm =5.0 1.0 mJy). This source has no optical counterpart in deep ACS imaging down to a limiting magnitude of i _ 775 =28.4 mag and its position is coincident with the position found in recent sub-millimeter mapping obtained at the SMA wan07 . Using deep VLA imaging at 20 cm, we find a radio source ( S_ 20 cm =32.7 4.3 Jy) at the same position that is significantly brighter than reported in Wang et al. The source is detected by Spitzer in IRAC as well as at 24 m.

We detected the dust continuum emission of GOODS 850-5 at 5 significance at S_ 1.25 mm =5.0 1.0 mJy. The derived position is RA(J2000) = 12:36:33.45, DEC(J2000) = 62:14:08.95 (hereafter: MM J123633 + 6214.1) with an estimated position uncertainty of 0.3 .
We conclude that the multi-wavelength data suggests that MM J123633 + 6314 is a starburst at z 4.

sources.summary: The survey identifies GN10 as the brightest GOODS-N CO(2-1) candidate, revises its redshift to z=5.3, and describes it as an optically dark dusty starburst with a large gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: COLDz.GN.0 : We identify the brightest candidate in the GOODS-N field with CO(2--1) line emission from GN10, a massive, bright dust-obscured starbursting galaxy Pope06, Dannerbauer08, Daddi09 . We find a CO redshift of z= 5.3, showing that the previous redshift determination ( z =4.04) was incorrect. Its properties are described in Riechers et al., in prep. This source is also detected at 1.4 GHz with a flux of 36 4 Jy Morrison10 , and by SCUBA-2 at 850 m in the SCUBA-2 Cosmology Legacy Survey (S2CLS) with a significance of 9.2 and a flux of 7.5 1.5 mJy Geach17 .

With this CO deep field study, we also further demonstrate that blind CO searches are sensitive to ``optically dark", dust-obscured galaxies at very high redshift, such as GN10 and HDF850.1. In particular, the massive molecular gas reservoirs of these galaxies are among the largest in our field (Riechers et al., in prep.).

sources.summary: The paper describes GN10 as a luminous HST-dark submillimeter galaxy in the overdensity, with heavy dust obscuration, multiple components suggesting merger nature, and NIRCam grism line detections at z=5.303.

quotes.quote: GN10 ( z=5.303 ; R.A. = 12:36:33.398, Decl. =+ 62 14 08 4) is another luminous SMG that is within the overdense environment. This source is not included in our photometric-redshift parent sample because of heavy dust obscuration, which leads to a large uncertainty of z_ phot . Figure fig:gn10 shows the NIRCam images and grism spectrum of GN10. GN10 is also an HST-dark SMG and only detected at above 2 .

Multiple components could be identified from the image, suggesting a hint of merger nature. In the NIRCam grism spectrum, we detect the \ ( (9.5 0.9) 10^ 18 erg.s^ 1 ^ 2 ) and 6583 ( (4.9 0.8) 10^ 18 erg.s^ 1 ^ 2 ) line emission at z=5.303 , the same redshift reported previously through CO lines riechers20 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a new overdensity around GN10, notes its dust-obscured nature, and finds its high-redshift environment with B19 and AH18 catalogs.

quotes.quote: We confirm all three previously discovered protoclusters, and we detect five new ones around the SMGs SMM J123634 ( z=1.225 ), ID.19 ( z=2.047 ), SMM J123607 ( z=2.487 ), SMM J123606 ( z=2.505 ), and GN10 ( z=5.303 ). Many SMGs are indeed undetected in the optical wavelengths, as for example it is the case for GN10 and HDF850.1, which are completely dust-obscured Daddi09, Walter12,Calvi21 , so that it is difficult to estimate their stellar masses.

For B19 we have eight detected overdensities around the SMGs, including GN20, GN10, and possibly HDF850.1 at the highest redshifts z 4-5 , even though for the last one the association of the SMG with the overdensity is uncertain and not reported in Fig. fig:redshift_histo_PPMoverdensities . Out of the four distant SMGs that fall in the z 3.35-6.8 redshift range of AH18, overdensities are detected around HDF850.1 and GN10, separately. In some cases (e.g., GN10, GN20, and GN20.2a) they have compact morphologies, similarly to CL J1001+0220 at z=2.51 in the COSMOS field Wang16 .
