Adopting a simple spherical model for the molecular outflow with radius of 70 pc and velocity 1000 , the implied outflow timescale is 0.07 Myr and thus the total outflowing mass is 10^ 10 . The CO (1--0) line is commonly used to probe the bulk molecular gas mass in galaxies in the high-redshift Universe, assuming a CO-to-H _ 2 conversion factor: adopting the 3 upper limit for the non-detection of CO (1--0) Sharon:2016 , a typical Greve:2014 conversion factor of 0.8 K km s^ 1 pc^ 2 and magnification factor of 10 Stacey:2018a , we estimate the molecular gas mass in the host galaxy is <10^ 9.5 Note that this would be a factor of 2 lower if we assume the dust magnification in Table table:lensmodels . Therefore, for an energy-driven wind model, the gas in the outflow from MG J0414+0534 would be more than twice as massive as the molecular gas in the disc: an unlikely scenario given the high SFR (Table table:sources ) and the massive molecular gas reservoirs of quasar hosts that are similar to normal starbursts Riechers:2011a,Riechers:2011b,Sharon:2016 .

sources.summary: The paper models MG 0414+0534 as a quadruply imaged anomalous lensed quasar, using microlensing to constrain the I-band emission-region radius while leaving smooth lens matter unconstrained.

quotes.quote: We apply our model to the anomalous lensed quasar MG 0414+0534, and find a 95 upper limit of 2.62 10^ 16 h^ 1/2 _ 70 (M/M_ )^ 1/2 cm on the radius of the I-band emission region. The smooth matter percentage in the lens is unconstrained.

The quadruply imaged quasar MG 0414+0534 is one example of a lensed quasar displaying anomalous flux ratios. These anomalies are observed in ratios between images A_2 , located at a saddle point in the time delay surface, and A_1 , located at a minimum. sm93 reported an A_2/A_1 I-band flux ratio of 0.45 0.06 in observations taken on 2-4 November 1991. This result was supported by subsequent observations: A_2/A_1 = 0.3 0.1 from CFHT in the I-band on 1 March 1992 a94 and A_2/A_1 = 0.47 0.01 from HST in the I-band on 8 November 1994 f97 .

Models that fit the observed image positions in MG 0414+0534 place images A_1 and A_2 either side of such a critical curve, and thus we would naively expect their magnification ratio, or flux ratio, to be 1 hereafter WMS95 wms95 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a strong new z=0.38 21-cm absorber toward the z=2.64 quasar MG J0414+0534, adding to host- and lens-redshift absorbers while OH remains undetected.

quotes.quote: Received ---; in original form --- firstpage - lastpage 2011 We report the detection of a third \ 21-cm absorber in the sight-line towards the z=2.64 quasar MG J0414+0534 (4C +05.19). In addition to the absorption at the host redshift and in the z=0.96 gravitational lens, we find, through a decimetre-wave spectral scan towards this source, strong absorption at z=0.38 .

As part of an ongoing project, scanning the entire redshift space towards highly reddened radio sources, in search for the object responsible for the obscuration of the optical light, we have detected a second intervening 21-cm absorber at z=0.38 towards the z= 2.64 quasar MG J0414+0534. The other intervening absorber arises in the z=0.96 gravitational lens cdbw07 and, combined with the absorption found at the host redshift mcm98 , gives a total of three \ absorbers so far detected along this sight-line. Although we cannot determine the T_ s /f degeneracy for any of the three 21-cm absorbers, thus deriving the column densities, the 21-cm line strength in this new absorber is by far the strongest detected along this sight-line, being four times stronger than in the host galaxy [ N_ HI =7.5 1.3 10^ 18 . (T_ s /f) ] and 19 times stronger than in the lens [ N_ HI =1.6 10^ 18 (T_ s /f) ] . In fact, the strongest intervening 21-cm absorber found to date. Despite the \ absorption strength and the very red colour of this source ( V-K = 10.26 ), OH remains undetected to very strong limits [ N_ OH /N_ HI 1.8 10^ 6 . (f_ HI /f_ OH ) . (T_ x /T_ s ) ] , inferring that this new absorber is not the primary cause of the red colour. OH is also undetected in the lens and the host galaxy, although the latter is to relatively weak limits not allowing us to rule out that this is where much of the reddening occurs.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the lensed quasar MG J0414+0534, finding an extremely luminous water maser with stable peak emission, variable integrated flux, and line structure favoring a jet-maser interpretation.

quotes.quote: After correcting for the lens magnification, we find that the total H _2 O isotropic luminosity of the maser in MG J0414+0534 is now 30,000 L _ , making this source the most luminous ever discovered. Both the main line peak and continuum flux densities are surprisingly stable throughout the period of the observations. The integrated flux density shows instead significant variations on monthly time scales, possibly due to changes in the individual velocity components. We place an upper limit on the velocity drift of the peak of the line emission of 2 km s ^ 1 yr ^ 1 .

The large line width of the main maser line and the absence of a clear triple-peak pattern in the maser spectrum of MG J0414+0534 favors the jet-maser scenario. However, the stability of the line and continuum emission, and the presence of the tentative new maser component, potentially identified as a high-velocity feature of a rotating disk, seems partly going against this interpretation. Sensitive monitoring on a longer time-scale and VLBI observations are mandatory to draw a definite conclusion.

sources.summary: The paper describes MG0414+0534 as a fold-caustic lensed quasar whose z=2.64 source is lensed by a z=0.96 elliptical, requiring object X or a sub/line-of-sight halo for fitting.

quotes.quote: MG0414+0534 The fold-caustic lens MG0414+0534 consists of two bright images A1 and A2, and two faint images B and C. The images A1 and B are minima, and A2 and C are saddles. A source quasar at z_S=2.64 is lensed by an elliptical galaxy at z_L=0.96 hewitt1992,lawrence1995, tonry1999 . A simple lens model, an SIE with an external shear (SIE-ES) cannot fit the image positions as well as the flux ratios. schechter1993 and Ros2000 suggested that another galaxy called ``X'' is necessary for fitting the relative image positions.

Although, the positions are well fitted by an SIE and an ES plus an SIS that accounts for object X, the flux ratios are not well fitted. A possible sub/line-of-sight halo near A2 significantly improves the fit minezaki2009,macleod2013 .

sources.summary: The paper models MG0414+0534 as a quadruply lensed quasar whose flux-ratio anomaly can be explained by line-of-sight minifilament or minivoid perturbations rather than lens-galaxy subhalos.

quotes.quote: We explore the weak lensing effects by ministructures in the line-of-sight in a quadruply lensed quasar MG0414+0534 that shows an anomaly in the flux ratios. We find that the observed flux-ratio anomaly can be explained by a presence of either a minifilament or a minivoid in the line-of-sight with a surface mass density of the order of 10^ 8-9 h^ 1 / arcsec ^2 without taking into account any subhalos in the lensing galaxy. The astrometric perturbation by a possible minifilament/minivoid at the primary lens plane is 0.001 arcsec and the amplitudes of convergence perturbations due to these perturbers are 0.004-0.008 at the place of an image that shows anomaly.

The fold-caustic lens MG0414+0534 consists of two bright images A1, A2 and two faint images B, C. The images A1 and B are minima, and A2 and C are saddles. A source quasar at z_S=2.64 is lensed by an elliptical galaxy (hereinafter referred to as G) at z_L=0.96 hewitt1992, lawrence1995, tonry1999 .

sources.summary: The paper models MG J0414+0534 as a lensed dusty star-forming quasar host with water-line emission, a circumnuclear molecular disk, and radio jets extending from the core.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of observations with the Atacama Large (sub-)Millimetre Array (ALMA) of the 380 GHz water emission line and CO (11--10) emission line from MG J0414+0534, a gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxy that hosts a type 1 quasar. We also present observations at 1.6 GHz with global very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) of the radio source. We confirm the previously reported detection of the 380 GHz water line and that the flux density ratio between the two merging lensed images is reversed with respect to the radio/mm continuum. We further find tentative evidence of variability in the integrated line intensity on timescales of days.

We show that the 380 GHz water line has two components of emission: a disc that is around 35 pc in diameter around the quasar, and another component of emission that is offset 600 pc perpendicular to the disc that lies close to the lensing caustic. With lens modelling of the multi-wavelength data sets, we construct a model for the quasar system consisting of a circumnuclear disc of molecular gas with a size of about 60 pc bisected by radio jets extending to a distance of about 200 pc from the radio core.

sources.summary: The paper describes MG J0414+0534 as a very red z=2.64 quasar whose sight line contains a record number of strong 21-cm absorbers, including host-galaxy absorption.

quotes.quote: For the reddest ( V - K = 10.26 ) of the targets, the z = 2.64 quasar MG J0414+0534 (4C +05.19), three 21-cm absorption systems have already been detected (see Table abs ).

We have now completed the analysis of the data along the entire redshift space towards MG J0414+0534 (see cwt+11 for details), upon which we find a further two possible absorption profiles, near 926 MHz and 1061 MHz.

We have now completed the analysis of a full-band decimetre wave spectral scan towards the very red ( V-K = 10.26 ) quasar MG J0414+0534. From the 46 of the band not completely ruined by RFI, we have found three (possibly four) strong intervening absorbers, in addition to the previously detected absorption in the host galaxy at z=2.64 mcm98 . At a total of four (or five), this represents a new record in the number of 21-cm absorbers found along a single sight-line, the previous being a total of two systems towards PKS 1830--211 lrj+96,cdn99 , which has V-K = 6.25 (see cwm+06 ).

sources.summary: MG0414+0534 is treated as a fold-caustic lensed quasar with four images; its simple SIE-ES model cannot fit positions and fluxes without an additional galaxy X.

quotes.quote: The fold-caustic lens MG0414+0534 consists of two bright images A1, A2 and two faint images B, C. The images A1, B are minima and A2, C are saddles. A source quasar at z_S=2.64 is lensed by an elliptical galaxy at z_L=0.96 hewitt92,lawrence1995, tonry1999 . A simple lens model, a singular isothermal ellipsoid with an external shear (SIE-ES) can not fit the image positions as well as the flux ratios. schechter1993 and Ros2000 suggested that another galaxy called ``X'' is necessary to account for the relative image positions.

sources.summary: The paper treats MG J0414+0534 as an anomalous lensed quasar whose flux ratios and satellite component require a more complex macro model.

quotes.quote: MG J0414+0534 has a luminous satellite (object X) that is detected in optical imaging Falco1997 , which we include into the lens model and allow its mass to be a free parameter; CLASS B0712+472 and CLASS B1555+375 both have an edge-on disc that lies across the merging images, where the flux-ratio anomalies are most significant Jackson1998,Hsueh2016,Hsueh2017 .

From Figure  fig:fsub , we notice that MG J0414+0534 is essentially an outlier, with a posterior probability that peaks at large values of f_ sub , outside our prior range. It is one of the most observed lensed quasars with a wide frequency coverage from radio, sub-millimetre molecular lines, far-infrared to the MIR. Recently, Stacay2018 have shown that the flux ratios for this system do not change with frequency. As different wavelengths should be sensitive to the different mass scale of the perturbation (due to a change in the source size), we conclude that the large anomaly observed in MG J0414+0534 is more likely due to more massive structures, for example, a more complex macro model.

sources.summary: The paper applies its fuzzy-dark-matter calculation to MG0414+0534, noting its measured convergence power spectrum, flux-ratio anomaly, satellite perturbation, and line-of-sight caveats.

quotes.quote: When this paper was almost completed, we found a paper by in which a new measurement of sub-galactic matter power spectrum for the strong lens system MG0414+0534 is presented. They measure the dimensionless convergence power spectrum of ^2_ 5 10^ 4 on 8  kpc scale and interpret the signal only in the context of the standard CDM model. We adopt lens model parameters of MG0414+0534 shown in 2014MNRAS.439.2494O to repeat the calculation in this paper, and find that quantum clumps in the FDM model with the mass of m 4 10^ 23  eV well explains the observed signal. However a caveat is that MG0414+0534 has been known as a quasar lens system exhibiting the significant flux ratio anomaly as well as strong perturbations on the lens potential due to a satellite galaxy e.g., 2009ApJ...697..610M , which suggests that the measured sub-galactic matter power spectrum might be biased high. The high source redshift of z=2.64 also implies that the effect of the line-of-sight structure may be more important than for SDSS J0252+0039.

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA measurements of the quadruply lensed quasar MG J0414+0534 to constrain WDM/MDM models from its lensing shift power spectrum.

quotes.quote: Then, leveraging recent measurements of the lensing shift power spectrum on an angular scale of approximately 1 arcsec towards the gravitationally lensed quasar MG J0414+0534 at the redshift of z_S=2.639 , we place constraints on the mass of warm dark matter (WDM) particles m_ WDM and their fraction in a mixed dark matter (MDM) model r_ WDM , in which WDM and cold dark matter coexist.

In our analysis, we constrain the WDM mass and the fraction of WDM in the MDM model using a lensing shift power spectrum, which was measured using ALMA (Atacama Large Milllimeter/submillimeter Array) observations of the quadruply lensed quasar MG J0414+0534 . The redshifts of the source and the primary lens (early type galaxy) of MG J0414+0534 are z_S=2.639 and z_L=0.9584 , respectively.

The lens system consists of a massive early type galaxy G, which acts as a primary lens, and an object X, which is possibly a less massive galaxy, and an object Y, which is possibly a dusty dwarf galaxy. The gravitational effects of object X and object Y were subtracted using a smooth potential for each object.

sources.summary: The paper treats MG J0414+0534 as a macrolensed QSO whose Fe K enhancement, including image-B equivalent-width amplification without continuum brightening, can be interpreted through microlensing of its accretion disk.

quotes.quote: The observed enhancement of the Fe K line in three gravitationally lensed QSOs (MG J0414+0534, QSO 2237+0305, H1413+117) is interpreted in terms of microlensing, even when equivalent X-ray continuum amplification is not observed. In order to interpret these observations, first we studied the effects of microlensing on quasars spectra, produced by straight fold caustic crossing over standard relativistic accretion disk.
Microlensing of the Fe K line has been reported at least in three macrolensed QSOs: MG J0414+0534 Chart02a , QSO 2237+0305 Dai03 , and H1413+117 Osh01, Pop03, Chart04 .

detected an increase of the Fe K equivalent width in the image B of the lensed QSO J0414+0534 that was not followed by the continuum. explained the non-enhancement of the continuum emission in the spectrum of image B by proposing that the thermal emission region of the disk and the Compton up-scattered emission region of the hard X-ray source lie within smaller radii than the iron-line reprocessing region.

sources.summary: The paper presents mid-infrared observations of the quadruply lensed radio-loud quasar MG0414+0534 and argues its persistent flux-ratio anomaly indicates low-mass substructure in the lens.

quotes.quote: Here we present mid-IR observations of the quadruply lensed, radio-loud z_s=2.64 quasar MG0414+0534, obtained using the Michelle camera on Gemini North. This system is known to be affected by extinction and microlensing in the optical and near-IR hew92,law95,fal97,sch02,bat08,bat11,poo12 , but the flux ratio anomaly persists into the mid-IR and radio.

Here, we show that the flux ratio anomaly in MG0414+0534 indicates the presence of low-mass substructure in the lens based on models of the individual or combined radio and mid-IR observations of this lens.
We observed MG0414+0534 with the Michelle camera roc04 on Gemini North at 11.2 m (F112B21 filter) on 2 Jan 2006.

sources.summary: The paper models the Fe K line enhancement in J0414+0534 as microlensing of a lensed QSO image by a compact object.

quotes.quote: An event of this type affecting the Fe K line has been reported in the quadruple imaged QSO J0414+0534 by Chartas et al. (2002). These authors observed a sudden increase in the iron line equivalent width from 190 eV to 900 eV only on image B of J0414+0534 proposing that it has been caused by a microlensing event. We can try to reproduce this enhancement with our models under the assumption of amplification by a straight fold caustic crossing.
We obtain that microlenses of 1 solar mass can explain the measured Fe K line excess in the J0414+0534 and H 1413+117 lens systems.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 4C +05.19 as the only sufficiently deep OH search, with extreme reddening whose origin may be a lens, background emitter, or foreground object.

quotes.quote: For the only source which has been, 4C +05.19 (J0414+054), there is much debate over the origin of its extremely red colour ( V-K =10.37 ), whether it arises in the intervening gravitational lens responsible for the \ absorption cdbw07 , the z_ em = 2.639 background emitter fls97,tk99 , which also exhibits strong \ 21-cm absorption mcm98 , or another possible foreground object eht+06,tcw+13 . We therefore flag the optical--near-infrared colour as V-K < 10.37 . Using this upper limit and the V-K lower limit to PKS 1830--21 (Sect. sec:colours ), results in a significance of S( )=2.52 for the normalised OH line-strength and 2.76 for the un-normalised line-strength.

sources.summary: The paper cites MG 0414+0534 as an optically extended, redder radio-selected lens with extended lensed optical emission, and argues it is not representative of bright optical quasars.

quotes.quote: The intrinsically fainter lenses are likely to be optically extended (as seen in HST observations of MG 0414+0534 (Falco et al. 1997) and CLASS 1608+656 (Jackson, Nair Browne 1997)) and redder than both bright radio sources and optically-selected quasars.

We see the population shift in our redshift survey with the rapidly rising fraction of (early-type) galaxies at fainter radio fluxes, and we see the population shift in the lensed sources by the frequent appearance of extended lensed optical emission (MG 0414+0534, CLASS 1608+656) and the frequent lack of the broad emission lines characteristic of quasars.

We also cannot use MG 0414+0534 to make simple inferences about lensed quasars because the very presence of extended lensed optical emission means that the source is very different from a bright optical quasar.

gmw_00001470.name: NGC 4417
gmw_00001470.overview: NGC 4417 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1907.10188, arXiv:2107.04449]. It exhibits a lopsided, broad tail of diffuse X-ray emission extending to its northwest, which is interpreted as evidence of ongoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2107.04449].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4417 as an edge-on lenticular/barred lenticular whose curvature and residuals indicate two inner components and an edge-on disk.

quotes.quote: NGC4417 is classified as SB0:edge-on RC3_1991,hinz2003 and also S0:edge-on sandage1981,nilson1973 . In the curvature plot of fig:NGC4417rkur , two peaks stand out in the first half of the R_p region: 1 and 2 seems to have a bulge morphology, while 3 is a disk. Analysing , it seems plausible that it distinguishes two components inside R/R_p 1.0 , the first part identified by the first peak and the second by the second peak. argued that the inner part (region 1) of this galaxy is an inner disk while the outer region (region 2) is the bulge found a prominent bulge ( B/T 0.88) to this galaxy.

However, in this case we could not differentiate the bulge from the bar. In the residual image it is clear that there are signs of two components, in region 1 a more oval shape while in region 2 a boxy one. Region 3 is related to the edge-on disk, since decreases after R/R_p 1.0 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4417 as an S0 Virgo galaxy with lopsided diffuse X-ray emission and a broad tail, interpreted as evidence for ongoing ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: Among the 79 galaxies in our sample, VCC 944 (= NGC 4417) and VCC 1154 (= NGC 4459) stand out as good candidates of on-going ram pressure stripping (Figure fig:fov_detected ), thanks to the relatively deep ACIS exposures of both galaxies. Located at a projected radius of 2.9^ ( 830 kpc, or about 80 of the virial radius of Virgo) from M87 to the southwest, NGC 4417 is an S0 galaxy with log M_*=10.4 . A broad tail-like feature is present to the northwest side of the highly-inclined galactic disk, with an extent of at least 100 ( 8 kpc), whereas there is no detectable diffuse emission on the southeast side. This extended and strongly lopsided morphology can only be understood as the presence of truly diffuse hot gas.

The broad tail appears to emanate from the galactic disk, reminiscent of HI tails in ram pressure stripping spiral galaxies, but here the host galaxy is an S0 that should not contain much cold gas. In fact, our inspection of archival HST/CFHT/2MASS images finds no obvious optical/near-infrared counterpart for the X-ray tails, nor did we find clear evidence of cold gas or dust within the disk.

gmw_00001471.name: NGC 4459
gmw_00001471.overview: NGC 4459 is a lenticular galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0705.4162, arXiv:1110.1275] that hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0109409, arXiv:1811.03160]. It features a central, regularly rotating disk of molecular gas and dust [arXiv:0712.4189, arXiv:0811.1381] that supports active, circumnuclear star formation [arXiv:0811.1381, arXiv:0912.0274].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4459 as a Virgo S0 control galaxy with round stellar isophotes, regular stellar rotation, central velocity dispersion, and very weak ionised gas.

quotes.quote: This S0 galaxy is in the Virgo cluster. It is the control non-active galaxy for NGC 2655. The \ data presented here (Fig.  pair1 ) are part of the \ project paper2 and were therefore already published elsewhere paper3, paper5 . The stellar component of NGC 4459 shows roundish isophotes, and its stellar velocity field presents a very regular rotation pattern the major-axis of which is parallel to the line-of-nodes (PA  77 ). The velocity dispersion rises towards the centre, reaching 200 km s ^ 1 (Fig.  pair1 ).

There is almost no ionised gas emission (our maps have been clipped at 3 ), except for the very centre ( R < 3 ).The emission lines velocity field seems to be dominated by rotation, and the velocity dispersion rises from about 80 km s ^ 1 for R > 3 to 160 km s ^ 1 for \ (200 km s ^ 1 for ) inside 3 . The / \ line ratio rises regularly towards the centre.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4459 has a compact central CO disk aligned with its dust disk, stellar isophotes, and stellar kinematics, with gas in the flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The CO emission from NGC 4459 is also in a central disk, with hints of a double-horned structure in the integrated spectrum (Figure n4459spect ). A double-horned spectrum is typically produced by gas in the flat part of the rotation curve. Emission is detected over a velocity range of 400 \ centered near a systemic velocity of 1210   20  \ (but see below). The CO velocity is thus in good agreement with the stellar velocity of 1232   40  \ from UZC and 1200   10 \ from emsellem04 .

The molecular gas kinematics are thus very well aligned with the stellar kinematics and the stellar isophotes. A tilted ring analysis suggests the systemic velocity to be 1196 2 . The major axis position-velocity diagram (Figure n4459majax ) is very strongly peaked at its maximum velocities, suggesting that the bulk of the molecular gas is located in the flat part of the galaxy's rotation curve.

sources.summary: NGC 4459 is a Virgo lenticular with a relaxed molecular/dust disk and compact 24 micron emission interpreted as active star formation in the gas disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4459 The Virgo Cluster lenticular NGC 4459 clearly shows a relaxed, thin dust disk of semimajor axis 9 \ = 0.7 kpc (Figure n4459 ), and the unsharp-masked HST image resolves the dust into a broad, flocculent outer annulus plus an inner ring of semimajor axis 2 . The molecular gas lies in this regularly rotating disk, as shown in the channel maps presented by ybc08 . The radio continuum emission from NGC 4459 is weak and unresolved-- in fact, it is unusually weak, and is discussed further in Section discussion .

The 24 \ emission is very symmetric and also centrally concentrated but clearly resolved; it is well described by the sum of two exponential disks of scale radii 2.1 \ (0.16 kpc) and 33 \ (2.6 kpc). The more compact disk has 78 of the total 24 \ flux density.

Thus it is possible that the compact 24 \ component and the radio continuum trace star formation activity in the central part of the gas disk (though we cannot rule out AGN activity on morphological grounds alone). Optical spectroscopy provides abundant evidence for active star formation in NGC 4459; the SAURON data show that the molecular disk coincides with an ionized gas disk of low [ O III ] /H ratio sarzi06 , strong Balmer absorption kuntschner06 , and a dynamically cold stellar subpopulation emsellem04 .

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 4459 with NGC 4435, finding its nuclear stellar dispersion agrees with model predictions and the black-hole scaling relation.

quotes.quote: We illustrate how these \ measurements can be useful for constraining the mass \ of supermassive black holes (SBHs) by concentrating on the cases of the lenticular galaxies NGC 4435 and NGC 4459. Whereas this was found to agree with our nuclear \ measurement for NGC 4459, for NGC 4435 the observed \ is remarkably smaller than the predicted one, which further suggests that this galaxy could host an undermassive SBH. We study in more detail NGC 4435 and NGC 4459 in Section  sec:twogal to illustrate how combining Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) photometry along with Spectrographic Areal Unit for Research on Optical Nebulae (SAURON) integral-field spectroscopy can constrain .

Indeed, whereas the values for \ (the stellar velocity dispersion measured within a circular aperture corresponding to the galaxy effective radius r_ e ) obtained by Cappellari2013 using SAURON integral-field spectroscopy are fairly similar for these two galaxies (amounting to = 152.8 7.6 and 158.1 7.9 \ for NGC 4435 and NGC 4459, respectively), our STIS measurements return a much smaller _ ,fix value for NGC 4435 than for NGC 4459 (amounting to 87.3^ +10.2 _ 8.8 and 214.6^ +11.2 _ 12.0 , respectively, see Table  tab:fit ). On the other hand, for NGC 4459 and a r_ e = 43 1 the same aperture correction returns a = 217.9 10.8 \ that is entirely consistent with our actual STIS measurement. Indeed, whereas NGC 4459 shows a \ value consistent with the \ relation Sarzi2001 , the \ of NGC 4435 falls significantly below it Coccato2006 in spite of their similar \ values.

sources.summary: The paper changes NGC 4459 from S0 to E2, describing its nuclear disc, prior anti-truncated-disc claims, neighboring-disc contamination, and preferred bulge plus nuclear-disc fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 4459 was the only galaxy originally labelled as an S0 prior to our changing it to an E2 designation. It contains a nuclear disc in the centre, as reported by Peterson1978 and Krajnovic2011 . Gutierrez2011 has suggested that it contains a Type III anti-truncated disc. Savorgnan2016A broke down the galaxy into a S e rsic bulge, a Gaussian nucleus (for the nuclear disc), and an exponential disc.

However, the neighbouring disc galaxy is likely the main contribution to the light in the `extended disc' at large radii. As we limit the fitting range to within 77 along the major axis, a nuclear exponential disc and a S e rsic bulge are found to suffice.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4459 as a circumnuclear star-forming fast rotator and says available PAH, CO, UV, ionized-gas, and kinematic tracers consistently indicate ongoing central-disk star formation.

quotes.quote: Circumnuclear Star-Forming Fast Rotators The \ galaxies (NGC 524, NGC 4459, NGC 4526, NGC 5838, NGC 5845) appear to represent a completely different physical state than the \ systems described above. In Figure sfrmstar , these galaxies are seen to be those star-forming systems with higher masses and therefore lower molecular gas fractions and specific star formation rates. Consequently, the current star formation events do not dominate the SSP ages, all of which are measured to be intermediate to old in the regions of star formation. Unlike in the \ galaxies, these regions of star formation are spatially very distinct, with all of the star formation, younger SSP ages, ionized gas, molecular gas, dust, and stellar kinematic structures being spatially coincident in well-defined central disks (see the definition of this class in SFdyn ).

In some systems (NGC 4459, NGC 4526), all star formation indicators (PAHs, CO, / , UV, low ionized gas velocity dispersion), where available, paint a consistent picture of on-going star formation in the region of the central disk.

sources.summary: NGC4459 is a Virgo lenticular with nuclear gas, dust, radial M/L variations, possible recent star formation near the ring, and a roughly Kroupa-like IMF.

quotes.quote: NGC4459 is a lenticular galaxy in the Virgo cluster with a nuclear disc of gas and dust, which is discussed in detail above. Analysis of its stellar population suggests some radial M/L variation, with a gentle rise in the M/L with radius, and a M/L dip around the 200pc ring/spiral features. This M/L feature could arise because the relative amount of recent star formation in this region is higher, as the molecular gas is concentrated at this radius after streaming inwards from the spiral spurs. Overall, however, despite these features the IMF appears to be consistent with (or slightly heavier than) Kroupa in this object.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4459 as a large S0 Virgo galaxy with southern diffuse X-ray emission and a tail-like feature, plus a possible semi-confined corona.

quotes.quote: Among the 79 galaxies in our sample, VCC 944 (= NGC 4417) and VCC 1154 (= NGC 4459) stand out as good candidates of on-going ram pressure stripping (Figure fig:fov_detected ), thanks to the relatively deep ACIS exposures of both galaxies.

A similar morphology is witnessed in NGC 4459, which is also an S0 galaxy and one of the largest galaxies ( log M_*=10.9 ) in our sample, located at a projected radius of 1.7^ ( 480 kpc, or about 45 of the virial radius of Virgo) from M87 to the northwest. The diffuse emission extends towards the south of the galaxy, where a tail-like feature slightly swings to the southeast, reaching to a projected distance of 100 . Juranova2020 also presented evidence for this tail-like feature with XMM-Newton observations, albeit at a lower resolution.
We note that the morphology of NGC 4459 may also be interpreted as having a semi-confined corona (to the north), although a sharp cold front is not unambiguously seen due to the limited S/N.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 4459 as Bubble, noting an extended dust disk, two southern ionized-gas blobs, west-east H-alpha emission, and molecular gas aligned with H-alpha and dust lanes.

quotes.quote: NGC 4459 (see Fig. Figure_AB6 ): This unbarred lenticular galaxy has an extended dust disc up to 8 \ from the nucleus and references therein Pagotto2019 . The ionised gas morphology is classified here as Bubble , as it shows two small blobs south from the nucleus, in the direction of the molecular gas kinematic minor axis Young2008 . The blobs are not seen in previous kinematic maps of the ionised gas H line in Sarzi2006 . The H emission is extended up to 10 from the nucleus in the west-east direction in agreement with Koopmann2001 , concentrated in a disc as the H emission Sarzi2006,Sarzi2010 . Various works have studied the molecular gas traced by the CO in the galaxy Combes2007,Young2008,Davis2017 which is detected along an oval shape, similar to that of the H , coincident with dust lanes (visible in our sharp-divided image, see third, right panel in Fig. Figure_AB6 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4459 as kinematically round, with high-q large-opening-angle velocity structure, a central position-angle change, low k5, and possible central component.

quotes.quote: NGC 4459 is a kinematically round map with large opening angles reflected in the high values of q . The position angle of the velocity map changes in the central region but remains constant beyond 7 . In the same region, the velocity reaches a maximum and then decreases, with a hint of a rise towards the edge of the map. Similarly to NGC 2974, the k_5 coefficient is small and below 2 in the investigated region. It is possible that there is a separate central velocity component, indicated by the change in and k_1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4459 as a normal S0 whose molecular disk is aligned with old stellar rotation, grouping it with S0 galaxies sharing similar far-infrared residuals and elevated star formation.

quotes.quote: In the remaining two S0 galaxies observed by Young, Bureau Cappellari (2008) -- NGC 4459 (disk radius: 0.67 kpc, mass: 1.6 10^8 M_ ) and NGC 4526 (disk radius: 1.1 kpc, mass: 5.7 10^8 M_ ) -- the molecular disks are well-aligned with the rotating system of old stars. These appear to be more normal S0 galaxies. NGC 4459 and 4526, together with NGC 3156 and 3489, form a tight group of four S0 galaxies in Figure 5 having nearly the same L_ FIR /L_ Ks and 24 m residuals, but with SFRs greater than normal elliptical galaxies. It is remarkable that among these four S0 galaxies both L_ FIR and L_ Ks vary by about an order of magnitude yet they share the same location in Figure 5.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4459's outer profile as III-d and describes spiral structure, dust features, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4459 (III-d; SA()0 ^+ ) : This object has a bright nucleus and a dust ring out to 8 (620 pc). In fact this ``ring'' has a very fine multi-armed spiral structure, clearly visible in HST ACS images see Fig. 7 of ferrarese06 . This structure gives the outer part of the bulge (which should really be termed a pseudobulge; kormendy04 Kormendy Kennicutt \ 2004) a ring-like appearance and a minimum in the ellipticity, as well as a small jump in the brightness profile. From 55 (4.3 kpc) to 270 (21 kpc) the profile is a double exponential, with the break at 120 (9.3 kpc); since the ellipticity is roughly constant out to at least 200 (16 kpc), we judge this to be a III-d profile. michard94 refer to this galaxy as a ``well-known S0 dominated by a disk''.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4459 as a Virgo S0 galaxy with a massive black hole and major-axis kinematic behavior consistent with a comparatively face-on or thick disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4459 is an S0 ^+ () galaxy ( M_B = --20.0) also located in Virgo and harbouring a 7.3 10^7 M _ black hole (Sarzi et al. \ 2001). The same velocity behavior as in NGC 3384 and NGC 4526 is observed along the major axis (see also Peterson 1978), although the minimum is shallower. The isovelocity contours are also less skewed, either indicating that the disk is seen more face-on or that it is intrinsically thicker (or both). This is supported by the absence of a clear disk signature in the velocity dispersion map.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC4459 as a CO-detected early-type galaxy with a large regular dust disk and properties similar to the especially CO-rich NGC 4526, though less extreme.

quotes.quote: NGC4459 and NGC4526 were detected with smaller signal-to-noise ratios with the Kitt Peak 12 m telescope by sw89 . Their larger beam suggests that the CO emission is not extended much beyond our own 23 beam.

The dust morphologies across the sample range from well developed large disks ( 10 diameter; e.g. \ NGC2685, NGC3032, NGC4459 and NGC4526) to small nuclear features ( 5 ; e.g. \ NGC3156, NGC4150 and NGC4477) and even larger scale irregular patches or lanes (e.g. \ NGC3156, NGC4150, NGC3489 and NGC4278).
The properties of NGC 4459 are similar though not as extreme.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4459 as a Virgo lenticular galaxy and compares two previous structural classifications and bulge/disk or Sersic measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC4459 is a Virgo lenticular galaxy. H09 modelled NGC4459 with two components (bulge/disk n_ bulge =2.5 ). KF09 classified NGC4459 as an elliptical galaxy and estimated a S e rsic index of n=3.16 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC4459 as a low-temperature early-type galaxy, among objects with preliminary SFR estimates, and with a double-peaked profile making SLED predictions more uncertain.

quotes.quote: The kinetic temperatures obtained for the best-fit models are either low ( T_ K =10 20 K for IC1024, NGC1222, NGC1266, NGC2764, NGC3032, NGC3665, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694, NGC4710 and NGC5866), intermediate ( 30 70 K for IC0676, NGC3607, PGC058114 and UGC09519) or high ( 120 K for NGC6014 and 150 K for NGC7465).

\ (in prep.) derived preliminary star formation rates for 8 of the molecular gas-rich ETGs in our study (NGC1266, NGC3032, NGC3607, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694 and NGC5866), with SFRs ranging from 0.06 to 0.59 M_ yr ^ 1 (see Table tab:5 ).

We know that for galaxies such as NGC3032, NGC3607, NGC3665, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC5866 and NGC6014, a double-peaked line profile is more realistic, so for these sources the SLEDs shown in Figure fig:9 are more uncertain. figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 Correlations with global parameters In Paper XI, ratios of molecular lines discussed in this paper were shown to correlate with some global physical parameters of the sample galaxies (e.g.

gmw_00001472.name: IRAS 13052-5711
gmw_00001472.overview: IRAS 13052-5711 is a likely starburst galaxy with a star formation rate of approximately 29.38 solar masses per year [arXiv:2307.16107]. It exhibits nonvariable GeV emission detected by the Fermi Large Area Telescope, making it the most distant starburst galaxy candidate discovered to date [arXiv:2307.16107].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 13052-5711 as a likely distant starburst galaxy detected by Fermi-LAT, with nonvariable GeV emission, elevated luminosity, and a hadronic spectral interpretation.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: First, we investigated the location of IRAS 13052-5711 is (R.A., decl. = 197 ^ .078, -57 ^ .458)

quotes.quote: A likely starburst galaxy (SBG), IRAS 13052-5711, which is the most distant SBG candidate discovered to date, was found by analyzing 14.4 years of data from the Fermi large-area telescope (Fermi-LAT). This SBG's significance level is approximately 6.55 in the 0.1-500 GeV band. Its spatial position is close to that of 4FGL J1308.9-5730, determined from the Fermi large telescope fourth-source Catalog (4FGL). Its power-law spectral index is approximately 2.1, and its light curve (LC) for 14.4 years has no significant variability. These characteristics are highly similar to those of SBGs found in the past.

We calculate the SBG's star formation rate (SFR) to be 29.38 M_ \ yr^ 1 , which is within the SFR range of SBGs found to date. Therefore, IRAS 13052-5711 is considered to be a likely SBG. In addition, its 0.1-500 GeV luminosity is (3.28 0.67) 10^ 42 \ erg \ s^ 1 , which deviates from the empirical relationship of the ray luminosity and the total infrared luminosity.

gmw_00001473.name: NGC 4650A
gmw_00001473.overview: NGC 4650A is a prototype polar disk galaxy composed of a central rotationally supported host spheroid and a perpendicular, extended polar disk [arXiv:1001.3083, arXiv:1402.7325]. This polar structure exhibits a sub-solar metallicity with a flat gradient [arXiv:1104.2052, arXiv:1402.7325] and hosts a milder, declining rate of star formation [arXiv:1511.05166].

sources.summary: MUSE data show NGC 4650A contains two perpendicular disks with distinct central kinematics, supporting a formation history involving accretion of material with different angular momentum.

quotes.quote: The polar disk galaxy NGC 4650A was observed during the commissioning of the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) at the ESO Very Large Telescope to obtain the first 2D map of the velocity and velocity dispersion for both stars and gas. The new MUSE data allow the analysis of the structure and kinematics towards the central regions of NGC 4650A, where the two components co-exist. These regions were unexplored by the previous long-slit literature data available for this galaxy. The stellar velocity field shows that there are two main directions of rotation, one along the host galaxy major axis ( P.A.=67 degrees) and the other along the polar disk ( P.A.=160 degrees).

The extended view of NGC 4650A given by the MUSE data is a galaxy made of two perpendicular disks that remain distinct and drive the kinematics right into the very centre of this object. In order to match this observed structure for NGC 4650A, we constructed a multicomponent mass model made by the combined projection of two disks. By comparing the observations with the 2D kinematics derived from the model, we found that the modelled mass distribution in these two disks can, on average, account for the complex kinematics revealed by the MUSE data, also in the central regions of the galaxy where the two components coexist. This result is a strong constraint on the dynamics and formation history of this galaxy; it further supports the idea that polar disk galaxies like NGC 4650A were formed through the accretion of material that has different angular momentum.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4650A as a massive polar disk galaxy with young polar-disk clusters, milder declining star formation, and incomplete constraints on its earliest formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4650A is a massive polar disk galaxy (see Fig. fig:selection , right). It is composed of a puffed up disk with no bulge as a central host galaxy HG; Iodice02 , and a polar structure that is rotationally supported and has young stellar population PRG catalog; Whitmore90 . The polar ring has the characteristic of a disk arnaboldi97,Iodice02 . perform a polygon photometry using optical and near-infrared data to estimate the age of the polar disk of < 0.5 Gyr and an age of 1 - 3 Gyr for the host galaxy in comparison with simple stellar population (SSP) models.

We reconstruct the star formation history of the two PRGs and find strong peaks in the star formation rate (SFR, 200M _ /yr) in NGC 3808 B, while NGC 4650 A shows milder (declining) star formation (SFR <10M_ /yr). This difference may support different evolutionary paths between these PRGs. The spatial distribution, masses, and peak star formation epoch of the clusters in NGC 3808 suggest for a tidally triggered star formation. Incompleteness at old ages prevents us from probing the SFR at earlier epochs of NGC 4650 A, where we observe the fading tail of CSS formation.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC4650A as a well-studied polar disk galaxy with perpendicular components, disk-like polar structure, low metallicity, and revised dynamical constraints on its dark halo.

quotes.quote: NGC4650A is a polar disk galaxy: this is a peculiar object composed by a central spheroidal component, the host galaxy (HG), and an extended disk made up by gas, stars and dust, which orbits nearly perpendicular to the plane of the central galaxy. The existence of two decoupled components of the angular momentum let this object the ideal laboratory i) to study gravitational interactions and merging and ii) to constrain the 3D shape of its dark matter halo.

NGC4650A is one of the best-investigated object among the class of PRGs, in the South hemisphere. Its luminous components, inner spheroid and polar structure, have been studied with optical and near-infrared (NIR) photometry and spectroscopy, and in the radio, HI 21 cm line and continuum. The polar structure in NGC 4650A has been shown to be a disk, rather than a ring. Its stars and dust can be reliably traced inward within the stellar spheroid to 1.2 kpc radius from the galaxy nucleus ( Iod02 ; Gal02 ).

Main results show i) that NGC4650A has metallicity lower than spiral galaxy disks of the same total luminosity (see Fig. fig1 left panel), where Z= 0.35 Z_ , which is consistent with values predicted for the formation of disks by cold accretion processes; ii) the absence of any metallicity gradient along the polar disk (see Fig.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4650A as a prototype polar-ring/disk galaxy whose massive polar disk has low metallicity and a flat gradient consistent with later metal-poor gas accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4650A is the prototype for PRGs (see Fig. slit ). Its luminous components, inner spheroid and polar structure were studied in optical and near-infrared (NIR) photometry, spectroscopy, and in the radio emission, HI 21 cm line and continuum ( Arn97 , Gal02 , Iod02 , Swa03 , Iod06 ). The polar structure in NGC 4650A is a disk, very similar to that of late-type spirals or LSB galaxies, rather than a ring. The polar disk stars and dust can be reliably traced to 1.2 kpc radius from the galaxy nucleus, and the surface brightness profiles have an exponential decrease ( Iod02 , Gal02 ).

The average metallicity values for the polar disk in NGC 4650A is Z=0.2 Z_ , and it is lower that the values measured for ordinary spirals of similar luminosity. Moreover the gradient of the metallicity is flat along the polar disk major axis, which implies none or negligible metal enrichment from the stars in the older central spheroid. The low metallicity value in the polar disk NGC 4650A and the flat metallicity gradient are both consistent with a later infall of metal-poor gas, as expected in the cold accretion processes.

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 4650A lacks clear evidence for a merger-built stellar halo, is well described by host plus polar structure, and is more likely produced by tidal accretion.

quotes.quote: Deep HST observations also find stars at large radii, but a detailed analysis is required to decide whether they belong to the faintest regions of the polar ring and the host galaxy or form a third component. Iodice et al. (2002a) have shown that the structure of NGC 4650A is well described, even at large radii, by a two-component model. This suggests that no stellar halo is required to account for the observed luminosity. Yet, we still do not know if really no halo is present, or if the halo is too faint to be detected.

Thus, the probability that a merging event has resulted in NGC 4650A is strongly reduced, so that NGC 4650A is much likely to be the result of a tidal accretion event. Polar disk or polar ring? Iodice (2001), and Iodice et al. (2002a) have proposed that the polar structure of NGC 4650A is more likely a disk than a ring.

sources.summary: The paper reports stellar and gas kinematics of NGC 4650A and argues its massive disklike polar ring supports a dissipational polar merger.

quotes.quote: Rubin Department of Terrestrial Magnetism, Carnegie Institution of Washington, 5241 Broad Branch Rd NW, Washington DC 20015; rubin@dtm.ciw.edu We present the first measurement of the stellar kinematics in the polar ring of NGC 4650A. There is well defined rotation, with the stars and gas rotating in the same direction, and with similar amplitude. The gaseous and stellar kinematics suggest an approximately flat rotation curve, providing further support for the hypothesis that the polar material resides in a disk rather than in a ring.

Its large luminous mass, combined with its disklike morphology, suggest the polar ring in NGC 4650A is a disk galaxy that is the victim of a dissipational polar merger of disks of approximately similar masses (Bekki 1998). Further support for a merger scenario comes from the location of NGC 4650A in a chain of galaxies in the Centaurus cluster (Sersic 1968). It seems unlikely that a single accretion event could transfer the required large mass, or that extended accretion over a period of time could produce sufficient coherence to form a ring. Although we did not detect counterrotating star streams in the polar ring of NGC 4650A, which would have been more exciting, we are continuing the study of this fascinating object.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4650A as a prototype polar-ring galaxy favoring accretion, with no detected stellar halo, a massive polar component, and kinematics useful for constraining its gravitational potential.

quotes.quote: Very massive polar rings can form through accretion, with polar components of comparable mass (or even larger) to the host component The prototype NGC 4650A is a good case for accretion, since no stellar halo is detected around the galaxy. Such a stellar halo is expected in the merging scenario to come from the dispersed stars from the destroyed satellite. The polar component is quite massive, 8 10 ^9 M _ of HI gas and 4 10 ^9 M _ in stars.

The stellar kinematics in NGC 4650A for instance does not reveal counter-rotation. A flat rotation curve in observed in the polar ring, which confirms that it is more a full plane than a ring. With the help of large telescopes, it is possible to have now much more precise and accurate kinematical information on the remote polar ring, as shown by the new VLT-FORS2 data on NGC 4650A (Iodice et al 2005). The detailed stellar kinematics on the major and minor axis of the host galaxy reveal a flat velocity dispersion, with a slight drop in the center, and will allow in the near future a better determination of the gravitational potential and its 3D shape.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4650A as a prototype polar disk galaxy, measuring spheroid kinematics, describing its polar structure, and favoring slow accretion as its formation path.

quotes.quote: We have obtained high angular resolution, high signal-to-noise spectra of the Calcium triplet absorption lines on the photometric axes of the stellar spheroid in the polar disk galaxy NGC 4650A. Along the major axis, the observed rotation and velocity dispersion measurements show the presence of a kinematically decoupled nucleus, and a flat velocity dispersion profile.

The new high resolution kinematic data suggest that the stellar component in NGC 4650A resembles a nearly-exponential oblate spheroid supported by rotation. The main implications of these results on the previous mass models for NGC 4650A are discussed. Moreover, the new kinematic data set constraints on current models for the formation scenarios of Polar Ring Galaxies (PRGs), supporting a slow accretion rather then a secondary strong dissipative event.

NGC 4650A is the prototype for PRGs. Its luminous components, inner spheroid and polar structure, have been studied with optical and near-infrared (NIR) photometry, optical spectroscopy, and in the radio ( \ 21 cm line and continuum).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4650A as a key polar-ring example, discussing its low gas metallicity, halo shape, and possible stable spiral structure in the polar ring.

quotes.quote: In this scenario, Maccio2006 and Brook2008 were able to construct an object very similar to the well-known PRG NGC 4650A using cosmological hydrodynamic simulations. Recently, additional observational arguments supporting the model of accretion from gas filaments were presented. For instance, Spavone2010 suggested that NGC 4650A has the gas metallicity lower than a spiral galaxy disc of the same total luminosity.

The complexity of the problem and the need for a detailed analysis of each galaxy is illustrated by the case of NGC4650A. Whitmore1987 have earlier noted that the gravitational potential of the galaxy is almost spherical, but the analysis of kinematics of more extended structures indicates a very strong oblateness of the halo, oriented in accordance with the polar ring Iodice2010 .
Numerical hydrodynamic models by Theis2006 also indicate the possibility of formation of a stable spiral structure in the polar ring of NGC4650A.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4650A as the observed prototype for polar-ring formation, noting its HST image, ring kinematics around a central S0, and similarity to a simulated cold-accretion polar ring.

quotes.quote: Within a large cosmological hydrodynamical simulation we find a system that closely resembles the classic polar ring galaxy NGC 4650A.

Figure fig:hub (a) shows the hubble space telescope image of NGC 4650A, a classic example of a polar ring galaxy Sersic1967 . Recent observations of its kinematics confirms that the ring of stars and gas rotates around the central S0 galaxy Arnaboldi97,Gallagher2002 . Figure fig:hub (b-d) show close up images of the galaxy that is forming at the centre of the halo in Figure fig:big . The central galaxy is a rotationally supported disk that could be classified as an S0 due to its featureless ``thick disk'' morphology. The galaxy has an extended ring of gas (and stars) in near circular motion perpendicular to the disk similar to NGC 4650A.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4650A as the prototype wide polar-ring galaxy, with polar-disk HII abundances, low metallicity, no metallicity gradient, and evidence used to test cold accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC4650A. NGC4650A is the prototype of the wide Polar Ring Galaxies (PRGs) and has been studied in detail to understand its formation and physical properties ( Arn97 ; Gal02 ; Iod02 ; Swa03 ; Bou03 ; Iod06 ) in order to constrain many of the processes at work during galaxy interactions and merging. Very recently, , by using deep longslit spectroscopy with FORS2@ESO-VLT, studied the abundance ratios and metallicities of the HII regions associated to the polar disk in NGC4650A, in order to test the cold accretion scenario for this object. The chemical abundance is one of the key parameters that can be estimated in a galaxy disk and directly compared with the theoretical predictions: in fact, if the gas is essentially primordial it should have a very low abundance of heavy elements. Main results obtained for NGC4650A show i) that it has metallicity lower than spiral galaxy disks of the same total luminosity, where Z = 0.2Z_ , which is consistent with values predicted for the formation of disks by cold accretion processes Age09 ; ii) the absence of any metallicity gradient along the polar disk, which suggests that the metal enrichment is not influenced by the stellar evolution of the older central spheroid and, thus, the disk was formed later by the infall of metal-poor gas from outside which is still forming the disk.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 4650A as the best-studied wide polar-disk galaxy, with a massive extended polar structure, rotating spheroid, subsolar flat metallicity, and evidence favoring cold gas accretion.

quotes.quote: The wide polar disk galaxy NGC 4650A is the best studied PRG, since a large wealth of data are available for this galaxy (see Sect.  obs ), including H I data Arnaboldi1997 , near-infrared and optical photometry Iodice02 , and long slit kinematics Swaters2003, Iodice2006 . Thus, for this object all the key physical parameters were derived and it can be considered the first test case to discriminate among different formation scenarios for PRGs. NGC 4650A has a large baryonic mass in the polar structure, which is equal or even larger than that in the HG see also Tab.  tab1 Iodice02 . The central spheroid is a rotationally-supported system with the maximum rotation velocity v_ rot 80 - 100  km s ^ 1 Iodice2006 . The polar disk has a sub-solar metallicity Z = 0.2 Z _ and there is no metallicity gradient along this component see also Fig.  fig3 Spavone2010 .

By comparing the above observed quantities with those expected by each formation mechanism, the following conclusions were derived for NGC 4650A: 1) the merging scenario is ruled out because, according to simulations e.g., Bou05 , it fails to form massive polar disk around an HG with rotation velocities as large as observed along the HG major axis; 2) both the large baryonic mass in the polar structure and its large extension can not be reconciled with a polar disk formed via the gradual disruption of a dwarf satellite galaxy; 3) the tidal accretion could form a such massive and extended polar disk if the donor galaxy has a large amount of gas at large radii (where it is not strongly gravitationally bound) and outside the stellar disk e.g., Bou03 ; 4) the measured metallicity and its flat gradient, together with the polar disk and HG morphologies, kinematics, gas content, and stellar ages, turn to be consistent with the predictions by Snaith2012 for a polar disk formed through the accretion of external cold gas from cosmic web filaments Spavone2010 .

gmw_00001474.name: NGC 3430
gmw_00001474.overview: NGC 3430 is a nearly face-on SBc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1706.01887, arXiv:1302.5011] that forms a pair with NGC 3424 [arXiv:1111.4462]. It features an extended ultraviolet disk [arXiv:1111.4462, arXiv:1302.5011] and exhibits a relatively undisturbed morphology characteristic of an early-stage interaction [arXiv:1302.5011].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3430 as a SIGS spiral-pair example whose SED and extended UV disk show that IR-dependent star-formation prescriptions can miss recent star formation.

quotes.quote: Figure 1 shows a subset of these observations for a typical sample pair of galaxies, NGC 3430 (top) and NGC 3424 (bottom). The apparent extent of a galaxy can vary significantly between the ultraviolet and the infrared.
Fig. 2 presents two typical SEDs from the subsample: NGC 3430, part of a spiral galaxy pair and M51B, a dwarf galaxy interacting with M51A. These SEDs demonstrate some of the variety observed.

However, NGC 3430 is a counter-example: its extended UV disk suggests that the IR-dependent prescriptions, which generally assume optical thickness, miss a significant portion of the recent star formation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3430 as the host of iPTF15eqv, adopting a 30.4 Mpc distance, classifying it as an SBc spiral, and estimating its SDSS-based photometric metallicity.

quotes.quote: Distance estimates to the host galaxy NGC 3430 range from 15.4 Mpc Theureau07 to 32.9 Mpc Lagattuta13 . We adopt an estimate of 30.4 Mpc with distance modulus = 32.41 0.43 Sorce14 . NGC 3430 is classified as a Type SBc spiral by , and also hosted the Type II SN 2004ez Nakano04,Filippenko04 .

NGC 3430 is in the imaging footprint of the SDSS, which allowed us to make use of the SDSS-calibrated Luminosity-Metallicity-Color relation (LZC) of to infer an independent estimate of galaxy metallicity from optical luminosity and color. Using the extinction corrected absolute magnitudes of the host galaxy in the SDSS DR12 cModel magnitudes Schlafly11,SDSSDR12 , M _g=-19.439 mag and M _r=-20.042 mag, the LZC photometric metallicity for NGC 3430 is ( O / H )+12=8.68 with a characteristic scatter of 0.07 dex in the N2 diagnostic calibration of the LZC relation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3430 as a nearly face-on galaxy with an extended UV disk, relatively undisturbed early-interaction morphology, and poorly constrained infrared SED peak.

quotes.quote: NGC 3430: Seen nearly face-on, this galaxy has a large and extended UV disk. This system provides a nice example of a system fairly early in the interaction sequence and hence fairly undisturbed morphologically. The IR peak of its SED is not very well constrained as this galaxy was not observed by PACS and did not have MIPS 70 and 160 fluxes available.

gmw_00001475.name: Arp 151
gmw_00001475.overview: Arp 151 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1505.04805, arXiv:1803.02318] hosting a highly variable active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1111.6604, arXiv:1502.02031]. Its broad-line region is modeled as an inclined thick disk [arXiv:1505.04805, arXiv:1803.02318] with dynamics dominated by infalling gas [arXiv:1008.1057, arXiv:1505.04805] around a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1104.4794, arXiv:1803.02318].

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 151, the most variable LAMP object, to test the expected inverse relation between H line width and continuum luminosity after host-continuum subtraction.

quotes.quote: In this section, we test this assumption by studying the relation between the line width and continuum luminosity from individual SE spectra of Arp 151, the object with the highest variability during the LAMP campaign. In Figure fig:arp151_variation we present the time variation of the line width and luminosity of Arp 151. Line width and luminosity are inversely correlated, although the variability amplitude is smaller in luminosity than in line width. Ideally, the luminosity variability should be four times as large as the line-width variability ( 0.042 0.001 dex and 0.027 0.003 dex, respectively, for \ and ). However, one must take into account the residual contamination from nonvariable sources to the observed continuum. In fact, the amplitude of the luminosity variability is significantly smaller than expected based on the line-width variability if the total luminosity is used (bottom panel). In contrast, providing a validation of our constant continuum subtraction procedure, the variability amplitude of the nuclear continuum is consistent with that expected from the line width, as we will further quantify below.

In Figure fig:arp151_virial , we compare measured luminosities and line widths in order to test whether they obey the expected relation V^2 L^ 0.5 . The continuum variation is shifted by the measured time lag, 4 days, to account for the time delay between the central engine and BLR variations and then matched with the corresponding epochs of line-width variations. Note that densely sampled light curves are required for this correction. As expected, the observed correlation between total flux L_ 5100,t and line width is steeper than the theoretical correlation.

sources.summary: The paper finds Arp 151 has the highest-quality LAMP 2008 light curves, decent RLI fits, a broad integrated response around four days, and a velocity-delay response centered near five days.

quotes.quote: Arp 151 Results for Arp 151 are presented in Fig. fig:result-arp151 for and Fig. fig:result-arp151-halpha and fig:result-arp151-hgamma for and respectively. Light curves for Arp 151 are the most variable and highest quality of the LAMP 2008 dataset, which is why we include only and for this object. RLI provides decent fits to the Arp 151 light curves, with some notable outliers at later epochs (e.g. , Fig. fig:result-arp151 ).

The integrated response function for (Fig. fig:result-arp151 ) has a plateau from 0 days out to about 7 days where it starts to decrease. The shape of the response function is broader than what is found by maximum entropy methods 2010ApJ...720L..46B . We find a median time delay of = 4.0^ +0.7 _ 0.8 days, consistent with results from cross-correlation ( = 4.0^ +0.5 _ 0.7 days) and ( = 3.6^ +0.7 _ 0.2 days).

The velocity--delay map for (Fig. fig:result-arp151 ) shows that the bulk of the response is centred on the emission line with a time delay of about 5 days, similar to the median delay calculated from the integrated response. There seems to be an area of increased response redward of the line centre from 0 - 1000 km / s .

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 151 as a test case for modeling AGN broad-line-region geometry and dynamics from single-epoch spectra, comparing results with reverberation-mapping campaigns.

quotes.quote: We select the AGN Arp 151 (also known as Mrk 40) for which three reverberation mapping campaigns have already been carried out ( pancoast18 and references therein). We extract single-epoch spectra from one of the multi-epoch monitoring datasets (see Section sec:data ) and model them individually using our modified version of the model. Each single-epoch spectrum that is modelled results in a predicted set of geometry and dynamics parameters for the BLR. The full monitoring Arp 151 dataset has been modelled with previous implementations of the model to determine the geometry and dynamics of the BLR ( brewer11 , pancoast14b , pancoast18 ). The existence of a monitoring dataset and the associated modelling results are essential in order to evaluate the present model's performance as we can compare the BLR parameters inferred from single-epoch modelling with the BLR parameters obtained from the full monitoring dataset modelling ( pancoast14b , pancoast18 ).

The BLR parameters can be divided into three groups, based on our analysis for the case of Arp 151: 1) parameters that are well constrained by single-epoch spectra; 2) parameters that depend on the epoch; 3) parameters that cannot be constrained by single-epoch spectra. We discuss each of these groups below. BLR parameters constrained by single-epoch spectra Some of the inferred parameters show a consistent trend towards the full-lightcurve result, independently of the epoch chosen: _ o , _ i , , f_ flow , _ e .

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 40 as an extreme H-lag case where strong red-wing/blue-wing differences imply near-radial infall, with disk warping as an alternative explanation.

quotes.quote: These theoretical curves can be compared with the four best examples of a strong velocity dependence in the H lag: the 1993-1995 monitoring of Mrk 6 sergeev+99 , the 2007 monitoring of NGC 3516 and NGC 3227 denney+09b , and the 2008 monitoring of Mrk 40 bentz+08 . NGC 3227 shows the opposite sign of the velocity dependence to the other three, so it has been plotted with the velocity scale reversed. The four AGNs have different line widths and total lags. To facilitate comparisons the lags of both observations and models have been normalized to a 15-day lag at zero velocity.

To reproduce the strong red-wing/blue-wing difference the inflow would need to be close to radial freefall ( bentz+10 have independently obtained the same result for Mrk 40 = Arp 151). BL-RESP shows that the velocity dependence of the lag for pure infall is essentially indistinguishable from the off-axis model shown in Fig.

Such warping is well-known in Galactic X-ray binaries such as SS 433 and Her X-1 (see ogilvie+dubus01 for discussion) and there are a number of possible mechanisms which could produce a similar warping in AGNs kinney+00 . bentz+10 have recently shown that an alternative explanation of the apparent strong inflow signature in Mrk 40 could be anisotropic illumination of the BLR due to warping of the inner accretion disk.

sources.summary: The review discusses Arp 151 as a Seyfert with deconvolved broad-line responses and direct modeling favoring an inclined thick-disk BLR with inflow.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:velres shows an example of the deconvolved response of the broad optical recombination lines in the spectrum of the Seyfert galaxy Arp 151 bentz10 . The differences in expected mean time delays for the lines is immediately apparent, and also interesting is the strong prompt response in the red wings of the Balmer lines compared to the lack of prompt response in the blue wings of those same emission lines. An asymmetric response such as this could be produced by either rotating gas with enhanced emission in one location (such as a hot spot or warp), or by infalling gas, or some combination of these simplistic models.

Similar asymmetries are also seen in the deconvolved responses of a handful of additional AGNs grier13b . Direct modeling, on the other hand, tests the data against specific geometric and kinematic models to constrain the family of models that best represent the observations. For the Arp 151 dataset above, direct modeling results prefer a thick disk BLR geometry, inclined at 25 \ to the observer's line of sight, and the kinematics are dominated by inflow with some contribution from rotation pancoast14 , in general agreement with the interpretation of the deconvolution results.

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 151 as a reverberation-mapping test case, recovering a transfer function and mean H-line lag consistent with earlier CCF, JAVELIN, and dynamical-modeling results.

quotes.quote: Arp 151 For the sake of comparison with the previous results, we use the B- band photometric light curve of Arp 151 as a surrogate of the continuum as in and . The magnitudes are first converted into fluxes.

The top panels of Figure  fig_example present the reconstruction for the light curves of the continuum and H line and the best recovered transfer function for Arp 151. The mean time lag from Equation ( eqn_lag ) is _ m = 3.33 0.26 days, consistent with the previous results from CCF analysis, _ CCF =4.08 0.60 days ( Bentz2009 ), and the JAVELIN method of , _ JAV =3.68 0.36 days ( Grier2013a ).

Our result well matches that from the dynamical modeling method but differs from the RLI result, which has a plateau from 0 to about 7 days. The shape of our obtained transfer function also coincides with that from the maximum entropy method (see Figure 1 in Bentz2010 ).

sources.summary: The paper applies TLDR to Arp 151 reverberation data, reconstructing its H velocity-delay map from LAMP spectra and Johnson B continuum data and finding broad agreement with MEM and RLI maps.

quotes.quote: As a final indication of the utility of TLDR, an initial reconstruction of the VDM for the H feature of Arp 151 from LAMP 2009ApJ...705..199B is provided. Using the continuum subtracted model spectra and the Johnson B continuum data used in for the Maximum Entropy Method reconstruction presented in , an initial VDM was calculated in accordance with Section SubSecInitialVDM with a smoothing hyper-parameter of 2.0 10^6 . The VDM was set up with the pixel spectral width matching the 2.0 steps of the original spectral observations and a temporal width of 1.0 days matching the global average sampling frequency of the continuum and spetral observations.

For this reconstruction, TLDR was run for 3.0 10^4 iterations with the parameters listed in Table tab:RecParams , selected from a grid of possible regularization hyper-parameters by proximity to ^2=1.0 . The reconstruction terminated with a reduced ^2 of 1.02, yielding the VDM shown in Fig. fig:RDVDM . Visual comparison between the VDM reconstructed with TLDR and those recovered by the Maximum Entropy Method 2010ApJ...720L..46B and Regularized Linear Inversion 2015MNRAS.454..144S largely agree.

sources.summary: The paper models reverberation data for the active nucleus Arp 151, inferring BLR geometry and a central black-hole mass of about 10^6.51 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We present a direct measurement of the mass of a black hole in an active galactic nucleus (Arp 151) based on the motion of the gas responsible for the broad emission lines. By analyzing and modeling spectroscopic and photometric time series, we find that the gas is well described by a disk or torus with an average radius of 3.99 1.25 light days and an opening angle of 68.9 ^ + 21.4 _ 17.2 degrees, viewed at an inclination angle of 67.8 7.8 degrees (that is, closer to face-on than edge-on). The black hole mass is inferred to be 10^ 6.51 0.28 M _ .

We overcome these difficulties by applying a new framework to modeling high-quality spectrophotometric monitoring data of the Type 1 active nucleus of Arp 151. The combination of dynamical models with high-quality data allows us to characterize the structure of the BLR and achieve a direct determination of the mass of a supermassive black hole using reverberation mapping.

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 151 reverberation-mapping data to build realistic simulated AGN datasets, including continuum cadence, emission-line spectra, noise, and instrumental resolution.

quotes.quote: In order to generate realistic simulated reverberation mapping datasets, we use the LAMP 2008 dataset of \ emission for Arp 151 walsh09, bentz09 to determine the sampling cadence, flux errors, instrumental smoothing, and approximate scale of the BLR. The Arp 151 dataset includes a B band continuum light curve and a time series of \ emission line profiles, where the broad and narrow \ flux is isolated from the spectrum using spectral decomposition techniques as described by park12a . As described in Section sect_dnest , the simulated datasets are created by first generating a model of the Arp 151 continuum light curve using Gaussian processes and sampling that model continuum light curve with the same cadence as for Arp 151.

We then add Gaussian noise to the continuum light curve using the error vector of the Arp 151 light curve. Next we set fixed the BLR geometry and dynamics model parameters to the values found in Table table_simparams and sample the \ emission line profile at the times given by the Arp 151 spectral dataset.

sources.summary: The paper models seven years of reverberation mapping for Seyfert 1 galaxy Arp 151, finding a stable face-on thick-disk BLR whose size doubled while inflow-dominated dynamics stayed consistent.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1 galaxy Arp 151 was monitored as part of three reverberation mapping campaigns spanning 2008-2015 . We present modeling of these velocity-resolved reverberation mapping datasets using a geometric and dynamical model for the broad line region (BLR). By modeling each of the three datasets independently, we infer the evolution of the BLR structure in Arp 151 over a total of seven years and constrain the systematic uncertainties in non-varying parameters such as the black hole mass. We find that the BLR geometry of a thick disk viewed close to face-on is stable over this time, although the size of the BLR grows by a factor of 2 . The dynamics of the BLR are dominated by inflow and the inferred black hole mass is consistent for the three datasets, despite the increase in BLR size.

gmw_00001476.name: SBS 1116+583A
gmw_00001476.overview: SBS 1116+583A is an active galactic nucleus with noisy emission-line light curves [arXiv:1502.02031]. Reverberation mapping of the source reveals a short mean time lag of approximately 2 to 2.5 days [arXiv:1502.02031, arXiv:1608.03741].

sources.summary: The paper cautions that SBS 1116+583A has noisy emission-line light curves, but finds a short integrated delay and a multimodal velocity-delay response with a 10-day central component.

quotes.quote: SBS 1116+583A Results for SBS 1116+583A are presented in Fig. fig:result-sbs1116 . The emission line light curve of SBS 1116+583A lacks significant features above the noise level. As a result the RLI fit does not match the emission line light curve well. The results for this object, including the velocity--delay map, should thus be interpreted with caution. The integrated response function prefers a relatively short time delay of = 2.0^ +1.1 _ 0.6 days, which is slightly shorter, but fully consistent with cross-correlation ( = 2.3^ +0.6 _ 0.5 days) and ( = 2.4^ +0.9 _ 0.9 days).

The velocity-resolved response map for SBS 1116+583A (Fig. fig:result-sbs1116 ) shows a multimodal response map with a broad response component at short time delays, around 2 days, and an additional isolated response at 10 days at the centre of the line. This is a good example of the flexibility of RLI to reconstruct complicated velocity--delay maps.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SBS 1116+583A with the new reverberation method, finding a 2.50-day mean lag and a transfer function without the second RLI component.

quotes.quote: SBS 1116+583A As we have done for for Arp 151, we use the B- band photometric light curve for this object. The range of the time lag in the calculations is set to (0-10) days, and the best number of Gaussian functions is K=5 according to the AICc.

The obtained mean time lag is _ m =2.50 0.70 days, which is again well consistent with the results based on CCF analysis, _ CCF =2.38 0.58 days ( Bentz2009 ), and the JAVELIN method, _ JAV =2.46 0.93 days ( Grier2013a ). The middle panel of Figure fig_tf compares the derived transfer functions from various methods. The RLI method yields a bimodal transfer function with a response component of around 2 days and an additional component of around 8 days ( Skielboe2015 ).

However, both the transfer functions obtained in this work and those from the dynamical modeling do not show the second component. An inspection of the reconstructed H light curves (see Figure 10 in Skielboe2015 ) indicates that our results better match the observations.
